On A100 GPU, at $32\times$ sparsity, incorporating HashAttention reduces attention latency by up to $4.3\times$ in GPT-FAST and $2.54\times$ in FlashDecode, and achieves up to $3.12\times$ higher throughput for GPT-FAST.
abstract_zh: 利用长上下文对先进AI系统至关重要，但注意力计算带来了可扩展性挑战。尽管缩放点积注意力（SDPA）存在令牌稀疏性，即仅有少数关键令牌对输出有显著贡献，但利用这种稀疏性仍面临挑战。现有方法要么存在质量下降问题，要么需要大量额外资源。我们证明，识别关键令牌属于最大内积搜索（MIPS）问题。但现有MIPS解决方案并不适配缩放点积注意力，因为它们对GPU不友好，且往往会因查询和键分布分离而表现不佳。本文提出HashAttention，将关键令牌识别转化为推荐问题。针对给定查询，HashAttention使用学习到的映射函数将键和查询编码到汉明空间，捕获所需的语义相似度。HashAttention通过位运算高效识别给定查询对应的关键令牌，仅使用这些令牌计算注意力，从而提升整体注意力效率。在通用数据上训练的HashAttention最多可将使用的令牌数减少16倍，且质量损失极小，每个令牌仅需32位辅助内存。通过特定任务微调，稀疏度可进一步提升至32倍。在A100 GPU上，稀疏度为32倍时，集成HashAttention可使GPT-FAST的注意力延迟最多降低4.3倍，FlashDecode的注意力延迟最多降低2.54倍，且GPT-FAST的吞吐量最多可提升3.12倍。
primary_research_object: AI 系统 / 基础设施
notes: 工作核心是通过改造注意力检索与计算路径来减少推理时参与 attention 的 token 数，属于长上下文推理优化；未聚焦解码策略、调度、内核或压缩。

title: Not All Heads Matter: A Head-Level KV Cache Compression Method with Integrated Retrieval and Reasoning
abstract: Key-Value (KV) caching is a common technique to enhance the computational efficiency of Large Language Models (LLMs), but its memory overhead grows rapidly with input length. Prior work has shown that not all tokens are equally important for text generation, proposing layer-level KV cache compression to selectively retain key information. Recognizing the distinct roles of attention heads in generation, we propose HeadKV, a head-level KV cache compression method, and HeadKV-R2, which leverages a novel contextual reasoning ability estimation for compression. Our approach operates at the level of individual heads, estimating their importance for contextual QA tasks that require both retrieval and reasoning capabilities. Extensive experiments across diverse benchmarks (LongBench, LooGLE), model architectures (e.g., Llama-3-8B-Instruct, Mistral-7B-Instruct), and long-context abilities tests demonstrate that our head-level KV cache compression significantly outperforms strong baselines, particularly in low-resource settings (KV size = 64 & 128). Notably, our method retains just 1.5% of the KV cache while achieving 97% of the performance of the full KV cache on the contextual question answering benchmark.
abstract_zh: 键值（KV）缓存是提升大语言模型（LLM）计算效率的常用技术，但其内存开销会随输入长度快速增长。已有研究表明，对文本生成而言并非所有令牌的重要性都相同，因此相关工作提出了层级KV缓存压缩方法来选择性保留关键信息。我们注意到注意力头在生成过程中承担的角色存在差异，因此提出头层级KV缓存压缩方法HeadKV，以及利用新型上下文推理能力评估机制实现压缩的HeadKV-R2。我们的方法以单个注意力头为操作粒度，评估各头对同时需要检索与推理能力的上下文问答任务的重要性。我们在各类基准测试（LongBench、LooGLE）、不同模型架构（如Llama-3-8B-Instruct、Mistral-7B-Instruct）以及长上下文能力测试中开展了大量实验，结果表明我们提出的头层级KV缓存压缩方法性能显著优于强基线方法，在低资源场景（KV缓存大小为64与128）下的优势尤为突出。值得注意的是，在上下文问答基准测试中，我们的方法仅保留1.5%的KV缓存，即可达到完整KV缓存97%的性能。
notes: 论文聚焦 LLM 推理阶段的 KV Cache 压缩，在 head-level 上进行重要性估计，并结合 retrieval 与 reasoning 场景评估，明显属于上下文与缓存优化。

title: CogVLA: Cognition-Aligned Vision-Language-Action Models via Instruction-Driven Routing & Sparsification
abstract:
Recent Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models built on pre-trained Vision-Language Models (VLMs) require extensive post-training, resulting in high computational overhead that limits scalability and deployment. Existing sparsification strategies—such as Mixture-of-Depths, layer skipping, and early exit—fall short by neglecting the semantic coupling across vision-language-action modalities, and focusing narrowly on intra-LLM computation while overlooking end-to-end coherence from perception to control. To address these challenges, we propose **CogVLA**, a Cognition-Aligned Vision-Language-Action framework that leverages instruction-driven routing and sparsification to improve both efficiency and performance. CogVLA draws inspiration from human multimodal coordination and introduces a 3-stage progressive architecture. 1) **Encoder-FiLM based Aggregation Routing (EFA-Routing)** injects instruction information into the vision encoder to selectively aggregate and compress dual-stream visual tokens, forming a instruction-aware latent representation. 2) Building upon this compact visual encoding, **LLM-FiLM based Pruning Routing (LFP-Routing)** introduces action intent into the language model by pruning instruction-irrelevant visually grounded tokens, thereby achieving token-level sparsity. 3) To ensure that compressed perception inputs can still support accurate and coherent action generation, we introduce **V‑L‑A Coupled Attention (CAtten)**, which combines causal vision-language attention with bidirectional action parallel decoding.
Extensive experiments on the LIBERO benchmark and real-world robotic tasks demonstrate that CogVLA achieves state-of-the-art performance with success rates of 97.4\% and 70.0\%, respectively, while reducing training costs by 2.5$\times$ and decreasing inference latency by 2.8$\times$ compared to OpenVLA.
abstract_zh: 当前基于预训练视觉语言模型（VLM）构建的视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型需要开展大量后训练，由此产生的极高计算开销限制了模型的可扩展性与部署落地。现有稀疏化策略如深度混合、层跳过、早退出等存在明显不足：这类策略忽略了视觉-语言-动作多模态之间的语义耦合，仅局限于关注大语言模型内部的计算过程，忽视了从感知到控制的端到端一致性。为解决上述挑战，我们提出CogVLA，一种认知对齐的视觉-语言-动作框架，借助指令驱动的路由与稀疏化机制同时提升效率与性能。CogVLA受人类多模态协调机制启发，采用三阶段渐进式架构：1）基于编码器FiLM的聚合路由（EFA-Routing）将指令信息注入视觉编码器，选择性聚合并压缩双流视觉令牌，生成具备指令感知能力的隐层表示。2）基于上述紧凑视觉编码结果，基于大语言模型FiLM的剪枝路由（LFP-Routing）通过剪除与指令无关的视觉接地令牌，将动作意图引入语言模型，从而实现令牌级稀疏化。3）为确保压缩后的感知输入仍可支持准确且一致的动作生成，我们提出视觉-语言-动作耦合注意力（CAtten），该模块将因果视觉-语言注意力与双向动作并行解码相结合。在LIBERO基准测试与真实世界机器人任务上开展的大量实验表明，CogVLA取得了当前最优性能，两类任务的成功率分别达到97.4%与70.0%，同时与OpenVLA相比，训练成本降低2.5倍，推理延迟降低2.8倍。
notes: 论文核心是通过指令驱动路由、token 压缩与剪枝来降低 VLA/VLM 推理开销，并用耦合注意力维持动作生成质量，属于明显的推理结构优化，并伴随压缩/稀疏化特征。

title: SLMRec: Distilling Large Language Models into Small for Sequential Recommendation
abstract:
Sequential Recommendation (SR) task involves predicting the next item a user is likely to interact with, given their past interactions. 
The SR models examine the sequence of a user's actions to discern more complex behavioral patterns and temporal dynamics. 
Recent research demonstrates the great impact of LLMs on sequential recommendation systems, either viewing sequential recommendation as language modeling or serving as the backbone for user representation. Although these methods deliver outstanding performance, there is scant evidence of the necessity of a large language model and how large the language model is needed, especially in the sequential recommendation scene. Meanwhile, due to the huge size of LLMs, it is inefficient and impractical to apply a LLM-based model in real-world platforms that often need to process billions of traffic logs daily. In this paper, we explore the influence of LLMs' depth by conducting extensive experiments on large-scale industry datasets. Surprisingly, our motivational experiments reveal that most intermediate layers of LLMs are redundant, indicating that pruning the remaining layers can still maintain strong performance.
Motivated by this insight, we empower small language models for SR, namely SLMRec, which adopt a simple yet effective knowledge distillation method. Moreover, SLMRec is orthogonal to other post-training efficiency techniques, such as quantization and pruning, so that they can be leveraged in combination. Comprehensive experimental results illustrate that the proposed SLMRec model attains the best performance using only 13\% of the parameters found in LLM-based recommendation models while simultaneously achieving up to 6.6x and 8.0x speedups in training and inference time costs, respectively. Besides, we provide a theoretical justification for why small language models can perform comparably to large language models in SR.
abstract_zh: 序列推荐（SR）任务旨在根据用户的历史交互记录，预测用户接下来可能交互的物品。序列推荐模型会分析用户的行为序列，以识别更复杂的行为模式和时间动态。近年研究表明大语言模型（LLM）对序列推荐系统有显著作用，相关研究要么将序列推荐视为语言建模任务，要么将大语言模型作为生成用户表征的主干网络。尽管这些方法表现优异，但鲜有研究验证大语言模型的必要性，以及究竟需要多大规模的语言模型，尤其是在序列推荐场景中。同时，由于大语言模型体量庞大，将基于大语言模型的模型部署到通常需要每日处理数十亿流量日志的实际平台中，效率极低且不具备实用性。本文通过在大规模行业数据集上开展大量实验，探究大语言模型深度带来的影响。令人意外的是，我们的动机实验显示大语言模型的大多数中间层都是冗余的，这意味着剪除这些冗余层后模型仍能保持较强的性能。受这一发现启发，我们提出了面向序列推荐任务的小语言模型SLMRec，该模型采用了一种简单却有效的知识蒸馏方法。此外，SLMRec与量化、剪枝等其他训练后效率提升技术正交，因此可以结合使用这些技术。综合实验结果表明，所提出的SLMRec模型仅使用基于大语言模型的推荐模型13%的参数就实现了最优性能，同时训练和推理的时间成本分别最高可实现6.6倍和8.0倍的加速。此外，我们还从理论上论证了小语言模型为何能在序列推荐任务中取得与大语言模型相当的表现。
notes: 题目明确是序列推荐；“Distilling ... into Small”直接表明通过蒸馏将大模型压缩为小模型，归入模型压缩。

title: AttentionPredictor: Temporal Patterns Matter for KV Cache Compression
abstract:
With the development of large language models (LLMs), efficient inference through Key-Value (KV) cache compression has attracted considerable attention, especially for long-context generation.
To compress the KV cache, recent methods identify critical KV tokens through static modeling of attention scores. However, these methods often struggle to accurately determine critical tokens as they neglect the *temporal patterns* in attention scores, resulting in a noticeable degradation in LLM performance. 
To address this challenge, we propose **AttentionPredictor**, which is the **first learning-based method to directly predict attention patterns for KV cache compression and critical token identification**. 
Specifically, AttentionPredictor learns a lightweight, unified convolution model to dynamically capture spatiotemporal patterns and predict the next-token attention scores. An appealing feature of AttentionPredictor is that it accurately predicts the attention score and shares the unified prediction model, which consumes negligible memory, among all transformer layers. Moreover, we propose a cross-token critical cache prefetching framework that hides the token estimation time overhead to accelerate the decoding stage. By retaining most of the attention information, AttentionPredictor achieves **13$\times$** KV cache compression and **5.6$\times$** speedup in a cache offloading scenario with comparable LLM performance, significantly outperforming the state-of-the-arts. The code is available at https://github.com/MIRALab-USTC/LLM-AttentionPredictor.
abstract_zh: 随着大语言模型（LLM）的发展，通过键值（KV）缓存压缩实现高效推理受到了广泛关注，尤其在长上下文生成场景中。为压缩KV缓存，现有方法通过对注意力分数进行静态建模来识别关键KV令牌，但这类方法由于忽略了注意力分数中的时间模式，往往难以准确判定关键令牌，导致大语言模型的性能出现明显下降。为解决这一挑战，我们提出AttentionPredictor，这是首个基于学习的可直接预测注意力模式，用于KV缓存压缩与关键令牌识别的方法。具体而言，AttentionPredictor学习一个轻量级的统一卷积模型，动态捕获时空模式并预测下一个令牌的注意力分数。AttentionPredictor的一个突出优势是它能够准确预测注意力分数，且该统一预测模型可在所有Transformer层之间共享，仅消耗可忽略不计的内存。此外，我们提出了跨令牌关键缓存预取框架，该框架可隐藏令牌估计的时间开销，从而加快解码阶段的速度。通过保留大部分注意力信息，在缓存卸载场景下，AttentionPredictor实现了13倍的KV缓存压缩比与5.6倍的速度提升，同时大语言模型性能与压缩前相当，表现显著优于现有最优方法。代码可从https://github.com/MIRALab-USTC/LLM-AttentionPredictor获取。
notes: 论文聚焦 LLM 推理阶段的 KV cache compression，并强调 long-context generation 场景下的效率优化，明显属于上下文与缓存优化方向。

title: Accelerating Inference of Retrieval-Augmented Generation via Sparse Context Selection
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) augmented with retrieval exhibit robust performance and extensive versatility by incorporating external contexts. However, the input length grows linearly in the number of retrieved documents, causing a dramatic increase in latency. In this paper, we propose a novel paradigm named Sparse RAG, which seeks to cut computation costs through sparsity. Speciﬁcally, Sparse RAG encodes retrieved documents in parallel, which eliminates latency introduced by long-range attention of retrieved documents. Then, LLMs selectively decode the output by only attending to highly relevant caches auto-regressively, which are chosen via prompting LLMs with special control tokens. It is notable that Sparse RAG combines the assessment of each individual document and the generation of the response into a single process. The designed sparse mechanism in a RAG system can facilitate the reduction of the number of documents loaded during decoding for accelerating the inference of the RAG system. Additionally, ﬁltering out undesirable contexts enhances the model’s focus on relevant context, inherently improving its generation quality. Evaluation results on four datasets show that Sparse RAG can be used to strike an optimal balance between generation quality and computational efﬁciency, demonstrating its generalizability across tasks.
abstract_zh: 结合检索功能增强的大语言模型（LLM）通过引入外部上下文，展现出强劲的性能与广泛的适用性。然而，输入长度随检索到的文档数量线性增长，会导致推理延迟大幅上升。本文提出了一种名为稀疏检索增强生成（Sparse RAG）的新范式，旨在通过稀疏性降低计算成本。具体而言，Sparse RAG对检索到的文档进行并行编码，消除了检索文档的长距离注意力带来的延迟。随后，大语言模型通过特殊控制令牌提示选出高度相关的缓存，仅自回归地关注这些缓存来选择性解码输出。值得注意的是，Sparse RAG将单篇文档的评估与响应生成整合到了同一个流程中。RAG系统中设计的这种稀疏机制能够减少解码阶段加载的文档数量，从而加快RAG系统的推理速度。此外，过滤掉无关上下文能够让模型更聚焦于相关上下文，从本质上提升生成质量。在四个数据集上的评估结果表明，Sparse RAG能够在生成质量与计算效率之间取得最优平衡，证明了其跨任务的泛化能力。
notes: 论文核心是通过稀疏上下文选择与按需访问相关缓存来加速 RAG 推理，重点落在解码阶段的注意力裁剪与上下文/缓存利用优化；未体现模型压缩。

general: 这是RAG检索的优化，不是模型推理加速

title: VL-Cache: Sparsity and Modality-Aware KV Cache Compression for Vision-Language Model Inference Acceleration
abstract: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have demonstrated impressive performance across a versatile set of tasks. A key challenge in accelerating VLMs is storing and accessing the large Key-Value (KV) cache that encodes long visual contexts, such as images or videos. While existing KV cache compression methods are effective for Large Language Models (LLMs), directly migrating them to VLMs yields suboptimal accuracy and speedup. To bridge the gap, we propose VL-Cache, a novel KV cache compression recipe tailored for accelerating VLM inference. In this paper, we first investigate the unique sparsity pattern of VLM attention by distinguishing visual and text tokens in prefill and decoding phases. Based on these observations, we introduce a layer-adaptive sparsity-aware cache budget allocation method that effectively distributes the limited cache budget across different layers, further reducing KV cache size without compromising accuracy. Additionally, we develop a modality-aware token scoring policy to better evaluate the token importance. Empirical results on multiple benchmark datasets demonstrate that retaining only 10% of KV cache achieves accuracy comparable to that with full cache. In a speed benchmark, our method accelerates end-to-end latency of generating 100 tokens by up to 2.33x and speeds up decoding by up to 7.08x, while reducing the memory footprint of KV cache in GPU by 90%.
abstract_zh: 视觉语言模型（VLM）已在各类任务中展现出优异性能。加速视觉语言模型的核心难点在于存储与访问编码了图像、视频等长视觉上下文的大型键值（KV）缓存。现有KV缓存压缩方法对大语言模型（LLM）有效，但直接迁移至视觉语言模型会导致精度和加速效果均不理想。为填补这一空白，我们提出VL-Cache，这是一种专为加速视觉语言模型推理定制的新型KV缓存压缩方案。本文首先通过区分预填充阶段与解码阶段的视觉令牌与文本令牌，探究了视觉语言模型注意力的独特稀疏模式。基于上述观测结果，我们提出了一种层自适应、感知稀疏性的缓存预算分配方法，可将有限的缓存预算有效分配至不同层，在不损失精度的前提下进一步缩小KV缓存的体积。此外，我们设计了一种感知模态的令牌评分策略，以更好地评估令牌的重要性。多个基准数据集上的实验结果表明，仅保留10%的KV缓存即可取得与全缓存相当的精度。在速度基准测试中，我们的方法生成100个令牌的端到端速度最高提升2.33倍，解码速度最高提升7.08倍，同时将GPU中KV缓存的内存占用降低90%。
notes: ['论文对象明确是 Vision-Language Models，因此 primary_research_object 归为 VLM/Diffusion。', '核心贡献集中在 KV cache compression、cache budget allocation 与 token scoring，最匹配 上下文与缓存优化。', '虽然论文报告端到端加速，但方法不属于解码策略、算子内核或系统调度主导贡献。']

title: $\text{D}_{2}\text{O}$: Dynamic Discriminative Operations for Efficient Long-Context Inference of Large Language Models
abstract: Efficient generative inference in Large Language Models (LLMs) is impeded by the growing memory demands of Key-Value (KV) cache, especially for longer sequences. Traditional KV Cache eviction strategies, which discard less critical KV-pairs based on attention scores, often degrade generation quality, leading to issues such as context loss or hallucinations. To address this, we introduce **D**ynamic **D**iscriminative **O**perations ($\mathbf{D_2 O}$), a novel method that optimizes KV cache size dynamically and discriminatively at two levels without fine-tuning, while preserving essential context. At **layer-level**, by observing the varying densities of attention weights between shallow and deep layers, we dynamically determine which layers should avoid excessive eviction via our proposed ***dynamic allocation strategy*** to minimize information loss. At **token-level**, for the eviction strategy in each layer, $\mathbf{D_2 O}$ innovatively incorporates a ***compensation mechanism*** that maintains a similarity threshold to re-discriminate the importance of currently discarded tokens, determining whether they should be recalled and merged with similar tokens. Extensive experiments on various benchmarks and LLM architectures have shown that $\mathbf{D_2 O}$ not only achieves significant memory savings and enhances inference throughput by more than 3$\times$ but also maintains high-quality long-text generation.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型的高效生成推理受限于键值（KV）缓存不断增长的内存需求，在处理更长序列时这一问题尤为突出。传统KV缓存淘汰策略基于注意力得分丢弃重要性较低的KV对，通常会降低生成质量，引发上下文丢失、幻觉等问题。为解决该问题，我们提出动态判别操作（D₂O），这一全新方法无需微调即可在两个层级动态、有判别性地优化KV缓存大小，同时保留关键上下文。在层级别，通过观察浅层与深层注意力权重的密度差异，我们通过提出的动态分配策略动态判定哪些层应避免过度淘汰，以最大限度减少信息损失。在令牌级别，针对每一层的淘汰策略，D₂O创新性地引入补偿机制，该机制维持一个相似度阈值，对当前已丢弃令牌的重要性进行二次判别，确定是否需要召回这些令牌并将其与相似令牌合并。我们在各类基准测试与不同大语言模型架构上开展的大量实验表明，D₂O不仅能大幅节省内存，将推理吞吐量提升3倍以上，还能维持高质量的长文本生成效果。
notes: 论文核心是长上下文推理中的 KV cache 动态裁剪、回收与合并，属于典型的上下文与缓存优化；虽有吞吐提升，但摘要未体现通用系统栈、调度器或内核层面的系统优化。

title: QuEST: Stable Training of LLMs with 1-Bit Weights and Activations
abstract:
One approach to reducing the massive costs of large language models (LLMs) is the use of quantized or sparse representations for training or deployment.
While post-training compression methods are very popular, the question of obtaining even more accurate compressed models by *directly training* over such representations, i.e., *Quantization-Aware Training (QAT)*, is still open: for example, a recent study put the "optimal" bit-width at which models can be trained using QAT, while staying accuracy-competitive with standard  FP16/BF16 precision, at 8-bits weights and activations. We advance this state-of-the-art via a new method called QuEST, for which we demonstrate optimality at 4-bits and stable convergence as low as 1-bit weights and activations. 
QuEST achieves this by improving two key aspects of QAT methods: 
(1) accurate and fast quantization of the (continuous) distributions of weights and activations via Hadamard normalization and MSE-optimal fitting; 
(2) a new *trust gradient estimator* based on the idea of explicitly minimizing the error between the noisy gradient computed over quantized states and the "true" (but unknown) full-precision gradient. 
Experiments on Llama-type architectures show that QuEST induces stable scaling laws across the entire range of hardware-supported precisions, and can be extended to sparse representations. 
We provide GPU kernel support showing that models produced by QuEST can be executed efficiently. Our code is available at [https://github.com/IST-DASLab/QuEST](https://github.com/IST-DASLab/QuEST).
abstract_zh: 降低大语言模型（LLM）巨额成本的一种思路是在训练或部署阶段使用量化或稀疏表示。虽然训练后压缩方法已得到广泛应用，但通过直接针对此类表示进行训练即量化感知训练（QAT）来获得精度更高的压缩模型这一问题仍未得到充分解决：例如，近期一项研究认为，使用量化感知训练且精度与标准FP16/BF16精度具备竞争力的模型，可训练的最优位宽为权重和激活均取8比特。我们通过名为QuEST的新方法推动了该领域的前沿发展，实验证明该方法可实现4比特位宽下的最优效果，且在权重和激活低至1比特时仍能稳定收敛。QuEST通过改进量化感知训练方法的两个核心维度实现了上述效果：一是通过哈达玛归一化和均方误差最优拟合，对权重和激活的连续分布进行准确、快速的量化；二是基于显式最小化量化状态下计算的含噪梯度与真实但未知的全精度梯度之间误差的思路，提出了全新的信任梯度估计器。针对Llama类架构的实验表明，QuEST在所有硬件支持的精度范围内均能实现稳定的缩放定律，且可扩展至稀疏表示场景。我们提供了GPU内核支持，证明QuEST生成的模型可高效运行。我们的代码可在https://github.com/IST-DASLab/QuEST获取。
notes: ['论文聚焦 LLM 的 1-bit 权重与激活训练稳定性，核心是量化/低比特表示带来的压缩与降成本。']

title: LeanQuant: Accurate and Scalable Large Language Model Quantization with Loss-error-aware Grid
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have shown immense potential across various domains, but their high memory requirements and inference costs remain critical challenges for deployment. Post-training quantization (PTQ) has emerged as a promising technique to reduce memory requirements and decoding latency. However, recent accurate quantization methods often depend on specialized computations or custom data formats to achieve better model quality, which limits their compatibility with popular frameworks, as they require dedicated inference kernels tailored to specific hardware and software platforms, hindering wider adoption. Furthermore, many competitive methods have high resource requirements and computational overhead for quantizing models, making it challenging to scale them to hundreds of billions of parameters. In response to these challenges, we propose LeanQuant (Loss-error-aware network Quantization), a novel quantization method that is accurate, versatile, and scalable. In the existing popular iterative loss-error-based quantization framework, we identify a critical limitation in prior methods: the min-max affine quantization grid fails to preserve model quality due to outliers in inverse Hessian diagonals. To overcome this fundamental issue, we propose learning loss-error-aware grids, instead of using non-adaptive min-max affine grids. Our approach not only produces quantized models that are more accurate but also generalizes to a wider range of quantization types, including affine and non-uniform quantization, enhancing compatibility with more frameworks. Extensive experiments with recent LLMs demonstrate that LeanQuant is highly accurate, comparing favorably against competitive baselines in model quality, and scalable, achieving very accurate quantization of Llama-3.1 405B, one of the largest open-source LLMs to date, using two Quadro RTX 8000-48GB GPUs in 21 hours. Our code is available at https://github.com/LeanModels/LeanQuant.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型在多个领域展现出巨大潜力，但其较高的内存需求与推理成本仍是部署过程中面临的关键挑战。训练后量化已成为降低内存需求和解码延迟的极具前景的技术。然而，现有高精度量化方法往往依赖专用计算或自定义数据格式来实现更优的模型质量，这限制了其与主流框架的兼容性，这类方法需要针对特定软硬件平台定制专用推理内核，阻碍了其更广泛的应用。此外，很多性能优异的方法在量化模型时资源需求高、计算开销大，很难扩展到参数规模达数千亿的模型上。针对这些挑战，我们提出LeanQuant，即损失误差感知网络量化，这是一种兼具高精度、通用性与可扩展性的新型量化方法。我们在当前主流的基于迭代损失误差的量化框架中，发现了现有方法的一个关键局限：由于逆海森矩阵对角线存在离群值，最小最大仿射量化网格无法保留模型质量。为解决这一根本问题，我们提出学习损失误差感知网格，而非使用无自适应能力的最小最大仿射网格。我们的方法不仅能生成精度更高的量化模型，还能适配更多量化类型，包括仿射量化和非均匀量化，提升了与更多框架的兼容性。针对最新大语言模型的大量实验表明，LeanQuant精度极高，在模型质量上优于同类主流基线方法，同时具备可扩展性，仅使用两块Quadro RTX 8000-48GB GPU耗时21小时，就完成了对目前最大的开源大语言模型之一Llama-3.1 405B的高精度量化。我们的代码可在https://github.com/LeanModels/LeanQuant获取。
notes: 论文核心是面向大语言模型的后训练量化方法，属于模型压缩；虽提到框架兼容性与推理内核限制，但主要贡献不在算子/内核或系统调度优化。

title: PolarQuant: Leveraging Polar Transformation for Key Cache Quantization and Decoding Acceleration
abstract:
The increasing demand for long-context generation has made the KV cache in large language models a bottleneck in memory consumption. Quantizing the cache to lower bit widths is an effective way to reduce memory costs; however, previous methods struggle with key cache quantization due to outliers, resulting in suboptimal performance. We propose a novel quantization approach PolarQuant, which provides a new perspective for key cache quantization and efficiently addresses the outlier dilemma. We observe that the distribution of the key states reveals well-structured patterns under polar transformation. Outliers generally appear in only one of the two dimensions, which are rotated together by a specific angle when rotary position embeddings are applied. When represented as two-dimensional vectors, these dimensions exhibit well-organized patterns, with radii and angles smoothly distributed in polar space. This alleviates the channel-wise outliers, making them well-suited for key cache quantization. PolarQuant divides key vectors into groups of two-dimensional sub-vectors, encoding them as the quantized radius and the polar angle, rather than quantizing original key vectors directly. PolarQuant achieves the superior efficiency in KV cache quantization and accelerates the decoding process by turning the query-key inner product into a table lookup, all while maintaining the downstream performance of full-precision models.
Our code is available at https://github.com/ericshwu/PolarQuant.
abstract_zh: 对长上下文生成的需求持续增长，使得大语言模型中的键值（KV）缓存成为内存消耗的瓶颈。将缓存量化为更低位宽是降低内存开销的有效方法，但现有方法受离群值影响，在键缓存量化方面表现不佳，导致性能不够理想。我们提出了一种全新的量化方法PolarQuant，为键缓存量化提供了新的研究视角，可有效解决离群值带来的困境。我们观察到，键状态的分布在极坐标变换下呈现出结构清晰的规律。离群值通常仅出现在两个维度中的一个，而在应用旋转位置嵌入时，这两个维度会共同旋转特定角度。当被表示为二维向量时，这两个维度展现出规整的模式，其半径和角度在极坐标空间中分布平滑。这一特性缓解了通道级离群值问题，使其非常适用于键缓存量化。PolarQuant将键向量划分为多个二维子向量组，将其编码为量化后的半径和极角，而非直接对原始键向量进行量化。PolarQuant在KV缓存量化中实现了优异的效率，并且通过将查询-键内积运算转换为查表操作加快了解码过程，同时还能保持全精度模型的下游任务性能。我们的代码可在https://github.com/ericshwu/PolarQuant获取。
notes: 论文核心是面向 LLM 长上下文场景的 KV cache 量化，并通过极坐标表示同时带来解码加速；更偏缓存优化，其次可视为量化压缩。

title: KVzip: Query-Agnostic KV Cache Compression with Context Reconstruction
abstract: Transformer-based large language models (LLMs) cache context as key-value (KV) pairs during inference. As context length grows, KV cache sizes expand, leading to substantial memory overhead and increased attention latency. This paper introduces \textit{KVzip}, a query-agnostic KV cache eviction method enabling effective reuse of compressed KV caches across diverse queries. KVzip quantifies the importance of a KV pair using the underlying LLM to reconstruct original contexts from cached KV pairs, subsequently evicting pairs with lower importance. Extensive empirical evaluations demonstrate that KVzip reduces KV cache size by $3$-$4\times$ and FlashAttention decoding latency by approximately $2\times$, with negligible performance loss in question-answering, retrieval, reasoning, and code comprehension tasks. Evaluations include various models such as LLaMA3.1, Qwen2.5, and Gemma3, with context lengths reaching up to 170K tokens. KVzip significantly outperforms existing query-aware KV eviction methods, which suffer from performance degradation even at a 90\% cache budget ratio under multi-query scenarios.
abstract_zh: 基于Transformer的大语言模型（LLM）在推理过程中将上下文缓存为键值（KV）对。随着上下文长度增长，KV缓存规模不断扩大，会产生大量内存开销并抬高注意力计算延迟。本文提出KVzip，这是一种与查询无关的KV缓存淘汰方法，可实现压缩后的KV缓存在各类不同查询中的有效复用。KVzip利用底层大语言模型从缓存的KV对重建原始上下文，以此量化KV对的重要性，随后淘汰重要性更低的KV对。大量实证评估表明，KVzip将KV缓存规模缩减3至4倍，将FlashAttention的解码延迟降低约2倍，且在问答、检索、推理和代码理解任务中的性能损失可忽略不计。评估覆盖了LLaMA3.1、Qwen2.5、Gemma3等多种模型，上下文长度最高可达17万token。KVzip的性能显著优于现有的查询感知KV淘汰方法，这类方法在多查询场景下，即使缓存预算比例为90%时也会出现性能下降。
notes: 论文核心是面向长上下文推理的 KV cache 压缩与驱逐，属于典型的上下文与缓存优化；摘要未体现解码策略、算子内核或系统调度层面的主要方法贡献。

title: polybasic Speculative Decoding Through a Theoretical Perspective
abstract: Inference latency stands as a critical bottleneck in the large-scale deployment of Large Language Models (LLMs). Speculative decoding methods have recently shown promise in accelerating inference without compromising the output distribution. However, existing work typically relies on a dualistic draft-verify framework and lacks rigorous theoretical grounding. In this paper, we introduce a novel \emph{polybasic} speculative decoding framework, underpinned by a comprehensive theoretical analysis. Specifically, we prove a fundamental theorem that characterizes the optimal inference time for multi-model speculative decoding systems, shedding light on how to extend beyond the dualistic approach to a more general polybasic paradigm. Through our theoretical investigation of multi-model token generation, we expose and optimize the interplay between model capabilities, acceptance lengths, and overall computational cost. Our framework supports both standalone implementation and integration with existing speculative techniques, leading to accelerated performance in practice. Experimental results across multiple model families demonstrate that our approach yields speedup ratios ranging from $3.31\times$ to $4.01\times$ for LLaMA2-Chat 7B, up to $3.87 \times$ for LLaMA3-8B, up to $4.43 \times$ for Vicuna-7B and up to $3.85 \times$ for Qwen2-7B---all while preserving the original output distribution. We release our theoretical proofs and implementation code to facilitate further investigation into polybasic speculative decoding.
abstract_zh: 推理延迟是大语言模型大规模部署中的关键瓶颈。近年来，推测解码方法在不损害输出分布的前提下实现推理加速方面展现出良好前景，但现有研究通常依赖二元起草-验证框架，且缺乏严谨的理论基础。本文提出一种全新的多元推测解码框架，该框架以完备的理论分析为支撑。具体而言，我们证明了一个刻画多模型推测解码系统最优推理时长的基础定理，阐明了如何从二元方法拓展至更通用的多元范式。通过对多模型令牌生成过程的理论研究，我们揭示并优化了模型能力、接受长度与整体计算成本之间的相互作用关系。我们的框架既支持独立实现，也可与现有推测技术集成，可在实际场景中实现更优的加速性能。在多个模型族上开展的实验结果表明，我们的方法在保留原始输出分布的前提下，对LLaMA2-Chat 7B的加速比可达3.31倍至4.01倍，对LLaMA3-8B的加速比最高可达3.87倍，对Vicuna-7B的加速比最高可达4.43倍，对Qwen2-7B的加速比最高可达3.85倍。我们公开了相关理论证明与实现代码，以推动多元推测解码领域的进一步研究。
notes: 论文核心是面向 LLM 推理加速的 speculative decoding 理论与方法扩展，直接对应解码策略优化；同时涉及多模型协同、接受长度与计算成本权衡，可归入系统与调度优化。

title: Scaling Speculative Decoding with Lookahead Reasoning
abstract:
Reasoning models excel by generating long chain-of-thoughts, but decoding the resulting thousands of tokens is slow. Token-level specualtive decoding (SD) helps, but its benefit is capped, 
because the chance that an entire $\gamma$-token guess is correct falls exponentially as $\gamma$ grows. This means allocating more compute for longer token drafts faces an algorithmic ceiling -- making the speedup modest and hardware-agnostic. We raise this ceiling with lookahead reasoning, which exploits a second, step-level layer of parallelism. 
Our key insight is that reasoning models generate step-by-step, and each step needs only to be semantically correct, not exact token matching.  In lookahead reasoning, a lightweight draft model proposes several future steps; the target model expands each proposal in one batched pass, and a verifier keeps semantically correct steps while letting the target regenerate any that fail. Token-level SD still operates within each reasoning step, so the two layers of parallelism multiply. We show lookahead reasoning lifts the peak speedup of SD both theoretically and empirically. Across GSM8K, AIME, and other benchmarks, lookahead reasoning improves the speedup of SD from 1.4x to 2.1x while preserving answer quality, and its speedup scales better with additional GPU throughput. Our code is available at https://github.com/hao-ai-lab/LookaheadReasoning
abstract_zh: 推理模型通过生成长思维链实现优异表现，但解码生成的数千个令牌速度很慢。令牌级推测解码（SD）对此有所帮助，但其效益存在上限，因为完整的γ令牌猜测正确的概率会随γ增长呈指数下降。这意味着为更长的令牌草稿分配更多算力会遇到算法天花板，导致提速幅度有限且与硬件无关。我们通过前瞻推理提高了这一上限，该方法利用了第二层步骤级并行性。我们的核心洞察是，推理模型采用逐步生成的模式，每一步仅需语义正确，无需严格的令牌匹配。在前瞻推理中，轻量级草稿模型会提出多个未来步骤；目标模型在一次批处理中扩展每个提议，验证器会保留语义正确的步骤，同时让目标模型重新生成所有未通过验证的步骤。令牌级推测解码仍在每个推理步骤内运行，因此两层并行性的效果会叠加。我们从理论和实证两方面证明，前瞻推理提升了推测解码的峰值提速幅度。在GSM8K、AIME及其他基准测试中，前瞻推理将推测解码的提速幅度从1.4倍提升至2.1倍，同时保持了答案质量，并且其提速效果会随GPU吞吐量的增加实现更优的扩展。我们的代码可在https://github.com/hao-ai-lab/LookaheadReasoning获取。
notes: 论文核心聚焦 speculative decoding 与 lookahead reasoning，属于推理阶段的解码加速优化。

title: Palu: KV-Cache Compression with Low-Rank Projection
abstract:
Post-training KV-Cache compression methods typically either sample a subset of effectual tokens or quantize the data into lower numerical bit width. However, these methods cannot exploit redundancy in the hidden dimension of the KV tenors. This paper presents a hidden dimension compression approach called Palu, a KV-Cache compression framework that utilizes low-rank projection to reduce inference-time LLM memory usage. Palu decomposes the linear layers into low-rank matrices, caches compressed intermediate states, and reconstructs the full keys and values on the fly. To improve accuracy, compression rate, and efficiency, Palu further encompasses (1) a medium-grained low-rank decomposition scheme, (2) an efficient rank search algorithm, (3) low-rank-aware quantization compatibility enhancements, and (4) an optimized GPU kernel with matrix fusion. Extensive experiments with popular LLMs show that Palu compresses KV-Cache by 50% while maintaining strong accuracy and delivering up to 1.89× speedup on the RoPE-based attention module. When combined with quantization, Palu’s
inherent quantization-friendly design yields small to negligible extra accuracy degradation while saving additional memory than quantization-only methods and achieving up to 2.91× speedup for the RoPE-based attention. Moreover, it maintains comparable or even better accuracy (up to 1.19 lower perplexity) compared to quantization-only methods. These results demonstrate Palu’s superior capability to effectively address the efficiency and memory challenges of LLM inference posed by KV-Cache. Our code is publicly available at: https://github.com/shadowpa0327/Palu.
abstract_zh: 训练后KV缓存压缩方法通常要么采样有效令牌的子集，要么将数据量化为更低的数值位宽，但这些方法无法利用KV张量隐藏维度中存在的冗余。本文提出了一种名为Palu的隐藏维度压缩方法，这是一种利用低秩投影降低大语言模型推理阶段内存占用的KV缓存压缩框架。Palu将线性层分解为低秩矩阵，缓存压缩后的中间状态，并动态重构完整的键与值。为提升准确率、压缩率与效率，Palu进一步包含以下设计：（1）中等粒度低秩分解方案，（2）高效秩搜索算法，（3）低秩感知量化兼容性增强模块，（4）支持矩阵融合的优化GPU内核。针对主流大语言模型的大量实验表明，Palu将KV缓存压缩50%的同时保持了优异的准确率，并且在基于RoPE的注意力模块上实现了最高1.89倍的加速。当与量化技术结合时，Palu本身的量化友好设计只会带来极小甚至可忽略的额外准确率下降，同时相比仅采用量化的方法能进一步节省内存，且在基于RoPE的注意力模块上实现最高2.91倍的加速。此外，与仅采用量化的方法相比，Palu的准确率与之相当甚至更优，困惑度最高可降低1.19。这些结果表明，Palu具备出色的能力，可有效解决KV缓存给大语言模型推理带来的效率与内存挑战。我们的代码已公开于https://github.com/shadowpa0327/Palu。
notes: 论文核心是面向 LLM 推理阶段的 KV-Cache 低秩压缩，并辅以 GPU kernel/matrix fusion 优化；不属于负样本。

title: Delta Attention: Fast and Accurate Sparse Attention Inference by Delta Correction
abstract: The attention mechanism of a transformer has a quadratic complexity, leading to high inference costs and latency for long sequences. However, attention matrices are mostly sparse, which implies that many entries may be omitted from computation for efficient inference. Sparse attention inference methods aim to reduce this computational burden; however, they also come with a troublesome performance degradation. We discover that one reason for this degradation is that the sparse calculation induces a distributional shift in the attention outputs. The distributional shift causes decoding-time queries to fail to align well with the appropriate keys from the prefill stage, leading to a drop in performance. We propose a simple, novel, and effective procedure for correcting this distributional shift, bringing the distribution of sparse attention outputs closer to that of quadratic attention. Our method can be applied on top of any sparse attention method, and results in an average 36\%pt performance increase, recovering 88\% of quadratic attention accuracy on the 131K RULER benchmark when applied on top of sliding window attention with sink tokens while only adding a small overhead. Our method can maintain approximately 98.5\% sparsity over full quadratic attention, making our model 32 times faster than Flash Attention 2 when processing 1M token prefills.
abstract_zh: Transformer的注意力机制具有二次复杂度，处理长序列时会导致推理成本高、延迟大。但注意力矩阵大多为稀疏矩阵，这意味着为实现高效推理可以省略大量矩阵条目的计算。稀疏注意力推理方法旨在降低这一计算负担，但这类方法也存在棘手的性能下降问题。我们发现，导致这种性能下降的一个原因是稀疏计算会引发注意力输出的分布偏移。这种分布偏移会导致解码阶段的查询无法与预填充阶段的匹配键良好对齐，进而造成性能下滑。我们提出了一种简单、新颖且有效的流程来校正这种分布偏移，使稀疏注意力输出的分布更接近二次注意力的输出分布。我们的方法可叠加在任意稀疏注意力方法之上，当将其应用于带水槽标记的滑动窗口注意力时，可实现平均36个百分点的性能提升，在131K RULER基准测试上恢复了88%的二次注意力准确率，且仅增加了极小的开销。相较于完整的二次注意力，我们的方法可保持约98.5%的稀疏度，在处理100万标记的预填充任务时，模型速度比Flash Attention 2快32倍。
notes: 论文聚焦 LLM 长上下文稀疏注意力推理，通过 delta correction 缓解 prefill-to-decoding 分布偏移；更偏向注意力/推理结构优化，并涉及长上下文阶段的 prefill-decoding 对齐。

title: ThinKV: Thought-Adaptive KV Cache Compression for Efficient Reasoning Models
abstract: The long-output context generation of large reasoning models enables extended chain of thought (CoT) but also drives rapid growth of the key–value (KV) cache, quickly overwhelming GPU memory. To address this challenge, we propose ThinKV, a thought-adaptive KV cache compression framework. ThinKV is based on the observation that attention sparsity reveals distinct thought types with varying importance within the CoT. It applies a hybrid quantization–eviction strategy, assigning token precision by thought importance and progressively evicting tokens from less critical thoughts as reasoning trajectories evolve. Furthermore, to implement ThinKV, we design a kernel that extends PagedAttention to enable efficient reuse of evicted tokens' memory slots, eliminating compaction overheads. Extensive experiments on DeepSeek-R1-Distill, GPT-OSS, and NVIDIA AceReason across mathematics and coding benchmarks show that ThinKV achieves near-lossless accuracy with less than 5% of the original KV cache, while improving performance with up to 5.8x higher inference throughput over SoTA baselines.
abstract_zh: 大型推理模型的长输出上下文生成可实现扩展思维链（CoT），但也会导致键值（KV）缓存快速增长，迅速占满GPU内存。为应对这一挑战，我们提出ThinKV，一种思维自适应KV缓存压缩框架。ThinKV的设计基于如下观察：注意力稀疏性能够反映思维链中重要性各不相同的不同思维类型。该框架采用混合量化-驱逐策略，根据思维重要性分配令牌精度，并随着推理轨迹的演进逐步驱逐重要性更低的思维所包含的令牌。此外，为实现ThinKV，我们设计了一款扩展分页注意力（PagedAttention）的内核，可高效复用被驱逐令牌的内存槽，消除了内存整理开销。我们在数学与编程基准测试集上针对DeepSeek-R1-Distill、GPT-OSS和NVIDIA AceReason开展了大量实验，结果表明，ThinKV在仅占用不到5%的原始KV缓存的前提下实现了近乎无损的精度，同时相较于当前最优基线方案，其推理吞吐量最高可提升5.8倍。
notes: 论文聚焦推理阶段 KV cache 压缩与实现优化，核心是 thought-adaptive 量化+驱逐，并配套 PagedAttention 内核扩展。

title: Efficient Low Rank Attention for Long-Context Inference in Large Language Models
abstract:
As the length of input text grows, the key-value (KV) cache in LLMs imposes prohibitive GPU memory costs and limits long‐context inference on resource‐constrained devices.
  Existing approaches, such as KV quantization and pruning, reduce memory usage but suffer from numerical precision loss or suboptimal retention of key-value pairs.
  We introduce Low Rank Query and Key attention (LRQK), a two‐stage framework that jointly decomposes the full‐precision query and key matrices into compact rank-\(r\) factors during the prefill stage, and then uses these low-dimensional projections to compute proxy attention scores in \(\mathcal{O}(lr)\) time at each decode step.
  By selecting only the top-\(k\) tokens and a small fixed set of recent tokens, LRQK employs a mixed GPU–CPU cache with a hit-and-miss mechanism that transfers only missing full-precision KV pairs, thereby preserving exact attention outputs while reducing CPU–GPU data movement.
  Extensive experiments on the RULER and LongBench benchmarks with LLaMA-3-8B and Qwen2.5-7B demonstrate that LRQK matches or surpasses leading sparse-attention methods in long context settings, while delivering significant memory savings with minimal loss in accuracy. Our code is available at \url{https://github.com/tenghuilee/LRQK}.
abstract_zh: 随着输入文本长度增长，大语言模型中的键值（KV）缓存会产生难以负担的GPU内存成本，限制了资源受限设备上的长上下文推理。现有方法如KV量化和剪枝虽能降低内存占用，但存在数值精度损失或键值对保留效果不佳的问题。我们提出低秩查询与键注意力（LRQK），这一两阶段框架在预填充阶段将全精度查询矩阵和键矩阵联合分解为紧凑的秩为r的因子，随后在每一步解码阶段使用这些低维投影以$\mathcal{O}(lr)$的时间复杂度计算代理注意力分数。通过仅选择前k个令牌和一小部分固定的最近令牌，LRQK采用带命中-失效机制的GPU-CPU混合缓存，仅传输缺失的全精度键值对，从而在保留精确注意力输出的同时减少CPU-GPU数据传输量。我们使用LLaMA-3-8B和Qwen2.5-7B模型在RULER和LongBench基准上开展了大量实验，结果表明，在长上下文场景下，LRQK的性能与领先的稀疏注意力方法相当甚至更优，同时能够大幅节省内存，且精度损失极小。我们的代码可从https://github.com/tenghuilee/LRQK获取。
notes: 论文聚焦 LLM 长上下文推理，通过低秩注意力缓解 KV cache 带来的显存开销，兼具缓存优化与推理侧结构优化特征。

title: SepLLM: Accelerate Large Language Models by Compressing One Segment into One Separator
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have exhibited exceptional performance across a spectrum of natural language processing tasks. However, their substantial sizes pose considerable challenges, particularly in computational demands and inference speed, due to their quadratic complexity. In this work, we have identified a key pattern: certain seemingly meaningless separator tokens (i.e., punctuations) contribute disproportionately to attention scores compared to semantically meaningful tokens. This observation suggests that information of the segments between these separator tokens can be effectively condensed into the separator tokens themselves without significant information loss. Guided by this insight, we introduce SepLLM, a plug-and-play framework that accelerates inference by compressing these segments and eliminating redundant tokens. Additionally, we implement efficient kernels for training acceleration. Experimental results across training-free, training-from-scratch, and post-training settings demonstrate SepLLM's effectiveness. Notably, using the Llama-3-8B backbone, SepLLM achieves over 50% reduction in KV cache on the GSM8K-CoT benchmark while maintaining comparable performance. Furthermore, in streaming settings, SepLLM effectively processes sequences of up to 4 million tokens or more while maintaining consistent language modeling capabilities.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型在各类自然语言处理任务中均展现出优异性能。然而，由于其复杂度为二次方，庞大的模型体量带来了极大挑战，尤其是在计算需求与推理速度方面。本研究发现了一项关键规律：与具备语义意义的词元相比，部分看似无意义的分隔符词元（即标点符号）对注意力得分的贡献远高于预期。这一观测结果表明，这些分隔符之间的片段信息可被有效压缩至分隔符词元本身，且不会造成显著信息损失。基于这一洞见，我们提出SepLLM，这是一种即插即用的框架，通过压缩上述片段、消除冗余词元来加快推理速度。此外，我们实现了高效内核以加速训练过程。在免训练、从头训练和训练后三种设置下的实验结果均证明了SepLLM的有效性。值得注意的是，采用Llama-3-8B作为骨干模型时，SepLLM在GSM8K-CoT基准测试中实现了50%以上的KV缓存缩减，同时保持了相当的性能水平。此外，在流式设置下，SepLLM可有效处理长度最高达400万甚至更长的序列，同时保持稳定的语言建模能力。
notes: 论文核心是基于分隔符的 token/segment 压缩来优化 LLM 推理，同时明确包含 KV cache 压缩与高效 kernels，因此这三类偏好标签都成立。

title: Memory-Efficient Visual Autoregressive Modeling with Scale-Aware KV Cache Compression
abstract: Visual Autoregressive (VAR) modeling has garnered significant attention for its innovative next-scale prediction approach, which yields substantial improvements in efficiency, scalability, and zero-shot generalization. Nevertheless, the coarse-to-fine methodology inherent in VAR results in exponential growth of the KV cache during inference, causing considerable memory consumption and computational redundancy. To address these bottlenecks, we introduce ScaleKV, a novel KV cache compression framework tailored for VAR architectures. ScaleKV leverages two critical observations: varying cache demands across transformer layers and distinct attention patterns at different scales. Based on these insights, ScaleKV categorizes transformer layers into two functional groups: drafters and refiners. Drafters exhibit dispersed attention across multiple scales, thereby requiring greater cache capacity. Conversely, refiners focus attention on the current token map to process local details, consequently necessitating substantially reduced cache capacity. ScaleKV optimizes the multi-scale inference pipeline by identifying scale-specific drafters and refiners, facilitating differentiated cache management tailored to each scale. Evaluation on the state-of-the-art text-to-image VAR model family, Infinity, demonstrates that our approach effectively reduces the required KV cache memory to 10% while preserving pixel-level fidelity.
abstract_zh: 视觉自回归（VAR）建模凭借其创新的下一尺度预测方法获得了广泛关注，该方法在效率、可扩展性和零样本泛化能力上实现了大幅提升。然而，VAR固有的从粗到细的方法会导致推理过程中KV缓存呈指数级增长，造成极高的内存消耗和计算冗余。为解决这些瓶颈，我们提出了ScaleKV，这是一种专为VAR架构设计的新型KV缓存压缩框架。ScaleKV基于两个关键观察：Transformer不同层的缓存需求存在差异，且不同尺度下的注意力模式各不相同。基于这些发现，ScaleKV将Transformer层划分为两个功能组：起草层与细化层。起草层的注意力分散在多个尺度上，因此需要更大的缓存容量。相反，细化层将注意力集中在当前令牌图上以处理局部细节，因此所需的缓存容量大幅降低。ScaleKV通过识别特定尺度对应的起草层与细化层优化了多尺度推理流水线，可实现适配各个尺度的差异化缓存管理。在最先进的文本到图像VAR模型系列Infinity上的评估表明，我们的方法可有效将所需KV缓存内存降至原有的10%，同时保持像素级保真度。
notes: 论文核心是面向视觉自回归生成推理的 KV cache 压缩与分层/分尺度缓存管理，最匹配上下文与缓存优化；同时包含按层与按尺度进行差异化资源分配，符合系统与调度优化。未见解码策略本身改造、模型压缩训练或算子内核级优化。

title: CodeGEMM: A Codebook-Centric Approach to Efficient GEMM in Quantized LLMs
abstract:
Weight-only quantization is widely used to mitigate the memory-bound nature of LLM inference. Codebook-based methods extend this trend by achieving strong accuracy in the extremely low-bit regime (e.g., 2-bit). However, current kernels rely on dequantization, which repeatedly fetches centroids and reconstructs weights, incurring substantial latency and cache pressure. We present CodeGEMM, a codebook-centric GEMM kernel that replaces dequantization with precomputed inner products between centroids and activations stored in a lightweight Psumbook. At inference, code indices directly gather these partial sums, eliminating per-element lookups and reducing the on-chip footprint. The kernel supports the systematic exploration of latency–memory–accuracy trade-offs under a unified implementation.
On Llama-3 models, CodeGEMM delivers 1.83x (8B) and 8.93x (70B) speedups in the 2-bit configuration compared to state-of-the-art codebook-based quantization at comparable accuracy and further improves computing efficiency and memory subsystem utilization.
abstract_zh: 仅权重量化被广泛用于缓解大语言模型推理的内存受限特性。基于码本的方法可在极低比特区间（例如2比特）实现优异精度，延续了这一技术发展趋势。然而，现有内核依赖反量化操作，需要反复读取质心并重建权重，会带来显著延迟与缓存压力。我们提出CodeGEMM，一种以码本为核心的通用矩阵乘法内核，它用预先计算、存储在轻量级部分和码本中的质心与激活值内积替代了反量化操作。推理阶段，码索引可直接调取这些部分和，消除了逐元素查找操作，同时降低了片上内存占用。该内核支持在统一实现框架下系统探索延迟、内存与精度三者的权衡关系。在Llama-3模型上的测试结果表明，与现有最优的基于码本的量化方法相比，在精度相当的2比特配置下，CodeGEMM在8B参数模型上实现了1.83倍的速度提升，在70B参数模型上实现了8.93倍的速度提升，且进一步提高了计算效率与内存子系统利用率。
notes: ['论文核心贡献是面向量化 LLM 推理的 GEMM 内核设计，而非提出新的压缩方法本身。', '量化与 codebook 是问题设定与目标场景，因此更适合作为 target_preference_labels 中的“模型压缩”。']

title: PPE: Positional Preservation Embedding for Token Compression in Multimodal Large Language Models
abstract: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have achieved strong performance on vision-language tasks, yet often suffer from inefficiencies due to redundant visual tokens. Existing token merging methods reduce sequence length but frequently disrupt spatial layouts and temporal continuity by disregarding positional relationships. In this work, we propose a novel encoding operator dubbed as **P**ositional **P**reservation **E**mbedding (**PPE**), which has the main hallmark of preservation of spatiotemporal structure during visual token compression. PPE explicitly introduces the disentangled encoding of 3D positions in the token dimension, enabling each compressed token to encapsulate different positions from multiple original tokens. Furthermore, we show that PPE can effectively support cascade clustering --- a progressive token compression strategy that leads to better performance retention. PPE is a parameter-free and generic operator that can be seamlessly integrated into existing token merging methods without any adjustments. Applied to state-of-the-art token merging framework, PPE achieves consistent improvements of 2\%~5\% across multiple vision-language benchmarks, including MMBench (general vision understanding), TextVQA (layout understanding)  and VideoMME (temporal understanding). These results demonstrate that preserving positional cues is critical for efficient and effective MLLM reasoning. Our code is available at https://github.com/MouxiaoHuang/PPE
abstract_zh: 多模态大语言模型（MLLM）在视觉语言任务上已取得出色表现，但通常因视觉token存在冗余而面临效率低下的问题。现有token合并方法可缩短序列长度，但由于忽略位置关系，往往会破坏空间布局与时间连续性。本研究提出一种名为位置保留嵌入（Positional Preservation Embedding，PPE）的新型编码算子，其核心特征是在视觉token压缩过程中保留时空结构。PPE在token维度显式引入三维位置的解耦编码，使得每个压缩后的token能够封装来自多个原始token的不同位置信息。此外，研究证明PPE可有效支持级联聚类，这是一种渐进式token压缩策略，能够更好地保留模型性能。PPE是无参数的通用算子，无需任何调整即可无缝集成到现有token合并方法中。将PPE应用于最先进的token合并框架后，在多个视觉语言基准测试中实现了2%~5%的稳定性能提升，涉及的基准包括MMBench（通用视觉理解）、TextVQA（布局理解）以及VideoMME（时间理解）。上述结果表明，保留位置线索对于实现高效且高性能的MLLM推理至关重要。本文代码可从https://github.com/MouxiaoHuang/PPE获取。
notes: 论文核心是针对多模态大模型视觉token压缩时的结构保持与压缩算子设计，属于推理阶段的模型结构优化，而非系统调度优化。

title: Progressive Mixed-Precision Decoding for Efficient LLM Inference
abstract:
In spite of the great potential of large language models (LLMs) across various tasks, their deployment on resource-constrained devices remains challenging due to their excessive computational and memory demands. Quantization has emerged as an effective solution by storing weights in reduced precision. However, utilizing low precisions (i.e.~2/3-bit) to substantially alleviate the memory-boundedness of LLM decoding, still suffers from prohibitive performance drop. In this work, 
we argue that existing approaches fail to explore the diversity in computational patterns, redundancy, and sensitivity to approximations of the different phases of LLM inference, resorting to a uniform quantization policy throughout.
Instead, we propose a novel phase-aware method that selectively allocates precision during different phases of LLM inference, achieving both strong context extraction during prefill and efficient memory bandwidth utilization during decoding. To further address the memory-boundedness of the decoding phase, we introduce Progressive Mixed-Precision Decoding (PMPD), a technique that enables the gradual lowering of precision deeper in the generated sequence, together with a spectrum of precision-switching schedulers that dynamically drive the precision-lowering decisions in either task-adaptive or prompt-adaptive manner. 
Extensive evaluation across diverse language tasks shows that when targeting Nvidia GPUs, PMPD achieves 1.4$-$12.2$\times$ speedup in matrix-vector multiplications over fp16 models, while when targeting an LLM-optimized NPU, our approach delivers a throughput gain of 3.8$-$8.0$\times$ over fp16 models and up to 1.54$\times$ over uniform quantization approaches while preserving the output quality.
abstract_zh: 尽管大语言模型（LLM）在各类任务中具备巨大潜力，但因其计算与内存需求过高，在资源受限设备上的部署仍面临挑战。量化技术通过以更低精度存储权重，已成为一种有效解决方案。然而，利用2/3位这类低精度大幅缓解LLM解码的内存受限问题时，仍会出现难以接受的性能下降。本研究认为，现有方法未探究LLM推理不同阶段在计算模式、冗余度以及对近似操作的敏感度上的差异，全程采用统一量化策略。对此，我们提出一种全新的阶段感知方法，在LLM推理的不同阶段选择性分配精度，既能够在预填充阶段实现强大的上下文提取能力，又能够在解码阶段实现高效的内存带宽利用。为进一步解决解码阶段的内存受限问题，我们引入渐进式混合精度解码（PMPD）技术，该技术可随生成序列的深度增加逐步降低精度，同时我们还提出一系列精度切换调度器，以任务自适应或提示自适应的方式动态做出精度降低决策。在各类语言任务上开展的广泛评估表明，针对英伟达GPU时，PMPD的矩阵向量乘法速度相较fp16模型提升1.4至12.2倍；针对经LLM优化的神经网络处理器（NPU）时，我们的方法吞吐量相较fp16模型提升3.8至8.0倍，相较统一量化方法最高提升1.54倍，同时能够保持输出质量。
notes: 论文聚焦 LLM 推理阶段的 mixed-precision decoding，并以量化/低比特精度为核心手段提升效率，属于解码策略优化与模型压缩交叉方向。

title: An Efficient Private GPT Never Autoregressively Decodes
abstract: The wide deployment of the generative pre-trained transformer (GPT) has raised privacy concerns for both clients and servers. While cryptographic primitives can be employed for secure GPT inference to protect the privacy of both parties, they introduce considerable performance overhead. To accelerate secure inference, this study proposes a public decoding and secure verification approach that utilizes public GPT models, motivated by the observation that securely decoding one and multiple tokens takes a similar latency. The client uses the public model to generate a set of tokens, which are then securely verified by the private model for acceptance. The efficiency of our approach depends on the acceptance ratio of tokens proposed by the public model, which we improve from two aspects: (1) a private sampling protocol optimized for cryptographic primitives and (2) model alignment using knowledge distillation. Our approach improves the efficiency of secure decoding while maintaining the same level of privacy and generation quality as standard secure decoding. Experiments demonstrate a $2.1\times \sim 6.0\times$ speedup compared to standard decoding across three pairs of public-private models and different network conditions.
abstract_zh: 生成式预训练Transformer（GPT）的广泛部署同时引发了客户端与服务器双方的隐私担忧。尽管可采用密码原语实现安全GPT推理以保护双方隐私，但这类方法会引入极高的性能开销。为加速安全推理，本研究受“安全解码单个令牌与多个令牌的延迟相近”这一观测结果的启发，提出了一种利用公开GPT模型的公开解码与安全验证方法。客户端使用公开模型生成一组令牌，随后由私有模型对该组令牌进行安全验证以确定是否接纳。本方法的效率取决于公开模型所生成令牌的接纳率，我们从两个方面对该接纳率进行提升：一是针对密码原语优化的私有采样协议，二是利用知识蒸馏实现的模型对齐。本方法在保持与标准安全解码同等隐私水平与生成质量的前提下，提升了安全解码的效率。实验表明，在三对公开-私有模型组合及不同网络条件下，本方法较标准解码实现了2.1倍~6.0倍的速度提升。
notes: 论文核心是面向隐私保护 GPT 推理的高效解码，通过公开模型提议、多 token 验证与蒸馏对齐提升安全推理效率；与解码策略优化最相关，同时包含系统协同与基于知识蒸馏的轻量模型对齐信号。

title: ParetoQ: Improving Scaling Laws in Extremely Low-bit LLM Quantization
abstract: The optimal bit-width for achieving the best trade-off between quantized model size and accuracy has been a subject of ongoing debate. While some advocate for 4-bit quantization, others propose that 1.58-bit offers superior results. However, the lack of a cohesive framework for different bits has left such conclusions relatively tenuous. We present ParetoQ, the first unified framework that facilitates rigorous comparisons across 1-bit, 1.58-bit, 2-bit, 3-bit, and 4-bit quantization settings. Our findings reveal a notable learning transition between 2 and 3 bits: For 3-bits and above, the fine-tuned models stay close to their original pre-trained distributions, whereas for learning 2-bit networks or below, the representations change drastically. By optimizing training schemes and refining quantization functions, ParetoQ surpasses all previous methods tailored to specific bit widths. Remarkably, our ParetoQ ternary 600M-parameter model even outperforms the previous SoTA ternary 3B-parameter model in accuracy, using only one-fifth of the parameters. Extensive experimentation shows that ternary, 2-bit, and 3-bit quantization maintains comparable performance in the size-accuracy trade-off and generally exceeds 4-bit and binary quantization. Considering hardware constraints, 2-bit quantization offers promising potential for memory reduction and speedup.
abstract_zh: 实现量化模型尺寸与精度最佳权衡的最优位宽一直是持续争论的议题。部分研究支持4比特量化，也有研究认为1.58比特量化可取得更优效果，但由于缺乏适用于不同比特位宽的统一框架，这类结论的可靠性相对薄弱。我们提出ParetoQ，这是首个可在1比特、1.58比特、2比特、3比特和4比特量化设置之间开展严谨对比的统一框架。研究结果显示，2比特与3比特之间存在显著的学习跃迁：当位宽为3比特及以上时，微调后的模型与原始预训练分布保持接近；而学习2比特及以下网络时，模型表征会发生剧烈变化。通过优化训练方案并改进量化函数，ParetoQ的性能超过了此前所有针对特定位宽设计的方法。值得注意的是，我们的ParetoQ三值6亿参数模型仅使用五分之一的参数量，其精度甚至超过了此前最先进的三值30亿参数模型。大量实验表明，三值、2比特和3比特量化在尺寸-精度权衡方面表现相当，且普遍优于4比特和二值量化。考虑到硬件约束，2比特量化在降低内存占用与提升运行速度方面具有可观的应用潜力。
notes: 论文核心是极低比特 LLM 量化，属于模型压缩；摘要未体现上下文缓存或系统调度层面的主要贡献。

title: SliM-LLM: Salience-Driven Mixed-Precision Quantization for Large Language Models
abstract:
Post-training quantization (PTQ) is an effective technique for compressing large language models (LLMs). However, while uniform-precision quantization is computationally efficient, it often compromises model performance. To address this, we propose SliM-LLM, a salience-driven mixed-precision quantization framework that allocates bit-widths at the group-wise with high accuracy. Our approach leverages the observation that important weights follow a structured distribution and introduces two key components: 1) Salience-Determined Bit Allocation adaptively assigns bit-widths to groups within each layer based on their salience; and 2) Salience-Weighted Quantizer Calibration optimizes quantizer parameters by incorporating element-level salience, retain essential information. With its structured group-wise partitioning, SliM-LLM provides a hardware-friendly solution that matches the efficiency of uniform quantization methods while significantly improving accuracy. Experiments show that SliM-LLM achieves superior performance across various LLMs at low bit-widths. For example, a 2-bit quantized LLaMA-7B model reduces memory usage by nearly 6x compared to the floating-point baseline, decreases perplexity by 48% compared to state-of-the-art gradient-free PTQ methods, and maintains GPU inference speed. Additionally, the extended version, SliM-LLM+, which incorporates gradient-based quantization, further reduces perplexity by 35.1%. Our code is
available at https://github.com/Aaronhuang-778/SliM-LLM.
abstract_zh: 训练后量化（PTQ）是压缩大语言模型（LLM）的有效技术。然而，统一精度量化虽然计算效率高，但往往会损害模型性能。为解决这一问题，我们提出SliM-LLM，这是一种由显著性驱动的混合精度量化框架，可按组实现高精度位宽分配。我们的方法基于重要权重遵循结构化分布这一观测结论，引入两个核心组件：1）显著性决定的位宽分配模块，根据各层内组的显著性为其自适应分配位宽；2）显著性加权量化器校准模块，通过纳入元素级显著性优化量化器参数，保留关键信息。凭借结构化组级划分，SliM-LLM提供了硬件友好的解决方案，既具备与统一量化方法相当的效率，又能显著提升精度。实验表明，SliM-LLM在低位宽场景下针对各类大语言模型均取得了更优性能。例如，经2比特量化的LLaMA-7B模型与浮点基线相比内存占用降低近6倍，与当前最优的无梯度训练后量化方法相比困惑度降低48%，同时还能保持GPU推理速度。此外，结合了基于梯度的量化的扩展版本SliM-LLM+可将困惑度进一步降低35.1%。我们的代码可从https://github.com/Aaronhuang-778/SliM-LLM获取。
notes: 论文核心是面向 LLM 的混合精度量化与压缩，重点在量化分配和校准，不属于解码、缓存、调度或内核优化。

title: SpeCache: Speculative Key-Value Caching for Efficient Generation of LLMs
abstract: Transformer-based large language models (LLMs) have already achieved remarkable results on long-text tasks, but the limited GPU memory (VRAM) resources struggle to accommodate the linearly growing demand for key-value (KV) cache as the sequence length increases, which has become a bottleneck for the application of LLMs on long sequences. Existing KV cache compression methods include eviction, merging, or quantization of the KV cache to reduce its size. However, compression results in irreversible information forgetting, potentially affecting the accuracy of subsequent decoding. In this paper, we propose SpeCache, which takes full advantage of the large and easily expandable CPU memory to offload the complete KV cache, and dynamically fetches KV pairs back in each decoding step based on their importance measured by low-precision KV cache copy in VRAM. To avoid inference latency caused by CPU-GPU communication, SpeCache speculatively predicts the KV pairs that the next token might attend to, allowing us to prefetch them before the next decoding step which enables parallelization of prefetching and computation. Experiments on LongBench and Needle-in-a-Haystack benchmarks verify that SpeCache effectively reduces VRAM usage while avoiding information forgetting for long sequences without re-training, even with a 10x high KV cache compression ratio.
abstract_zh: 基于Transformer的大语言模型（LLM）已在长文本任务上取得显著成效，但随着序列长度增加，键值（KV）缓存的需求呈线性增长，有限的GPU显存（VRAM）资源难以满足该需求，这已成为大语言模型应用于长序列场景的瓶颈。现有KV缓存压缩方法包括对KV缓存进行逐出、合并或量化以减小其体积，然而压缩会导致不可逆的信息遗忘，可能影响后续解码的准确性。本文提出SpeCache，该方法充分利用容量大且易扩展的CPU内存来卸载完整的KV缓存，同时基于显存中低精度KV缓存副本测得的KV对重要性，在每个解码步骤动态取回所需KV对。为避免CPU-GPU通信带来的推理延迟，SpeCache会推测性预测下一个令牌可能关注的KV对，从而可在下一个解码步骤开始前预取这些KV对，实现预取与计算的并行化。在LongBench和大海捞针（Needle-in-a-Haystack）基准测试上开展的实验验证表明，SpeCache无需重新训练，即可在长序列场景下有效降低显存占用，同时避免信息遗忘，即便在KV缓存压缩比高达10倍的情况下仍具备上述效果。
notes: ['核心贡献是 KV cache 的分层存储、按重要性回取与推测式预取，属于缓存优化并带有系统调度特征。', '虽然摘要提到 high KV cache compression ratio，但方法重点不是量化/剪枝式模型压缩，而是无遗忘的缓存卸载与预取。']

title: A Training-Free Sub-quadratic Cost Transformer Model Serving Framework with Hierarchically Pruned Attention
abstract:
In modern large language models (LLMs), increasing the context length is crucial for improving comprehension and coherence in long-context, multi-modal, and retrieval-augmented language generation. 
While many recent transformer models attempt to extend their context length over a million tokens, they remain impractical due to the quadratic time and space complexities.
Although recent works on linear and sparse attention mechanisms can achieve this goal, their real-world applicability is often limited by the need to re-train from scratch and significantly worse performance. In response, we propose a novel approach, Hierarchically Pruned Attention (HiP), which reduces the time complexity of the attention mechanism to $O(T \log T)$ and the space complexity to $O(T)$, where $T$ is the sequence length. 
We notice a pattern in the attention scores of pretrained LLMs where tokens close together tend to have similar scores, which we call "attention locality". Based on this observation, we utilize a novel tree-search-like algorithm that estimates the top-$k$ key tokens for a given query on the fly, which is mathematically guaranteed to have better performance than random attention pruning. In addition to improving the time complexity of the attention mechanism, we further optimize GPU memory usage by implementing KV cache offloading, which stores only $O(\log T)$ tokens on the GPU while maintaining similar decoding throughput. Experiments on benchmarks show that HiP, with its training-free nature, significantly reduces both prefill and decoding latencies, as well as memory usage, while maintaining high-quality generation with minimal degradation.
HiP enables pretrained LLMs to scale up to millions of tokens on commodity GPUs, potentially unlocking long-context LLM applications previously deemed infeasible.
abstract_zh: 在现代大语言模型（LLM）中，提升上下文长度对改善长上下文、多模态以及检索增强语言生成场景下的理解能力与连贯性至关重要。尽管近期诸多Transformer模型尝试将上下文长度扩展至百万以上令牌，但受限于二次时间与空间复杂度，这类模型仍不具备实用性。虽然现有针对线性注意力与稀疏注意力机制的研究可实现这一目标，但这类方案通常需要从头重新训练模型且性能大幅下降，实际应用场景受限。为此，我们提出了一种名为分层剪枝注意力（HiP）的全新方法，可将注意力机制的时间复杂度降至O(T log T)，空间复杂度降至O(T)，其中T为序列长度。我们观察到预训练大语言模型的注意力得分存在相邻令牌得分相近的规律，我们将其命名为“注意力局部性”。基于这一发现，我们采用一种类似树搜索的全新算法，可针对给定查询即时估算得分最高的前k个关键令牌，该算法在数学上被证明性能优于随机注意力剪枝方案。除优化注意力机制的时间复杂度外，我们还通过实现KV缓存卸载进一步优化GPU显存占用，该机制仅在GPU上存储O(log T)量级的令牌，同时可维持相近的解码吞吐量。基准测试实验表明，具备免训练特性的HiP可显著降低预填充与解码延迟以及显存占用，同时维持高质量的生成效果，性能退化极小。HiP可让预训练大语言模型在普通消费级GPU上扩展至数百万令牌的上下文长度，有望支撑此前被认为无法实现的长上下文大语言模型应用落地。
notes: 标题明确聚焦于 Transformer serving framework 与 pruned attention，属于推理/服务阶段优化；摘要强调长上下文能力，因此可归入上下文与缓存优化，同时层次化剪枝注意力属于模型结构侧推理优化。

title: ReST-KV: Robust KV Cache Eviction with Layer-wise Output Reconstruction and Spatial-Temporal Smoothing
abstract:
Large language models (LLMs) face growing challenges in efficient generative inference due to the increasing memory demands of Key-Value (KV) caches, especially for long sequences.
Existing eviction methods typically retain KV pairs with high attention weights but overlook the impact of attention redistribution caused by token removal, as well as the spatial-temporal dynamics in KV selection.
In this paper, we propose ReST-KV, a robust KV eviction method that combines layer-wise output **Re**construction and **S**patial-**T**emporal smoothing to provide a more comprehensive perspective for the KV cache eviction task. 
Specifically, ReST-KV formulates KV cache eviction as an optimization problem that minimizes output discrepancies through efficient layer-wise reconstruction. By directly modeling how each token’s removal affects the model output, our method naturally captures attention redistribution effects, going beyond simplistic reliance on raw attention weights.
To further enhance robustness, we design exponential moving average smoothing to handle temporal variations and an adaptive window-based mechanism to capture spatial patterns.
Our method, ReST-KV, significantly advances performance on long-context benchmarks. It surpasses state-of-the-art baselines by 2.58\% on LongBench and 15.2\% on RULER. Additionally, ReST-KV consistently outperforms existing methods on Needle-in-a-Haystack and InfiniteBench, all while achieving a remarkable 10.61$\times$ reduction in decoding latency at 128k context length. The code is included in the supplementary material and is designed for easy reproduction.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型的生成推理效率正面临日益严峻的挑战，其原因在于键值（KV）缓存的内存需求不断增长，长序列场景下该问题尤为突出。现有缓存淘汰方法通常会保留注意力权重较高的键值对，却忽略了令牌移除导致的注意力重分布影响，也未考虑键值选择过程中的时空动态特性。本文提出ReST-KV，这是一种鲁棒键值缓存淘汰方法，结合逐层输出重建与时空平滑，为键值缓存淘汰任务提供了更全面的分析视角。具体而言，ReST-KV将键值缓存淘汰建模为一个优化问题，通过高效的逐层重建最小化输出偏差。该方法直接对每个令牌的移除对模型输出的影响进行建模，无需简单依赖原始注意力权重，自然捕获了注意力重分布效应。为进一步提升鲁棒性，我们设计了指数移动平均平滑处理时序波动，还采用基于自适应窗口的机制捕获空间模式。本文提出的ReST-KV在长上下文基准测试上的性能实现了显著提升，在LongBench上超过当前最优基线2.58%，在RULER上超出15.2%。此外，ReST-KV在大海捞针测试（Needle-in-a-Haystack）和InfiniteBench测试中始终优于现有方法，同时在128k上下文长度下解码延迟大幅降低了10.61倍。相关代码包含在补充材料中，易于复现。
notes: 论文核心聚焦于长序列生成中的 KV cache eviction，与上下文/缓存优化直接相关。

title: Star Attention: Efficient LLM Inference over Long Sequences
abstract: Inference with Transformer-based Large Language Models (LLMs) on long sequences is both costly and slow due to the quadratic complexity of the self-attention mechanism. We introduce Star Attention, a two-phase block-sparse approximation that improves computational efficiency by sharding attention across multiple hosts while minimizing communication overhead. In the first phase, the context is processed using blockwise-local attention across hosts, in parallel. In the second phase, query and response tokens attend to all prior cached tokens through sequence-global attention. Star Attention integrates seamlessly with most Transformer-based LLMs trained with global attention, reducing memory requirements and inference time by up to 11x while preserving 97-100% of accuracy.
abstract_zh: 基于Transformer的大语言模型（LLM）在长序列上执行推理时，由于自注意力机制存在二次复杂度，存在成本高昂、速度缓慢的问题。我们提出星型注意力（Star Attention），这是一种两阶段块稀疏近似方法，通过在多台主机间对注意力进行分片，同时最小化通信开销，以此提升计算效率。第一阶段，跨主机并行采用块级局部注意力处理上下文。第二阶段，查询令牌与响应令牌通过序列全局注意力对所有先前缓存的令牌执行注意力计算。星型注意力可与大多数采用全局注意力训练的基于Transformer的大语言模型无缝集成，最多可将内存需求和推理时间降低11倍，同时保持97%至100%的准确率。
notes: 论文聚焦长上下文LLM推理，通过块稀疏注意力、跨主机分片与缓存token全局访问联合优化推理效率，属于明显的正样本。

title: Accelerating Multimodal Large Language Models via Dynamic Visual-Token Exit and the Empirical Findings
abstract:
In this paper, we study the visual redundancy problem of multimodal large language models (MLLMs) from the perspective of attention behaviors. 
Via extensive empirical experiments, we observe and conclude three main inference stages of MLLMs:
(i) Early fusion between tokens is first accomplished quickly. 
(ii) Intra-modality modeling then comes to play. 
(iii) Multimodal reasoning resumes and lasts until the end of inference. 
In particular, we reveal that visual tokens will stop contributing to reasoning when the text tokens receive enough image information.
Based on this observation, we propose an effective method to improve the efficiency of MLLMs, termed dynamic visual-token exit (DyVTE), which is orthogonal but collaborative to previous token-wise visual compression methods.
To validate the efficacy of DyVTE, we apply it to a set of MLLMs, including LLaVA, VILA, EAGLE and InternVL.
The experimental results not only show the effectiveness of our DyVTE in improving MLLMs' efficiency, e.g., DyVTE reduces the computation overhead of LLaVA-1.5 by up to 45.7% without performance drop, but also reveal a general pattern across multiple MLLMs, well facilitating the in-depth analysis of MLLMs. 
Our code is anonymously released at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/AnonymousDyVTE-26AB/.
abstract_zh: 本文从注意力行为的视角研究多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）的视觉冗余问题。通过大量实证实验，我们观察并总结出多模态大语言模型的三个主要推理阶段：（i）首先快速完成令牌间的早期融合；（ii）随后进入模态内建模阶段；（iii）多模态推理启动并持续至推理结束。我们特别发现，当文本令牌获取到足够的图像信息后，视觉令牌将不再对推理产生贡献。基于这一观察，我们提出一种可提升多模态大语言模型效率的有效方法，名为动态视觉令牌退出（DyVTE），该方法与此前的逐令牌视觉压缩方法正交且可协同运作。为验证DyVTE的有效性，我们将其应用于一系列多模态大语言模型，包括LLaVA、VILA、EAGLE与InternVL。实验结果不仅证明了DyVTE在提升多模态大语言模型效率方面的有效性，例如DyVTE可将LLaVA-1.5的计算开销最高降低45.7%且不会造成性能下降，还揭示了多种多模态大语言模型之间存在的通用模式，可为多模态大语言模型的深度分析提供有力支持。我们的代码已匿名发布于https://anonymous.4open.science/r/AnonymousDyVTE-26AB/。
notes: 论文聚焦多模态大模型推理阶段的动态视觉 token 退出，属于通过模型侧机制减少冗余计算的推理优化方向。

title: Retrospective Sparse Attention for Efficient Long-Context Generation
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in long-context tasks such as reasoning, code generation, and multi-turn dialogue. However, inference over extended contexts is bottlenecked by the Key-Value (KV) cache, whose memory footprint grows linearly with sequence length and dominates latency at each decoding step. While recent KV cache compression methods identify and load important few tokens, they focus predominantly on input contexts and fail to address the cumulative attention errors that arise during long decoding. In this paper, we introduce RetroAttention, a novel KV cache update technique that retrospectively revises past attention outputs using newly arrived KV entries from subsequent decoding steps. By maintaining a lightweight output cache, RetroAttention enables past queries to be efficiently supplemented with more contexts, while incurring minimal latency overhead. This breaks the fixed-attention-output paradigm and allows continual correction of prior approximations. Extensive experiments on long-generation benchmarks show that RetroAttention consistently outperforms state-of-the-art (SOTA) KV compression methods, increasing effective KV exposure by up to 1.6$\times$ and accuracy by up to 21.9\%. We provide anonymized code in the supplementary material.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）正越来越多地应用于推理、代码生成、多轮对话等长上下文任务。然而，扩展上下文的推理受到键值（KV）缓存的瓶颈限制，该缓存的内存占用随序列长度线性增长，且主导了每一步解码的延迟。虽然近期的KV缓存压缩方法能够识别并加载少数重要令牌，但它们主要聚焦于输入上下文，未能解决长解码过程中产生的累积注意力误差。本文提出了RetroAttention，这是一种全新的KV缓存更新技术，可利用后续解码步骤中新产生的KV条目回溯修正过往的注意力输出。通过维护一个轻量级的输出缓存，RetroAttention能够为过往查询高效补充更多上下文，同时仅产生极小的延迟开销。这打破了固定注意力输出的范式，可实现对先前近似值的持续校正。在长生成基准测试上开展的大量实验表明，RetroAttention的表现始终优于现有最先进的（SOTA）KV压缩方法，有效KV暴露度最高提升1.6倍，准确率最高提升21.9%。我们在补充材料中提供了匿名化代码。
notes: ['核心贡献集中在长上下文生成时的 KV cache 更新与注意力输出回溯修正，属于推理期注意力机制优化，不是解码策略或系统调度主导工作。']

title: Randomization Boosts KV Caching, Learning Balances Query Load: A Joint Perspective
abstract:
KV caching is a fundamental technique for accelerating Large Language Model (LLM) inference by reusing key-value (KV) pairs from previous queries, but its effectiveness under limited memory is highly sensitive to the eviction policy. 
The default Least Recently Used (LRU) eviction algorithm struggles with dynamic online query arrivals, especially in multi-LLM serving scenarios, where balancing query load across workers and maximizing cache hit rate of each worker are inherently conflicting objectives.
We give the first unified mathematical model that captures the core trade-offs between KV cache eviction and query routing.
Our analysis reveals the theoretical limitations of existing methods and leads to principled algorithms that integrate provably competitive randomized KV cache eviction with learning-based methods to adaptively route queries with evolving patterns, thus balancing query load and cache hit rate. 
Our theoretical results are validated by extensive experiments across 4 benchmarks and 3 prefix-sharing settings, demonstrating improvements of up to **6.92$\times$** in cache hit rate, **11.96$\times$** reduction in latency, **14.06$\times$** reduction in time-to-first-token (TTFT), and **77.4%** increase in throughput over the state-of-the-art methods.
Our code is available at https://github.com/fzwark/KVRouting.
abstract_zh: KV缓存是通过复用先前查询的键值（KV）对来加速大语言模型（LLM）推理的基础技术，但在内存有限的场景下，其有效性对驱逐策略高度敏感。默认的最近最少使用（LRU）驱逐算法难以应对动态到达的在线查询，尤其是在多LLM服务场景中，平衡各工作节点的查询负载与最大化每个工作节点的缓存命中率本质上是相互冲突的目标。我们首次提出了统一的数学模型，可刻画KV缓存驱逐与查询路由之间的核心权衡关系。我们的分析揭示了现有方法的理论局限性，并基于此设计了原则性算法，该算法将具备可证明竞争力的随机KV缓存驱逐策略与基于学习的方法相结合，可对模式持续演变的查询进行自适应路由，从而实现查询负载与缓存命中率的平衡。我们的理论结果通过覆盖4个基准测试、3种前缀共享设置的大量实验得到验证，实验结果表明，与现有最优方法相比，本方法的缓存命中率最高提升6.92倍，延迟最高降低11.96倍，首令牌生成时间（TTFT）最高降低14.06倍，吞吐量提升达77.4%。我们的代码可从https://github.com/fzwark/KVRouting获取。
notes: 论文核心明确围绕 LLM 推理阶段的 KV cache 复用与受限内存下的淘汰策略优化；标题中的 query load balancing 也指向调度/负载均衡视角。

title: EasySpec: Layer-Parallel Speculative Decoding for Efficient Multi-GPU Utilization
abstract: Speculative decoding is an effective and lossless method for Large Language Model (LLM) inference acceleration. It employs a smaller model to generate a draft token sequence, which is then verified by the original base model. In multi-GPU systems, inference latency can be further reduced through tensor parallelism (TP), while the optimal TP size of the draft model is typically smaller than that of the base model, leading to GPU idling during the drafting stage. We observe that such inefficiency stems from the sequential execution of layers, which is seemingly natural but actually unnecessary. Therefore, we propose EasySpec, a layer-parallel speculation strategy that optimizes the efficiency of multi-GPU utilization. EasySpec breaks the inter-layer data dependencies in the draft model, enabling multiple layers to run simultaneously across multiple devices as ``fuzzy'' speculation. After each drafting-and-verification iteration, the draft model’s key-value cache is calibrated in a single forward pass, preventing long-term fuzzy-error accumulation at minimal additional latency. EasySpec is a training-free and plug-in method. We evaluated EasySpec on several mainstream open-source LLMs, using smaller versions of models from the same series as drafters. The results demonstrate that EasySpec can achieve a peak speedup of 4.17x compared to vanilla decoding, while preserving the original distributions of the base LLMs. Specifically, the drafting stage can be accelerated by up to 1.62x with a maximum speculation accuracy drop of only 7\%. The code is available at https://github.com/Yize-Wu/EasySpec.
abstract_zh: 推测解码是实现大语言模型（LLM）推理加速的一种有效无损方法，它利用更小的模型生成草稿令牌序列，再由原始基础模型对该序列进行验证。在多GPU系统中，可通过张量并行（TP）进一步降低推理延迟，但草稿模型的最优张量并行度通常小于基础模型的最优张量并行度，这会导致草稿生成阶段出现GPU空闲。我们观察到，这种效率低下问题的根源是层的顺序执行，这种执行方式看似理所当然，实则并非必要。因此，我们提出EasySpec，这是一种可优化多GPU利用效率的层并行推测策略。EasySpec打破了草稿模型的层间数据依赖，使得多层可以作为“模糊”推测任务在多台设备上同时运行。每次草稿生成与验证迭代完成后，草稿模型的键值缓存会通过单次前向传播进行校准，能够以极低的额外延迟避免模糊误差的长期累积。EasySpec是一种无需训练、可即插即用的方法。我们在多个主流开源大语言模型上对EasySpec进行了评估，采用同系列模型的更小版本作为草稿生成器。结果表明，与普通解码相比，EasySpec可实现最高4.17倍的加速比，同时能够保留基础大语言模型的原始输出分布。具体而言，草稿生成阶段最多可获得1.62倍的加速，而推测准确率的最大降幅仅为7%。相关代码可从https://github.com/Yize-Wu/EasySpec获取。
notes: ['论文主体是 LLM 推理加速中的 speculative decoding，并针对多 GPU 利用率做层并行调度优化。', '不属于算子与内核优化，摘要未涉及 kernel/operator 级实现或底层算子改写。']

title: LookaheadKV: Fast and Accurate KV Cache Eviction by Glimpsing into the Future without Generation
abstract: Transformer-based large language models (LLMs) rely on key–value (KV) caching to avoid redundant computation during autoregressive inference. While this mechanism greatly improves efficiency, the cache size grows linearly with the input sequence length, quickly becoming a bottleneck for long‑context tasks. Existing solutions mitigate this problem by evicting prompt KV that are deemed unimportant, guided by estimated importance scores. Notably, a recent line of work improves eviction quality by “glimpsing into the future”, in which a low‑cost draft generator first produces a surrogate response that mimics the target model's true response, which is subsequently used to estimate the importance scores of cached KV. In this paper, we propose LookaheadKV, a lightweight eviction framework that leverages the strength of surrogate future response without the need for costly draft generation. LookaheadKV augments transformer layers with parameter‑efficient modules trained to predict true importance scores with high accuracy. Our design ensures negligible runtime overhead comparable to existing inexpensive heuristics, while achieving accuracy superior to more costly approximation methods. Extensive experiments on long-context understanding benchmarks, across a wide range of models, demonstrate that our method not only outperform recent competitive baseline by long-context understanding tasks by $25$\%, but also reduces the eviction cost by up to $14.5$×, leading to significantly faster time-to-first-token (TTFT).
abstract_zh: 基于Transformer的大语言模型依赖键值缓存来避免自回归推理过程中的冗余计算。尽管该机制极大提升了推理效率，但缓存大小会随输入序列长度线性增长，很快会成为长上下文任务的性能瓶颈。现有解决方案通过驱逐被判定为不重要的提示键值缓存来缓解这一问题，驱逐操作由估算得到的重要性分数引导。值得注意的是，近期的一类研究通过前瞻未来提升了驱逐质量：这类方法先用低成本的草稿生成器生成一个模拟目标模型真实响应的代理响应，再利用该代理响应估算缓存键值的重要性分数。本文提出LookaheadKV，这是一种轻量级键值缓存驱逐框架，无需执行成本高昂的草稿生成，就能发挥代理未来响应的优势。LookaheadKV在Transformer层中加入参数高效模块，这类模块经过训练可高精度预测真实重要性分数。我们的设计保证了可忽略的运行时开销，与现有低成本启发式方法的开销相当，同时准确率优于成本更高的近似方法。针对长上下文理解基准、覆盖多种模型的大量实验表明，我们的方法不仅在长上下文理解任务上较近期的优秀基线性能高出25%，还将驱逐成本最高降低了14.5倍，进而显著缩短了首令牌生成时间。
notes: 论文核心是面向长上下文推理的 KV Cache eviction / compression 优化，直接对应上下文与缓存优化；未明显涉及解码策略或系统调度层面的核心方法贡献。

title: RocketKV: Accelerating Long-Context LLM Inference via Two-Stage KV Cache Compression
abstract: Transformer-based Large Language Models rely critically on the KV cache to efficiently handle extended contexts during the decode phase. Yet, the size of the KV cache grows proportionally with the input length, burdening both memory bandwidth and capacity as decoding progresses. To address this challenge, we present RocketKV, a training-free KV cache compression strategy containing two consecutive stages. In the first stage, it performs coarse-grain permanent KV cache eviction on the input sequence tokens. In the second stage, it adopts a hybrid sparse attention method to conduct fine-grain top-k sparse attention, approximating the attention scores by leveraging both head and sequence dimensionality reductions. We show that RocketKV provides a compression ratio of up to 400×, end-to-end speedup of up to 3.7× as well as peak memory reduction of up to 32.6% in the decode phase on an NVIDIA A100 GPU compared to the full KV cache baseline, while achieving negligible accuracy loss on a variety of long-context tasks. We also propose a variant of RocketKV for multi-turn scenarios, which consistently outperforms other existing methods and achieves accuracy nearly on par with an oracle top-k attention scheme.
abstract_zh: 基于Transformer的大语言模型极为依赖KV缓存，以在解码阶段高效处理扩展上下文。然而，KV缓存的大小随输入长度成正比增长，随着解码进程推进，会对内存带宽和容量都造成负担。为应对这一挑战，我们提出RocketKV，这是一种包含两个连续阶段的免训练KV缓存压缩策略。在第一阶段，它对输入序列令牌执行粗粒度的KV缓存永久逐出。在第二阶段，它采用混合稀疏注意力方法开展细粒度top-k稀疏注意力，同时利用头维度降维和序列维度降维来近似计算注意力得分。我们的实验表明，与全KV缓存基线相比，在NVIDIA A100 GPU上运行时，RocketKV在解码阶段可实现最高400倍的压缩率、最高3.7倍的端到端加速比，且峰值内存最高降低32.6%，同时在各类长上下文任务上的准确率损失可忽略不计。我们还针对多轮场景提出了一种RocketKV变体，其表现始终优于其他现有方法，且准确率几乎与理想top-k注意力方案相当。
notes: 论文核心是长上下文 LLM 推理中的两阶段 KV cache 压缩与稀疏注意力加速，明显属于缓存/上下文优化，并直接作用于解码阶段推理效率；未见算子或内核级实现细节，故不标注算子与内核优化。

title: Dialogue Without Limits: Constant-Sized KV Caches for Extended Response in LLMs
abstract:
Autoregressive Transformers rely on Key-Value (KV) caching to accelerate inference. However, the linear growth of the KV cache with context length leads to excessive memory consumption and bandwidth constraints. Existing methods drop distant tokens or compress states in a lossy manner, sacrificing accuracy by discarding vital context or introducing bias.

We propose ${MorphKV}$, an inference-time technique that maintains a constant-sized KV cache while preserving accuracy. MorphKV balances long-range dependencies and local coherence during text generation. It eliminates early-token bias while retaining high-fidelity context by adaptively ranking tokens through correlation-aware selection. Unlike heuristic retention or lossy compression, MorphKV iteratively refines the KV cache via lightweight updates guided by attention patterns of recent tokens. This approach captures inter-token correlation with greater accuracy, which is crucial for tasks like content creation and code generation. Our studies on long-response tasks show 52.9\% memory savings and 18.2\% higher accuracy on average compared to state-of-the-art prior works, enabling efficient deployment.
abstract_zh:
自回归Transformer依赖键值（KV）缓存来加速推理。然而，KV缓存随上下文长度线性增长会导致内存消耗过高以及带宽受限。现有方法要么丢弃远期令牌，要么以有损方式压缩状态，会因丢弃重要上下文或引入偏差而牺牲准确率。
我们提出MorphKV，这是一种推理时技术，可在保持准确率的同时维持大小恒定的KV缓存。MorphKV在文本生成过程中平衡长距离依赖与局部连贯性，通过相关性感知选择对令牌进行自适应排序，在保留高保真上下文的同时消除早期令牌偏差。与启发式保留或有损压缩不同，MorphKV以近期令牌的注意力模式为指导，通过轻量级更新迭代优化KV缓存。该方法能够更准确地捕获令牌间的相关性，这对于内容创作、代码生成等任务至关重要。我们在长响应任务上的研究表明，与现有最先进的方法相比，该方法平均可节省52.9%的内存，准确率平均高出18.2%，能够实现高效部署。
notes: 论文核心聚焦 LLM 推理阶段的 KV cache 常数化与长上下文/长响应支持，最匹配“上下文与缓存优化”。

title: HShare: Fast LLM Decoding by Hierarchical Key-Value Sharing
abstract: The frequent retrieval of Key-Value (KV) cache data has emerged as a significant factor contributing to the inefficiency of the inference process in large language models. Previous research has demonstrated that a small subset of critical KV cache tokens largely influences attention outcomes, leading to methods that either employ fixed sparsity patterns or dynamically select critical tokens based on the query. While dynamic sparse patterns have proven to be more effective, they introduce significant computational overhead, as critical tokens must be reselected for each self-attention computation. In this paper, we reveal substantial similarities in KV cache token criticality across neighboring queries, layers, and heads. Motivated by this insight, we propose HShare, a hierarchical KV sharing framework. HShare facilitates the sharing of critical KV cache token indices across layers, heads, and queries, which significantly reduces the computational overhead associated with query-aware dynamic token sparsity. In addition, we introduce a greedy algorithm that dynamically determines the optimal layer-level and head-level sharing configuration for the decoding phase. We evaluate the effectiveness and efficiency of HShare across various tasks using three models: LLaMA2-7b, LLaMA3-70b, and Mistral-7b. Experimental results demonstrate that HShare achieves competitive accuracy with different sharing ratios, while delivering up to an $8.6\times$ speedup in self-attention operations and a $2.7\times$ improvement in end-to-end throughput compared with FlashAttention2 and GPT-fast respectively. The source code is publicly available at ~\url{https://github.com/wuhuaijin/HShare}.
abstract_zh: 键值（KV）缓存数据的频繁检索已成为导致大语言模型推理过程低效的重要因素。既往研究表明，一小部分关键KV缓存令牌在很大程度上影响注意力输出，因此现有方法要么采用固定稀疏模式，要么基于查询动态选择关键令牌。尽管动态稀疏模式已被证实效果更优，但由于每次自注意力计算都必须重新选择关键令牌，会产生巨大的计算开销。本文揭示了相邻查询、层与注意力头之间的KV缓存令牌重要性存在显著相似性。受这一发现启发，我们提出了分层KV共享框架HShare。HShare支持在层、注意力头与查询之间共享关键KV缓存令牌索引，可大幅降低查询感知动态令牌稀疏性带来的计算开销。此外，我们引入了一种贪心算法，可在解码阶段动态确定最优的层级与头级共享配置。我们使用LLaMA2-7b、LLaMA3-70b和Mistral-7b三个模型在多项任务上评估了HShare的有效性与效率。实验结果表明，HShare在不同共享比例下均能达到具有竞争力的准确率，同时与FlashAttention2和GPT-fast相比，其自注意力运算速度最高提升8.6倍，端到端吞吐量最高提升2.7倍。源代码公开于https://github.com/wuhuaijin/HShare。
notes: 论文核心是面向 LLM 解码阶段的分层 KV cache 共享，通过跨 layer/head/query 复用关键 token 索引来降低动态稀疏注意力开销；不属于模型压缩。

title: Preserving LLM Capabilities through Calibration Data Curation: From Analysis to Optimization
abstract: Post-training compression has been a widely employed approach to scale down large language model (LLM) and facilitate efficient inference. In various proposed compression methods, including pruning and quantization, calibration data plays a vital role by informing the weight importance and activation dynamic ranges. However, how calibration data impacts the LLM capability after compression is less explored. Few of the existing works, though recognizing the significance of this study, only investigate the language modeling or commonsense reasoning performance degradation from limited angles, like the data sources or sample amounts.
