More systematic research is still needed to examine the impacts on  different LLM capabilities in terms of compositional properties and domain correspondence of calibration data. In this work, we aim at bridging this gap and further analyze underlying influencing mechanisms from the activation pattern perspective. Especially, we explore the calibration data's impacts on high-level complex reasoning capabilities, like math problem solving and code generation. Delving into the underlying mechanism, we find that the representativeness and diversity in activation space more fundamentally determine the quality of calibration data. Finally, we propose a calibration data curation framework based on such observations and analysis, enhancing the performance of existing post-training compression methods on preserving critical LLM capabilities. Our code is provided in [Link](https://github.com/BokwaiHo/COLA.git).
abstract_zh: 训练后压缩是被广泛采用的技术，可缩小大语言模型（LLM）规模，实现高效推理。在剪枝、量化等各类已提出的压缩方法中，校准数据通过提供权重重要性与激活动态范围信息发挥着关键作用。然而，校准数据如何影响压缩后大语言模型的性能，目前相关研究仍较为匮乏。现有研究虽已意识到该方向的研究价值，但仅有极少数工作从数据源、样本量等有限角度探究语言建模或常识推理的性能下降问题，仍需开展更系统的研究，从校准数据的组合属性、域对应性等维度分析其对大语言模型不同能力的影响。本研究旨在填补这一研究空白，进一步从激活模式视角分析底层影响机制，重点探究校准数据对数学问题求解、代码生成等高阶复杂推理能力的影响。通过深挖底层机制，我们发现激活空间的代表性与多样性是决定校准数据质量的更根本因素。最后，基于上述观测与分析，我们提出了一种校准数据遴选框架，可提升现有训练后压缩方法保留大语言模型核心能力的性能。我们的代码可在https://github.com/BokwaiHo/COLA.git获取。
notes: 主题聚焦于 LLM 后训练压缩中的校准数据筛选与能力保持，属于模型压缩；未体现系统与调度优化的核心贡献。

title: A Little Goes a Long Way: Efficient Long Context Training and Inference with Partial Contexts
abstract:
Training and serving long-context large language models (LLMs) incurs substantial overhead. 
To address this, two critical steps are often required: a pretrained LLM typically undergoes a separate stage for context length extension by training on long-context data, followed by architectural modifications to reduce the overhead of KV cache during serving. 
This paper argues that integrating length extension with a GPU-friendly KV cache reduction architecture not only reduces training overhead during length extension, but also achieves better long-context performance. 
This leads to our proposed LongGen, which finetunes a pretrained LLM into an efficient architecture during length extension. 
LongGen builds on three key insights: 
(1) Sparse attention patterns, such as window attention (attending to recent tokens), attention sink (initial ones), and blockwise sparse attention (strided token blocks) are well-suited for building efficient long-context models, primarily due to their GPU-friendly memory access patterns, enabling efficiency gains not just theoretically but in practice as well. 
(2) It is essential for the model to have direct access to all tokens. 
A hybrid architecture with 1/3 full attention layers and 2/3 efficient ones achieves a balanced trade-off between efficiency and long-context performance.
(3) Lightweight training on 5B long-context data is sufficient to extend the hybrid model's context length from 4K to 128K.

We evaluate LongGen on both Llama-2 7B and Llama-2 70B, demonstrating its effectiveness across different scales. 
During training with 128K-long contexts, LongGen achieves 1.55x training speedup and reduces wall-clock time by 36%, compared to a full-attention baseline. 
During inference, LongGen reduces KV cache memory by 62%, achieving 1.67x prefilling speedup and 1.41x decoding speedup.
Compared to baselines that apply KV-cache reduction techniques to full-attention long-context LLMs, LongGen achieves substantially stronger performance not only on the Needle-in-a-Haystack retrieval task, but also on more challenging long-context reasoning tasks, including BABILong and RULER.
abstract_zh:
训练和部署长上下文大语言模型（LLM）会产生极高的开销。为解决这一问题，通常需要两个关键步骤：预训练大语言模型一般会先经过独立的上下文长度扩展阶段，在长上下文数据上训练，之后再进行架构修改，以降低部署阶段的KV缓存开销。本文认为，将长度扩展与对GPU友好的KV缓存缩减架构相整合，不仅可以降低长度扩展过程中的训练开销，还能取得更优的长上下文性能。由此我们提出了LongGen，该方法可在长度扩展过程中将预训练大语言模型微调为高效架构。
LongGen基于三项核心洞察：（1）窗口注意力（关注最近令牌）、注意力沉底（关注初始令牌）、分块稀疏注意力（跨步令牌块）等稀疏注意力模式非常适合构建高效长上下文模型，这主要是因为它们具备对GPU友好的内存访问模式，不仅在理论上可实现效率提升，在实际应用中也能取得效率增益。（2）让模型能够直接访问所有令牌至关重要。由1/3全注意力层和2/3高效注意力层组成的混合架构可在效率与长上下文性能之间实现平衡权衡。（3）在5B规模的长上下文数据上开展轻量训练，足以将混合模型的上下文长度从4K扩展到128K。
我们在Llama-2 7B和Llama-2 70B上对LongGen开展评估，证明其在不同模型规模下均具备有效性。在128K长度上下文的训练过程中，与全注意力基线模型相比，LongGen实现了1.55倍的训练加速，挂钟时间降低36%。在推理阶段，LongGen将KV缓存内存占用降低62%，实现1.67倍的预填充加速和1.41倍的解码加速。与将KV缓存缩减技术应用于全注意力长上下文大语言模型的基线方法相比，LongGen不仅在“大海捞针”检索任务上取得了明显更优的性能，在BABILong、RULER等难度更高的长上下文推理任务上也表现更佳。
notes: 论文聚焦长上下文训练与推理效率，通过 partial contexts 降低长上下文开销，属于上下文处理效率优化。

title: Improved Adversarial Diffusion Compression for Real-World Video Super-Resolution
abstract: While many diffusion models have achieved impressive results in real-world video super-resolution (Real-VSR) by generating rich and realistic details, their reliance on multi-step sampling leads to slow inference. One-step networks like SeedVR2, DOVE, and DLoRAL alleviate this through condensing generation into one single step, yet they remain heavy, with billions of parameters and multi-second latency. Recent adversarial diffusion compression (ADC) offers a promising path via pruning and distilling these models into a compact AdcSR network, but directly applying it to Real-VSR fails to balance spatial details and temporal consistency due to its lack of temporal awareness and the limitations of standard adversarial learning. To address these challenges, we propose an improved **ADC** method for Real-**VSR**. Our approach distills a large diffusion Transformer (DiT) teacher DOVE equipped with 3D spatio-temporal attentions, into a pruned 2D Stable Diffusion (SD)-based AdcSR backbone, augmented with lightweight 1D temporal convolutions, achieving significantly higher efficiency. In addition, we introduce a dual-head adversarial distillation scheme, in which discriminators in both pixel and feature domains explicitly disentangle the discrimination of details and consistency into two heads, enabling both objectives to be effectively optimized without sacrificing one for the other. Experiments demonstrate that the resulting compressed **AdcVSR** model reduces complexity by **95%** in parameters and achieves an **8$\times$** acceleration over its DiT teacher DOVE, while maintaining competitive video quality and efficiency.
abstract_zh: 尽管众多扩散模型通过生成丰富且逼真的细节，在真实场景视频超分辨率（Real-VSR）任务中取得了出色成果，但这类模型依赖多步采样，导致推理速度缓慢。SeedVR2、DOVE、DLoRAL等单步网络将生成过程压缩为单步以缓解这一问题，但这类网络仍然体量庞大，参数量达数十亿，延迟长达数秒。近期提出的对抗扩散压缩（ADC）技术通过剪枝和蒸馏将上述模型压缩为紧凑的AdcSR网络，为该问题提供了极具前景的解决路径，但由于ADC缺乏时间感知能力，且标准对抗学习存在局限性，直接将其应用于Real-VSR任务时无法平衡空间细节与时间一致性。为解决这些挑战，我们提出了一种面向Real-VSR的改进ADC方法。我们的方法将配备三维时空注意力的大型扩散Transformer（DiT）教师模型DOVE蒸馏到经剪枝的、基于二维稳定扩散（SD）的AdcSR骨干网络中，并为该骨干网络添加轻量一维时间卷积，实现了显著的效率提升。此外，我们提出了双头对抗蒸馏方案，像素域和特征域的判别器将细节判别与一致性判别明确解耦到两个头中，使得两个目标都能得到有效优化，无需为其中一个牺牲另一个。实验表明，最终得到的压缩AdcVSR模型相较于其DiT教师模型DOVE，参数量复杂度降低95%，推理速度提升8倍，同时还能保持具备竞争力的视频质量与效率。
notes: 核心贡献是针对扩散式 Real-VSR 的剪枝与蒸馏压缩，主要对应模型压缩；加速来自压缩后的轻量化模型，而非系统/调度层优化。

title: Compress, Gather, and Recompute: REFORMing Long-Context Processing in Transformers
abstract: As large language models increasingly gain popularity in real-world applications, processing extremely long contexts, often exceeding the model’s pre-trained context limits, has emerged as a critical challenge. While existing approaches to efficient long-context processing show promise, recurrent compression-based methods struggle with information preservation, whereas random access approaches require substantial memory resources. We introduce REFORM, a novel inference framework that efficiently handles long contexts through a two-phase approach. First, it incrementally processes input chunks while maintaining a compressed KV cache, constructs cross-layer context embeddings, and utilizes early exit strategy for improved efficiency. Second, it identifies and gathers essential tokens via similarity matching and selectively recomputes the KV cache. Compared to baselines, REFORM achieves over 50% and 27% performance gains on RULER and BABILong respectively at 1M context length. It also outperforms baselines on ∞-Bench, RepoEval, and MM-NIAH, demonstrating flexibility across diverse tasks and domains. Additionally, REFORM reduces inference time by 30% and peak memory usage by 5%, achieving both efficiency and superior performance.
abstract_zh: 随着大语言模型在现实应用中愈发普及，处理通常超出模型预训练上下文限制的极长上下文已成为一项关键挑战。现有高效长上下文处理方法虽展现出一定潜力，但基于循环压缩的方法存在信息留存困难的问题，而随机访问方法则需要大量内存资源。我们提出REFORM，这是一种新型推理框架，通过两阶段方法高效处理长上下文。第一阶段，该框架在维护压缩键值（KV）缓存的同时增量处理输入分块，构建跨层上下文嵌入，并采用早退出策略提升效率。第二阶段，它通过相似度匹配识别并收集关键词元，有选择性地重计算KV缓存。与基线方法相比，在100万上下文长度下，REFORM在RULER和BABILong任务上的性能分别提升了50%以上和27%。它在∞-Bench、RepoEval和MM-NIAH任务上的表现同样优于基线方法，展现出适配不同任务与领域的灵活性。此外，REFORM将推理时间缩短了30%，峰值内存占用降低了5%，兼顾了效率与优异性能。
notes: 论文核心是长上下文推理框架，重点在压缩与重计算 KV cache，以及 early exit 和跨层上下文表征；不属于典型解码策略优化。

title: Accelerating Linear Recurrent Neural Networks for the Edge with Unstructured Sparsity
abstract:
Linear recurrent neural networks enable powerful long-range sequence modeling with constant memory usage and time-per-token during inference. These architectures hold promise for streaming applications at the edge, but deployment in resource-constrained environments requires hardware-aware optimizations to minimize latency and energy consumption. 
Unstructured sparsity offers a compelling solution, enabling substantial reductions in compute and memory requirements--when accelerated by compatible hardware platforms. 
In this paper, we conduct a scaling study to investigate the Pareto front of performance and efficiency across inference compute budgets.
We find that highly sparse linear RNNs *consistently* achieve better efficiency-performance trade-offs than dense baselines, with $2\times$ less compute and $36$\% less memory at iso-accuracy.
Our models achieve state-of-the-art results on a real-time streaming task for audio denoising.
By quantizing our sparse models to fixed-point arithmetic and deploying them on the Intel Loihi 2 neuromorphic chip for real-time processing, we translate model compression into tangible gains of $42\times$ lower latency and $149\times$ lower energy consumption compared to a dense model on an edge GPU.
Our findings showcase the transformative potential of unstructured sparsity, paving the way for highly efficient recurrent neural networks in real-world, resource-constrained environments.
abstract_zh:
线性循环神经网络可实现性能优异的长程序列建模，推理过程中内存占用恒定，每令牌处理时间固定。这类架构在边缘端流式应用领域具有良好前景，但要在资源受限环境中部署，需要开展硬件感知优化以最大限度降低延迟与能耗。
非结构化稀疏提供了极具吸引力的解决方案，在兼容硬件平台的加速支持下，可大幅削减计算与内存需求。
本文开展缩放研究，探究不同推理计算预算下性能与效率的帕累托最优前沿。我们发现，高稀疏度线性循环神经网络的效率-性能权衡表现始终优于稠密基线模型，在同精度条件下，计算量降低2倍，内存占用减少36%。我们的模型在音频降噪实时流式任务上取得了当前最优结果。通过将稀疏模型量化为定点运算，并部署在英特尔Loihi 2神经形态芯片上开展实时处理，我们将模型压缩效果转化为实际收益：与运行在边缘GPU上的稠密模型相比，延迟降低42倍，能耗降低149倍。我们的研究结果展现了非结构化稀疏的变革性潜力，为高效循环神经网络应用于现实资源受限环境铺平了道路。
notes: 论文核心聚焦边缘部署加速与资源约束下的硬件感知优化；“非结构化稀疏”直接对应模型压缩信号。

title: LLaVA-Mini: Efficient Image and Video Large Multimodal Models with One Vision Token
abstract: The advent of real-time large multimodal models (LMMs) like GPT-4o has sparked considerable interest in efficient LMMs. LMM frameworks typically encode visual inputs into vision tokens (continuous representations) and integrate them and textual instructions into the context of large language models (LLMs), where large-scale parameters and numerous context tokens (predominantly vision tokens) result in substantial computational overhead. Previous efforts towards efficient LMMs always focus on replacing the LLM backbone with smaller models, while neglecting the crucial issue of token quantity. In this paper, we introduce LLaVA-Mini, an efficient LMM with minimal vision tokens. To achieve a high compression ratio of vision tokens while preserving visual information, we first analyze how LMMs understand vision tokens and find that most vision tokens only play a crucial role in the early layers of LLM backbone, where they mainly fuse visual information into text tokens. Building on this finding, LLaVA-Mini introduces modality pre-fusion to fuse visual information into text tokens in advance, thereby facilitating the extreme compression of vision tokens fed to LLM backbone into one token. LLaVA-Mini is a unified large multimodal model that can support the understanding of images, high-resolution images, and videos in an efficient manner. Experiments across 11 image-based and 7 video-based benchmarks demonstrate that LLaVA-Mini outperforms LLaVA-v1.5 with just 1 vision token instead of 576. Efficiency analyses reveal that LLaVA-Mini can reduce FLOPs by 77%, deliver low-latency responses within 40 milliseconds, and process over 10,000 frames of video on the GPU hardware with 24GB of memory.
abstract_zh: GPT-4o等实时大型多模态模型（LMM）的出现引发了人们对高效大型多模态模型的浓厚兴趣。大型多模态模型框架通常将视觉输入编码为视觉令牌（连续表示），再将其与文本指令一同整合到大型语言模型（LLM）的上下文中，而大规模参数与大量上下文令牌（以视觉令牌为主）会产生极高的计算开销。此前针对高效大型多模态模型的研究始终聚焦于用更小的模型替换大型语言模型主干，却忽略了令牌数量这一关键问题。本文提出LLaVA-Mini，这是一款视觉令牌数量极少的高效大型多模态模型。为了在保留视觉信息的同时实现视觉令牌的高压缩比，我们首先分析了大型多模态模型对视觉令牌的理解机制，发现绝大多数视觉令牌仅在大型语言模型主干的前几层发挥关键作用，主要负责将视觉信息融合到文本令牌中。基于这一发现，LLaVA-Mini引入模态预融合机制，提前将视觉信息融合到文本令牌中，从而可将输入至大型语言模型主干的视觉令牌极致压缩为单个令牌。LLaVA-Mini是一款统一的大型多模态模型，能够高效支持普通图像、高分辨率图像及视频的理解任务。在11个图像类基准测试和7个视频类基准测试上开展的实验表明，LLaVA-Mini仅使用1个视觉令牌而非LLaVA-v1.5的576个，性能便优于LLaVA-v1.5。效率分析显示，LLaVA-Mini可减少77%的浮点运算量，能在40毫秒内生成低延迟响应，且在显存为24GB的GPU硬件上可处理超过10000帧视频。
notes: 论文核心是通过模态预融合与单视觉 token 设计降低多模态推理开销，明显属于多模态 LLM；偏好上更直接对应上下文 token 压缩与模型结构侧推理优化，而非系统调度或算子内核层优化。

title: PM-KVQ: Progressive Mixed-precision KV Cache Quantization for Long-CoT LLMs
abstract:
Recently, significant progress has been made in developing reasoning-capable Large Language Models (LLMs) through long Chain-of-Thought (CoT) techniques.
However, this long-CoT reasoning process imposes substantial memory overhead due to the large Key-Value (KV) Cache memory overhead.
Post-training KV Cache quantization has emerged as a promising compression technique and has been extensively studied in short-context scenarios.
However, directly applying existing methods to long-CoT LLMs causes significant performance degradation due to the following two reasons: 
(1) Large cumulative error: Existing methods fail to adequately leverage available memory, and they directly quantize the KV Cache during each decoding step, leading to large cumulative quantization error.
(2) Short-context calibration: Due to Rotary Positional Embedding (RoPE), the use of short-context data during calibration fails to account for the distribution of less frequent channels in the Key Cache, resulting in performance loss.
We propose Progressive Mixed-Precision KV Cache Quantization (PM-KVQ) for long-CoT LLMs to address the above issues in two folds:
(1) To reduce cumulative error, we design a progressive quantization strategy to gradually lower the bit-width of KV Cache in each block. Then, we propose block-wise memory allocation to assign a higher bit-width to more sensitive transformer blocks. 
(2) To increase the calibration length without additional overhead, we propose a new calibration strategy with positional interpolation that leverages short calibration data with positional interpolation to approximate the data distribution of long-context data.
Extensive experiments on 7B–70B long-CoT LLMs show that PM-KVQ improves reasoning benchmark performance by up to 8% over SOTA baselines under the same memory budget and achieves 2.73–5.18$\times$ throughput over the original 16-bit LLMs.
Our code will be released soon.
abstract_zh: 近年来，通过长思维链（CoT）技术开发具备推理能力的大语言模型（LLM）已取得显著进展。然而，这种长思维链推理过程会因庞大的键值（KV）缓存内存开销带来极高的内存负担。训练后KV缓存量化作为一种极具前景的压缩技术，已在短上下文场景中得到广泛研究。但直接将现有方法应用于长思维链大语言模型会导致性能严重下降，原因主要有两点：（1）累积误差大：现有方法无法充分利用可用内存，会在每个解码步骤直接量化KV缓存，导致量化累积误差偏大。（2）短上下文校准：受旋转位置嵌入（RoPE）影响，校准时使用短上下文数据无法覆盖键缓存中低频通道的分布，进而造成性能损失。我们针对长思维链大语言模型提出渐进式混合精度KV缓存量化（PM-KVQ）方法，从两个方面解决上述问题：（1）为降低累积误差，我们设计了渐进式量化策略，逐步降低每个模块中KV缓存的位宽；随后提出逐模块内存分配机制，为敏感度更高的Transformer模块分配更高的位宽。（2）为在不引入额外开销的前提下提升校准长度，我们提出一种结合位置插值的新型校准策略，利用经过位置插值处理的短校准数据来近似长上下文数据的分布。在7B至70B参数规模的长思维链大语言模型上开展的大量实验表明，在相同内存预算下，PM-KVQ相较现有最优基线的推理基准性能最高提升8%，且吞吐量可达原始16位大语言模型的2.73至5.18倍。我们的代码即将发布。
notes: 论文聚焦长链路推理LLM中的KV Cache量化，通过渐进式混合精度压缩缓存，直接对应上下文/缓存优化与模型压缩。

title: AdaDecode: Accelerating LLM Decoding with Adaptive Layer Parallelism
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used for long-content generation (e.g., long Chain-of-Thought reasoning) where decoding efficiency becomes a critical bottleneck: Autoregressive decoding is inherently limited by its sequential token generation process, where each token must be generated before the next can be processed. This sequential dependency restricts the ability to fully leverage modern hardware’s parallel processing capabilities. Existing methods like speculative decoding and layer skipping offer potential speedups but have notable drawbacks: speculative decoding relies on an auxiliary “drafter” model, which can be challenging to acquire and increases memory overhead, while layer skipping may introduce discrepancies in the generated outputs due to the missing key-value cache at skipped layers. In this work, we propose AdaDecode, which accelerates LLM decoding without requiring auxiliary models or changes to the original model parameters, while ensuring output consistency. AdaDecode leverages the insight that many tokens—particularly simple or highly-predictable ones—can accurately be generated at intermediate layers, as further layers often do not significantly alter predictions once the model reaches a certain confidence. By adaptively generating tokens at intermediate layers when confidence is high, AdaDecode enables the next token’s computation to begin immediately. The remaining layer computations for early-predicted tokens are deferred and executed in parallel with subsequent tokens when needed, maximizing hardware utilization and reducing decoding latency. A final verification step ensures that early predictions match the results of standard autoregressive decoding, preserving output parity. Experiments across diverse generation tasks shows that AdaDecode consistently achieves superior decoding throughput compared to baselines with up to 1.73$\times$ speedup, while guaranteeing output parity with standard autoregressive decoding.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）正被越来越多地用于长内容生成场景（例如长思维链推理），这类场景下解码效率成为关键瓶颈：自回归解码本质上受限于其序列式词元生成过程，必须先生成当前词元才能处理下一个词元。这种序列依赖关系限制了对现代硬件并行处理能力的充分利用。投机解码、层跳过等现有方法虽具备提速潜力，但存在明显缺陷：投机解码依赖辅助“草稿”模型，这类模型获取难度较高，还会增加内存开销；而层跳过方法由于被跳过的层缺失键值缓存，可能导致生成输出出现偏差。本研究提出AdaDecode方法，无需辅助模型，也无需修改原始模型参数即可加速LLM解码，同时还能保障输出一致性。AdaDecode基于如下洞见：许多词元，尤其是简单或可预测性极高的词元，可在中间层准确生成，因为一旦模型达到一定置信度，后续层通常不会明显改变预测结果。当置信度较高时，AdaDecode会自适应地在中间层生成词元，从而可立即启动下一个词元的计算。提前预测得到的词元的剩余层计算会被延后，在需要时与后续词元的计算并行执行，最大化硬件利用率并降低解码延迟。最后的验证步骤可确保提前预测结果与标准自回归解码的结果一致，维持输出对等性。在多种生成任务上开展的实验表明，与基线方法相比，AdaDecode始终具备更优的解码吞吐量，提速幅度最高可达1.73倍，同时能够保证与标准自回归解码的输出完全一致。
notes: 论文核心是 LLM 推理解码加速，通过自适应中间层提前预测与分层并行执行提升吞吐，明确属于解码优化；同时涉及并行执行与硬件利用率提升，属于系统与调度优化。摘要提到 KV cache 问题主要是对比现有方法的缺陷，AdaDecode 本身并非直接提出新的缓存机制，因此该标签可保留但证据相对次要。

title: Adaptive Pruning of Pretrained Transformer via Differential Inclusions
abstract: Large transformers have demonstrated remarkable success, making it necessary to compress these models to reduce inference costs while preserving their performance. Current compression algorithms prune transformers at fixed compression ratios, requiring a unique pruning process for each ratio, which results in high computational costs. In contrast, we propose pruning of pretrained transformers at any desired ratio within a single pruning stage, based on a differential inclusion for a mask parameter. This dynamic can generate the whole regularization solution path of the mask parameter, whose support set identifies the network structure. Therefore, the solution path identifies a Transformer weight family with various sparsity levels, offering greater flexibility and customization.In this paper, weintroduce such an effective pruning method, termed SPP (Solution Path Pruning). To achieve effective pruning, we segment the transformers into paired modules, including query-key pairs, value-projection pairs, and sequential linear layers, and apply low-rank compression to these pairs, maintaining the output structure while enabling structural compression within the inner states. Extensive experiments conducted on various well-known transformer backbones have demonstrated the efficacy of SPP.
abstract_zh: 大型Transformer已取得极为出色的表现，因此有必要对这类模型进行压缩，在保留其性能的同时降低推理成本。现有压缩算法以固定压缩率对Transformer执行剪枝，每种压缩率都需要单独的剪枝流程，导致计算成本极高。与之不同，我们基于掩码参数的微分包含，提出可在单次剪枝阶段内以任意目标压缩率对预训练Transformer执行剪枝的方法。这种动态过程可以生成掩码参数的完整正则化解路径，其支撑集可确定网络结构。因此，该解路径可得到具备不同稀疏度水平的Transformer权重族，灵活性与可定制性更强。本文提出了这种高效剪枝方法，命名为解路径剪枝（Solution Path Pruning，SPP）。为实现有效剪枝，我们将Transformer拆分为成对模块，包括查询-键对、值-投影对以及连续线性层，对这些成对模块应用低秩压缩，在维持输出结构的同时可在内层状态中实现结构压缩。在多个知名Transformer骨干网络上开展的大量实验证明了SPP的有效性。
notes: 论文核心是对预训练 Transformer 做可变压缩率的剪枝与低秩压缩，属于模型压缩；未体现系统调度、缓存或内核优化。

title: NestedFP: High-Performance, Memory-Efficient Dual-Precision Floating Point Support for LLMs
abstract: Meeting service-level objectives (SLOs) in Large Language Models (LLMs) serving is critical, but managing the high variability in load presents a significant challenge. Recent advancements in FP8 inference, backed by native hardware support, offer a potential solution: executing FP16 models by default, while switching to FP8 models during sudden load surges to achieve higher throughput at the cost of a slight quality degradation. Although this approach facilitates effective SLO management, it introduces additional memory overhead due to storing two versions of the same model. In response, this paper proposes NestedFP, an LLM serving technique that supports both FP16 and FP8 models in a memoryefficient manner by overlaying FP8 parameters onto FP16 parameters, allowing both models to share the same FP16 memory footprint. By leveraging a compact data format for the overlay and a specialized GEMM kernel optimized for this format, NestedFP ensures minimal degradation in both model quality and inference throughput across both FP8 and FP16 modes. NestedFP provides a flexible platform for dynamic, SLO-aware precision selection. The code is available at https://github.com/SNU-ARC/NestedFP.
abstract_zh: 在大语言模型（LLM）服务中满足服务水平目标（SLO）至关重要，但管控负载的高波动性是一项重大挑战。近期获得原生硬件支持的FP8推理技术进展提供了一种潜在解决方案：默认运行FP16模型，在负载突发飙升时切换到FP8模型，以略微降低质量为代价实现更高吞吐量。尽管该方法可实现高效的SLO管理，但由于需要存储同一模型的两个版本，会引入额外的内存开销。针对这一问题，本文提出NestedFP，这是一种LLM服务技术，通过将FP8参数叠加到FP16参数上，允许两种模型共享相同的FP16内存占用，从而以内存高效的方式同时支持FP16和FP8模型。通过为叠加层采用紧凑数据格式，以及针对该格式优化的专用GEMM内核，NestedFP确保FP8和FP16两种模式下的模型质量与推理吞吐量均仅有极小幅度的下降。NestedFP为动态、SLO感知的精度选择提供了灵活平台。代码可在https://github.com/SNU-ARC/NestedFP获取。
notes: 论文聚焦LLM推理服务中的双精度切换与内存复用，核心贡献覆盖SLO感知调度、专用GEMM内核以及以共享FP16占用实现FP8/FP16双模型支持的压缩式表示；未见与上下文或KV缓存优化直接相关的内容。

title: Rotated Runtime Smooth: Training-Free Activation Smoother for accurate INT4 inference
abstract:
Large language models have demonstrated promising capabilities upon scaling up parameters. However, serving large language models incurs substantial computation and memory movement costs due to their large scale. Quantization methods have been employed to reduce service costs and latency. Nevertheless, outliers in activations hinder the development of INT4 weight-activation quantization. Existing approaches separate outliers and normal values into two matrices or migrate outliers from activations to weights, suffering from high latency or accuracy degradation. Based on observing activations from large language models, outliers can be classified into channel-wise and spike outliers.
In this work, we propose Rotated Runtime Smooth (**RRS**), a plug-and-play activation smoother for quantization, consisting of Runtime Smooth and the Rotation operation. Runtime Smooth (**RS**) is introduced to eliminate **channel-wise outliers** by smoothing activations with channel-wise maximums during runtime. The Rotation operation can narrow the gap between **spike outliers** and normal values, alleviating the effect of victims caused by channel-wise smoothing.
The proposed method outperforms the state-of-the-art method in the LLaMA and Qwen families and improves WikiText-2 perplexity from 57.33 to 6.66 for INT4 inference.
abstract_zh:
大型语言模型在参数规模扩大后展现出可观的性能。然而，由于规模庞大，部署大型语言模型会产生极高的计算与内存移动开销。量化方法已被用于降低服务成本与延迟，但激活值中的异常值阻碍了INT4权重-激活量化的发展。现有方法要么将异常值与正常值拆分至两个矩阵，要么将异常值从激活值迁移到权重中，存在延迟过高或精度下降的问题。基于对大型语言模型激活值的观察，异常值可分为通道级异常值和尖峰异常值。
本研究提出旋转运行时平滑（RRS），这是一种用于量化场景的即插即用激活平滑器，由运行时平滑与旋转操作两部分构成。我们引入运行时平滑（RS），在运行期间通过通道最大值对激活值进行平滑处理，以消除通道级异常值。旋转操作能够缩小尖峰异常值与正常值之间的差距，缓解通道级平滑引发的受损值带来的负面影响。
所提方法在LLaMA与Qwen系列模型上的表现优于当前最优方法，将INT4推理的WikiText-2困惑度从57.33优化至6.66。
notes: 论文面向大语言模型推理量化，核心是通过 training-free activation smoother 改善 INT4 weight-activation quantization 的精度与可用性，属于模型压缩方向。

title: Compute or Load KV Cache? Why Not Both?
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in large-scale online services, enabling sophisticated applications. However, the computational overhead of generating key-value (KV) caches in the prefill stage presents a major bottleneck, particularly for long-context inputs. Prefix caching mitigates this issue by storing KV caches for reuse, reducing redundant computation. Despite its advantages, prefix caching suffers from high latency due to the limited I/O bandwidth of storage devices, constraining inference efficiency. To address this challenge, we introduce Cake, a novel KV cache loading system that optimally utilizes both computational and I/O resources in parallel. Cake employs a bidirectional scheduling strategy that dynamically balances KV cache computation and loading, ensuring efficient resource utilization. Additionally, Cake incorporates an adaptive scheduling mechanism that seamlessly integrates with non-prefix caching requests, improving system throughput and adapting to fluctuating resource availabilty. Through extensive evaluations across various hardware configurations, datasets, and storage conditions, Cake achieves on average 2.6× reduction in Time to First Token (TTFT) compared to compute-only and I/O-only methods. Our findings highlight Cake as an effective and practical solution for optimizing long-context LLM inference, bridging the gap between computation and I/O efficiency in large-scale AI deployments.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）正越来越多地部署在大规模在线服务中，为各类复杂应用提供支撑。然而，预填充阶段生成键值（KV）缓存的计算开销是一项主要瓶颈，在长上下文输入场景下尤为突出。前缀缓存通过存储KV缓存以供复用、减少冗余计算，能够缓解该问题。尽管存在上述优势，前缀缓存因存储设备I/O带宽有限存在高延迟问题，制约了推理效率。为应对这一挑战，我们提出Cake，这是一种新型KV缓存加载系统，可并行最优利用计算与I/O资源。Cake采用双向调度策略，动态平衡KV缓存的计算与加载过程，确保资源高效利用。此外，Cake集成了自适应调度机制，可与非前缀缓存请求无缝集成，提升系统吞吐量并适配波动的资源可用性。我们在不同硬件配置、数据集和存储条件下开展了大量评估，结果表明，与仅采用计算或仅采用I/O的方法相比，Cake的首令牌生成时间（TTFT）平均降低2.6倍。研究结果表明，Cake是优化长上下文大语言模型推理的有效实用方案，能够弥合大规模AI部署中计算效率与I/O效率之间的差距。
notes: 论文聚焦长上下文 LLM 推理中的 KV cache 复用与加载，核心贡献同时落在缓存优化与系统调度优化。

title: Relaxed Recursive Transformers: Effective Parameter Sharing with Layer-wise LoRA
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are expensive to deploy. Parameter sharing offers a possible path towards reducing their size and cost, but its effectiveness in modern LLMs remains fairly limited. In this work, we revisit "layer tying" as form of parameter sharing in Transformers, and introduce novel methods for converting existing LLMs into smaller "Recursive Transformers" that share parameters across layers, with minimal loss of performance. Here, our Recursive Transformers are efficiently initialized from standard pretrained Transformers, but only use a single block of unique layers that is then repeated multiple times in a loop. We further improve  performance by introducing Relaxed Recursive Transformers that add flexibility to the layer tying constraint via depth-wise low-rank adaptation (LoRA) modules, yet still preserve the compactness of the overall model. We show that our recursive models (e.g., recursive Gemma 1B) outperform both similar-sized vanilla pretrained models (such as TinyLlama 1.1B and Pythia 1B) and knowledge distillation baselines---and can even recover most of the performance of the original "full-size" model (e.g., Gemma 2B with no shared parameters). Finally, we propose Continuous Depth-wise Batching, a promising new inference paradigm enabled by the Recursive Transformer when paired with early exiting. In a theoretical analysis, we show  that this has the potential to lead to significant (2-3$\times$) gains in inference throughput.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）部署成本高昂。参数共享是减小其规模、降低成本的可行路径，但在现代大语言模型中的有效性仍十分有限。本研究重新审视作为Transformer参数共享形式的“层绑定”，并提出新方法将现有大语言模型转换为更小的“递归Transformer”，这类模型在层间共享参数，性能损失极小。本文提出的递归Transformer可从标准预训练Transformer高效初始化，仅使用单组唯一的层块，再将该层块循环重复多次。我们进一步引入松弛递归Transformer以提升性能，该模型通过深度方向低秩适配（LoRA）模块为层绑定约束增加灵活性，同时仍能保持整体模型的紧凑性。实验表明，我们的递归模型（例如递归Gemma 1B）既优于同等规模的普通预训练模型（如TinyLlama 1.1B和Pythia 1B）以及知识蒸馏基线，甚至可以恢复原“全尺寸”模型（如无参数共享的Gemma 2B）的大部分性能。最后，我们提出连续深度批处理，这是递归Transformer结合提前退出机制时可实现的一种极具潜力的新推理范式。理论分析表明，该范式有望带来推理吞吐量的显著提升（可达2-3倍）。
notes: ['核心贡献是通过层共享与depth-wise LoRA构造更紧凑的Recursive Transformer，明显属于模型压缩。', 'Continuous Depth-wise Batching配合early exiting直接面向推理吞吐优化，可归入系统与调度优化。', '摘要未涉及算子/内核级实现细节，也不属于解码策略优化。']

title: AnyBCQ: Hardware Efficient Flexible Binary-Coded Quantization for Multi-Precision LLMs
abstract:
The deployment of large language models (LLMs) is increasingly constrained by memory and latency bottlenecks, motivating the need for quantization techniques that flexibly balance accuracy and efficiency. 
Recent work has introduced multi-precision models, which enable inference at multiple precisions within a single model depending on runtime constraints.
To support such flexibility, quantized weights are often stored as bit-planes, where hardware efficiency improves when the compute operates directly at the bit-plane level and activates only the precision required by each request.
In this work, we present AnyBCQ, a hardware-friendly multi-precision extension of Binary-Coded Quantization (BCQ) that supports direct bit-plane operations.
By representing weights as binary bit-planes with corresponding scale factors, AnyBCQ enables bit-plane–level computation and maps naturally to accelerator-friendly, bit-parallel arithmetic.
Our progressive precision expansion mechanism incrementally refines scaling factors while reusing previously assigned binary codes, yielding monotonic improvements in accuracy as additional bits are enabled. 
We further co-design a specialized kernel that exploits the BCQ structure to support dynamic per-request precision selection with negligible overhead. 
Experiments on recent LLMs demonstrate that AnyBCQ significantly narrows the accuracy drop in the low-bit regime (e.g. 2-bit), remains competitive at higher precision, and achieves throughput gains of up to $3.0\times$ over half precision and $1.2\times$ over state-of-the-art multi-precision methods. 
By aligning algorithmic flexibility with hardware efficiency, AnyBCQ provides a practical foundation for multi-precision LLM deployment across diverse service-level objectives.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型的部署正日益受到内存和延迟瓶颈的限制，这推动了对能够灵活平衡精度与效率的量化技术的需求。最新研究提出了多精度模型，这类模型可根据运行时约束在单个模型内以多种精度完成推理。为支撑这种灵活性，量化后的权重通常以位平面形式存储，当计算直接在位平面层面执行且仅激活每个请求所需的精度时，硬件效率会得到提升。本研究提出AnyBCQ，这是二进制编码量化（BCQ）的硬件友好型多精度扩展方案，支持直接位平面操作。AnyBCQ将权重表示为带有对应缩放因子的二进制位平面，从而支持位平面级计算，并且可自然适配适合加速器的位并行算术。我们提出的渐进式精度扩展机制在复用已分配的二进制代码的同时逐步优化缩放因子，可在启用更多比特位时实现精度的单调提升。我们进一步协同设计了专用内核，该内核利用BCQ结构支持动态的逐请求精度选择，且开销可忽略不计。在最新大语言模型上开展的实验表明，AnyBCQ大幅降低了低比特区间（如2比特）的精度损失，在更高精度下性能仍具备竞争力，并且相较于半精度实现了最高3.0倍的吞吐量提升，相较于最先进的多精度方法实现了最高1.2倍的吞吐量提升。通过将算法灵活性与硬件效率相匹配，AnyBCQ为面向不同服务级别目标的多精度大语言模型部署提供了实用基础。
notes: 论文核心是面向LLM部署的量化方法，目标直指压缩模型表示并改善内存/时延效率，属于模型压缩方向。

title: LycheeDecode: Accelerating Long-Context LLM Inference via Hybrid-Head Sparse Decoding
abstract: The proliferation of long-context large language models (LLMs) exposes a key bottleneck: the rapidly expanding key-value cache during decoding, which imposes heavy memory and latency costs. While recent approaches attempt to alleviate this by sharing a single set of crucial tokens across layers, such coarse-grained sharing undermines model performance by neglecting the functional diversity of attention heads. To address this, we propose LycheeDecode, an efficient decoding method centered on a fine-grained hybrid-head attention mechanism that employs a hardware-efficient top-$k$ selection strategy. Specifically, the novel HardKuma-based mechanism partitions attention heads into a small subset of retrieval heads that dynamically identify crucial tokens and a majority of sparse heads that reuse them for efficient computation. Through extensive experiments on leading models like Llama3 and Qwen3 across diverse benchmarks for long-context understanding (e.g., LongBench, RULER) and complex reasoning (e.g., AIME24, OlympiadBench), we demonstrate that LycheeDecode achieves generative quality comparable to, and at times surpassing even the full-attention baseline. Crucially, this is accomplished with up to a 2.7x speedup at a 128K context length. By preserving the functional diversity of attention heads, our fine-grained strategy overcomes the performance bottlenecks of existing methods, providing a powerful and validated pathway to both efficient and high-quality long-context LLM inference. The implementation code, kernels, and models will be publicly available.
abstract_zh: 长上下文大语言模型的普及暴露出一个关键瓶颈：解码过程中键值缓存快速膨胀，带来了极高的内存与延迟成本。尽管现有方案尝试通过跨层共享单组关键令牌缓解这一问题，但这种粗粒度共享忽略了注意力头的功能多样性，会损害模型性能。针对该问题，我们提出LycheeDecode，这是一种以细粒度混合头注意力机制为核心的高效解码方法，采用了硬件高效的top-k选择策略。具体而言，这种基于HardKuma的全新机制将注意力头划分为两类：一小部分检索头负责动态识别关键令牌，占绝大多数的稀疏头则复用这些令牌以实现高效计算。我们针对Llama3、Qwen3等主流模型，在长上下文理解（如LongBench、RULER）和复杂推理（如AIME24、OlympiadBench）的各类基准测试上开展了大量实验，结果表明LycheeDecode的生成质量与全注意力基线相当，有时甚至更优。尤为重要的是，在128K上下文长度下，LycheeDecode最多可实现2.7倍加速。我们的细粒度策略保留了注意力头的功能多样性，克服了现有方法的性能瓶颈，为实现高效且高质量的长上下文大语言模型推理提供了一条经过验证的有效路径。本工作的实现代码、内核与模型将公开发布。
notes: 论文聚焦长上下文 LLM 推理加速，核心是基于混合头稀疏注意力的解码与 KV/关键 token 复用；包含硬件高效 top-k 与 kernels 表述，但未明显涉及系统级调度。

title: Lexico: Extreme KV Cache Compression via Sparse Coding over Universal Dictionaries
abstract: We introduce Lexico, a novel KV cache compression method that leverages sparse coding with a universal dictionary. Our key finding is that key-value cache in modern LLMs can be accurately approximated using sparse linear combination from a small, input-agnostic dictionary of ~4k atoms, enabling efficient compression across different input prompts, tasks and models. Using orthogonal matching pursuit for sparse approximation, Lexico achieves flexible compression ratios through direct sparsity control. On GSM8K, across multiple model families (Mistral, Llama 3, Qwen2.5), Lexico maintains 90-95% of the original performance while using only 15-25% of the full KV-cache memory, outperforming both quantization and token eviction methods. Notably, Lexico remains effective in low memory regimes where 2-bit quantization fails, achieving up to 1.7x better compression on LongBench and GSM8K while maintaining high accuracy.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了Lexico，一种借助通用字典稀疏编码实现的新型KV缓存压缩方法。我们的核心发现是，现代大语言模型中的键值缓存可通过由约4000个原子组成的小型、与输入无关的字典的稀疏线性组合得到精准近似，从而能够在不同输入提示、任务和模型间实现高效压缩。通过正交匹配追踪算法完成稀疏近似，Lexico可通过直接控制稀疏度实现灵活的压缩比调节。在GSM8K数据集上，跨多个模型系列（Mistral、Llama 3、Qwen2.5）测试时，Lexico仅占用15%~25%的全量KV缓存内存，同时保留了原始模型90%~95%的性能，表现优于量化和令牌逐出两类方法。值得注意的是，在2比特量化失效的低内存场景下Lexico仍然有效，在LongBench和GSM8K数据集上可实现最高1.7倍的压缩比提升，同时保持较高准确率。
notes: ['主题直接聚焦 KV cache 压缩，属于上下文与缓存优化。', '方法通过稀疏编码和通用字典降低缓存存储开销，也符合模型压缩。']

title: Speculate Deep and Accurate: Lossless and Training-Free Acceleration for Offloaded LLMs via Substitute Speculative Decoding
abstract:
The immense model sizes of large language models (LLMs) challenge deployment on memory-limited consumer GPUs.
    Although model compression and parameter offloading are common strategies to address memory limitations, compression can degrade quality, and offloading maintains quality but suffers from slow inference.
    Speculative decoding presents a promising avenue to accelerate parameter offloading, utilizing a fast draft model to propose multiple draft tokens, which are then verified by the target LLM in parallel with a single forward pass. This method reduces the time-consuming data transfers in forward passes that involve offloaded weight transfers.
    Existing methods often rely on pretrained weights of the same family, but require additional training to align with custom-trained models. Moreover, approaches that involve draft model training usually yield only modest speedups. This limitation arises from insufficient alignment with the target model, preventing higher token acceptance lengths.
    To address these challenges and achieve greater speedups, we propose SubSpec, a plug-and-play method to accelerate parameter offloading that is lossless and training-free. SubSpec constructs a highly aligned draft model by generating low-bit quantized substitute layers from offloaded target LLM portions. Additionally, our method shares the remaining GPU-resident layers and the KV-Cache, further reducing memory overhead and enhance alignment.
    SubSpec achieves a high average acceptance length, delivering 9.1$\times$ speedup for Qwen2.5 7B on MT-Bench (8GB VRAM limit) and an average of 12.5$\times$ speedup for Qwen2.5 32B on popular generation benchmarks (24GB VRAM limit).
abstract_zh: 大语言模型庞大的模型尺寸对其在内存有限的消费级GPU上的部署构成了挑战。模型压缩和参数卸载是解决内存限制问题的常用策略，但压缩会降低模型质量，卸载虽能保留模型质量却存在推理速度缓慢的问题。投机解码为加速参数卸载提供了极具前景的方向，该方法利用快速草稿模型生成多个草稿令牌，随后仅通过目标大语言模型的一次前向传播即可并行完成所有草稿令牌的验证，减少了前向传播过程中涉及卸载权重传输的耗时数据传输操作。现有方法通常依赖同一系列的预训练权重，需要额外训练才能与自定义训练的模型对齐，此外涉及草稿模型训练的方法通常只能实现有限的加速效果，这一局限源于其与目标模型的对齐度不足，无法实现更高的令牌接受长度。为应对上述挑战并实现更显著的加速效果，我们提出了SubSpec，这是一种可即插即用的参数卸载加速方法，具备无损且无需训练的特点。SubSpec通过从已卸载的目标大语言模型部分生成低比特量化的替代层，构建出对齐度极高的草稿模型。此外，该方法共用了剩余的GPU驻留层和KV缓存，进一步降低了内存开销并提升了对齐度。SubSpec实现了较高的平均接受长度，在MT-Bench基准测试中，针对8GB显存限制下的Qwen2.5 7B模型实现了9.1倍加速；在常用生成基准测试中，针对24GB显存限制下的Qwen2.5 32B模型实现了平均12.5倍的加速。
notes: 论文核心是面向离线/卸载式 LLM 推理的 speculative decoding 加速；主标签应为解码策略优化，同时与 offloading 场景下的系统侧推理调度密切相关。

title: Speculative Speculative Decoding
abstract: Autoregressive decoding is bottlenecked by its *sequential* nature. Speculative decoding has become a standard way to accelerate inference by using a fast *draft model* to predict upcoming tokens from a slower *target model*, and then verifying them *in parallel* with a single target model forward pass. However, speculative decoding itself relies on a *sequential* dependence between speculation and verification. We introduce *speculative speculative decoding* (SSD) to parallelize these operations. While a verification is ongoing, the draft model *predicts* likely verification outcomes and prepares speculations pre-emptively for them. If the actual verification outcome is then in the predicted set, a speculation can be returned immediately, thereby eliminating all speculation overhead.  We identify three key challenges presented by speculative speculative decoding, and put forth principled methods to solve each after theoretical analysis. The result is **Saguaro**, an optimized SSD algorithm which is up to twice as fast as optimized speculative decoding baselines and up to 5× faster than autoregressive decoding with open source inference engines.  **Saguaro** can be combined with existing methods like EAGLE and token tree speculation for further gains, and permits scaling draft compute to better predict verification outcomes, introducing new tradeoffs between compute and latency.
abstract_zh: 自回归解码的瓶颈在于其序列属性。推测解码已成为推理加速的标准方法，它使用快速的草稿模型预测更慢的目标模型的后续词元，再通过目标模型的单轮前向传播并行验证这些词元。但推测解码本身在推测步骤与验证步骤之间存在序列依赖关系。我们提出推测式推测解码（SSD）来并行化这两类操作。当验证正在进行时，草稿模型会预测可能的验证结果，并预先为这些结果准备好推测内容。如果后续实际验证结果落在预测集合内，就可以立即返回推测结果，从而消除所有推测开销。我们明确了推测式推测解码存在的三项核心挑战，经过理论分析后，提出了有理论依据的方法逐一解决上述挑战。最终得到的优化SSD算法Saguaro，在开源推理引擎下的速度最高可达优化后的推测解码基线的2倍，最高可达自回归解码的5倍。Saguaro可与EAGLE、词元树推测等现有方法结合以进一步提升性能，同时支持扩展草稿计算量以更好地预测验证结果，为计算量与延迟之间带来了新的权衡空间。
notes: 论文聚焦 LLM 推理解码加速，通过将 speculation 与 verification 进一步并行化来降低时延，既属于解码策略优化，也涉及推理流程中的并行调度与系统级延迟优化。

title: KV-Cache Transform Coding for Compact Storage in LLM Inference
abstract: Serving large language models (LLMs) at scale necessitates efficient key-value (KV) cache management. KV caches can be reused across conversation turns via shared-prefix prompts that are common in iterative code editing and chat. However, stale caches consume scarce GPU memory, require offloading, or force recomputation. We present KVTC, a lightweight transform coder that compresses KV caches for compact on-GPU and off-GPU storage. Drawing on classical media compression, KVTC combines PCA-based feature decorrelation, adaptive quantization, and entropy coding. It requires only a brief initial calibration and leaves model parameters unchanged. By exploiting redundancies in KV caches, KVTC achieves up to 20x compression, while maintaining reasoning and long-context accuracy in Llama 3.1, Mistral-NeMo, and R1-Qwen 2.5. Across benchmarks including AIME25, LiveCodeBench, GSM8K, MMLU, Qasper, RULER, and MATH500, KVTC consistently outperforms inference-time baselines such as token eviction, quantization, and SVD-based methods, delivering substantially higher compression ratios. These results support KVTC as a practical building block for memory-efficient LLM serving with reusable KV caches.
abstract_zh: 规模化提供大语言模型（LLM）推理服务需要高效的键值（KV）缓存管理。KV缓存可在多轮对话中通过共享前缀提示词复用，这类共享前缀提示在迭代式代码编辑和对话场景中十分常见。然而，陈旧缓存会消耗稀缺的GPU显存，需要被卸载，或是迫使系统重新计算缓存。我们提出KVTC这一轻量级变换编码器，可对KV缓存进行压缩，实现GPU内存和GPU外存储的紧凑存储。KVTC借鉴经典媒体压缩技术，结合了基于主成分分析（PCA）的特征去相关、自适应量化和熵编码三种技术。它仅需短暂的初始校准，且不会修改模型参数。通过利用KV缓存中的冗余信息，KVTC可实现最高20倍的压缩比，同时在Llama 3.1、Mistral-NeMo和R1-Qwen 2.5模型上保持了推理能力和长上下文准确率。在AIME25、LiveCodeBench、GSM8K、MMLU、Qasper、RULER和MATH500等基准测试中，KVTC的表现始终优于推理阶段的基线方法，包括令牌驱逐、量化和基于奇异值分解（SVD）的方法，可实现显著更高的压缩比。这些结果表明，KVTC可作为支持可复用KV缓存的内存高效型LLM推理服务的实用组件。
notes: 论文核心是针对 LLM 推理中的 KV cache 做压缩与存储优化，最匹配上下文与缓存优化，其次是模型压缩；未直接聚焦系统调度或算子内核实现。

title: What Layers When: Learning to Skip Compute in LLMs with Residual Gates
abstract: We introduce GateSkip, a simple residual-stream gating mechanism that enables token-wise layer skipping in decoder-only LMs. Each Attention/MLP branch is equipped with a sigmoid-linear gate that compresses the branch’s output before it re-enters the residual stream. During inference we rank tokens by the gate and skip low-importance ones using a per-layer budget. While early-exit or router-based Mixture-of-Depths models are known to be unstable and need extensive retraining, our smooth, differentiable gates fine-tune stably on top of pretrained models. On long-form reasoning, we save up to 15% compute while retaining >90% of baseline accuracy. On instruction-tuned models we see accuracy gains at full compute and match baseline quality near 50% savings. The learned gates give insight into transformer information flow (e.g., BOS tokens act as anchors), and the method combines easily with quantization, pruning, and self-speculative decoding.
abstract_zh: 我们提出GateSkip，这是一种简单的残差流门控机制，能够在仅解码器大语言模型中实现逐令牌的层跳过。每个注意力/MLP分支都配备一个sigmoid线性门，在分支输出重新进入残差流之前对其进行压缩。推理阶段，我们根据门控值对令牌排序，再通过每层计算预算跳过低重要性的令牌。现有早期退出或基于路由的深度混合模型普遍存在不稳定、需要大量重新训练的问题，而我们的平滑可微分门控可在预训练模型的基础上稳定微调。在长文本推理任务上，我们最多可节省15%的计算量，同时保持90%以上的基线准确率。在指令微调模型上，我们的方法在满计算量下可实现准确率提升，在节省近50%计算量时性能可与基线水平持平。学习得到的门控为理解Transformer的信息流提供了新视角（例如序列起始（BOS）令牌起到锚点作用），且该方法可轻松与量化、剪枝、自投机解码等技术结合使用。
notes: 工作核心是通过残差门控实现按 token 的层跳过，属于模型结构侧的推理计算优化，而非解码策略本身。

title: Portcullis: A Scalable and Verifiable Privacy Gateway for Third-Party LLM Inference
abstract:
Businesses using third-party LLMs face privacy risks from exposed prompts. This paper presents Portcullis, a privacy-preserving gateway that safeguards sensitive data while supporting efficient and accurate LLM responses. Portcullis functions as a mediator, anonymizing sensitive data in prompts through parallel substitution, securely interacting with LLMs, and accurately reconstructing responses. It ensures all data processing occurs within secure encrypted memory. The gateway is attested to ensure trustworthiness and protect user privacy. Portcullis is the first of its kind, offering a verifiable and scalable privacy gateway for third-party LLM inferences.

We assess Portcullis's efficiency as a confidential container platform, demonstrating that its startup time scales linearly, ensuring scalability. Additionally, we evaluate its runtime performance using the PII and Enron Email Dataset. For masking and unmasking workloads, Portcullis outperforms Hide-and-Seek by 96x speed up, while maintaining equal or better false positive and false negative rates compared to existing solutions. On the Enron dataset, Portcullis achieves notably higher accuracy, surpassing Hide-and-Seek by over 0.1 for GPT-4o mini.
abstract_zh:
使用第三方大语言模型的企业面临提示词泄露引发的隐私风险。本文提出Portcullis，这是一款隐私保护网关，能够在支持大语言模型高效、准确返回响应的同时防护敏感数据。Portcullis充当中介角色，通过并行替换实现提示词中敏感数据的匿名化，与大语言模型安全交互，并准确重建响应内容。它可确保所有数据处理操作均在安全加密内存中完成。该网关经过可信证明，可保障自身可信度并保护用户隐私。Portcullis是同类方案中的首创，能够为第三方大语言模型推理提供可验证、可扩展的隐私网关。
我们将Portcullis作为机密容器平台评估其效率，实验结果表明其启动时间呈线性增长，可保障可扩展性。此外，我们使用个人可识别信息（PII）数据集与安然电子邮件数据集评估其运行时性能。在掩码与去掩码工作负载下，Portcullis的性能较Hide-and-Seek提升96倍，同时与现有解决方案相比，其假阳性率与假阴性率相当甚至更优。在安然数据集上，Portcullis的准确率显著更高，在针对GPT-4o mini的测试中，其表现较Hide-and-Seek高出0.1以上。
primary_research_object: AI 系统 / 基础设施
notes: 该工作主要面向第三方 LLM 推理场景下的隐私保护与可验证系统基础设施，属于 AI 系统/基础设施方向；虽提到并行替换与可扩展性，但不构成给定偏好标签中的优化主题。

title: Zero-Shot Learning for Materials Science Texts: Leveraging Duck Typing Principles
abstract: Materials science text mining (MSTM), involving tasks like property extraction and synthesis action retrieval, is pivotal for advancing research by deriving critical insights from scientific literature. Descriptors, serving as essential task labels, often vary in meaning depending on researchers' usage purposes across different mining tasks. (e.g., 'Material' can refer to both synthesis components and participants in fuel cell experiment). This meaning difference makes it difficult for existing methods, fine-tuned to specific task, to handle the same descriptors in other tasks. To overcome above limitation, we propose MatDuck, a simple and effective approach for Zero-Shot MSTM by evoking material knowledge within Large Language Models (LLMs). Specifically, inspired by the Duck Typing principles in programming languages, we present a ClassDefinition-Style Descriptor generation method that evokes task-specific characteristics to address usage variation. Subsequently, we introduce code-style in-context learning for zero-shot tasks, reframing them into code to leverage LLMs' proficiency in code understanding. Extensive experiments on eight benchmark datasets demonstrate that MatDuck, as a plug-and-play approach, significantly improves the Zero-Shot MSTM performance of LLMs by an average of 11.3% across seven tasks.
abstract_zh: 材料科学文本挖掘（MSTM）包含属性提取、合成动作检索等任务，可从科学文献中提炼关键洞见，对推动研究发展至关重要。作为核心任务标签的描述符，其含义往往会根据不同挖掘任务中研究者的使用目的发生变化，例如“材料”既可以指合成组分，也可以指燃料电池实验中的参与物。这种含义差异使得针对特定任务微调的现有方法难以处理其他任务中出现的相同描述符。为克服上述局限，我们提出MatDuck，这是一种通过调用大语言模型（LLM）内部存储的材料知识实现零样本材料科学文本挖掘的简单有效方法。具体而言，受编程语言中鸭子类型原则的启发，我们提出了类定义风格描述符生成方法，该方法可调用任务专属特征以解决描述符的使用差异问题。随后我们为零样本任务引入了代码风格的上下文学习方法，将任务重写为代码形式，以利用大语言模型出色的代码理解能力。在8个基准数据集上开展的大量实验表明，作为即插即用方法的MatDuck可显著提升大语言模型的零样本材料科学文本挖掘性能，在7项任务上的性能平均提升11.3%。
notes: 论文主体是面向材料科学文本挖掘的零样本LLM方法，核心贡献是描述符构造与code-style in-context learning，属于LLM应用/提示方法，不属于解码、缓存、调度、算子、推理结构优化或模型压缩。

title: HiRED: Attention-Guided Token Dropping for Efficient Inference of High-Resolution Vision-Language Models
abstract: High-resolution Vision-Language Models (VLMs) are widely used in multimodal tasks to enhance accuracy by preserving detailed image information. However, these models often generate an excessive number of visual tokens due to the need to encode multiple partitions of a high-resolution image input. Processing such a large number of visual tokens poses significant computational challenges, particularly for resource-constrained commodity GPUs. To address this challenge, we propose High-Resolution Early Dropping (HiRED), a plug-and-play token-dropping method designed to operate within a fixed token budget. HiRED leverages the attention of CLS token in the vision transformer (ViT) to assess the visual content of the image partitions and allocate an optimal token budget for each partition accordingly. The most informative visual tokens from each partition within the allocated budget are then selected and passed to the subsequent Large Language Model (LLM). We showed that HiRED achieves superior accuracy and performance, compared to existing token-dropping methods. Empirically, HiRED-20% (i.e., a 20% token budget) on LLaVA-Next-7B achieves a 4.7x increase in token generation throughput, reduces response latency by 78%, and saves 14% of GPU memory for single inference on an NVIDIA TESLA P40 (24 GB). For larger batch sizes (e.g., 4), HiRED-20% prevents out-of-memory errors by cutting memory usage by 30%, while preserving throughput and latency benefits.
abstract_zh: 高分辨率视觉语言模型（VLM）广泛应用于多模态任务，可通过保留图像细节信息提升准确率。然而，由于需要对高分辨率输入图像的多个分块进行编码，这类模型通常会生成数量过多的视觉令牌。处理如此大量的视觉令牌会带来极大的计算挑战，在资源受限的消费级GPU上问题尤为突出。为解决这一难题，我们提出高分辨率早丢弃（HiRED）方法，这是一种即插即用的令牌丢弃方法，专为在固定令牌预算下运行而设计。HiRED利用视觉Transformer（ViT）中<[BOS_never_used_51bce0c785ca2f68081bfa7d91973934]>令牌的注意力权重评估各图像分块的视觉内容，据此为每个分块分配最优令牌预算，随后从每个分块中选取分配预算内信息量最高的视觉令牌，传入后续的大语言模型（LLM）。实验结果表明，与现有令牌丢弃方法相比，HiRED实现了更优异的准确率与性能。实证测试显示，在LLaVA-Next-7B上部署HiRED-20%（即设置20%的令牌预算）后，在NVIDIA TESLA P40（24GB）显卡上进行单样本推理时，令牌生成吞吐量提升至4.7倍，响应延迟降低78%，同时GPU显存占用减少14%。针对更大的批次规模（例如批次大小为4），HiRED-20%可将显存占用降低30%，避免出现显存不足错误，同时保留吞吐量与延迟方面的优势。
notes: 论文核心是针对高分辨率VLM推理阶段的视觉token裁剪与预算分配，属于模型结构侧推理优化，而非缓存、调度或算子内核优化。

title: Ultra-High-Definition Dynamic Multi-Exposure Image Fusion via Infinite Pixel Learning
abstract: With the continuous improvement of device imaging resolution, the popularity of Ultra-High-Definition (UHD) images is increasing. Unfortunately, existing methods for fusing multi-exposure images in dynamic scenes are designed for low-resolution images, which makes them inefficient for generating high-quality UHD images on a resource-constrained device. To alleviate the limitations of extremely long-sequence inputs, inspired by the Large Language Model (LLM) for processing infinitely long texts, we propose a novel learning paradigm to achieve UHD multi-exposure dynamic scene image fusion on a single consumer-grade GPU, named Infinite Pixel Learning (IPL). The design of our approach comes from three key components: The first step is to slice the input sequences to relieve the pressure generated by the model processing the data stream; Second, we develop an attention cache technique, which is similar to the KV cache for infinite data stream processing; Finally, we design a method for attention cache compression to alleviate the storage burden of the cache on the device. In addition, we provide a new UHD benchmark to evaluate the effectiveness of our method. Extensive experimental results show that our method maintains high-quality visual performance while fusing UHD dynamic multi-exposure images in real-time (>40fps) on a single consumer-grade GPU.
abstract_zh: 随着设备成像分辨率的不断提升，超高清（UHD）图像的普及度持续增长。遗憾的是，现有动态场景多曝光图像融合方法均针对低分辨率图像设计，这导致它们在资源受限设备上生成高质量超高清图像时效率低下。为缓解极长序列输入带来的限制，受大语言模型（LLM）处理无限长度文本的启发，我们提出了一种可在单块消费级GPU上实现超高清多曝光动态场景图像融合的新型学习范式，命名为无限像素学习（IPL）。本方法的设计包含三个核心组件：第一步是对输入序列进行切分，以缓解模型处理数据流时产生的压力；其次，我们开发了一种注意力缓存技术，其作用与用于无限数据流处理的KV缓存类似；最后，我们设计了一种注意力缓存压缩方法，以缓解缓存在设备上带来的存储负担。此外，我们提供了一个全新的超高清基准测试集，用于评估所提方法的有效性。大量实验结果表明，我们的方法在单块消费级GPU上实时（帧率高于40fps）融合超高清动态多曝光图像的同时，能够保持高质量的视觉表现。
notes: 该工作本体属于计算机视觉中的UHD多曝光图像融合；与偏好最直接相关的是长序列处理中的缓存与压缩机制，未见算子/内核级优化的明确证据。

title: Recoverable Compression: A Multimodal Vision Token Recovery Mechanism Guided by Text Information
abstract: With the advancement of large-scale language modeling techniques, large multimodal models combining visual encoders with large language models have demonstrated exceptional performance in various visual tasks. Most of the current large multimodal models achieve this by mapping visual features obtained from the visual encoder into a large language model and using them as inputs alongside text for downstream tasks. Therefore, the number of visual tokens directly affects the training and inference speed of the model. There has been significant work on token pruning for visual transformers, but for large multimodal models, only relying on visual information for token pruning or compression may lead to significant loss of important information. On the other hand, the textual input in the form of a question may contain valuable information that can aid in answering the question, providing additional knowledge to the model. To address the potential oversimplification and excessive pruning that can occur with most purely visual token pruning methods, we propose a text information-guided dynamic visual token recovery mechanism that does not require training. This mechanism leverages the similarity between the question text and visual tokens to recover visually meaningful tokens with important text information while merging other less important tokens, to achieve efficient computation for large multimodal models. Experimental results demonstrate that our proposed method achieves comparable performance to the original approach while compressing the visual tokens to an average of 10\% of the original quantity.
abstract_zh: 随着大规模语言建模技术的发展，结合视觉编码器与大语言模型的大多模态模型已在各类视觉任务中展现出优异性能。当前大多数大多模态模型通过将视觉编码器提取得到的视觉特征映射至大语言模型，将其与文本共同作为下游任务的输入以实现上述性能。因此，视觉令牌的数量直接影响模型的训练与推理速度。目前已有大量针对视觉Transformer的令牌剪枝研究，但对于大多模态模型而言，仅依靠视觉信息进行令牌剪枝或压缩可能导致重要信息出现显著损失。另一方面，以问题形式存在的文本输入可能包含可辅助回答问题的有价值信息，可为模型提供额外知识。为解决绝大多数纯视觉令牌剪枝方法可能存在的过于简化、过度剪枝问题，我们提出了一种无需训练、由文本信息引导的动态视觉令牌恢复机制。该机制利用问题文本与视觉令牌之间的相似性，恢复携带重要文本信息、具备视觉意义的令牌，同时合并其余重要性更低的令牌，以实现大多模态模型的高效计算。实验结果表明，我们提出的方法在将视觉令牌平均压缩至原有数量的10%的同时，取得了与原始方法相当的性能。
notes: 论文聚焦多模态大模型中的视觉 token 压缩与恢复，通过文本引导的动态恢复机制在大幅减少视觉 token 数量的同时保持性能，最匹配“LLM/多模态”和“模型压缩”。

title: Visual Prompting Upgrades Neural Network Sparsification: A Data-Model Perspective
abstract: The rapid development of large-scale deep learning models questions the affordability of hardware platforms, which necessitates the pruning to reduce their computational and memory footprints. Sparse neural networks as the product, have demonstrated numerous favorable benefits like low complexity, undamaged generalization, etc. Most of the prominent pruning strategies are invented from a model-centric perspective, focusing on searching and preserving crucial weights by analyzing network topologies. However, the role of data and its interplay with model-centric pruning has remained relatively unexplored. In this research, we introduce a novel data-model co-design perspective: to promote superior weight sparsity by learning important model topology and adequate input data in a synergetic manner. Specifically, customized Visual Prompts are mounted to upgrade neural Network sparsification in our proposed VPNs framework. As a pioneering effort, this paper conducts systematic investigations about the impact of different visual prompts on model pruning and suggests an effective joint optimization approach. Extensive experiments with 3 network architectures and 8 datasets evidence the substantial performance improvements from VPNs over existing start-of-the-art pruning algorithms. Furthermore, we find that subnetworks discovered by VPNs from pre-trained models enjoy better transferability across diverse downstream scenarios. These insights shed light on new promising possibilities of data-model co-designs for vision model sparsification.
abstract_zh: 大规模深度学习模型的快速发展对硬件平台的承载能力提出了挑战，亟需通过剪枝降低其计算和内存占用。作为剪枝产物的稀疏神经网络已展现出低复杂度、泛化能力不受损等诸多优势。目前主流的剪枝策略大多从以模型为中心的视角提出，重点通过分析网络拓扑结构搜索并保留关键权重。然而，数据的作用及其与以模型为中心的剪枝方法的相互作用仍相对缺乏探索。本研究提出了一种全新的数据-模型协同设计视角：通过协同学习重要模型拓扑结构与适配的输入数据来实现更优的权重稀疏化效果。具体而言，我们在所提出的VPNs框架中引入定制化视觉提示，以提升神经网络稀疏化效果。作为一项开创性工作，本文系统研究了不同视觉提示对模型剪枝的影响，并提出了一种有效的联合优化方法。基于3种网络架构、8个数据集开展的大量实验证明，VPNs相较现有最先进的剪枝算法实现了显著的性能提升。此外，我们发现VPNs从预训练模型中挖掘出的子网络在各类下游场景中具备更优的迁移能力。这些发现为面向视觉模型稀疏化的数据-模型协同设计揭示了新的有前景的可能性。
notes: 论文核心是计算机视觉场景下的网络剪枝/稀疏化，通过视觉提示优化稀疏子网发现，明显属于模型压缩，不属于 LLM 上下文缓存或系统调度优化。

title: Real-Time Neural Denoising with Render-Aware Knowledge Distillation
abstract: Real-time Monte Carlo (MC) ray tracing with low sampling rates demands a denoising algorithm that adeptly balances the trade-off between quality and efficiency. Previous works have paid much attention on designing delicate denoising architecture while ignoring model compression. In this work, we present a render-aware knowledge distillation (RAKD) framework, specifically designed for Monte Carlo denoising. We meticulously delineate the Knowledge Distillation (KD) process within RAKD, emphasizing three pivotal techniques: the strategic incorporation of an auxiliary unlabeled dataset, the integration of adversarial learning through generative adversarial network (GAN), and the application of parameter transfer for robust model initialization. These approaches are harmoniously combined to distill knowledge effectively, enabling our student model to adeptly strike a balance between preserving high-frequency details and reducing low-frequency noise. Finally, our results demonstrate that RAKD achieves state-of-the-art quality while upholding real-time performance, successfully tackling the computational constraints faced by resource-limited devices.
abstract_zh: 低采样率下的实时蒙特卡洛（MC）光线追踪亟需一种能够巧妙平衡质量与效率权衡关系的去噪算法。以往研究大多重点关注设计精细的去噪架构，却忽略了模型压缩问题。本工作提出一种专门针对蒙特卡洛去噪设计的渲染感知知识蒸馏（RAKD）框架。我们详细阐释了RAKD中的知识蒸馏（KD）流程，重点介绍三项关键技术：策略性引入辅助无标注数据集、通过生成对抗网络（GAN）融入对抗学习，以及应用参数迁移实现鲁棒的模型初始化。这些方法有机融合，可实现高效的知识蒸馏，使得学生模型能够在保留高频细节与降低低频噪声之间实现良好平衡。最终实验结果表明，RAKD在保持实时性能的同时达到了当前最优的去噪质量，成功解决了资源受限设备面临的计算约束难题。
notes: 论文主题是实时蒙特卡洛渲染去噪，属于计算机视觉方向；偏好标签中仅“模型压缩”有直接证据，'系统与调度优化'证据不足。

title: Neural Block Compression: Variable Bitrates Feature Blocks for Texture Representation
abstract: The imperative for compression of material textures emerges from the critical demand for high-quality rendering, which necessitates sophisticated textures that, in turn, require substantial storage and memory resources. Thus, low-bitrate compression is crucial, especially in modern games demanding higher texture resolutions. Concurrent methodologies in texture compression predominantly employ a block-based paradigm based on color space, which inevitably leads to representational redundancies and a limited compression scope, particularly at lower bitrates. In the context of mobile devices, bandwidth during texture loading and runtime memory are major bottlenecks, making existing compression algorithms inadequate for high-resolution textures. To mitigate these limitations, we propose a novel multi-resolution texture compression scheme, Neural Block Compression (NBC), developed within the neural feature domain. Our encoding scheme is constructed on a hierarchy of multi-resolution neural feature blocks, and the key ingredient is the variable bitrates quantization scheme. It allocates higher bitrates to higher feature mip-levels and lower bitrates to lower feature mip-levels, thereby extending the concept of block compression from color domain into neural feature domain. Extensive experiments demonstrate the superior texture compression quality achieved by the proposed scheme, especially at low bitrates.
abstract_zh: 材质纹理压缩的迫切需求源于高质量渲染的核心要求，高质量渲染需要使用精细复杂的纹理，而这类纹理反过来需要占用大量存储与内存资源。因此低比特率压缩至关重要，尤其是在对纹理分辨率要求更高的现代游戏中。当前的纹理压缩方法主要采用基于色彩空间的分块范式，这不可避免地会导致表征冗余，且压缩范围有限，在低比特率下该问题尤为突出。在移动设备场景中，纹理加载阶段的带宽与运行时内存是主要瓶颈，导致现有压缩算法无法适配高分辨率纹理的使用需求。为缓解这些局限，我们提出了一种全新的多分辨率纹理压缩方案——神经分块压缩（NBC），该方案在神经特征域内开发实现。我们的编码方案构建于多分辨率神经特征块的层级结构之上，其核心组成是可变比特率量化机制。该机制为更高的特征mip层级分配更高的比特率，为更低的特征mip层级分配更低的比特率，由此将分块压缩的概念从色彩域拓展到神经特征域。大量实验表明，所提方案实现了更优异的纹理压缩质量，在低比特率下的优势尤为突出。
primary_research_object: AI 系统 / 基础设施
notes: 论文主题是纹理表示与纹理压缩，属于计算机视觉/图形相关压缩方法。虽涉及量化与可变码率，但压缩对象是纹理特征块而非模型参数、KV cache、调度系统或推理算子，因此不归入给定偏好标签。

title: Thinking in Granularity: Dynamic Quantization for Image Super-Resolution by Intriguing Multi-Granularity Clues
abstract: Dynamic quantization has attracted rising attention in image super-resolution (SR) as it expands the potential of heavy SR models onto mobile devices while preserving competitive performance. Most current methods explore layer-to-bit configuration upon varying local regions, adaptively allocating the bit to each layer and patch. Despite the benefits, they still fall short in the tradeoff of SR accuracy and quantization efficiency. Apart from this, adapting the quantization level for each layer individually can disturb the original inter-layer relationships, thus diminishing the representation capability of quantized models. In this work, we propose Granular-DQ, which takes advantage of multi-granularity clues and local patch statistics, achieving a distinctive patch-wise and layer-invariant dynamic quantization paradigm. Specifically, Granular-DQ initiates by developing a granularity-bit controller to apprehend the coarse-to-fine granular representations of local patches, matching their proportional contribution to the entire image to determine the proper bit-width allocation. On this premise, we investigate the interrelationships between bit-width and information density within high-bit patches, establishing a soft gate that enables further fine-grained dynamic bit adaption. Extensive experiments validate the superiority of Granular-DQ in the trade-off between efficiency and accuracy over recent state-of-the-art methods on various SR models.
abstract_zh: 动态量化在图像超分辨率（SR）领域受到越来越多的关注，它拓展了参数量庞大的超分辨率模型部署到移动设备的潜力，同时还能保持具有竞争力的性能。当前大多数方法针对不同局部区域探索层与比特的对应配置，为每一层和每个图像块自适应分配比特位宽。尽管这类方法具备一定优势，但它们仍难以在超分辨率精度与量化效率之间实现理想的权衡。除此之外，单独调整每一层的量化位宽会破坏模型原有的层间关系，从而降低量化后模型的表征能力。本研究提出Granular-DQ方法，该方法利用多粒度线索与局部图像块统计特征，实现了一种独特的逐图像块调整、层不变的动态量化范式。具体而言，Granular-DQ首先构建了一个粒度-比特控制器，用于获取局部图像块从粗到细的粒度表征，再根据各图像块对整张图像的贡献占比确定合理的位宽分配。在此基础上，我们探究了高位宽图像块中位宽与信息密度之间的相互关系，构建了一个软门控模块以实现更细粒度的动态位宽自适应。大量实验验证，在多种超分辨率模型上，Granular-DQ在效率与精度的权衡表现优于现有最新前沿方法。
notes: 论文核心是面向图像超分辨率的动态量化，通过自适应比特宽度分配提升移动端部署效率，属于计算机视觉中的模型压缩方向，不涉及LLM或系统调度优化。

title: Achieving Lightweight Super-Resolution for Real-Time Computer Graphics
abstract:
Image super-resolution (SR) is essential for bridging the gap between modern hardware and real-time computer graphics (CG) applications. It reduces CG workload by allowing low-resolution rendering, with original quality restored later via mathematical operations or machine learning. However, recent learning-based SR methods often rely on complex models, demanding high computational resources and undermining the benefits of reduced rendering workload.

Our qualitative and quantitative analysis of the SR process and rendering reveals that readily accessible rendering information can significantly enhance neural network design by serving as additional features. To capitalize on this, we propose CGSR, an optimization framework designed for lightweight real-time super-resolution. CGSR utilizes rendering information to boost both network extensibility and efficiency. It utilizes progressively available rendering information from the pipeline, which arrives earlier than the rendered frame, enabling pre-processing and masking of latency. These features are then integrated into a selected SR network backbone to form a CG-enhanced network. This network is further optimized and refined into a CG-optimized version using neural architecture search (NAS). To improve runtime performance, CGSR also employs rendering-aware hybrid pruning, which dynamically prunes the network based on temporal rendering data. Evaluation results show that CGSR significantly reduces parameter size, multi-add operations, and inference time while maintaining high SR quality across various backbone SR networks.
abstract_zh:
图像超分辨率（SR）是缩小现代硬件与实时计算机图形（CG）应用差距的核心技术。它支持低分辨率渲染，后续通过数学运算或机器学习恢复原始画质，以此降低计算机图形工作负载。然而，现有基于学习的超分辨率方法通常依赖复杂模型，需要占用大量计算资源，抵消了渲染工作负载降低带来的收益。
我们对超分辨率流程与渲染过程的定性、定量分析表明，易于获取的渲染信息可作为额外特征，显著优化神经网络设计。为利用这一特性，我们提出CGSR，一种面向轻量实时超分辨率的优化框架。CGSR利用渲染信息同时提升网络的可扩展性与效率。它利用渲染管线中逐步生成、且早于渲染帧就绪的渲染信息，实现预处理并隐藏延迟。随后将这些特征融入选定的超分辨率网络骨干，得到CG增强网络，再通过神经架构搜索（NAS）对该网络进一步优化精炼，得到CG优化版本。为提升运行时性能，CGSR还采用渲染感知混合剪枝技术，可根据时序渲染数据动态剪枝网络。评估结果表明，在多种骨干超分辨率网络上，CGSR可在保持高超分辨率画质的同时，显著降低参数量、乘加运算量与推理时间。
notes: 论文主题是面向实时计算机图形的图像超分辨率，属于计算机视觉方向。摘要强调轻量化与降低计算开销，但未明确出现解码、缓存、调度、算子内核、压缩等具体推理优化技术，因此不标为正例；可视为与模型结构侧轻量化目标相关的域内样本。

title: Unified Knowledge Maintenance Pruning and Progressive Recovery with Weight Recalling for Large Vision-Language Models
abstract:
Large Vision-Language Model (LVLM), leveraging Large Language Model (LLM) as the cognitive core, has recently become one of the most representative multimodal model paradigms.
However, with the expansion of unimodal branches, \emph{i.e.} visual encoder and LLM, the storage and computational burdens intensify, posing challenges for deployment.
Structured pruning has proved promising in compressing large models by trimming a large portion of insignificant network structures.
Nevertheless, most of them are predominantly designed for LLMs, either relying on unitary importance metrics that fail to deal with modality-wise imbalances or adopting generic pruning and recovery paradigms that overlook the unique calibration status and capability requirements of large models, leading to substantial performance degradation.
To address these issues, we propose a novel structured pruning approach for LVLMs, dubbed Unified Knowledge Maintenance Pruning and Progressive Recovery with Weight Recalling (UKMP). Specifically, we design a Unified Knowledge Maintenance Importance (UKMI) metric, which simultaneously considers balancing the block-wise and modality-wise importance by adaptive normalization, optimizing the importance estimation by refining gradient-based criteria, and maintaining the knowledge capacity of LVLMs by using the angle distribution information entropy. Moreover, we develop a LoRA-based Progressive Distillation (LPD) method that recalls the pruned weights and performs progressive distillation for comprehensive recovery.
Extensive experimental results across various vision-language tasks demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach, comparing to the state-of-the-art structured pruning methods.
abstract_zh: 大视觉语言模型（LVLM）以大语言模型（LLM）为认知核心，是近年来最具代表性的多模态模型范式之一。然而，随着视觉编码器、大语言模型等单模态分支的规模扩张，存储与计算负担持续加剧，给模型部署带来了挑战。结构化剪枝通过裁剪大量非重要网络结构实现大模型压缩，已被证实具备良好的应用前景。不过现有结构化剪枝方法大多针对大语言模型设计，要么采用单一重要性度量，无法应对模态间的不平衡问题，要么采用通用剪枝与恢复范式，忽略了大模型独特的校准状态与能力需求，最终会导致性能大幅下降。为解决上述问题，我们提出一种面向大视觉语言模型的新型结构化剪枝方法，名为带权重召回的统一知识维护剪枝与渐进恢复（UKMP）。具体而言，我们设计了统一知识维护重要性（UKMI）度量，该度量通过自适应归一化同时平衡块级与模态级重要性，通过优化基于梯度的准则改善重要性估计，还利用角度分布信息熵维持大视觉语言模型的知识容量。此外，我们开发了基于LoRA的渐进蒸馏（LPD）方法，该方法召回被剪枝的权重并执行渐进蒸馏，以实现全面的性能恢复。我们在各类视觉语言任务上开展了大量实验，结果表明，与现有最优的结构化剪枝方法相比，所提方法具备更优的有效性。
notes: 题目直接指向 LVLM；“Pruning”与“Weight Recalling”表明核心方法聚焦模型压缩/剪枝及恢复机制。

title: RetouchGPT: LLM-based Interactive High-Fidelity Face Retouching via Imperfection Prompting
abstract: Face retouching aims to remove facial imperfections from image and videos while at the same time preserving face attributes. The existing methods are designed to perform non-interactive end-to-end retouching, while the ability to interact with users is highly demanded in downstream applications. In this paper, we propose RetouchGPT, a novel framework that leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) to guide the interactive retouching process. Towards this end, we design an instruction-driven imperfection prediction module to accurately identify imperfections by integrating textual and visual features. To learn imperfection prompts, we further incorporate a LLM-based embedding module to fuse multi-modal conditioning information. The prompt-based feature modification is performed in each transformer block, such that the imperfection features are suppressed and replaced with the features of normal skin progressively. Extensive experiments have been performed to verify effectiveness of our design elements and demonstrate that RetouchGPT is a useful tool for interactive face retouching and achieves superior performance over state-of-the-arts.
abstract_zh: 人脸修图旨在移除图像与视频中的人脸瑕疵，同时保留人脸属性。现有方法均针对非交互式端到端修图设计，而下游应用对用户交互能力存在很高需求。本文提出RetouchGPT，这是一种利用大语言模型（LLM）引导交互式修图流程的新型框架。为此，我们设计了指令驱动的瑕疵预测模块，通过融合文本与视觉特征准确识别瑕疵。为学习瑕疵提示，我们进一步纳入基于大语言模型的嵌入模块以融合多模态条件信息。我们在每个Transformer块中执行基于提示的特征修改，从而逐步抑制瑕疵特征，将其替换为正常皮肤的特征。我们开展了大量实验验证各设计模块的有效性，结果表明RetouchGPT是实用的交互式人脸修图工具，性能优于现有最先进方法。
notes: 论文核心是面向人脸修图的多模态/LLM交互式视觉生成与编辑，不涉及解码、上下文缓存、系统调度、算子内核、推理优化或模型压缩等偏好方向。

title: DCA: Dividing and Conquering Amnesia in Incremental Object Detection
abstract: Incremental object detection (IOD) aims to cultivate an object detector that can continuously localize and recognize novel classes while preserving its performance on previous classes. Existing methods achieve certain success by improving knowledge distillation and exemplar replay for transformer-based detection frameworks, but the intrinsic forgetting mechanisms remain underexplored. In this paper, we dive into the cause of forgetting and discover forgetting imbalance between localization and recognition in transformer-based IOD, which means that localization is less-forgetting and can generalize to future classes, whereas catastrophic forgetting occurs primarily on recognition. Based on these insights, we propose a Divide-and-Conquer Amnesia (DCA) strategy, which redesigns the transformer-based IOD into a localization-then-recognition process. DCA can well maintain and transfer the localization ability, leaving decoupled fragile recognition to be specially conquered. To reduce feature drift in recognition, we leverage semantic knowledge encoded in pre-trained language models to anchor class representations within a unified feature space across incremental tasks. This involves designing a duplex classifier fusion and embedding class semantic features into the recognition decoding process in the form of queries. Extensive experiments validate that our approach achieves state-of-the-art performance, especially for long-term incremental scenarios. For example, under the four-step setting on MS-COCO, our DCA strategy significantly improves the final AP by 6.9%.
abstract_zh: 增量目标检测（IOD）旨在训练得到能够持续定位并识别新类别，同时保留其在先前类别上性能的目标检测器。现有方法通过改进基于Transformer的检测框架的知识蒸馏与样本回放机制取得了一定成效，但其内在的遗忘机制仍未得到充分探究。本文深入研究遗忘的成因，发现基于Transformer的增量目标检测中存在定位与识别之间的遗忘不平衡现象，即定位的遗忘程度更低，且能够泛化到未来类别，而灾难性遗忘主要发生在识别环节。基于上述发现，我们提出分治遗忘（DCA）策略，将基于Transformer的增量目标检测重构为先定位后识别的流程。DCA能够很好地保留并迁移定位能力，从而可以专门攻克解耦后的脆弱的识别模块。为降低识别过程中的特征漂移，我们利用预训练语言模型中编码的语义知识，在跨增量任务的统一特征空间内锚定类别表征。这一过程包括设计双工分类器融合机制，以及将类别语义特征以查询的形式嵌入到识别解码流程中。大量实验验证了我们的方法达到了当前最优性能，尤其在长时增量场景下表现突出。例如，在MS-COCO数据集的四步增量设置下，我们的DCA策略将最终平均精度（AP）显著提升了6.9%。
notes: 论文核心是增量目标检测中的遗忘分析与检测框架改造，属于计算机视觉；虽使用预训练语言模型语义特征辅助识别，但不属于 LLM/VLM 推理优化，也不匹配给定偏好标签。

title: ST3: Accelerating Multimodal Large Language Model by Spatial-Temporal Visual Token Trimming
abstract: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) enhance their perceptual capabilities by integrating visual and textual information. However, processing the massive number of visual tokens incurs a significant computational cost. Existing analysis of the MLLM attention mechanisms remains shallow, leading to coarse-grain token pruning strategies that fail to effectively balance speed and accuracy. In this paper, we conduct a comprehensive investigation of MLLM attention mechanisms with LLaVA. We find that numerous visual tokens and partial attention computations are redundant during the decoding process. Based on this insight, we propose Spatial-Temporal Visual Token Trimming (ST3), a framework designed to accelerate MLLM inference without retraining. ST3 consists of two primary components: 1) Progressive Visual Token Pruning (PVTP), which eliminates inattentive visual tokens across layers, and 2)  Visual Token Annealing (VTA), which dynamically reduces the number of visual tokens in each layer as the generated tokens grow. Together, these techniques deliver around 2x faster inference with only about 30% KV cache memory compared to the original LLaVA, while maintaining consistent performance across various datasets. Crucially, ST3 can be seamlessly integrated into existing pre-trained MLLMs, providing a plug-and-play solution for efficient inference.
abstract_zh: 多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）通过融合视觉与文本信息提升感知能力，但处理海量视觉令牌会产生极高的计算成本。现有针对多模态大语言模型注意力机制的分析仍较为浅层，导致令牌剪枝策略粒度较粗，无法有效平衡速度与精度。本文以LLaVA为研究对象，对多模态大语言模型的注意力机制展开全面探究，发现解码过程中存在大量视觉令牌与部分注意力计算冗余。基于这一发现，我们提出时空视觉令牌裁剪（ST3）框架，无需重新训练即可实现多模态大语言模型的推理加速。ST3包含两个核心组件：一是渐进式视觉令牌剪枝（PVTP），可跨层去除未被关注的视觉令牌；二是视觉令牌退火（VTA），可随生成令牌数量的增长动态减少各层的视觉令牌数量。两种技术结合后，与原始LLaVA相比，推理速度提升约2倍，KV缓存内存仅为原模型的30%左右，同时在各类数据集上均能保持性能稳定。尤为重要的是，ST3可无缝集成到现有预训练多模态大语言模型中，为高效推理提供即插即用的解决方案。
notes: 论文聚焦多模态大模型推理加速，核心是解码过程中的视觉 token 裁剪与 KV cache 降低；不属于模型压缩训练范式。

title: OAC: Output-adaptive Calibration for Accurate Post-training Quantization
abstract: Deployment of Large Language Models (LLMs) has major computational costs, due to their rapidly expanding size.  Compression of LLMs reduces the memory footprint, latency, and energy required for their inference. Post-training Quantization (PTQ) techniques have been developed to compress LLMs while avoiding expensive re-training. Most PTQ approaches formulate the quantization error based on a layer-wise Euclidean loss, ignoring the model output. Then, each layer is calibrated using its layer-wise Hessian to update the weights towards minimizing the quantization error. The Hessian is also used for detecting the most salient weights to quantization. Such PTQ approaches are prone to accuracy drop in low-precision quantization. We propose Output-adaptive Calibration (OAC) to incorporate the model output in the calibration process. We formulate the quantization error based on the distortion of the output cross-entropy loss. OAC approximates the output-adaptive Hessian for each layer under reasonable assumptions to reduce the computational complexity. The output-adaptive Hessians are used to update the weight matrices and detect the salient weights towards maintaining the model output. Our proposed method outperforms the state-of-the-art baselines such as SpQR and BiLLM, especially, at extreme low-precision (2-bit and binary) quantization.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）的规模快速扩张，其部署需要极高的计算成本。对大语言模型进行压缩可降低推理阶段的内存占用、延迟与能耗。训练后量化（PTQ）技术的开发目标是在避免成本高昂的重训练的前提下压缩大语言模型。大多数训练后量化方法基于逐层欧氏损失构建量化误差，忽略了模型输出。这类方法利用逐层海森矩阵校准每一层，更新权重以最小化量化误差，海森矩阵还被用于识别对量化最敏感的显著权重。这类训练后量化方法在低精度量化场景下容易出现精度下降问题。我们提出输出自适应校准（OAC）方法，将模型输出纳入校准流程。我们基于输出交叉熵损失的失真度构建量化误差。OAC在合理假设下近似计算每一层的输出自适应海森矩阵，以降低计算复杂度。这类输出自适应海森矩阵被用于更新权重矩阵、识别显著权重，从而维持模型输出效果。我们提出的方法优于SpQR、BiLLM等当前最优基线方法，尤其在极低精度（2比特与二值）量化场景下优势更为突出。
notes: 论文核心是面向 LLM 的后训练量化（PTQ）校准方法，用输出自适应 Hessian 改进低比特量化精度，属于模型压缩，不涉及上下文缓存或系统调度优化。

title: MPQ-DM: Mixed Precision Quantization for Extremely Low Bit Diffusion Models
abstract: Diffusion models have received wide attention in generation tasks. However, the expensive computation cost prevents the application of diffusion models in resource-constrained scenarios. Quantization emerges as a practical solution that significantly saves storage and computation by reducing the bit-width of parameters. However, the existing quantization methods for diffusion models still cause severe degradation in performance, especially under extremely low bit-widths (2-4 bit). The primary decrease in performance comes from the significant discretization of activation values at low bit quantization. Too few activation candidates are unfriendly for outlier significant weight channel quantization, and the discretized features prevent stable learning over different time steps of the diffusion model. This paper presents MPQ-DM, a Mixed-Precision Quantization method for Diffusion Models.  The proposed MPQ-DM mainly relies on two techniques: (1) To mitigate the quantization error caused by outlier severe weight channels, we propose an Outlier-Driven Mixed Quantization (OMQ) technique that uses Kurtosis to quantify outlier salient channels and apply optimized intra-layer mixed-precision bit-width allocation to recover accuracy performance within target efficiency. (2) To robustly learn representations crossing time steps, we construct a Time-Smoothed Relation Distillation (TRD) scheme between the quantized diffusion model and its full-precision counterpart, transferring discrete and continuous latent to a unified relation space to reduce the representation inconsistency. Comprehensive experiments demonstrate that MPQ-DM achieves significant accuracy gains under extremely low bit-widths compared with SOTA quantization methods. MPQ-DM achieves a 58% FID decrease under W2A4 setting compared with baseline, while all other methods even collapse.
abstract_zh: 扩散模型在生成任务中已获得广泛关注。然而其高昂的计算成本阻碍了扩散模型在资源受限场景中的应用。量化是一种实用的解决方案，可通过降低参数位宽显著节省存储与计算资源。但现有针对扩散模型的量化方法仍会导致严重的性能下降，尤其是在2-4比特的极低位宽下。性能下降主要源于低比特量化时激活值存在严重的离散化问题：可用的激活候选值过少，不利于存在显著离群值的权重通道的量化，且离散化特征会阻碍扩散模型在不同时间步上的稳定学习。本文提出MPQ-DM，一种面向扩散模型的混合精度量化方法。所提出的MPQ-DM主要依托两项技术：（1）为缓解权重通道离群值严重带来的量化误差，我们提出离群值驱动的混合量化（OMQ）技术，该技术使用峰度量化离群值显著的通道，并采用优化的层内混合精度位宽分配策略，在目标效率范围内恢复精度性能。（2）为实现跨时间步的鲁棒表征学习，我们在量化扩散模型与其全精度对应模型之间构建了时间平滑关系蒸馏（TRD）机制，将离散隐变量与连续隐变量映射到统一的关系空间中，以减少表征不一致问题。综合实验表明，与当前最优的量化方法相比，MPQ-DM在极低位宽下实现了显著的精度提升。在W2A4设置下，MPQ-DM较基线方法的FID降低了58%，而其他所有方法甚至出现性能崩溃。
notes: 论文核心是扩散模型在极低比特下的量化压缩与精度恢复，属于 VLM/Diffusion 方向下的模型压缩。

title: Multimodal Promptable Token Merging for Diffusion Models
abstract: Token compression techniques, such as token merging and pruning, are essential for alleviating the substantial computational burden caused by the proliferation of tokens within attention mechanisms. However, current methods often rely on token-to-token distances or similarity metrics to evaluate token importance, which is inadequate in the context of modern promptable designs and frameworks that are gaining prominence. To address this limitation, we introduce a novel and effective merging strategy called  “Multimodal Promptable Token Merging” (MPTM). The proposed method leverages a multimodal, prompt-centric methodology, assessing the proximity between tokens of each input modality and the multimodal prompt to efficiently eliminate redundant tokens while preserving those rich in information. Extensive experiments demonstrate that MPTM significantly reduces computational costs without compromising essential information in generative image tasks. When integrated into diffusion-based detection architectures, MPTM outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods by 2.3% in object detection tasks. Additionally, when applied to multimodal diffusion models, MPTM maintains high-quality output while achieving a 2.9-fold increase in throughput, highlighting its versatility.
abstract_zh: 令牌压缩技术（如令牌合并与剪枝）是缓解注意力机制中令牌数量激增导致的巨额计算负担的关键技术。然而现有方法通常依赖令牌间距离或相似度指标评估令牌重要性，无法适配当前日益普及的现代可提示设计与框架的应用场景。为解决这一局限，我们提出了一种新颖高效的合并策略，即多模态可提示令牌合并（MPTM）。该方法采用以多模态提示为核心的多模态技术思路，通过评估各输入模态的令牌与多模态提示之间的接近度，在保留高信息含量令牌的同时高效剔除冗余令牌。大量实验证明，在生成式图像任务中，MPTM能够在不损失关键信息的前提下显著降低计算成本。将其集成至基于扩散的检测架构中时，MPTM在目标检测任务上的表现优于现有最优方法2.3%。此外，应用于多模态扩散模型时，MPTM可在维持高质量输出的同时实现2.9倍的吞吐量提升，凸显了其通用性。
notes: 论文核心是面向 diffusion/multimodal diffusion 的 token merging 与冗余 token 消减，属于生成模型中的压缩式推理加速；未见系统调度或缓存层面的主要贡献。

title: BigMac: A Communication-Efficient Mixture-of-Experts Model Structure for Fast Training and Inference
abstract:
The Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) structure scales the Transformer-based large language models (LLMs) and improves their performance with only the sub-linear increase in computation resources. Recently, a fine-grained DeepSeekMoE structure is proposed, which can further improve the computing efficiency of MoE without performance degradation. However, the All-to-All communication introduced by MoE has become a bottleneck, especially for the fine-grained structure, which typically involves and activates more experts, hence contributing to heavier communication overhead.

In this paper, we propose a novel MoE structure named BigMac, which is also fine-grained but with high communication efficiency. The innovation of BigMac is mainly due to that we abandon the Communicate-Descend-Ascend-Communicate (CDAC) manner used by fine-grained MoE, which leads to the All-to-All communication  always taking place at the highest dimension. Instead, BigMac designs an efficient Descend-Communicate-Communicate-Ascend (DCCA) manner. Specifically, we add a descending and ascending projection at the entrance and exit of the expert, respectively, which enables the communication to perform at a very low dimension. Furthermore, to adapt to DCCA, we re-design the structure of small experts, ensuring that the expert in BigMac has enough complexity to address tokens.
