Experimental results show that BigMac achieves comparable or even better model quality than fine-grained MoEs  with the same number of experts and a similar number of total parameters. Equally importantly, BigMac reduces the end-to-end latency by up to 3.09 x for training and increases the throughput by up to 3.11 x for inference on state-of-the-art AI computing frameworks including Megatron, Tutel, and DeepSpeed-Inference.
abstract_zh:
混合专家（MoE）架构可对基于Transformer的大语言模型（LLM）进行扩展，仅需亚线性增长的计算资源即可提升模型性能。近期研究提出了细粒度的DeepSeekMoE架构，能够在不降低性能的前提下进一步提升MoE的计算效率。然而，MoE引入的全对全通信已成为瓶颈，对于细粒度架构而言尤其突出——这类架构通常会调度并激活更多专家，因此会带来更高的通信开销。
本文提出了一种名为BigMac的新型MoE架构，该架构同样属于细粒度架构，但通信效率极高。BigMac的核心创新在于，我们摒弃了细粒度MoE采用的“通信-降维-升维-通信”（CDAC）模式，该模式会导致全对全通信始终在最高维度下进行。与之不同，BigMac设计了高效的“降维-通信-通信-升维”（DCCA）模式。具体而言，我们分别在专家的入口和出口处添加了降维投影与升维投影，使通信可在极低维度下进行。此外，为适配DCCA模式，我们重新设计了小型专家的结构，确保BigMac中的专家具备足够的复杂度以处理词元。实验结果表明，在专家数量相同、总参数量相近的条件下，BigMac的模型质量与现有细粒度MoE相当甚至更优。同等重要的是，在Megatron、Tutel、DeepSpeed-Inference等当前最先进的AI计算框架上，BigMac最多可将训练阶段的端到端延迟降低3.09倍，最多可将推理吞吐量提升3.11倍。
notes: 论文面向LLM中的MoE结构，核心问题是降低专家激活带来的All-to-All通信开销，并通过结构设计提升训练与推理效率。

title: Singular Value Scaling: Efficient Generative Model Compression via Pruned Weights Refinement
abstract: While pruning methods effectively maintain model performance without extra training costs, they often focus solely on preserving crucial connections, overlooking the impact of pruned weights on subsequent fine-tuning or distillation, leading to inefficiencies. Moreover, most compression techniques for generative models have been developed primarily for GANs, tailored to specific architectures like StyleGAN, and research into compressing Diffusion models has just begun. Even more, these methods are often applicable only to GANs or Diffusion models, highlighting the need for approaches that work across both model types. In this paper, we introduce Singular Value Scaling (SVS), a versatile technique for refining pruned weights, applicable to both model types. Our analysis reveals that pruned weights often exhibit dominant singular vectors, hindering fine-tuning efficiency and leading to suboptimal performance compared to random initialization. Our method enhances weight initialization by minimizing the disparities between singular values of pruned weights, thereby improving the fine-tuning process. This approach not only guides the compressed model toward superior solutions but also significantly speeds up fine-tuning. Extensive experiments on StyleGAN2, StyleGAN3 and DDPM demonstrate that SVS improves compression performance across model types without additional training costs.
abstract_zh: 虽然剪枝方法能够在无需额外训练成本的情况下有效维持模型性能，但它们往往仅侧重于保留关键连接，忽略了被剪枝权重对后续微调或蒸馏的影响，进而导致效率低下。此外，大多数生成式模型压缩技术主要针对生成对抗网络（GAN）开发，适配StyleGAN等特定架构，而扩散模型的压缩研究才刚刚起步。更有甚者，这些方法通常仅适用于GAN或扩散模型，凸显了对可同时适用于两类模型的方法的需求。本文提出奇异值缩放（SVS），这是一种用于优化被剪枝权重的通用技术，可适用于上述两类模型。我们的分析表明，被剪枝权重通常会呈现出主导奇异向量的特征，与随机初始化相比，这会阻碍微调效率并导致次优性能。我们的方法通过最小化被剪枝权重的奇异值之间的差异来优化权重初始化，从而改善微调过程。该方法不仅能够引导压缩后的模型获得更优的解，还能显著加快微调速度。在StyleGAN2、StyleGAN3和DDPM上开展的大量实验表明，SVS无需额外训练成本即可提升不同类型模型的压缩性能。
notes: 论文核心是生成模型压缩，方法通过奇异值缩放改进剪枝权重初始化，覆盖GAN与Diffusion；不属于系统与调度优化。

title: RILQ: Rank-Insensitive LoRA-Based Quantization Error Compensation for Boosting 2-Bit Large Language Model Accuracy
abstract: Low-rank adaptation (LoRA) has become the dominant method for parameter-efficient LLM fine-tuning, with LoRA-based quantization error compensation (LQEC) emerging as a powerful tool for recovering accuracy in compressed LLMs. However, LQEC has underperformed in sub-4-bit scenarios, with no prior investigation into understanding this limitation. We propose RILQ (Rank-Insensitive LoRA-based Quantization Error Compensation) to boost 2-bit LLM accuracy. Based on rank analysis revealing model-wise activation discrepancy loss's rank-insensitive nature, RILQ employs this loss to adjust adapters cooperatively across layers, enabling robust error compensation with low-rank adapters. Evaluations on LLaMA-2 and LLaMA-3 demonstrate RILQ's consistent improvements in 2-bit quantized inference across various state-of-the-art quantizers and enhanced accuracy in task-specific fine-tuning. RILQ maintains computational efficiency comparable to existing LoRA methods, enabling adapter-merged weight-quantized LLM inference with significantly enhanced accuracy, making it a promising approach for boosting 2-bit LLM performance.
abstract_zh: 低秩适配（LoRA）已成为大语言模型参数高效微调的主流方法，基于LoRA的量化误差补偿（LQEC）也已成为恢复压缩大语言模型精度的有力工具。然而LQEC在4比特以下场景中表现不佳，且此前尚无研究对这一局限性的成因展开探究。我们提出RILQ（基于秩不敏感LoRA的量化误差补偿）来提升2比特大语言模型的精度。秩分析结果显示，模型级激活差异损失具有秩不敏感特性，基于这一结论，RILQ利用该损失跨层协同调整适配器，通过低秩适配器实现鲁棒的误差补偿。在LLaMA-2与LLaMA-3上的评估表明，针对各类最先进的量化器，RILQ均能持续提升其2比特量化推理性能，同时还能提高特定任务微调的精度。RILQ保持了与现有LoRA方法相当的计算效率，可在经过适配器融合的权重量化大语言模型推理过程中实现精度的显著提升，是提升2比特大语言模型性能的极具应用前景的方案。
notes: ['论文核心是针对2-bit量化LLM的误差补偿与精度恢复，属于模型压缩方向。']

title: Numerical Pruning for Efficient Autoregressive Models
abstract: Transformers have emerged as the leading architecture in deep learning, proving to be versatile and highly effective across diverse domains beyond language and image processing. However, their impressive performance often incurs high computational costs due to their substantial model size. This paper focuses on compressing decoder-only transformer-based autoregressive models through structural weight pruning to improve the model efficiency while preserving performance for both language and image generation tasks. Specifically, we propose a training-free pruning method that calculates a numerical score with Newton's method for the Attention and MLP modules, respectively. Besides, we further propose another compensation algorithm to recover the pruned model for better performance. To verify the effectiveness of our method, we provide both theoretical support and extensive experiments. Our experiments show that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance with reduced memory usage and faster generation speeds on GPUs.
abstract_zh: Transformer已成为深度学习领域的主流架构，事实证明其通用性强，除语言与图像处理外，在各类不同领域均具备极佳表现。但由于模型体量庞大，Transformer出色的性能往往伴随极高的计算成本。本文聚焦于基于Transformer的仅解码器自回归模型的压缩问题，通过结构权重剪枝方法，在保留语言、图像生成任务性能的同时提升模型效率。具体而言，本文提出一种无需训练的剪枝方法，该方法分别采用牛顿法为注意力模块与多层感知器（MLP）模块计算数值得分。此外，本文进一步提出一种补偿算法，对剪枝后的模型进行修复以获取更优性能。为验证所提方法的有效性，本文同时提供了理论支撑与大量实验。实验结果表明，所提方法在GPU上运行时可降低内存占用、提升生成速度，且取得了当前最优性能。
notes: 核心贡献是针对自回归 Transformer 的结构化剪枝与补偿恢复，明显属于模型压缩；摘要未体现解码、缓存、调度或内核优化的直接方法贡献。

title: ScaleOT: Privacy-utility-scalable Offsite-tuning with Dynamic LayerReplace and Selective Rank Compression
abstract: Offsite-tuning is a privacy-preserving method for tuning large language models (LLMs) by sharing a lossy compressed emulator from the LLM owners with data owners for downstream task tuning. This approach protects the privacy of both the model and data owners. However, current offsite tuning methods often suffer from adaptation degradation, high computational costs, and limited protection strength due to uniformly dropping LLM layers or relying on expensive knowledge distillation. To address these issues, we propose ScaleOT, a novel privacy-utility-scalable offsite-tuning framework that effectively balances privacy and utility. ScaleOT introduces a novel layerwise lossy compression algorithm that uses reinforcement learning to obtain the importance of each layer. It employs lightweight networks, termed harmonizers, to replace the raw LLM layers. By combining important original LLM layers and harmonizers in different ratios, ScaleOT generates emulators tailored for optimal performance with various model scales for enhanced privacy protection. Additionally, we present a rank reduction method to further compress the original LLM layers, significantly enhancing privacy with negligible impact on utility. Comprehensive experiments show that ScaleOT can achieve nearly lossless offsite tuning performance compared with full fine-tuning while obtaining better model privacy.
abstract_zh: 异地微调是一种用于大语言模型微调的隐私保护方法，由大语言模型所有者向数据所有者共享有损压缩的模拟器，供后者开展下游任务微调，该方法可同时保护模型所有者与数据所有者的隐私。然而当前的异地微调方法通常存在自适应性能下降、计算成本高、防护强度有限的问题，原因在于这类方法要么统一丢弃大语言模型的部分层，要么依赖成本高昂的知识蒸馏。为解决这些问题，我们提出ScaleOT，这是一种新型的隐私-效用可扩展异地微调框架，可有效平衡隐私性与实用性。ScaleOT引入了一种新颖的分层有损压缩算法，采用强化学习获取每一层的重要性，使用被称为协调器的轻量级网络替换原始大语言模型层。通过以不同比例组合重要的原始大语言模型层与协调器，ScaleOT可生成适配多种模型规模、性能最优的模拟器，以增强隐私保护效果。此外，我们提出了一种秩降低方法，可进一步压缩原始大语言模型层，在对实用性影响可忽略的前提下大幅提升隐私性。综合实验表明，与全量微调相比，ScaleOT可实现几乎无损的异地微调性能，同时具备更优的模型隐私保护效果。
notes: 论文主体是面向LLM的离线调优压缩框架，核心贡献集中在分层有损压缩、层替换与低秩压缩，明显属于模型压缩；“系统与调度优化”证据不足，未纳入。

title: Apollo-Forecast: Overcoming Aliasing and Inference Speed Challenges in Language Models for Time Series Forecasting
abstract:
Encoding time series into tokens and using language models for processing has been shown to substantially augment the models' ability to generalize to unseen tasks. 
However, existing language models for time series forecasting encounter several obstacles, including aliasing distortion and prolonged inference times, primarily due to the limitations of quantization processes and the computational demands of large models.
This paper introduces Apollo-Forecast, a novel framework that tackles these challenges with two key innovations: the Anti-Aliasing Quantization Module (AAQM) and the Race Decoding (RD) technique. AAQM adeptly encodes sequences into tokens while mitigating high-frequency noise in the original signals, thus enhancing both signal fidelity and overall quantization efficiency. RD employs a draft model to enable parallel processing and results integration, which markedly accelerates the inference speed for long-term predictions, particularly in large-scale models. 
Extensive experiments on various real-world datasets show that Apollo-Forecast outperforms state-of-the-art methods by 35.41% and 18.99% in WQL and MASE metrics, respectively, in zero-shot scenarios. Furthermore, our method achieves an acceleration of 1.9X-2.7X in inference speed over the baseline methods.
abstract_zh: 将时间序列编码为令牌并采用语言模型进行处理已被证实可大幅增强模型对未见过的任务的泛化能力。然而，现有面向时间序列预测的语言模型面临若干障碍，包括混叠失真和推理时间过长，这些问题主要源于量化过程的局限性以及大模型的计算需求。本文提出全新框架Apollo-Forecast，通过两项核心创新应对上述挑战：抗混叠量化模块（AAQM）和竞速解码（RD）技术。AAQM可巧妙地将序列编码为令牌，同时抑制原始信号中的高频噪声，从而提升信号保真度与整体量化效率。RD采用草稿模型实现并行处理与结果整合，可显著提升长期预测的推理速度，在大规模模型中效果尤为突出。在多个真实世界数据集上开展的大量实验表明，在零样本场景下，Apollo-Forecast的表现优于当前最优方法，在WQL和MASE指标上分别实现35.41%和18.99%的性能提升。此外，所提方法的推理速度相较基线方法提升了1.9倍至2.7倍。
notes: 论文面向语言模型在时间序列预测中的混叠与推理速度问题，标题直接指向推理阶段效率/结构改进；给定摘要片段不足以支持缓存、调度、内核或压缩方向。

title: Treasures in Discarded Weights for LLM Quantization
abstract: In recent years, large language models (LLMs) have developed rapidly and revolutionized natural language processing. However, high storage overhead and computing costs limit LLM deployment in resource-constrained environments. Quantization algorithms can effectively compress LLMs and accelerate inference, but they lead to loss in precision, especially in low-bit scenarios. In this paper, we find that the discarded weight values caused by quantization in fact contain treasures to improve LLMs' accuracy. To excavate those hidden treasures, we construct search spaces around these discarded weights and those weights within the search space can seamlessly be incorporated into the original quantization weights. To determine which weights should be merged, we design a plug-and-play weight compensation framework to capture global information and keep the weights with the highest potential benefits. Our framework can be combined with various LLM quantization algorithms to achieve higher precision without additional inference overhead. We validate the effectiveness of our approach on widely used benchmark datasets for LLMs.
abstract_zh: 近年来，大语言模型（LLM）发展迅速，彻底革新了自然语言处理领域。然而，较高的存储开销与计算成本限制了大语言模型在资源受限环境中的部署。量化算法可有效压缩大语言模型并加快推理速度，但会引发精度损失，在低比特场景下尤为显著。本文发现，量化过程中被丢弃的权值实际上蕴含着提升大语言模型精度的宝藏。为挖掘这些隐藏的宝藏，我们围绕这些被丢弃的权值构建了搜索空间，该搜索空间内的权值可无缝融入原始量化权值中。为确定应当合并哪些权值，我们设计了一个即插即用的权值补偿框架，用于捕获全局信息并保留潜在收益最高的权值。该框架可与多种大语言模型量化算法结合，在不增加额外推理开销的前提下实现更高的精度。我们在常用的大语言模型基准数据集上验证了所提方法的有效性。
notes: 论文核心是面向 LLM 量化的精度补偿与压缩优化，重点属于模型压缩，不属于系统与调度优化。

title: ABQ-LLM: Arbitrary-Bit Quantized Inference Acceleration for Large Language Models
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have revolutionized natural language processing tasks. However, their practical application is constrained by substantial memory and computational demands. Post-training quantization (PTQ) is considered an effective method to accelerate LLM inference. Despite its growing popularity in LLM model compression, PTQ deployment faces two major challenges. First, low-bit quantization leads to performance degradation. Second, restricted by the limited integer computing unit type on GPUs, quantized matrix operations with different precisions cannot be effectively accelerated. To address these issues, we introduce a novel arbitrary-bit quantization algorithm and inference framework, ABQ-LLM. It achieves superior performance across various quantization settings and enables efficient arbitrary-precision quantized inference on the GPU. ABQ-LLM introduces several key innovations: (1) a distribution correction method for transformer blocks to mitigate distribution differences caused by full quantization of weights and activations, improving performance at low bit-widths. (2) the bit balance strategy to counteract performance degradation from asymmetric distribution issues at very low bit-widths (e.g., 2-bit). (3) an innovative quantization acceleration framework that reconstructs the quantization matrix multiplication of arbitrary precision combinations based on BTC (Binary TensorCore) equivalents, gets rid of the limitations of INT4/INT8 computing units. ABQ-LLM can convert each component bit width gain into actual acceleration gain, maximizing performance under mixed precision(e.g., W6A6, W2A8). Based on W2*A8 quantization configuration on LLaMA-7B model, it achieved a WikiText2 perplexity of 7.59  (2.17⬇ vs 9.76 in AffineQuant). Compared to SmoothQuant, we realized 1.6x acceleration improvement and 2.7x memory compression gain.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型已彻底变革了自然语言处理任务，但其实际应用受限于极高的内存与计算需求。训练后量化被认为是加速大语言模型推理的有效方法，尽管其在大语言模型压缩领域的应用愈发广泛，训练后量化的部署仍面临两大核心挑战：其一，低比特量化会导致性能下降；其二，受限于GPU上整数计算单元类型有限，不同精度的量化矩阵运算无法得到有效加速。为解决上述问题，我们提出了一种全新的任意比特量化算法与推理框架ABQ-LLM，该框架在各类量化设置下均能实现优异性能，并且可在GPU上实现高效的任意精度量化推理。ABQ-LLM包含多项关键创新：一是针对Transformer模块的分布校正方法，可缓解权值与激活全量化带来的分布差异，提升低比特宽度下的性能；二是比特平衡策略，用于抵消极低比特宽度如2比特下非对称分布问题导致的性能下降；三是创新的量化加速框架，该框架基于等价二进制张量核心重构任意精度组合的量化矩阵乘法，摆脱了INT4/INT8计算单元的限制。ABQ-LLM可将各组分的比特宽度增益转化为实际加速增益，在混合精度如W6A6、W2A8下实现性能最大化。在LLaMA-7B模型上采用W2*A8量化配置时，该框架的WikiText2困惑度达到7.59，与AffineQuant的9.76相比下降2.17，与SmoothQuant相比，我们实现了1.6倍的加速提升与2.7倍的内存压缩收益。
notes: 论文核心是面向 LLM 推理的任意比特量化与加速框架，直接对应模型压缩，并包含针对量化矩阵乘法与 GPU/BTC 的底层算子加速设计；未见上下文缓存优化或系统调度层面的核心贡献。

title: Channel Merging: Preserving Specialization for Merged Experts
abstract: Lately, the practice of utilizing task-specific fine-tuning has been implemented to improve the performance of large language models (LLM) in subsequent tasks. Through the integration of diverse LLMs, the overall competency of LLMs is significantly boosted. Nevertheless, traditional ensemble methods are notably memory-intensive, necessitating the simultaneous loading of all specialized models into GPU memory. To address the inefficiency, model merging strategies have emerged, merging all LLMs into one model to reduce the memory footprint during inference. Despite these advances, model merging often leads to parameter conflicts and performance decline as the number of experts increases. Previous methods to mitigate these conflicts include post-pruning and partial merging. However, both approaches have limitations, particularly in terms of performance and storage efficiency when merged experts increase. To address these challenges, we introduce Channel Merging, a novel strategy designed to minimize parameter conflicts while enhancing storage efficiency. This method initially clusters and merges channel parameters based on their similarity to form several groups offline. By ensuring that only highly similar parameters are merged within each group, it significantly reduces parameter conflicts. During inference, we can instantly look up the expert parameters from the merged groups, preserving specialized knowledge. Our experiments demonstrate that Channel Merging consistently delivers high performance, matching unmerged models in tasks like English and Chinese reasoning, mathematical reasoning, and code generation. Moreover, it obtains results comparable to model ensemble with just 53% parameters when used with a task-specific router.
abstract_zh: 近来，为提升大语言模型（LLM）在后续任务中的表现，使用任务特定微调的做法已得到落地应用。通过集成多种不同的大语言模型，可显著提升大语言模型的整体能力。但传统集成方法的内存占用极高，需要将所有专用模型同时加载到GPU显存中。为解决这一低效问题，模型合并策略应运而生，该策略将所有大语言模型合并为单一模型，以降低推理阶段的内存开销。尽管取得了上述进展，但随着专家数量的增加，模型合并往往会引发参数冲突，导致性能下降。以往缓解这些冲突的方法包括后剪枝和部分合并，但两种方法均存在局限，尤其当合并的专家数量增加时，其在性能和存储效率方面的缺陷会更为突出。为应对这些挑战，我们提出了通道合并这一新型策略，旨在尽可能减少参数冲突，同时提升存储效率。该方法首先在离线阶段根据通道参数的相似性对其进行聚类和合并，形成若干分组。由于每个分组内仅合并相似度极高的参数，因此可大幅减少参数冲突。在推理阶段，我们可从合并后的分组中即时查询专家参数，从而保留专业知识。我们的实验表明，通道合并始终能保持优异性能，在中英文推理、数学推理、代码生成等任务中，其表现与未合并的模型相当。此外，当搭配任务特定路由使用时，该方法仅需53%的参数量，就能取得与模型集成相当的结果。
notes: 论文核心是面向多个专家模型的参数合并与存储/参数量压缩，重点在减少内存占用并保留专家能力，最匹配“模型压缩”。

title: ASER: Activation Smoothing and Error Reconstruction for Large Language Model Quantization
abstract: Quantization stands as a pivotal technique for large language model (LLM) serving, yet it poses significant challenges particularly in achieving effective low-bit quantization. The limited numerical mapping makes the quantized model produce a non-trivial error, bringing out intolerable performance degration.  This paper is anchored in the basic idea of model compression objectives, and delves into the layer-wise error distribution of LLMs during post-training quantization. Subsequently, we introduce ASER, an algorithm consisting of (1) Error Reconstruction: low-rank compensation for quantization error with LoRA-style matrices constructed by whitening SVD; (2) Activation Smoothing: outlier extraction to gain smooth activation and better error compensation. ASER is capable of quantizing typical LLMs to low-bit ones, particularly preserving accuracy even in W4A8 per-channel setup. Experimental results show that ASER is competitive among the state-of-the-art quantization algorithms, showing potential to activation quantization, with minor overhead.
abstract_zh: 量化是大语言模型（LLM）部署的核心技术，但实现有效的低比特量化仍面临诸多重大挑战。有限的数值映射会导致量化模型产生显著误差，进而造成难以接受的性能下降。本文以模型压缩目标的核心思路为基础，深入探究了大语言模型训练后量化过程中的逐层误差分布。在此基础上，我们提出ASER算法，该算法包含两个组成部分：（1）误差重构：通过白化奇异值分解（SVD）构造LoRA风格矩阵，对量化误差进行低秩补偿；（2）激活平滑：通过提取离群值得到平滑的激活值，实现更优的误差补偿效果。ASER可将典型大语言模型量化为低比特模型，即便在逐通道W4A8配置下仍能很好地保留模型精度。实验结果表明，ASER在现有顶尖量化算法中具备竞争力，仅产生极小的额外开销，在激活量化方向具有应用潜力。
notes: ['论文核心是 LLM 低比特量化与量化误差补偿，明确属于模型压缩。', '未见系统调度、缓存、解码或算子内核层面的核心贡献证据。']

title: Dynamic Operator Optimization for Efficient Multi-Tenant LoRA Model Serving
abstract: Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) has become increasingly popular for efficiently fine-tuning large language models (LLMs) with minimal resources. However, traditional methods that serve multiple LoRA models independently result in redundant computation and low GPU utilization. This paper addresses these inefficiencies by introducing Dynamic Operator Optimization (Dop), an advanced automated optimization technique designed to dynamically optimize the Segmented Gather Matrix-Vector Multiplication (SGMV) operator based on specific scenarios. SGMV's unique design enables batching GPU operations for different LoRA models, significantly improving computational efficiency. The Dop approach leverages a Search Space Constructor to create a hierarchical search space, dividing the program space into high-level structural sketches and low-level implementation details, ensuring diversity and flexibility in operator implementation. Furthermore, an Optimization Engine refines these implementations using evolutionary search, guided by a cost model that estimates program performance. This iterative optimization process ensures that SGMV implementations can dynamically adapt to different scenarios to maintain high performance. We demonstrate that Dop can improve throughput by 1.30-1.46 times in a SOTA multi-tenant LoRA serving.
abstract_zh: 低秩适配（LoRA）因能够以极少资源高效微调大语言模型（LLM），目前应用愈发广泛。但传统的多LoRA模型独立服务方法会产生计算冗余，导致GPU利用率低下。本文针对这类效率问题，引入动态算子优化（Dop），这是一种先进的自动优化技术，旨在根据具体场景动态优化分段收集矩阵向量乘法（SGMV）算子。SGMV的独特设计支持对不同LoRA模型的GPU操作做批处理，可显著提升计算效率。Dop方法利用搜索空间构造器创建分层搜索空间，将程序空间划分为高层结构草图与低层实现细节，确保算子实现的多样性与灵活性。此外，优化引擎在可估算程序性能的成本模型的引导下，采用进化搜索对这些实现方案进行优化。这种迭代优化过程可保证SGMV实现能够动态适配不同场景，维持高性能。实验表明，在当前最先进的多租户LoRA服务中，Dop可将吞吐量提升1.30~1.46倍。
notes: 论文核心是面向多租户 LoRA serving 的动态算子优化，并同时涉及批处理执行与 GPU 利用率提升，属于 LLM 场景下的系统/调度优化与算子/内核优化。

title: Falcon: Faster and Parallel Inference of Large Language Models Through Enhanced Semi-Autoregressive Drafting and Custom-Designed Decoding Tree
abstract: Striking an optimal balance between minimal drafting latency and high speculation accuracy to enhance the inference speed of Large Language Models remains a significant challenge in speculative decoding. In this paper, we introduce Falcon, an innovative semi-autoregressive speculative decoding framework fashioned to augment both the drafter's parallelism and output quality. Falcon incorporates the Coupled Sequential Glancing Distillation technique, which fortifies inter-token dependencies within the same block, leading to increased speculation accuracy. We offer a comprehensive theoretical analysis to illuminate the underlying mechanisms. Additionally, we introduce a Custom-Designed Decoding Tree, which permits the drafter to generate multiple tokens in a single forward pass and accommodates multiple forward passes as needed, thereby boosting the number of drafted tokens and significantly improving the overall acceptance rate. Comprehensive evaluations on benchmark datasets such as MT-Bench, HumanEval, and GSM8K demonstrate Falcon's superior acceleration capabilities. The framework achieves a lossless speedup ratio ranging from 2.91x to 3.51x when tested on the Vicuna and LLaMA2-Chat model series. These results outstrip existing speculative decoding methods for LLMs, including Eagle, Medusa, Lookahead, SPS, and PLD, while maintaining a compact drafter architecture equivalent to merely two Transformer layers.
abstract_zh: 在推测解码领域，实现最低草稿生成延迟与高推测准确率之间的最优平衡以提升大语言模型的推理速度，仍是一项重大挑战。本文提出Falcon，这是一种创新的半自回归推测解码框架，旨在同时提升草稿生成器的并行性与输出质量。Falcon引入耦合序列扫视蒸馏技术，强化了同一模块内的词元间依赖关系，进而提高了推测准确率。我们开展了全面的理论分析以阐明其内在机制。此外，我们提出了定制解码树，该结构支持草稿生成器在单次前向传播中生成多个词元，且可根据需要执行多次前向传播，由此增加了草稿词元生成量，显著提升了整体接受率。在MT-Bench、HumanEval、GSM8K等基准数据集上开展的综合评估表明，Falcon具备更优异的加速能力。在Vicuna与LLaMA2-Chat模型系列上测试时，该框架实现了2.91倍至3.51倍的无损加速比。上述结果优于Eagle、Medusa、Lookahead、SPS、PLD等现有大语言模型推测解码方法，同时Falcon的草稿生成器架构十分紧凑，仅相当于两层Transformer层。
notes: ['论文核心是面向 LLM 推理加速的 speculative decoding 改进，最匹配解码策略优化。', '涉及提升 drafter 并行性与多次 forward pass 的组织方式，可归入系统与调度优化。', '“compact drafter architecture equivalent to merely two Transformer layers”更像效率设计结果，不足以单独判为模型压缩主标签。']

title: Multi-Branch Self-Drafting for LLM Inference Acceleration
abstract: The autoregressive decoding paradigm endows large language models (LLMs) with superior language generation capabilities; however, its step-by-step decoding process inherently limits decoding speed. To mitigate these constraints, the prevalent “draft and validation” strategy enables parallel validation of candidate drafts, allowing LLMs to decode multiple tokens simultaneously during one model forward propagation. However, existing methodologies for obtaining drafts often incur additional overhead in communication or training process, or statistical biases from the corpus. To this end, we propose an innovative draft generation and maintenance approach that leverages the capabilities of LLM itself. Specifically, we extend the autoregressive decoding paradigm to a multi-branch drafting procedure, which can efficiently generate draft sequences without any additional models or training process, while preserving the quality of the generated content by maintaining LLM parameters. Experiments across various open-source benchmarks show that our method generates 2.0 to 3.2 tokens per forward step and achieves around 2 times improvement of end-to-end throughput compared to the autoregressive decoding strategy.
abstract_zh: 自回归解码范式赋予大语言模型（LLM）出色的语言生成能力，但其逐步骤解码过程从本质上限制了解码速度。为缓解这一局限，当前主流的“草稿生成与验证”策略支持对候选草稿进行并行验证，使大语言模型可在单次模型前向传播过程中同时解码多个令牌。然而，现有草稿获取方法往往会带来额外的通信或训练流程开销，或是存在源自语料的统计偏差。对此，我们提出一种利用大语言模型自身能力的创新性草稿生成与维护方法。具体而言，我们将自回归解码范式扩展至多分支草稿生成流程，无需任何额外模型或训练流程即可高效生成草稿序列，同时通过保留大语言模型参数保障生成内容的质量。在各类开源基准上开展的实验表明，我们的方法每个前向步骤可生成2.0到3.2个令牌，与自回归解码策略相比，端到端吞吐量实现了约2倍的提升。
notes: 论文核心是面向 LLM 推理加速的自起草多分支解码，主要落在解码策略优化；同时强调吞吐提升与无额外模型/训练开销，可视为涉及推理阶段系统效率优化。

title: Beyond Prompt Engineering: A Reinforced Token-Level Input Refinement for Large Language Models
abstract: In the rapidly developing field of automatic text generation and understanding, the quality of input data has been shown to be a key factor affecting the efficiency and accuracy of large language model (LLM) output. With the advent of advanced tools such as ChatGPT, input refinement work has mainly focused on prompt engineering. However, existing methods are often too dependent on specific contexts and are easily affected by individual expert experience and potential biases, limiting their wide applicability in diverse real-world applications. To address this problem, this study develops an Reinforced Token-Level Input Refinement, called RTLIR. We choose to optimize the input data at the fine-grained level of tokens, cleverly preserving the original text structure. Operationally, each state is defined by the token set of the current text, and each action is a binary decision process to decide whether to retain a specific token information. The agent automatically calculates and determines the selection probability of each token based on the current state, thereby optimizing the entire decision process. Through continuous exploration and learning, the agent can autonomously learn to identify the key inputs that have the greatest impact on the generation results and achieve refinement of the input data. In addition, RTLIR is a plug-and-play, LLM-agnostic module that can be used for a wide range of tasks and models. Experimental results show that RTLIR improves the performance of LLM in various input scenarios and tasks, with an average accuracy increase of 6%.
abstract_zh: 在自动文本生成与理解领域快速发展的背景下，输入数据质量已被证实是影响大语言模型（LLM）输出效率与准确性的关键因素。随着ChatGPT等先进工具的问世，输入优化工作主要围绕提示词工程展开。但现有方法往往过度依赖特定语境，且易受专家个体经验与潜在偏差的影响，限制了其在各类现实应用中的广泛适用性。为解决这一问题，本研究开发了一种名为RTLIR的强化令牌级输入优化方法。我们选择在令牌这一细粒度层面优化输入数据，可巧妙保留原始文本结构。在操作层面，每个状态由当前文本的令牌集定义，每个动作是用于决定是否保留特定令牌信息的二元决策过程。智能体基于当前状态自动计算并确定每个令牌的选择概率，进而优化整个决策过程。通过持续探索与学习，智能体能够自主学习识别对生成结果影响最大的关键输入，实现输入数据的优化。此外，RTLIR是一个即插即用、与大语言模型无关的模块，可适配广泛的任务与模型。实验结果表明，RTLIR在各类输入场景与任务中均提升了大语言模型的性能，平均准确率提升了6%。
notes: 论文属于 LLM 输入优化/提示改写方向，核心是基于强化学习进行 token 级输入筛选，不是解码、KV cache、调度、算子内核、推理结构优化或模型压缩。

title: Pruning Large Language Models with Semi-Structural Adaptive Sparse Training
abstract: The remarkable success of Large Language Models (LLMs) relies heavily on their substantial scale, which poses significant challenges during model deployment in terms of latency and memory consumption. Recently, numerous studies have attempted to compress LLMs using one-shot pruning methods. However, these methods often suffer from considerable performance degradation on complex language understanding tasks, raising concerns about the feasibility of pruning in LLMs. To address this issue, we propose Adaptive Sparse Trainer (AST), a novel and efficient retraining framework tailored for semi-structured sparse models. AST enables models to learn optimal masks during the weight update process without incurring additional computational overhead. Furthermore, we demonstrate that incorporating knowledge distillation significantly improves retraining efficiency and enhances model performance under fixed computational constraints. Additionally, a supplementary set of well-initialized parameters is integrated to further augment the model's efficacy. AST achieves state-of-the-art performance with minimal training cost. When applied to the LLaMA2-7B model, AST reduces the perplexity and zero-shot accuracy gap between dense and 2:4 semi-structured sparse models to 0.6 and 1.16%, respectively, utilizing less than 0.4% of the pretraining tokens and GPU hours. Our work demonstrates the feasibility of deploying semi-structured sparse LLMs and offers a promising alternative for achieving highly compressed models when combined with existing quantization techniques.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型的显著成功高度依赖其庞大的规模，这在模型部署阶段的延迟和内存消耗方面带来了严峻挑战。近年来，大量研究尝试采用一次性剪枝方法压缩大语言模型，但这类方法通常会在复杂语言理解任务上出现大幅性能下降，引发了人们对大语言模型剪枝可行性的担忧。为解决这一问题，我们提出自适应稀疏训练器，这是一种专为半结构化稀疏模型定制的新型高效重训练框架。自适应稀疏训练器可让模型在权重更新过程中学习最优掩码，不会产生额外计算开销。此外，我们证明引入知识蒸馏可大幅提升重训练效率，在固定计算约束下增强模型性能。我们还整合了一组初始化良好的补充参数，进一步提升模型效能。自适应稀疏训练器以极低的训练成本实现了当前最优性能。将其应用于LLaMA2-7B模型时，仅使用不到0.4%的预训练令牌量和GPU时长，自适应稀疏训练器就将稠密模型与2:4半结构化稀疏模型之间的困惑度差距和零样本准确率差距分别缩小至0.6和1.16%。本研究证明了部署半结构化稀疏大语言模型的可行性，且与现有量化技术结合时，可为实现高度压缩的模型提供一种极具前景的替代方案。
notes: 论文核心是面向 LLM 的半结构化稀疏剪枝与重训练，属于模型压缩方向，不属于上下文缓存、系统调度或算子内核优化本身。

title: C3oT: Generating Shorter Chain-of-Thought Without Compromising Effectiveness
abstract: Generating Chain-of-Thought (CoT) before deriving the answer can effectively improve the reasoning capabilities of large language models (LLMs) and significantly improve the accuracy of the generated answer. However, in most cases, the length of the generated CoT is much longer than the desired final answer, which results in additional decoding costs. Furthermore, existing research has discovered that shortening the reasoning steps in CoT, even while preserving the key information, diminishes LLMs' abilities. These phenomena make it difficult to use LLMs and CoT in many real-world applications that only require the final answer and are sensitive to latency, such as search and recommendation. To reduce the costs of model decoding and shorten the length of the generated CoT, this paper presents Conditioned Compressed Chain-of-Thought (C3oT), a CoT compression framework that involves a compressor to compress an original longer CoT into a shorter CoT while maintaining key information and interpretability, a conditioned training method to train LLMs with both longer CoT and shorter CoT simultaneously to learn the corresponding relationships between them, and a conditioned inference method to gain the reasoning ability learned from longer CoT by generating shorter CoT. We conduct experiments over four datasets from arithmetic and commonsense scenarios, showing that the proposed method is capable of compressing the length of generated CoT by up to more than 50% without compromising its effectiveness.
abstract_zh: 在推导答案之前生成思维链（CoT）可有效提升大语言模型（LLM）的推理能力，显著提高生成答案的准确率。但在大多数情况下，生成的思维链长度远长于所需的最终答案，会产生额外的解码成本。此外，现有研究发现，即便保留关键信息，缩短思维链中的推理步骤也会削弱大语言模型的能力。这些现象使得大语言模型与思维链难以应用在众多仅需最终答案、对延迟敏感的现实应用中，例如搜索与推荐。为降低模型解码成本、缩短生成的思维链长度，本文提出条件压缩思维链（C3oT），这一思维链压缩框架包含：可将原始长思维链压缩为同时保留关键信息与可解释性的短思维链的压缩器，同时利用长思维链与短思维链训练大语言模型以学习二者对应关系的条件训练方法，以及通过生成短思维链获得从长思维链中学到的推理能力的条件推理方法。我们在算术和常识场景的四个数据集上开展实验，结果表明，所提方法能够将生成的思维链长度最多压缩50%以上，且不会损失其有效性。
notes: 核心贡献是压缩推理链长度以降低解码开销，属于解码阶段优化；未体现算子、内核、调度或系统级基础设施改进。

title: Dynamic-Width Speculative Beam Decoding for LLM Inference
abstract:
Large language models (LLMs) based on transformer architecture have shown outstanding performance across numerous real-world tasks. However, the autoregressive nature of these models makes the inference process slow and costly. 
Speculative decoding has emerged as a promising solution, leveraging a smaller auxiliary model to draft future tokens, which are then validated simultaneously by the larger model, achieving a speed-up of 1-2x. Although speculative decoding matches the same distribution as multinomial sampling, multinomial sampling itself is prone to suboptimal outputs, where as beam sampling is widely recognized for producing higher-quality results by maintaining multiple candidate sequences at each step.
This paper explores the novel integration of speculative decoding with beam sampling. However, there are four key challenges: (1) how to generate multiple sequences from the larger model's distribution given drafts sequences from the small model; (2) how to dynamically optimize the number of beams to balance efficiency and accuracy; (3) how to efficiently verify the multiple drafts in parallel; and (4) how to address the extra memory costs inherent in beam sampling.
To address these challenges, we propose dynamic-width speculative beam decoding (DSBD). Specifically, we first introduce a novel draft and verification scheme that generates multiple sequences following the large model's distribution based on beam sampling trajectories from the small model. Then, we introduce an adaptive mechanism to dynamically tune the number of beams based on the context, optimizing efficiency and effectiveness. Besides, we extend tree-based parallel verification to handle multiple trees simultaneously, accelerating the verification process. Finally, we illustrate a simple modification to our algorithm to mitigate the memory overhead of beam sampling.
Experimental results show that our approach achieves a 1.5-1.9x speed-up and1.8-2.5x lower energy consumption compared to beam sampling, with no loss in downstream performance. Moreover, it can produce significantly higher-quality outputs than speculative decoding, while maintaining similar time, memory, and energy costs. In summary, our method offers a more efficient and effective inference process for LLMs.
abstract_zh: 基于Transformer架构的大语言模型（LLM）已在众多现实任务中展现出优异性能，但这类模型的自回归特性导致推理过程速度慢、成本高。推测解码是一种颇具前景的解决方案，它利用更小的辅助模型生成未来词元草稿，再由大模型同时验证这些草稿，可实现1-2倍的速度提升。尽管推测解码的输出分布与多项式采样一致，但多项式采样本身易产生次优输出，而束采样因每一步都维护多个候选序列，被广泛认可能够生成质量更高的结果。本文探索将推测解码与束采样进行创新性融合，但该融合存在四大核心挑战：（1）给定小模型生成的草稿序列时，如何生成符合大模型分布的多个序列；（2）如何动态优化束数以平衡效率与准确率；（3）如何高效并行验证多个草稿；（4）如何解决束采样固有的额外内存成本问题。为应对上述挑战，我们提出动态宽度推测束解码（DSBD）。具体而言，我们首先提出一种全新的草稿生成与验证机制，该机制基于小模型生成的束采样轨迹，生成符合大模型分布的多个序列。其次，我们引入一种自适应机制，可根据上下文动态调整束数，优化效率与效果。此外，我们扩展了基于树的并行验证方法，使其能够同时处理多棵树，加快验证过程。最后，我们对算法进行了一项简单调整，以缓解束采样带来的内存开销。实验结果表明，与束采样相比，我们的方法实现了1.5-1.9倍的速度提升，能耗仅为束采样的1/1.8至1/2.5，且不会造成下游任务性能损失。此外，该方法的输出质量明显高于推测解码，同时保持了与推测解码相近的时间、内存与能耗成本。综上，我们的方法为大语言模型提供了一种更高效、更有效的推理流程。
notes: 论文面向LLM推理，核心方法是speculative beam decoding，属于解码阶段的推理加速优化。

title: QJL: 1-Bit Quantized JL Transform for KV Cache Quantization with Zero Overhead
abstract: Serving LLMs requires substantial memory due to the storage requirements of Key-Value (KV) embeddings in the KV cache, which grows with sequence length. An effective approach to compress KV cache is quantization. However, traditional quantization methods face significant memory overhead due to the need to store quantization constants (at least a zero point and a scale) in full precision per data block. Depending on the block size, this overhead can add 1 or 2 bits per quantized number. We introduce QJL, a new quantization approach that consists of a Johnson-Lindenstrauss (JL) transform followed by sign-bit quantization. In contrast to existing methods, QJL eliminates memory overheads by removing the need for storing quantization constants. We propose an asymmetric estimator for the inner product of two vectors and demonstrate that applying QJL to one vector and a standard JL transform without quantization to the other provides an unbiased estimator with minimal distortion. We have developed an efficient implementation of the QJL sketch and its corresponding inner product estimator, incorporating a lightweight CUDA kernel for optimized computation. When applied across various LLMs and NLP tasks to quantize the KV cache to only 3 bits, QJL demonstrates a more than fivefold reduction in KV cache memory usage without compromising accuracy, all while achieving faster runtime.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型服务需要占用大量内存，因为KV缓存中存储的键值（KV）嵌入的内存需求会随序列长度增长。量化是压缩KV缓存的有效方法，但传统量化方法需要为每个数据块存储全精度量化常量（至少包含一个零点和一个缩放因子），因此存在显著的内存开销。根据块大小的不同，这一开销会为每个量化数值额外增加1到2比特。我们提出QJL这一新型量化方法，该方法先执行约翰逊-林登施特劳斯（JL）变换，再进行符号位量化。与现有方法不同，QJL无需存储量化常量，因此消除了内存开销。我们提出了一种用于两向量内积的非对称估计器，并证明对其中一个向量应用QJL、对另一个向量应用未量化的标准JL变换，可得到失真极小的无偏估计器。我们开发了QJL概要及其对应内积估计器的高效实现方案，引入了轻量级CUDA内核以优化计算。在各类大语言模型和自然语言处理任务中应用QJL将KV缓存量化至仅3比特时，QJL可将KV缓存的内存占用降低五倍以上，同时不会损失精度，还能实现更快的运行速度。
notes: ['核心贡献是 KV cache 量化，直接对应上下文与缓存优化。', '量化压缩方法本身属于模型压缩范畴。', '文中包含 lightweight CUDA kernel 与更快 runtime，可归入算子与内核优化，但不宜扩展到系统与调度优化。']

title: CSR:Achieving 1 Bit Key-Value Cache via Sparse Representation
abstract: The emergence of long-context text applications utilizing large language models (LLMs) has presented significant scalability challenges, particularly in memory footprint. The linear growth of the Key-Value (KV) cache, which stores attention keys and values to reduce redundant computations, can significantly increase memory usage and may prevent models from functioning properly in memory-constrained environments. To address this issue, we propose a novel approach called Cache Sparse Representation (CSR), which converts the KV cache by transforming the dense Key-Value cache tensor into sparse indexes and weights, offering a more memory-efficient representation during LLM inference. Furthermore, we introduce NeuralDict, a novel neural network-based method to automatically generate the dictionary used in our sparse representation. Our extensive experiments demonstrate that CSR matches the performance of state-of-the-art KV cache quantization algorithms while ensuring robust functionality in memory-constrained environments.
abstract_zh: 利用大语言模型（LLM）的长上下文文本应用的涌现带来了显著的可扩展性挑战，在内存占用方面尤为突出。用于存储注意力键与值以减少冗余计算的键值（KV）缓存容量呈线性增长，会大幅提升内存用量，甚至可能导致模型在内存受限环境中无法正常运行。为解决这一问题，我们提出了名为缓存稀疏表示（CSR）的新型方法，该方法将密集的键值缓存张量转换为稀疏索引与权重，可为大语言模型推理阶段提供内存效率更高的表示形式。此外，我们引入了神经字典（NeuralDict），这是一种基于神经网络的全新方法，可自动生成上述稀疏表示所使用的字典。大量实验证明，CSR的性能与当前最先进的KV缓存量化算法相当，同时可在内存受限环境中保障稳定运行。
notes: 论文聚焦 LLM 推理阶段的 KV cache 稀疏表示与量化压缩，在长上下文和内存受限场景下降低缓存占用。

title: Hierarchical Context Pruning: Optimizing Real-World Code Completion with Repository-Level Pretrained Code LLMs
abstract: Some of the latest released Code Large Language Models (Code LLMs) have been trained on repository-level code data, enabling them to perceive repository structures and utilize cross-file code information. This capability allows us to directly concatenate the content of repository code files in prompts to achieve repository-level code completion. However, in real development scenarios, directly concatenating all code repository files in a prompt can easily exceed the context window of Code LLMs, leading to a significant decline in completion performance. Additionally, overly long prompts can increase completion latency, negatively impacting the user experience. In this study, we conducted extensive experiments, including completion error analysis, topology dependency analysis, and cross-file content analysis, to investigate the factors affecting repository-level code completion. Based on the conclusions drawn from these preliminary experiments, we proposed a strategy called **Hierarchical Context Pruning (HCP)** to construct high-quality completion prompts. We applied the **HCP** to six Code LLMs and evaluated them on the CrossCodeEval dataset. The experimental results showed that, compared to previous methods, the prompts constructed using our **HCP** strategy achieved higher completion accuracy on five out of six Code LLMs. Additionally, the **HCP** managed to keep the prompt length around 8k tokens (whereas the full repository code is approximately 50k tokens), significantly improving completion throughput. Our code and data will be publicly available.
abstract_zh: 最新发布的部分代码大语言模型已基于仓库级代码数据完成训练，具备感知仓库结构、利用跨文件代码信息的能力。依托这一能力，我们可直接将代码仓库的多个文件内容拼接至提示词中，实现仓库级代码补全。但在实际开发场景中，直接将代码仓库的所有文件内容拼接至提示词极易超出代码大语言模型的上下文窗口，导致补全性能大幅下降。此外，过长的提示词还会增加补全延迟，对用户体验造成负面影响。本研究开展了包含补全错误分析、拓扑依赖分析、跨文件内容分析在内的大量实验，探究影响仓库级代码补全效果的各类因素。基于上述预实验得出的结论，我们提出了名为分层上下文剪枝（HCP）的策略，用于构建高质量的补全提示词。我们将分层上下文剪枝策略应用于6个代码大语言模型，并在CrossCodeEval数据集上开展评估。实验结果表明，与既往方法相比，使用分层上下文剪枝策略构建的提示词在6个代码大语言模型中的5个上取得了更高的补全准确率。此外，分层上下文剪枝可将提示词长度维持在8k词元左右，而完整仓库代码的长度约为50k词元，这一策略显著提升了补全吞吐量。本研究的代码与数据将公开发布。
notes: ['核心贡献是仓库级代码补全中的上下文裁剪与提示构造优化，直接对应上下文管理而非模型压缩。', '虽然提到吞吐提升，但主要手段是缩减与筛选上下文，因此优先标注为上下文与缓存优化。']

title: Leveraging Attention to Effectively Compress Prompts for Long-Context LLMs
abstract:
Prompt compression is increasingly studied for its potential to reduce computational costs and alleviate the burden on language models when processing lengthy prompts. Prior research has assessed token retention and removal by calculating information entropy. However, prompt compression encounters two significant challenges: (1) Information entropy, while widely used, may not be the optimal compression metric; and (2) The semantic significance of tokens is context-dependent, which renders independent token retention decisions inadequate.
    
We posit that the solution to these challenges lies in the intrinsic mechanism of language models. Large language models (LLMs) exhibit robust contextual processing capabilities, with recent studies on their internal dynamics revealing that the attention mechanism plays a crucial role in modeling how LLMs leverage long contexts. Building on this insight, we introduce AttnComp, a novel approach that exploits the attention mechanism within language models to guide prompt compression. Our method employs causal cross-attention from the query to the context to evaluate the significance of each token, and we develop a graph-based algorithm to efficiently cluster tokens into semantic units, thus mitigating the issue of independent dependencies.

We conduct experiments on datasets for retrieval-augmented generation and multiple long tasks involving single or multi-document QA. Our proposed method, AttnComp, outperforms previous baselines and validates the contributions of our components through analytical experiments. Compared to other methods that use a causal LM for prompt compression, our approach results in shorter latency and improved performance.
abstract_zh:
提示词压缩因具备降低计算成本、减轻语言模型处理长提示词负担的潜力，相关研究正受到越来越多的关注。现有研究通过计算信息熵评估词元的保留与移除，但提示词压缩面临两大显著挑战：（1）信息熵虽然应用广泛，却可能并非最优的压缩指标；（2）词元的语义重要性具有上下文依赖性，这使得独立制定词元保留决策的方式存在不足。
我们认为这些挑战的解决方案蕴藏在语言模型的内在机制中。大语言模型具备强大的上下文处理能力，近期针对其内部动态的研究表明，注意力机制在建模大语言模型利用长上下文的方式方面发挥着关键作用。基于这一洞见，我们提出AttnComp这一全新方法，该方法利用语言模型内部的注意力机制指导提示词压缩。我们的方法采用从查询到上下文的因果交叉注意力评估每个词元的重要性，同时开发了一种基于图的算法，可高效将词元聚类为语义单元，从而缓解独立依赖问题。
我们在检索增强生成数据集以及包含单文档或多文档问答的多项长任务数据集上开展了实验。我们提出的AttnComp方法表现优于此前的基线方法，且我们通过分析实验验证了各组件的贡献。与其他使用因果语言模型实现提示词压缩的方法相比，我们的方法延迟更低，性能更优。
notes: 论文聚焦长上下文场景下的提示压缩，核心目标是降低长提示处理成本并提升上下文压缩效果，最匹配“上下文与缓存优化”。

title: Compression-Aware Computing for Scalable and Sustainable AI
abstract: This talk explores the challenge of customizing large-scale AI models, particularly generative AI, on cost-effective devices with limited memory and energy resources. Modern AI models demand substantial computational power, often relying on specialized hardware such as GPUs. To address this, the talk introduces compression-aware computing, a framework enabling AI models to recognize and adapt to their compressed states while preserving performance. Compression-aware computing integrates compression techniques like sparsification, quantization, and low-rank decomposition to enhance the efficiency and accuracy of AI models, broadening these models' accessibility across diverse devices. Additionally, this talk highlights one rationale of scalable and sustainable AI in advancing Alzheimer’s research by facilitating the analysis of large single-cell transcriptomics datasets for gene-gene interaction discovery.
abstract_zh: 本报告探讨了在内存与能源资源有限的高性价比设备上定制大规模人工智能模型（尤其是生成式人工智能）所面临的挑战。现代人工智能模型需要庞大的算力支持，通常依赖GPU等专用硬件。为解决这一问题，本报告引入了感知压缩计算框架，该框架可使人工智能模型识别并适配自身的压缩状态，同时保持性能不受影响。感知压缩计算融合了稀疏化、量化、低秩分解等压缩技术，以提升人工智能模型的效率与准确率，扩大这些模型在各类设备上的可及性。此外，本报告还阐释了可扩展、可持续的人工智能推进阿尔茨海默病研究的一项逻辑：这类人工智能可助力分析大规模单细胞转录组数据集，从而发现基因与基因之间的相互作用。
notes: 主题聚焦生成式与大规模AI模型在受限设备上的压缩感知计算，核心对应模型压缩；未见上下文缓存、系统调度或算子内核层面的具体方法证据。

title: Scalable and Efficient Probabilistic Inference for Bayesian Deep Learning and Generative Modeling
abstract: Probabilistic inference is a fundamental challenge in machine learning, spanning tasks from approximate Bayesian inference to generative AI. In this talk, I will present theoretically-guaranteed scalable and efficient probabilistic inference with applications in Bayesian deep learning and generative modeling. First, I will introduce a new compute paradigm for probabilistic inference that leverages modern accelerators, specifically low-precision and sparsity, to significantly speed up inference while preserving accuracy. Next, I will present a new framework for efficient inference in discrete domains, utilizing gradient information—a largely overlooked feature of discrete distributions—to enable more informed and directional exploration. Finally, I will showcase experimental results demonstrating the effectiveness of these methods across various ML tasks, including Bayesian neural networks, energy-based models, and large language models.
abstract_zh: 概率推理是机器学习领域的一项基础挑战，涵盖从近似贝叶斯推理到生成式人工智能的各类任务。在本次报告中，我将介绍具备理论保障的可扩展高效概率推理方法，及其在贝叶斯深度学习与生成式建模中的应用。首先，我将提出一种全新的概率推理计算范式，该范式利用现代加速器的低精度与稀疏性特性，能够在保留精度的同时大幅加快推理速度。接下来，我将介绍一种适用于离散域的高效推理新框架，该框架利用梯度信息——这一在离散分布中被广泛忽视的特征——实现更具依据、更有方向性的探索。最后，我将展示对应的实验结果，证明这些方法在贝叶斯神经网络、基于能量的模型、大语言模型等各类机器学习任务中的有效性。
notes: ['主题核心是可扩展高效的概率推断，覆盖贝叶斯深度学习与生成建模，不是以 LLM 为唯一主对象。', '“low-precision and sparsity”直接对应压缩/稀疏化方向；“leverages modern accelerators”体现系统实现与推理加速取向。', '离散域利用梯度信息进行更有方向的探索属于推断算法改进，但不直接落入给定偏好标签集合。']

title: Not All Bits Are Equal: How Model Scale Changes Memory-Optimal Reasoning
abstract:
While 4-bit quantization has emerged as a memory-optimal choice for non-reasoning models and zero-shot tasks across scales, we show that this universal prescription fails for reasoning models, where KV cache rather than model size can dominate memory. 
Through systematic experiments on mathematical, code generation, and knowledge-intensive reasoning tasks, we find a scale-dependent trade-off: models with an effective size below 8-bit 4B parameters achieve better accuracy by allocating memory to larger weights, rather than longer generation, while larger models benefit from the opposite strategy. 
This scale threshold also determines when parallel scaling becomes memory-efficient and whether KV cache eviction outperforms KV quantization. 
Our findings show that memory optimization for LLMs cannot be scale-agnostic, while providing principled guidelines: for small reasoning models, prioritize model capacity over test-time compute, while for large ones, maximize test-time compute. 
Our results suggest that optimizing reasoning models for deployment requires fundamentally different strategies than those established for non-reasoning ones.
abstract_zh: 虽然4位量化已成为各类规模下非推理模型和零样本任务的内存最优选择，但我们发现这一通用方案并不适用于推理模型，在推理模型中占据内存主导地位的是KV缓存而非模型大小。通过在数学、代码生成和知识密集型推理任务上开展的系统性实验，我们发现了一种随规模变化的权衡规律：有效规模低于8位精度下40亿参数的模型将内存分配给更大的权重而非更长的生成过程可实现更高精度，而更大规模的模型则会从相反策略中获益。这一规模阈值还决定了并行扩展何时具备内存效率，以及KV缓存驱逐的表现是否优于KV量化。我们的研究结果表明，大语言模型的内存优化无法脱离模型规模存在，同时我们提供了原则性指导：对于小型推理模型，应优先保障模型容量而非测试阶段计算量，对于大型推理模型则应最大化测试阶段计算量。我们的结果显示，针对推理模型的部署优化需要采用与非推理模型已确立的成熟方案存在本质差异的策略。
notes: 论文聚焦推理模型的内存最优配置，核心证据直接涉及 KV cache 与量化位宽选择，属于缓存/上下文优化与模型压缩交叉主题。

title: NSNQuant: A Double Normalization Approach for Calibration-Free Low-Bit Vector Quantization of KV Cache
abstract:
Large Language Model (LLM) inference is typically memory-intensive, especially when processing large batch sizes and long sequences, due to the large size of key-value (KV) cache.
Vector Quantization (VQ) is recently adopted to alleviate this issue, but we find that the existing approach is susceptible to distribution shift due to its reliance on calibration datasets.
To address this limitation, we introduce $\textbf{NSNQuant}$, a calibration-free Vector Quantization (VQ) technique designed for low-bit compression of the KV cache. 
By applying a three-step transformation—$\textbf{1)}$ a token-wise normalization ($\textbf{N}$ormalize), $\textbf{2)}$ a channel-wise centering ($\textbf{S}$hift), and $\textbf{3)}$ a second token-wise normalization ($\textbf{N}$ormalize)—with Hadamard transform, NSNQuant effectively aligns the token distribution with the standard normal distribution. 
This alignment enables robust, calibration-free vector quantization using a single reusable codebook.
Extensive experiments show that NSNQuant consistently outperforms prior methods in both 1-bit and 2-bit settings, offering strong generalization and up to 3$\times$ throughput gains over full-precision baselines.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）推理通常内存密集度较高，尤其在处理大批次和长序列时，这一问题更为突出，其原因在于键值（KV）缓存体量庞大。近年来矢量量化（VQ）被用于缓解该问题，但我们发现现有方法由于依赖校准数据集，易受分布偏移影响。为解决这一局限，我们提出NSNQuant，这是一种无需校准的矢量量化技术，专为KV缓存的低比特压缩设计。通过结合哈达玛变换执行三步变换——1）逐令牌归一化（Normalize）、2）逐通道居中（Shift）、3）二次逐令牌归一化（Normalize），NSNQuant可有效将令牌分布与标准正态分布对齐。这种对齐能够支持使用单一可复用码本实现鲁棒、无需校准的矢量量化。大量实验表明，NSNQuant在1比特和2比特设置下的性能始终优于现有方法，具备较强的泛化能力，相比全精度基线的吞吐量最高可提升3倍。
notes: 论文聚焦 LLM 推理阶段的 KV cache 低比特量化，以降低缓存带来的内存开销，属于缓存优化与模型压缩交叉方向。

title: Attention Sinks and Compression Valleys in LLMs are Two Sides of the Same Coin
abstract: Attention sinks and compression valleys have attracted significant attention as two puzzling phenomena in large language models, but have been studied in isolation. In this work, we present a surprising connection between attention sinks and compression valleys, tracing both to the formation of massive activations in the residual stream. We prove theoretically that massive activations necessarily produce representational compression and establish bounds on the resulting entropy reduction. Through experiments across several models (410M--120B parameters), we confirm that when the beginning-of-sequence token develops extreme activation norms in the middle layers, both compression valleys and attention sinks emerge simultaneously. Targeted ablation validates our theoretical predictions. This unified view motivates us to propose the Mix-Compress-Refine theory of information flow, as an attempt to explain how LLMs organize their computation in depth by controlling attention and representational compression via massive activations. Specifically, we posit that Transformer-based LLMs process tokens in three distinct phases: (1) broad mixing in the early layers, (2) compressed computation with limited mixing in the middle layers, and (3) selective refinement in the late layers. Our framework helps explain why embedding tasks perform best at intermediate layers, whereas generation tasks benefit from full-depth processing, clarifying differences in task-dependent representations.
abstract_zh: 注意力沉底和压缩谷是大语言模型中两类令人费解的现象，已经受到广泛关注，但现有研究一直对二者单独展开分析。本研究揭示了注意力沉底与压缩谷之间出人意料的关联，发现二者都源于残差流中大规模激活的形成。我们从理论上证明，大规模激活必然会产生表征压缩，并给出了由此产生的熵减少的边界。我们在多个参数规模为4.1亿至1200亿的模型上开展实验，证实当序列起始令牌在中间层出现极高的激活范数时，压缩谷和注意力沉底会同时出现。针对性消融实验验证了我们的理论预测。这一统一视角促使我们提出面向信息流的“混合-压缩-精炼”理论，试图解释大语言模型如何通过大规模激活控制注意力和表征压缩，进而在不同深度层级组织计算。具体而言，我们认为基于Transformer的大语言模型分三个不同阶段处理令牌：（1）在浅层进行广泛混合，（2）在中层进行混合受限的压缩计算，（3）在深层进行选择性精炼。我们的框架有助于解释为何嵌入任务在中间层表现最优，而生成任务则能从全深度处理中获益，厘清了不同任务对应的表征之间的差异。
notes: 该工作主要是解释 LLM 内部表征压缩与注意力汇聚现象的理论与实验研究，属于模型机理分析，不直接对应给定的推理优化偏好标签。

title: DuoAttention: Efficient Long-Context LLM Inference with Retrieval and Streaming Heads
abstract:
Deploying long-context large language models (LLMs) is essential but poses significant computational and memory challenges.
Caching all Key and Value (KV) states across all attention heads consumes substantial memory.
Existing KV cache pruning methods either damage the long-context capabilities of LLMs or offer only limited efficiency improvements.
In this paper, we identify that only a fraction of attention heads, a.k.a, Retrieval Heads, are critical for processing long contexts and require full attention across all tokens.
In contrast, all other heads, which primarily focus on recent tokens and attention sinks—referred to as Streaming Heads—do not require full attention.
Based on this insight, we introduce DuoAttention, a framework that only applies a full KV cache to retrieval heads while using a light-weight, constant-length KV cache for streaming heads, which reduces both LLM's decoding and pre-filling memory and latency without compromising its long-context abilities.
DuoAttention uses a lightweight, optimization-based algorithm with synthetic data to identify retrieval heads accurately.
Our method significantly reduces long-context inference memory by up to 2.55$\times$ for MHA and 1.67$\times$ for GQA models while speeding up decoding by up to 2.18$\times$ and 1.50$\times$ and accelerating pre-filling by up to 1.73$\times$ and 1.63$\times$ for MHA and GQA models, respectively, with minimal accuracy loss compared to full attention.
Notably, combined with quantization, DuoAttention enables Llama-3-8B decoding with 3.33 million context length measured on a single A100 GPU. Code is provided in https://github.com/mit-han-lab/duo-attention.
abstract_zh: 部署长上下文大语言模型（LLM）至关重要，但面临严峻的计算与内存挑战。缓存所有注意力头的全部键（Key）与值（Value，KV）状态会消耗大量内存。现有的KV缓存剪枝方法要么会损害大语言模型的长上下文处理能力，要么仅能实现有限的效率提升。本文研究发现，仅占少数的注意力头（又称检索头）对长上下文处理至关重要，需要对所有词元执行全注意力计算。与之相反，其余所有头主要关注近期词元和注意力槽，这类头被称为流头，不需要执行全注意力计算。基于这一发现，我们提出DuoAttention框架，该框架仅对检索头采用全量KV缓存，同时对流头使用轻量的定长KV缓存，从而在不损害大语言模型长上下文能力的前提下，降低其解码和预填充阶段的内存占用与延迟。DuoAttention采用一种基于优化的轻量算法，结合合成数据即可准确识别检索头。与全注意力模式相比，我们的方法在精度损失极小的情况下，可大幅降低长上下文推理内存占用：多头注意力（MHA）模型的内存占用最高可降至原来的1/2.55，分组查询注意力（GQA）模型最高可降至原来的1/1.67；同时MHA和GQA模型的解码速度分别最高提升2.18倍和1.50倍，预填充速度分别最高提升1.73倍和1.63倍。值得注意的是，结合量化技术后，DuoAttention可在单张A100 GPU上实现上下文长度达330万的Llama-3-8B模型解码。相关代码可访问https://github.com/mit-han-lab/duo-attention获取。
notes: 论文聚焦长上下文 LLM 推理效率；“Retrieval and Streaming Heads”体现对注意力头进行结构化划分与推理时优化，同时长上下文与显存/计算开销直接对应上下文与缓存优化。

title: Polar Sparsity: High Throughput Batched LLM Inferencing with Scalable Contextual Sparsity
abstract: Accelerating large language model (LLM) inference is critical for real-world deployments requiring high throughput and low latency. Contextual sparsity, where each token dynamically activates only a small subset of the model parameters, shows promise but does not scale to large batch sizes due to union of active neurons quickly approaching dense computation. We introduce Polar Sparsity, highlighting a key shift in sparsity importance from MLP to Attention layers as we scale batch size and sequence length. While MLP layers become more compute-efficient under batching, their sparsity vanishes. In contrast, attention becomes increasingly more expensive at scale, while their head sparsity remains stable and batch-invariant. We develop Selective Head Attention with hardware-efficient, sparsity-aware GPU kernels, delivering up to \(2.2\times\) end-to-end speedups for models like OPT, LLaMA-2 \& 3, Qwen, Mistral across various batch sizes and sequence lengths without compromising accuracy. To our knowledge, this is the first work to demonstrate that contextual sparsity can scale effectively to large batch sizes, delivering substantial inference acceleration with minimal changes, making Polar Sparsity practical for large-scale, high-throughput LLM deployment systems.
abstract_zh: 加速大语言模型（LLM）推理对于要求高吞吐量、低延迟的实际部署至关重要。上下文稀疏是指每个词元仅动态激活一小部分模型参数，该技术具备可观应用前景，但由于激活神经元的并集会快速趋近于稠密计算，无法适配大批次场景。我们提出极地稀疏（Polar Sparsity）方法，揭示了随着批次规模和序列长度扩展，稀疏性的重要性从多层感知器（MLP）层向注意力层转移的关键变化。批次处理场景下MLP层的计算效率会有所提升，但其稀疏性会消失。与之相反，注意力的计算成本随规模扩展不断升高，但其头稀疏性保持稳定且具备批次不变性。我们开发了选择性头注意力机制，搭配硬件高效、感知稀疏的GPU内核，在不损失精度的前提下，针对OPT、LLaMA-2与3、Qwen、Mistral等模型，在不同批次规模和序列长度下实现了最高2.2倍的端到端加速。据我们所知，这是首个证实上下文稀疏可有效扩展至大批次规模的研究，仅需极小改动即可实现显著的推理加速，使得极地稀疏可切实应用于大规模、高吞吐量的LLM部署系统。
notes: 论文聚焦大批量 LLM 推理加速，核心贡献在注意力头稀疏与稀疏感知 GPU kernel；未直接讨论 KV cache/上下文管理，也不是典型模型压缩或调度论文。

title: Training Free Exponential Context Extension via Cascading KV Cache
abstract: The transformer's context window is vital for tasks such as few-shot learning and conditional generation as it preserves previous tokens for active memory. However, as the context lengths increase, the computational costs grow quadratically, hindering the deployment of large language models (LLMs) in real-world, long sequence scenarios. Although some recent key-value caching (KV Cache) methods offer linear inference complexity, they naively manage the stored context, prematurely evicting tokens and losing valuable information. Moreover, they lack an optimized prefill/prompt stage strategy, resulting in higher latency than even quadratic attention for realistic context sizes. In response, we introduce a novel mechanism that leverages cascading sub-cache buffers to selectively retain the most relevant tokens, enabling the model to maintain longer context histories without increasing the cache size. Our approach outperforms linear caching baselines across key benchmarks, including streaming perplexity, question answering, book summarization, and passkey retrieval, where it retains better retrieval accuracy at 1M tokens after four doublings of the cache size of 65K. Additionally, our method reduces prefill stage latency by a factor of 6.8 when compared to flash attention on 1M tokens. These innovations not only enhance the computational efficiency of LLMs but also pave the way for their effective deployment in resource-constrained environments, enabling large-scale, real-time applications with significantly reduced latency.
abstract_zh: Transformer的上下文窗口对少样本学习、条件生成等任务至关重要，因为它会将先前词元保存在活跃内存中。然而，随着上下文长度增加，计算成本会呈二次方增长，阻碍了大语言模型（LLM）在现实世界长序列场景下的部署。尽管近期的一些键值缓存（KV Cache）方法实现了线性推理复杂度，但它们对存储的上下文管理方式较为粗放，会过早淘汰词元，丢失有价值的信息。此外，这些方法缺乏优化的预填充/提示阶段策略，在实际上下文规模下，其延迟甚至高于二次方注意力。针对这些问题，我们提出了一种全新机制，利用级联子缓存缓冲区选择性保留相关性最高的词元，让模型无需扩大缓存容量即可维持更长的上下文历史。我们的方法在流式困惑度、问答、书籍摘要、密钥检索等核心基准测试上的表现优于线性缓存基线，其中在将65K缓存容量四次翻倍后，该方法在1M词元场景下仍保持了更优的检索准确率。此外，在1M词元场景下，我们的方法相比FlashAttention将预填充阶段延迟降低了6.8倍。这些创新不仅提升了大语言模型的计算效率，还为其在资源受限环境中的有效部署铺平了道路，可在大幅降低延迟的同时支持大规模实时应用。
notes: 核心贡献是面向长上下文推理的 KV Cache 管理与 prefill 阶段延迟优化，属于上下文与缓存优化，并涉及推理阶段系统流程优化；不属于算子/内核级实现，也不是模型压缩。

title: The Unseen Frontier: Pushing the Limits of LLM Sparsity with Surrogate-Free ADMM
abstract:
Neural network pruning is a promising technique to mitigate the excessive computational and memory requirements of large language models (LLMs).
Despite its promise, however, progress in this area has diminished, as conventional methods are seemingly unable to surpass moderate sparsity levels (50-60\%) without severely degrading model accuracy.
This work breaks through the current impasse, presenting a principled and effective method called $ \text{Elsa}$, which achieves extreme sparsity levels of up to 90\% while retaining high model fidelity.
This is done by identifying several limitations in current practice, all of which can be traced back to their reliance on a surrogate objective formulation.
$ \text{Elsa}$ tackles this issue directly and effectively via standard and well-established constrained optimization techniques based on ADMM.
Our extensive experiments across a wide range of models and scales show that $ \text{Elsa}$ achieves substantial improvements over existing methods;
e.g., it achieves 7.8$ \times$ less perplexity than the best existing method on LLaMA-2-7B at 90\% sparsity.
Moreover, we show that $ \text{Elsa}$ remains stable even at extreme sparsity (e.g., 95\%), yielding up to $\times$3.98 inference speedup and $\times$7.80 memory compression over its dense counterpart.
We also present $ \text{Elsa}_ {-L}$, a quantized variant that scales to extremely large models (27B), and establish its theoretical convergence guarantees.
These results highlight meaningful progress in advancing the frontier of LLM sparsity, while promising that significant opportunities for further advancement may remain in directions that have so far attracted limited exploration.
abstract_zh: 神经网络剪枝是缓解大语言模型过高计算与内存需求的极具前景的技术。然而尽管其前景可观，该领域的进展已经放缓，因为传统方法似乎无法在不严重降低模型准确率的前提下超越中等稀疏度水平（50%-60%）。本研究打破了当前的僵局，提出了一种原理清晰且高效的方法Elsa，该方法可实现高达90%的极端稀疏度，同时保持较高的模型保真度。这一成果是通过识别当前实践中的若干局限实现的，这些局限的根源均可追溯至现有方法依赖代理目标公式的设计思路。Elsa借助基于交替方向乘子法（ADMM）的成熟标准约束优化技术，直接有效地解决了这一问题。我们在多种不同模型与规模上开展的大量实验表明，Elsa相比现有方法实现了大幅性能提升；例如，在90%稀疏度下，其在LLaMA-2-7B上的困惑度比现有最优方法低7.8倍。此外，我们证明Elsa即使在极端稀疏度（例如95%）下仍能保持稳定，与稠密版本相比，其推理速度最高可提升3.98倍，内存压缩率最高可达7.80倍。我们还提出了可扩展至超大规模模型（27B）的量化变体Elsa-L，并给出了其理论收敛性保证。这些结果标志着大语言模型稀疏化前沿领域取得了实质性进展，同时也表明，目前受关注程度有限的研究方向上仍存在大量可供进一步突破的重要机遇。
notes: 标题与摘要明确聚焦于LLM剪枝/稀疏化，属于模型压缩方向。

title: Bottlenecked Transformers: Periodic KV Cache Consolidation for Generalised Reasoning
abstract: Transformer LLMs have been shown to exhibit strong reasoning ability that scales with inference-time compute, most prominently through token-space “thinking” (i.e., chains of thought). A growing line of work pushes this extra computation into the model’s latent space (adjacent to standard decoding) which we term Auxiliary Latent-Space Computation (ALSC). Existing ALSC methods largely fall into three buckets: (i) token-mediated latent or special-token rollouts, (ii) residual/activation steering, and (iii) memory compression via cache pruning, merging, or summarization. An underexplored alternative is memory consolidation and reconsolidation, two processes in the brain that are responsible for stabilising newly formed memory traces, and, upon recall, transiently rendering established traces plastic such they can integrate new contextual information before restabilising. In a Transformer LLM, this can be seen as analogous to performing in-place global rewrites of incoming KV segments, and rewrites of past segments conditioned on newly observed tokens. In this work, we give a theoretical justification as to why memory (re)consolidation via KV cache rewrites is beneficial for improved reasoning. We do this through the lens of Information Bottleneck (IB) theory, which posits that model generalisation emerges from an optimal balance between input information compression and retention of predictive information in latent representations. We prove using IB theory that Vanilla decoder-only Transformers are inherently constrained in their ability to form task-optimal sequence representations. We then introduce the Bottlenecked Transformer, which augments a decoder-only backbone LLM with a lightweight Cache Processor, an auxiliary Transformer that performs periodic, non-causal, in-place KV rewrites at newline-delimited reasoning step boundaries. The processor consolidates recently written KV entries and reconsolidates a small, top-$k$ attention-selected set of prior entries, conditioned on recent context. We evaluate our Bottlenecked Transformer architecture on seven mathematical reasoning benchmarks, with four backbone LLMs. Our model sees consistent performance gains over vanilla Transformers and pause-token augmented Transformer baselines, with gains of up to +6.6pp for selected tasks and backbones.
abstract_zh: Transformer大语言模型已被证明具备随推理时计算量提升而增强的强推理能力，最典型的实现方式是令牌空间“思考”（即思维链）。越来越多的研究将这类额外计算转移到模型的隐空间中（与标准解码并行），我们将这类方法称为辅助隐空间计算（ALSC）。现有ALSC方法主要分为三类：（i）令牌介导的隐空间或特殊令牌生成，（ii）残差/激活调控，（iii）通过缓存剪枝、合并或摘要实现的内存压缩。目前尚未得到充分探索的一类替代方案是内存巩固与再巩固，这是大脑中的两种过程，负责稳定新形成的记忆痕迹，并且在回忆时暂时让已形成的痕迹具备可塑性，使其能够在重新稳定前整合新的上下文信息。在Transformer大语言模型中，这一过程可以类比为对输入的键值（KV）段执行原位全局改写，以及基于新观测到的令牌改写过往KV段。在本研究中，我们从理论层面论证了为何通过KV缓存改写实现的内存（再）巩固有助于提升推理能力。我们借助信息瓶颈（IB）理论展开分析，该理论认为模型的泛化能力源自输入信息压缩与隐表征中预测信息留存之间的最优平衡。我们利用IB理论证明，原生纯解码器Transformer在生成任务最优序列表征的能力上存在固有局限。随后我们提出瓶颈Transformer，该架构在纯解码器主干大语言模型的基础上增加了一个轻量级缓存处理器，这一辅助Transformer会在换行符分隔的推理步骤边界处，定期执行非因果的原位KV改写。该缓存处理器会巩固最近写入的KV条目，同时基于最近上下文，再巩固少量由注意力机制选出的前序top-k条目。我们在7个数学推理基准上，使用4种主干大语言模型对瓶颈Transformer架构进行了评估。相较于原生Transformer和暂停令牌增强的Transformer基线模型，我们的模型实现了一致的性能提升，在选定任务和主干模型上的提升幅度最高可达6.6个百分点。
notes: 核心贡献是为解码器式 LLM 增加辅助 Cache Processor，在推理步骤边界周期性重写 KV cache，属于推理期结构增强与上下文/KV 缓存优化；并非主要提出解码策略，也不是以压缩率为目标的模型压缩方法。

title: Multi-band Frequency Reconstruction for Neural Psychoacoustic Coding
abstract: Achieving high-fidelity audio compression while preserving perceptual quality across diverse audio types remains a significant challenge in Neural Audio Coding (NAC). This paper introduces MUFFIN, a fully convolutional NAC framework that leverages psychoacoustically guided multi-band frequency reconstruction. Central to MUFFIN is the Multi-Band Spectral Residual Vector Quantization (MBS-RVQ) mechanism, which quantizes latent speech across different frequency bands. This approach optimizes bitrate allocation and enhances fidelity based on psychoacoustic studies, achieving efficient compression with unique perceptual features that separate content from speaker attributes through distinct codebooks. MUFFIN integrates a transformer-inspired convolutional architecture with proposed modified snake activation functions to capture fine frequency details with greater precision. Extensive evaluations on diverse datasets (LibriTTS, IEMOCAP, GTZAN, BBC) demonstrate MUFFIN’s ability to consistently surpass existing performance in audio reconstruction across various domains. Notably, a high-compression variant achieves an impressive SOTA 12.5 kHz rate while preserving reconstruction quality. Furthermore, MUFFIN excels in downstream generative tasks, demonstrating its potential as a robust token representation for integration with large language models. These results establish MUFFIN as a groundbreaking advancement in NAC and as the first neural psychoacoustic coding system. Speech demos and codes are available at \url{https://demos46.github.io/muffin/} and \url{https://github.com/dianwen-ng/MUFFIN}.
abstract_zh: 在神经音频编码（NAC）领域，实现高保真音频压缩同时保留各类音频的感知质量仍是一项重大挑战。本文提出MUFFIN，这是一种利用心理声学引导的多频带频率重构的全卷积神经音频编码框架。MUFFIN的核心是多频带谱残差矢量量化（MBS-RVQ）机制，该机制对不同频带的潜在语音表示进行量化。该方法基于心理声学研究优化码率分配并提升保真度，通过不同码本将内容与说话人属性分离，实现了具备独特感知特性的高效压缩。MUFFIN将受Transformer启发的卷积架构与所提出的改进型snake激活函数相结合，能够以更高精度捕获精细的频率细节。在LibriTTS、IEMOCAP、GTZAN、BBC等多样化数据集上开展的大量评估表明，MUFFIN在多个领域的音频重构性能始终优于现有方法。值得注意的是，其高压缩变体在12.5kHz码率下达到了令人瞩目的当前最优水平，同时可保持重构质量。此外，MUFFIN在下游生成任务中表现出色，证明其可作为鲁棒的令牌表示，具备与大语言模型集成的潜力。这些结果证实MUFFIN是神经音频编码领域的突破性进展，也是首个神经心理声学编码系统。语音演示和代码可从https://demos46.github.io/muffin/ 与https://github.com/dianwen-ng/MUFFIN 获取。
notes: 该论文核心是神经音频编码与心理声学压缩，主对象应归为“语音 / 音频”。摘要未体现允许标签中的推理优化或模型压缩取向，因此偏好标签置空。

title: QoS-Efficient Serving of Multiple Mixture-of-Expert LLMs Using Partial Runtime Reconfiguration
abstract: The deployment of mixture-of-experts (MoE) large language models (LLMs) presents significant challenges due to their high memory demands. These challenges become even more pronounced in multi-tenant environments, where shared resources must accommodate multiple models, limiting the effectiveness of conventional virtualization techniques. This paper addresses the problem of efficiently serving multiple fine-tuned MoE-LLMs on a single GPU. We propose a serving system that employs \textit{similarity-based expert consolidation} to reduce the overall memory footprint by sharing similar experts across models. To ensure output quality, we introduce \textit{runtime partial reconfiguration}, dynamically replacing non-expert layers when processing requests from different models. As a result, our approach achieves competitive output quality while maintaining throughput comparable to serving a single model, and incurs only a negligible increase in time-to-first-token (TTFT). Experiments on a server with a single NVIDIA A100 GPU (80GB) using Mixtral-8x7B models demonstrate an 85\% average reduction in turnaround time compared to NVIDIA's multi-instance GPU (MIG). Furthermore, experiments on Google's Switch Transformer Base-8 model with up to four variants demonstrate the scalability and resilience of our approach in maintaining output quality compared to other model merging baselines, highlighting its effectiveness.
abstract_zh: 混合专家（MoE）大语言模型（LLM）的部署因内存需求较高面临严峻挑战，这类挑战在多租户环境中会进一步凸显，该环境下共享资源需承载多个模型，导致传统虚拟化技术的效用受限。本文针对在单个GPU上高效为多个微调后的MoE-LLM提供服务的问题展开研究，提出了一套服务系统，该系统采用基于相似度的专家整合策略，通过跨模型共享相似专家来降低整体内存占用。为保障输出质量，我们引入运行时部分重配置机制，在处理来自不同模型的请求时动态替换非专家层。最终本方法实现了具备竞争力的输出质量，同时保持了与服务单个模型相当的吞吐量，且仅带来可忽略不计的首令牌生成时间（TTFT）增长。在搭载单块80GB显存NVIDIA A100 GPU的服务器上使用Mixtral-8x7B模型开展的实验表明，与NVIDIA的多实例GPU（MIG）技术相比，本方法的周转时间平均降低了85%。此外，使用最多包含4种变体的谷歌Switch Transformer Base-8模型开展的实验证明，与其他模型合并基线方法相比，本方法在维持输出质量方面具备可扩展性与鲁棒性，验证了其有效性。
notes: 论文核心是多租户单卡上的多MoE-LLM高效服务，重点在服务系统设计与MoE结构级重构，不直接聚焦缓存、解码或底层算子内核优化。

title: BanditSpec: Adaptive Speculative Decoding via Bandit Algorithms
abstract: Speculative decoding has emerged as a popular method to accelerate the inference of Large Language Models (LLMs) while retaining their superior text generation performance. Previous methods either adopt a fixed speculative decoding configuration regardless of the prefix tokens, or train draft models in an offline or online manner to align them with the context. This paper proposes a training-free online learning framework to adaptively choose the configuration of the hyperparameters for speculative decoding as text is being generated. We first formulate this hyperparameter selection problem as a Multi-Armed Bandit problem and provide a general speculative decoding framework BanditSpec. Furthermore, two bandit-based hyperparameter selection algorithms, UCBSpec and EXP3Spec, are designed and analyzed in terms of a novel quantity, the stopping time regret. We upper bound this regret under both  stochastic and adversarial reward settings. By deriving an information-theoretic impossibility result, it is shown that the regret performance of UCBSpec is optimal up to universal constants. Finally, extensive empirical experiments with LLaMA3 and Qwen2 demonstrate that our algorithms are effective compared to existing methods, and  the throughput is close to the oracle best hyperparameter in simulated real-life LLM serving scenarios with diverse input prompts.
abstract_zh: 推测解码已成为广受青睐的大语言模型（LLM）推理加速方法，可在保留大语言模型出色文本生成性能的前提下提升推理速度。现有方法要么忽略前缀令牌的差异采用固定的推测解码配置，要么通过离线或在线方式训练草稿模型以使其与上下文对齐。本文提出一种无需训练的在线学习框架，可在文本生成过程中自适应选择推测解码的超参数配置。我们首先将该超参数选择问题建模为多臂老虎机问题，并提出通用的推测解码框架BanditSpec。此外，我们设计了UCBSpec和EXP3Spec两种基于老虎机算法的超参数选择算法，并基于新提出的指标停止时间后悔值对算法进行分析。我们在随机奖励设置和对抗奖励设置下均给出了该后悔值的上界。通过推导信息论层面的不可能性结果，我们证明UCBSpec的后悔值性能在相差通用常数的范围内达到最优。最后，基于LLaMA3和Qwen2开展的大量实证实验表明，与现有方法相比，本文提出的算法更为有效，在输入提示多样的模拟真实大语言模型服务场景中，其吞吐量接近最优超参数预言机的水平。
notes: ['核心贡献是对 speculative decoding 的在线自适应超参数选择，明确属于解码阶段优化。', '涉及真实服务场景下的吞吐提升与在线配置选择，可兼容归入系统与调度优化，但重点仍是解码策略。']

title: SCBench: A KV Cache-Centric Analysis of Long-Context Methods
abstract: Long-context Large Language Models (LLMs) have enabled numerous downstream applications but also introduced significant challenges related to computational and memory efficiency. To address these challenges, optimizations for long-context inference have been developed, centered around the KV cache. However, existing benchmarks often evaluate in single-request, neglecting the full lifecycle of the KV cache in real-world use. This oversight is particularly critical, as KV cache reuse has become widely adopted in LLMs inference frameworks, such as vLLM and SGLang, as well as by LLM providers, including OpenAI, Microsoft, Google, and Anthropic. To address this gap, we introduce SCBENCH (SharedContextBENCH), a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating long-context methods from a KV cache centric perspective: 1) KV cache generation, 2) KV cache compression, 3) KV cache retrieval, and 4) KV cache loading. Specifically, SCBench uses test examples with shared context, ranging 12 tasks with two shared context modes, covering four categories of long-context capabilities: string retrieval, semantic retrieval, global information, and multi-task. With SCBench, we provide an extensive KV cache-centric analysis of eight categories long-context solutions, including Gated Linear RNNs (Codestal-Mamba), Mamba-Attention hybrids (Jamba-1.5-Mini), and efficient methods such as sparse attention, KV cache dropping, quantization, retrieval, loading, and prompt compression. The evaluation is conducted on six Transformer-based long-context LLMs: Llama-3.1-8B/70B, Qwen2.5-72B/32B, Llama-3-8B-262K, and GLM-4-9B. Our findings show that sub-O(n) memory methods suffer in multi-turn scenarios, while sparse encoding with O(n) memory and sub-O(n^2) pre-filling computation perform robustly. Dynamic sparsity yields more expressive KV caches than static patterns, and layer-level sparsity in hybrid architectures reduces memory usage with strong performance. Additionally, we identify attention distribution shift issues in long-generation scenarios.
abstract_zh: 长上下文大语言模型（LLM）已支撑起众多下游应用，但也带来了计算与内存效率方面的重大挑战。为应对这些挑战，目前已开发出以KV缓存为核心的长上下文推理优化方案。然而现有基准通常仅在单请求场景下开展评估，忽略了真实应用中KV缓存的完整生命周期。这种疏漏的影响尤为关键，因为KV缓存复用已在vLLM、SGLang等LLM推理框架，以及OpenAI、微软、谷歌、Anthropic等LLM服务商中得到广泛采用。为填补这一空白，我们推出SCBENCH（共享上下文基准，SharedContextBENCH），这是一套从KV缓存核心视角评估长上下文方法的综合基准，覆盖四大评估维度：1）KV缓存生成、2）KV缓存压缩、3）KV缓存检索、4）KV缓存加载。具体而言，SCBench采用包含共享上下文的测试样例，涵盖12项任务、两种共享上下文模式，覆盖四类长上下文能力：字符串检索、语义检索、全局信息处理与多任务处理。借助SCBench，我们针对八大类长上下文解决方案开展了全面的以KV缓存为核心的分析，涉及的方案包括门控线性循环神经网络（Codestal-Mamba）、Mamba-注意力混合架构（Jamba-1.5-Mini），以及稀疏注意力、KV缓存丢弃、量化、检索、加载、提示压缩等高效方法。本次评估在六款基于Transformer的长上下文大语言模型上开展，分别为Llama-3.1-8B/70B、Qwen2.5-72B/32B、Llama-3-8B-262K以及GLM-4-9B。我们的研究发现，次O(n)内存复杂度的方法在多轮场景下表现较差，而具备O(n)内存复杂度、次O(n²)预填充计算复杂度的稀疏编码方法表现稳定。动态稀疏性相比静态模式能够生成表达能力更强的KV缓存，混合架构中的层级稀疏性可在保持优异性能的同时降低内存占用。此外，我们还发现了长生成场景下的注意力分布偏移问题。
notes: ['论文核心是面向长上下文LLM的KV cache中心化评测与分析，属于LLM方向。', '偏好标签同时覆盖缓存复用/加载/检索，混合架构与稀疏机制，以及量化/压缩相关方法。']

title: Global Resolution: Optimal Multi-Draft Speculative Sampling via Convex Optimization
abstract: Speculative sampling reduces the latency of autoregressive decoding for target model LLMs without sacrificing inference quality, by using a cheap draft model to suggest a candidate token and a verification criterion to accept or resample this token. To improve acceptance and decoding efficiency, recent work has explored the multi-draft extension, where at each step $n$ draft tokens are generated, and the verification criterion is a distribution conditioned on these. When this criterion maximizes the probability of accepting some draft token, it is called the optimal transport (OT). However, finding the OT is difficult, as it is the solution of a linear program (OTLP) in over $V^n$ variables, with $V$ being the vocabulary size. Two recent theoretical works have reframed the OTLP in terms of importance sampling or subset selection. In this work, we prove that these formulations are equivalent to an exponentially large relaxed OTLP, so it remains infeasible to solve. Then, we reverse engineer subset selection to formulate the OTLP as a max-flow problem. With a novel application of polymatroid theory, we reduce the exponentially large OTLP to a convex optimization problem in at most $V$ variables. This allows us to devise an algorithm for optimal $n$-draft speculative sampling when the $n$ tokens are chosen i.i.d. from a single draft model, which can be tuned to arbitrary accuracy. Finally, we measure acceptance rates and algorithm runtimes for various $n$ and top-$k$ draft sampling settings. Our findings give the first multi-draft algorithm with 90\% acceptance and under 100 ms of overhead per generated token with negligible deviation from the target model distribution.
abstract_zh: 推测采样通过使用低成本的草稿模型生成候选词元，并利用验证准则接受该词元或对其重新采样，在不损失推理质量的前提下降低了大语言模型目标模型自回归解码的延迟。为提升接受率与解码效率，近期研究探索了多草稿扩展方案：每一步生成n个草稿词元，验证准则为以这些词元为条件的分布。当该准则最大化接受某一草稿词元的概率时，即被称为最优传输（OT）。然而求解最优传输难度极大，因为它是变量数超过Vⁿ的线性规划（OTLP）的解，其中V为词表大小。近期两项理论研究分别从重要性采样或子集选择的角度重新构建了OTLP的表述。本研究证明这些表述等价于一个变量规模呈指数级增长的松弛OTLP，因此仍然无法求解。随后我们对子集选择进行逆向推导，将OTLP重构为最大流问题。通过多拟阵理论的创新性应用，我们将规模呈指数级的OTLP简化为至多包含V个变量的凸优化问题。这使得我们能够设计出适用于n个词元从单一草稿模型独立同分布采样场景下的最优n草稿推测采样算法，该算法可调试至任意精度。最后我们在不同n值与top-k草稿采样设置下测算了接受率与算法运行时间。我们的研究成果提出了首个接受率达90%、每个生成词元的开销低于100毫秒、且与目标模型分布的偏差可忽略不计的多草稿算法。
notes: 论文核心聚焦于多草稿 speculative sampling 的最优解码算法与接受率/时延改进，属于推理阶段的解码策略优化；未直接涉及系统调度、内核或压缩实现。

title: Prima.cpp: Fast 30-70B LLM Inference on Heterogeneous and Low-Resource Home Clusters
abstract: On-device inference offers privacy, offline use, and instant response, but consumer hardware restricts large language models (LLMs) to low throughput and capability. To overcome this challenge, we present prima.cpp, a distributed on-device inference system that runs 30-70B LLMs on consumer home clusters with mixed CPUs/GPUs, insufficient RAM/VRAM, slow disks, Wi-Fi links, and heterogeneous OSs. We introduce pipelined-ring parallelism (PRP) to overlap disk I/O with compute and communication, and address the prefetch-release conflict in mmap-based offloading. We further propose Halda, a heterogeneity-aware scheduler that co-optimizes per-device CPU/GPU workloads and device selection under RAM/VRAM constraints. On four consumer home devices, a 70B model reaches 674 ms/token TPOT with <6% memory pressure, and a 32B model with speculative decoding achieves 26 tokens/s. Compared with llama.cpp, exo, and dllama, our proposed prima.cpp achieves 5-17× lower TPOT, supports fine-grained model sizes from 8B to 70B, ensures broader cross-OS and quantization compatibility, and remains OOM-free, while also being Wi-Fi tolerant, privacy-preserving, and hardware-independent. The code is available at https://gitee.com/zonghang-li/prima.cpp.
abstract_zh: 端侧推理具备隐私保护、可离线使用、响应即时的优势，但消费级硬件的限制导致大语言模型在这类设备上吞吐量低、性能受限。为克服这一挑战，我们提出prima.cpp，这是一款分布式端侧推理系统，可在消费级家庭集群上运行300亿至700亿参数规模的大语言模型，该类集群存在CPU/GPU混合部署、内存/显存不足、磁盘速度慢、依赖Wi-Fi连接、操作系统异构等问题。我们提出流水线环形并行（PRP）机制，使磁盘I/O与计算、通信过程相互重叠，并解决了基于内存映射（mmap）的卸载方案中存在的预取-释放冲突问题。我们进一步提出异构感知调度器Halda，可在内存/显存约束下，对单设备CPU/GPU工作负载分配与设备选择进行协同优化。在四台消费级家用设备组成的集群上，700亿参数模型的单令牌生成时间（TPOT）可达674毫秒/令牌，内存压力低于6%；启用投机解码的320亿参数模型推理速度可达26令牌/秒。与llama.cpp、exo、dllama相比，我们提出的prima.cpp的单令牌生成时间降低了5至17倍，支持80亿至700亿参数的细粒度模型规格，具备更广泛的跨操作系统与量化方案兼容性，不会出现内存不足（OOM）问题，同时还可适配Wi-Fi环境、保护隐私且与硬件无关。代码可从https://gitee.com/zonghang-li/prima.cpp获取。
notes: ['论文核心是大模型推理系统，面向低资源异构家庭集群上的 30B-70B LLM 部署与加速。', '未看到算子与内核级优化或模型压缩本身作为主要贡献，因此不标注这两类偏好。']

title: InfiniPot-V: Memory-Constrained KV Cache Compression for Streaming Video Understanding
abstract: Modern multimodal large language models (MLLMs) can reason over hour-long video, yet their key–value (KV) cache grows linearly with time—quickly exceeding the fixed memory of phones, AR glasses, and edge robots. Prior compression schemes either assume the whole video and user query are available offline or must first build the full cache, so memory still scales with stream length. InfiniPot-V is the first training-free, query-agnostic framework that enforces a hard, length-independent memory cap for \textit{streaming} video understanding. During video encoding it monitors the cache and, once a user-set threshold is reached, runs a lightweight compression pass that (i) removes temporally redundant tokens via Temporal-axis Redundancy (TaR) metric and (ii) keeps semantically significant tokens via Value-Norm (VaN) ranking. Across four open-source MLLMs and four long-video and streaming-video benchmarks, InfiniPot-V cuts peak GPU memory by up to 94\%, sustains real-time generation, and matches or surpasses full-cache accuracy—even in multi-turn dialogues. By dissolving the KV cache bottleneck without retraining or query knowledge, InfiniPot-V closes the gap for on-device streaming video assistants.
abstract_zh: 现代多模态大语言模型（MLLM）可对长达数小时的视频进行推理，但其键值（KV）缓存会随时间线性增长，很快就会超出手机、AR眼镜和边缘机器人的固定内存容量。现有压缩方案要么假设完整视频和用户查询均可离线获取，要么必须先构建完整缓存，因此内存占用仍会随视频流长度增长。InfiniPot-V是首个无需训练、与查询无关的框架，可为流式视频理解设置严格的、与流长度无关的内存上限。在视频编码阶段，它会持续监控缓存，一旦达到用户设定的阈值，就会执行轻量压缩流程：一是通过时间轴冗余（TaR）指标移除存在时间冗余的令牌，二是通过值范数（VaN）排序保留语义重要的令牌。在四款开源多模态大语言模型以及四个长视频、流式视频基准测试中，InfiniPot-V最多可将GPU峰值内存降低94%，可维持实时生成能力，准确率能够匹配甚至超过全缓存方案，即便在多轮对话场景下也是如此。InfiniPot-V无需重新训练、无需预先获取查询信息即可消除KV缓存瓶颈，扫清了端侧流式视频助理的落地障碍。
notes: 核心贡献是流式视频理解中的 KV cache 压缩与固定内存上限控制，属于多模态推理阶段的上下文与缓存优化；未见算子/内核级实现优化细节。

title: Mixture Compressor for Mixture-of-Experts LLMs Gains More
abstract:
Mixture-of-Experts large language models (MoE-LLMs) marks a significant step forward of language models, however, they encounter two critical challenges in practice: 1) expert parameters lead to considerable memory consumption and loading latency; and 2) the current activated experts are redundant, as many tokens may only require a single expert. Motivated by these issues, we investigate the MoE-LLMs and make two key observations: a) different experts exhibit varying behaviors on activation reconstruction error, routing scores, and activated frequencies, highlighting their differing importance, and b) not all tokens are equally important-- only a small subset is critical.
