Building on these insights, we propose MC, a training-free Mixture-Compressor for MoE-LLMs, which leverages the significance of both experts and tokens to achieve an extreme compression. First, to mitigate storage and loading overheads, we introduce Pre-Loading Mixed-Precision Quantization (PMQ), which formulates the adaptive bit-width allocation as a Linear Programming (LP) problem, where the objective function balances multi-factors reflecting the importance of each expert. Additionally, we develop Online Dynamic Pruning (ODP),  which identifies important tokens to retain and dynamically select activated experts for other tokens during inference to optimize efficiency while maintaining performance. Our MC integrates static quantization and dynamic pruning to collaboratively achieve extreme compression for MoE-LLMs with less accuracy loss, ensuring an optimal trade-off between performance and efficiency Extensive experiments confirm the effectiveness of our approach. For instance, at 2.54 bits, MC compresses 76.6% of the model, with only a 3.8% average accuracy loss. During dynamic inference, we further reduce activated parameters by 15%, with a performance drop of less than 0.6%. Remarkably, MC even surpasses floating-point 13b dense LLMs with significantly smaller parameter sizes, suggesting that mixture compression in MoE-LLMs has the potential to outperform both comparable and larger dense LLMs.  Our code is
available at https://github.com/Aaronhuang-778/MC-MoE
abstract_zh: 混合专家大语言模型（MoE-LLM）是语言模型领域的一项重大进展，但在实际应用中面临两大关键挑战：1）专家参数会导致极高的内存消耗和加载延迟；2）当前被激活的专家存在冗余，因为许多token可能仅需单个专家即可处理。受这些问题驱动，我们对MoE-LLM展开研究，得到两项关键发现：a）不同专家在激活重构误差、路由得分和激活频率方面表现存在差异，凸显出其重要性各不相同；b）不同token的重要性并非均等，仅有小部分子集是关键的。基于这些洞察，我们提出MC，一种无需训练的MoE-LLM混合压缩器，它同时利用专家和token的重要性实现极致压缩。首先，为降低存储和加载开销，我们提出预加载混合精度量化（PMQ）方法，将自适应位宽分配建模为线性规划（LP）问题，其目标函数可平衡反映各专家重要性的多重因素。此外，我们开发了在线动态剪枝（ODP）方法，该方法能够识别需要保留的重要token，并在推理过程中为其他token动态选择激活的专家，从而在维持性能的同时优化效率。我们的MC整合了静态量化和动态剪枝，协同实现MoE-LLM的极致压缩，同时精度损失更小，确保在性能和效率之间取得最优权衡。大量实验验证了我们方法的有效性。例如，在2.54比特位宽下，MC可压缩76.6%的模型参数，平均精度损失仅为3.8%。在动态推理过程中，我们进一步将激活参数减少15%，性能下降不到0.6%。值得注意的是，MC的参数规模远小于130亿参数的浮点型稠密大语言模型，性能却更优，说明MoE-LLM的混合压缩方案有潜力超越同等规模甚至更大的稠密大语言模型。我们的代码可在https://github.com/Aaronhuang-778/MC-MoE获取。
notes: 论文聚焦 MoE-LLM 的训练后压缩与推理期动态专家裁剪；核心贡献同时覆盖量化压缩和基于 token/expert 重要性的动态推理优化。

title: Dynamic-LLaVA: Efficient Multimodal Large Language Models via Dynamic Vision-language Context Sparsification
abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have achieved remarkable success in vision understanding, reasoning, and interaction. However, the inference computation and memory increase progressively with the generation of output tokens during decoding, directly affecting the efficacy of MLLMs. Existing methods attempt to reduce the vision context redundancy to achieve efficient MLLMs. Unfortunately, the efficiency benefits of the vision context reduction in the prefill stage gradually diminish during the decoding stage. To address this problem, we proposed a dynamic vision-language context sparsification framework Dynamic-LLaVA, which dynamically reduces the redundancy of vision context in the prefill stage and decreases the memory and computation overhead of the generated language context during decoding. Dynamic-LLaVA designs a tailored sparsification inference scheme for different inference modes, i.e., prefill, decoding with and without KV cache, to achieve efficient inference of MLLMs. In practice, Dynamic-LLaVA can reduce computation consumption by $\sim$75\% in the prefill stage. Meanwhile, throughout the entire generation process of MLLMs, Dynamic-LLaVA reduces the $\sim$50\% computation consumption under decoding without KV cache, while saving $\sim$50\% GPU memory overhead when decoding with KV cache, due to the vision-language context sparsification. Extensive experiments also demonstrate that Dynamic-LLaVA achieves efficient inference for MLLMs with negligible understanding and generation ability degradation or even performance gains compared to the full-context inference baselines. Code is available at https://github.com/Osilly/dynamic_llava.
abstract_zh: 多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）已在视觉理解、推理与交互领域取得了显著成果。然而，解码过程中推理计算量与内存占用会随输出词元的生成逐步提升，直接影响多模态大语言模型的使用效能。现有方法试图通过降低视觉上下文冗余来构建高效多模态大语言模型，但预填充阶段缩减视觉上下文带来的效率增益会在解码阶段逐渐消退。为解决这一问题，我们提出了动态视觉-语言上下文稀疏化框架Dynamic-LLaVA，该框架可在预填充阶段动态削减视觉上下文冗余，同时降低解码阶段已生成语言上下文带来的内存与计算开销。Dynamic-LLaVA针对不同的推理模式，即预填充、带KV缓存的解码、不带KV缓存的解码，设计了专属的稀疏化推理方案，以实现多模态大语言模型的高效推理。实际应用中，Dynamic-LLaVA可在预填充阶段降低约75%的计算消耗。同时，在多模态大语言模型的整个生成流程中，得益于视觉-语言上下文稀疏化，Dynamic-LLaVA在无KV缓存的解码场景下可降低约50%的计算消耗，在有KV缓存的解码场景下可节省约50%的GPU内存开销。大量实验还表明，与全上下文推理基线相比，Dynamic-LLaVA在实现多模态大语言模型高效推理的同时，其理解与生成能力的退化可忽略不计，甚至能获得性能提升。代码可从https://github.com/Osilly/dynamic_llava获取。
notes: 论文聚焦多模态大模型推理阶段的视觉-语言上下文稀疏化，核心收益体现在降低解码期计算与 KV cache/上下文内存开销，最匹配“上下文与缓存优化”。

title: OSCAR: Online Soft Compression for RAG
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) enhances large language models (LLMs) by integrating external knowledge, leading to improved accuracy and relevance. However, scaling RAG pipelines remains computationally expensive as context length grows. On one hand, hard compression methods have recently proposed to prune the retrieved text on-the-fly with a limited compression ration. On the other hand, soft compression method performs a costly offline compression thanks a dedicated LLM but with a higher compression rate. In this paper, we introduce OSCAR, a novel query-dependent online soft compression method for RAG. OSCAR bridges the gap between online hard and offline soft compression methods, bringing the best of both: OSCAR dynamically compresses retrieved information at inference time, eliminating storage overhead and enabling higher compression rates than existing methods. Our experiments demonstrate state-of-the-art performance with a 2-5x speed-up in inference and minimal, if any, accuracy loss, for LLMs ranging from 1B to 24B parameters.
abstract_zh: 检索增强生成（RAG）通过整合外部知识对大语言模型（LLM）进行增强，可提升准确性和相关性。但随着上下文长度增长，扩展RAG流水线的计算成本仍然居高不下。一方面，近年提出的硬压缩方法可对检索到的文本进行即时剪枝，但压缩比有限。另一方面，软压缩方法需借助专用大语言模型执行成本高昂的离线压缩，但压缩比更高。本文提出OSCAR，一种面向RAG的新型查询依赖型在线软压缩方法。OSCAR填补了在线硬压缩与离线软压缩方法之间的空白，兼具二者优势：其可在推理时动态压缩检索到的信息，消除了存储开销，且压缩比高于现有方法。我们的实验表明，对于参数量在10亿到240亿区间的各类大语言模型，该方法实现了最先进的性能，推理速度提升2至5倍，且准确率损失极小，甚至不存在损失。
notes: 论文核心是在 RAG 推理时对检索上下文做在线软压缩，直接针对上下文长度与推理开销，不是模型参数压缩或算子/调度层优化。

general: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) enhances large language models (LLMs) by integrating external knowledge

title: GlowQ: Group-Shared LOw-Rank Approximation for Quantized LLMs
abstract:
Quantization techniques such as BitsAndBytes, AWQ, and GPTQ are widely used as a standard method in deploying large language models but often degrades accuracy when using low-bit representations, e.g., 4 bits. Low-rank correction methods (e.g., LQER, QERA, ASER) has been proposed to mitigate this issue, however, they restore all layers and insert error-correction modules into every decoder block, which increases latency and memory overhead. To address this limitation, we propose GlowQ, a group-shared low-rank approximation for quantized LLMs that caches a single shared right factor per input-sharing group and restores only the groups or layers that yield the highest accuracy benefit. 
GlowQ computes the high-precision projection once per input-sharing group and reuses it across its modules, reducing parameter and memory overhead, and retaining the expressivity of layer-specific corrections. We also propose a selective variant, GlowQ-S, that applies the cached shared module only where it provides the largest benefit. Compared with strong baselines, our approach reduces TTFB by \(5.6\%\) and increases throughput by \(9.6\%\) on average, while reducing perplexity on WikiText-2 by \(0.17\%\) and increasing downstream accuracy by 0.42 percentage points. The selective model GlowQ-S further reduces latency, cutting TTFB by \(23.4\%\) and increasing throughput by \(37.4\%\), while maintaining accuracy within 0.2 percentage points on average.
abstract_zh:
诸如BitsAndBytes、AWQ、GPTQ等量化技术是大语言模型部署中广泛使用的标准方法，但在采用4位等低位表示时往往会出现精度下降的问题。低秩校正方法（如LQER、QERA、ASER）已被提出用于缓解这一问题，但这类方法需要修复所有层，且在每个解码器块中都插入误差校正模块，会增加延迟和内存开销。为解决这一局限，我们提出GlowQ，一种面向量化大语言模型的组共享低秩近似方法，该方法为每个输入共享组缓存单个共享右因子，且仅修复能带来最高精度收益的组或层。
GlowQ针对每个输入共享组仅计算一次高精度投影，并在其所属模块中复用该投影，能够减少参数与内存开销，同时保留层专属校正的表达能力。我们还提出了选择性变体GlowQ-S，仅在缓存共享模块能带来最大收益的位置应用该模块。与强基线方法相比，我们的方法平均将首令牌生成时间（TTFB）降低5.6%，吞吐量提升9.6%，同时将WikiText-2数据集上的困惑度降低0.17%，下游任务精度提升0.42个百分点。选择性模型GlowQ-S进一步降低了延迟，其首令牌生成时间降幅达23.4%，吞吐量提升37.4%，同时平均精度波动控制在0.2个百分点以内。
notes: ['核心对象是量化 LLM，因此归入 LLM/多模态。', '论文主轴是量化与低秩校正，明确属于模型压缩；同时通过 group-shared cache 和选择性恢复降低时延/内存，次级对应系统与调度优化。']

general: Quantization techniques such as BitsAndBytes, AWQ, and GPTQ are widely used as a standard method in deploying large language models but often degrades accuracy when using low-bit representations, e.g., 4 bits.

title: ProtoKV: Long-context Knowledges Are Already Well-Organized Before Your Query
abstract: Modern Large Language Models (LLMs) face fundamental challenges in processing long text sequences due to the quadratic complexity of attention mechanisms. Key-Value (KV) cache retention strategies mitigate this issue by selectively preserving salient KV pairs for autoregressive generation. However, existing methods fail to adequately and efficiently preserve the semantic integrity of the compressed representations. In this paper, we discover a prevalent phenomenon in LLM: within the key embedding space, while most tokens exhibit similarity with their contextual neighbors (we term position-determined tokens), a small subset of special tokens serving as semantic anchors consistently show local deviation property and form one or several clusters (we term semantic-anchored tokens). Motivated by this observation, we propose ProtoKV that separately processes these two token categories for KV cache compression. Within this framework, we first construct semantic prototypes based on the inherent characteristics of the two token categories, and subsequently form clusters of semantically similar tokens as basic compression units. This approach preserves semantic integrity with high computational efficiency. Experiments on LongBench demonstrate that ProtoKV achieves 2.11% higher accuracy than state-of-the-art methods under matched memory constraints.
abstract_zh: 现代大型语言模型（LLM）受注意力机制的二次复杂度限制，在处理长文本序列时面临根本性挑战。键值（KV）缓存保留策略通过选择性保留用于自回归生成的显著键值对缓解了这一问题，但现有方法无法充分、高效地保留压缩表征的语义完整性。本文发现大型语言模型中存在一个普遍现象：在键嵌入空间内，大多数令牌与其上下文邻接令牌表现出相似性，我们将其命名为位置决定型令牌，而一小部分作为语义锚点的特殊令牌始终呈现局部偏离特性，并形成一个或多个簇，我们将其命名为语义锚定型令牌。受这一观测结果启发，我们提出ProtoKV方法，对上述两类令牌分别处理以实现KV缓存压缩。在该框架下，我们首先基于两类令牌的固有特性构建语义原型，随后将语义相似的令牌聚为簇作为基本压缩单元，该方法可在保证高计算效率的同时保留语义完整性。在LongBench上开展的实验表明，在相同内存约束条件下，ProtoKV的准确率较当前最优方法高出2.11%。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=25；命中=kv cache, cache compression, serving, compression, compressed, generation, autoregressive, language model

title: MPCache: MPC-Friendly KV Cache Eviction for Efficient Private LLM Inference
abstract: Private large language model (LLM) inference based on secure multi-party computation (MPC) achieves formal data privacy protection but suffers from significant latency overhead, especially for long input sequences. While key-value (KV) cache eviction and sparse attention algorithms have been proposed for efficient LLM inference in plaintext, they are not designed for MPC and cannot benefit private LLM inference directly. In this paper, we propose an accurate and MPC-friendly KV cache eviction framework, dubbed MPCache, building on the observation that historical tokens in a long sequence may have different effects on the downstream decoding. Hence, MPCache combines a look-once static eviction algorithm to discard unimportant KV cache and a query-aware dynamic selection algorithm to activate only a small subset of KV cache for attention computation. MPCache further incorporates a series of optimizations for efficient dynamic KV cache selection, including MPC-friendly similarity approximation, hierarchical KV cache clustering, and cross-layer index-sharing strategy. Extensive experiments demonstrate that MPCache consistently outperforms prior-art KV cache eviction baselines across different generation tasks and achieves 1.8 ~ 2.01x and 3.39 ~ 8.37x decoding latency and communication reduction on different sequence lengths, respectively.
abstract_zh: 基于安全多方计算（MPC）的私有大语言模型（LLM）推理可实现形式化的数据隐私保护，但存在显著的延迟开销，针对长输入序列时该问题尤为突出。尽管键值（KV）缓存驱逐与稀疏注意力算法已被提出用于提升明文场景下的LLM推理效率，但这类算法并非为MPC设计，无法直接为私有LLM推理带来性能增益。本文观察到长序列中的历史令牌对下游解码的影响存在差异，基于这一观察，我们提出了一种准确且适配MPC的KV缓存驱逐框架，命名为MPCache。MPCache结合了单次遍历静态驱逐算法以丢弃不重要的KV缓存，同时采用查询感知动态选择算法，仅激活小部分KV缓存用于注意力计算。MPCache还进一步集成了一系列面向高效动态KV缓存选择的优化策略，包括适配MPC的相似度近似、分层KV缓存聚类以及跨层索引共享策略。大量实验表明，在不同生成任务中，MPCache的性能始终优于现有最先进的KV缓存驱逐基线，针对不同序列长度，MPCache分别实现了1.8~2.01倍的解码延迟降低，以及3.39~8.37倍的通信量减少。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=25；命中=kv cache, cache compression, cache eviction, latency, compression, inference, decoding, generation

title: Dobi-SVD: Differentiable SVD for LLM Compression and Some New Perspectives
abstract:
Large language models (LLMs) have sparked a new wave of AI applications; however, their substantial computational costs and memory demands pose significant challenges to democratizing access to LLMs for a broader audience. Singular Value Decomposition (SVD), a technique studied for decades, offers a hardware-independent and flexibly tunable solution for LLM compression. In this paper, we present new directions using SVD: we first theoretically analyze the optimality of truncating weights and truncating activations, then we further identify three key issues on SVD-based LLM compression, including (1) How can we determine the optimal truncation position for each weight matrix in LLMs? (2) How can we efficiently update the weight matrices based on truncation position? (3) How can we address the inherent "injection" nature that results in the information loss of the SVD? We propose an effective approach, **Dobi-SVD**, to tackle the three issues. 
First, we propose a **differentiable** truncation-value learning mechanism, along with gradient-robust backpropagation, enabling the model to adaptively find the optimal truncation positions. Next, we utilize the Eckart-Young-Mirsky theorem to derive a theoretically **optimal** weight update formula through rigorous mathematical analysis. Lastly, by observing and leveraging the quantization-friendly nature of matrices after SVD decomposition, we reconstruct a mapping between truncation positions and memory requirements, establishing a **bijection** from truncation positions to memory. 
Experimental results show that with a 40\% parameter-compression rate, our method achieves a perplexity of 9.07 on the Wikitext2 dataset with the compressed LLama-7B model, a 78.7\% improvement over the state-of-the-art SVD for LLM compression method. 
We emphasize that Dobi-SVD is the first to achieve such a high-ratio LLM compression with minimal performance drop.  We also extend our Dobi-SVD to VLM compression, achieving a 20\% increase in throughput with minimal performance degradation. We hope that the inference speedup—up to 12.4x on 12GB NVIDIA Titan Xp GPUs and 3x on 80GB A100 GPUs for LLMs, and 1.2x on 80GB A100 GPUs for VLMs—will bring significant benefits to the broader community such as robotics.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型掀起了人工智能应用的新浪潮，但其极高的计算成本与内存需求，对向更广泛受众普及大语言模型构成了重大挑战。奇异值分解是一项已被研究数十年的技术，可为大语言模型压缩提供不依赖硬件、可灵活调优的解决方案。本文提出了使用奇异值分解的新方向：我们首先从理论上分析权重截断与激活截断的最优性，随后进一步明确了基于奇异值分解的大语言模型压缩存在的三个核心问题，分别是如何确定大语言模型中每个权重矩阵的最优截断位置，如何基于截断位置高效更新权重矩阵，如何解决奇异值分解固有的会导致信息损失的注入特性。我们提出了一种有效方法Dobi-SVD来解决这三个问题。首先，我们提出了一种可微截断值学习机制以及梯度鲁棒反向传播方法，使模型能够自适应找到最优截断位置。其次，我们利用Eckart-Young-Mirsky定理，通过严格的数学分析推导出了理论上最优的权重更新公式。最后，通过观察并利用奇异值分解后矩阵的量化友好特性，我们重构了截断位置与内存需求之间的映射关系，建立了从截断位置到内存的双射。实验结果表明，在参数压缩率为40%的情况下，我们的方法使用压缩后的LLama-7B模型在Wikitext2数据集上取得了9.07的困惑度，较现有最优的基于奇异值分解的大语言模型压缩方法提升了78.7%。我们强调，Dobi-SVD是首个在实现如此高比例大语言模型压缩的同时保持性能下降极小的方法。我们还将Dobi-SVD扩展到视觉语言模型压缩场景，在性能下降极小的情况下实现了20%的吞吐量提升。我们希望本方法带来的推理速度提升，即大语言模型在12GB NVIDIA Titan Xp GPU上最高可达12.4倍、在80GB A100 GPU上可达3倍，视觉语言模型在80GB A100 GPU上可达1.2倍，能够为机器人等更广泛的领域群体带来显著收益。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=25；命中=throughput, serving, quantization, compression, compressed, inference, language model, large language model

title: EvoPress: Accurate Dynamic Model Compression via Evolutionary Search
abstract:
The high computational costs of large language models (LLMs) have led to a flurry of research on LLM compression, via methods such as quantization, sparsification, or structured pruning. A new frontier in this area is given by dynamic, non-uniform compression methods, which adjust the compression levels (e.g., sparsity) per-block or even per-layer in order to minimize accuracy loss, while guaranteeing a global compression threshold. 
Yet, current methods rely on estimating the "importance" of a given layer, implicitly assuming that layers contribute independently to the overall compression error. 
We begin from the motivating observation that this independence assumption does not generally hold for LLM compression: pruning a model further may even significantly recover performance.
To address this, we propose EvoPress, a novel evolutionary framework for dynamic LLM compression. By formulating dynamic compression as a general optimization problem, EvoPress identifies optimal compression profiles in a highly efficient manner, and generalizes across diverse models and compression techniques. Via EvoPress, we achieve state-of-the-art performance for dynamic compression of Llama, Mistral, and Phi models, setting new benchmarks for structural pruning (block/layer dropping), unstructured sparsity, and quantization with dynamic bitwidths.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）的高计算成本催生了大量大语言模型压缩相关研究，常用方法包括量化、稀疏化、结构化剪枝等。该领域的新兴研究方向是动态非均匀压缩方法，这类方法按块甚至按层调整压缩级别（例如稀疏度），从而在保证满足全局压缩阈值的同时最大限度降低精度损失。然而现有方法依赖于估计给定层的“重要性”，隐含假设各层对整体压缩误差的贡献相互独立。我们的核心驱动观察是，这种独立性假设在大语言模型压缩场景中通常并不成立：对模型进行进一步剪枝甚至可能显著恢复性能。为解决该问题，我们提出EvoPress，一种面向动态大语言模型压缩的新型进化框架。通过将动态压缩形式化为一个通用优化问题，EvoPress能够以极高的效率识别最优压缩配置，并且可泛化到不同的模型与压缩技术中。借助EvoPress，我们在Llama、Mistral和Phi模型的动态压缩任务上取得了当前最优性能，在结构化剪枝（块/层丢弃）、非结构化稀疏化以及动态位宽量化方向上均设定了新的基准。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=17；命中=quantization, pruning, sparsity, compression, language model, large language model, llm

title: CAS-Spec: Cascade Adaptive Self-Speculative Decoding for On-the-Fly Lossless Inference Acceleration of LLMs
abstract:
Speculative decoding has become a widely adopted as an effective technique for lossless inference acceleration when deploying large language models (LLMs).
While on-the-fly self-speculative methods offer seamless integration and broad utility, they often fall short of the speed gains achieved by methods relying on specialized training.
Cascading a hierarchy of draft models promises further acceleration and flexibility, but the high cost of training multiple models has limited its practical application.
In this paper, we propose a novel Cascade Adaptive Self-Speculative Decoding (CAS-Spec) method which constructs speculative draft models by leveraging dynamically switchable inference acceleration (DSIA) strategies, including layer sparsity and activation quantization.
Furthermore, traditional vertical and horizontal cascade algorithms are inefficient when applied to self-speculative decoding methods.
We introduce a Dynamic Tree Cascade (DyTC) algorithm that adaptively routes the multi-level draft models and assigns the draft lengths, based on the heuristics of acceptance rates and latency prediction.
Our CAS-Spec method achieves state-of-the-art acceleration compared to existing on-the-fly speculative decoding methods, with an average speedup from $1.1\times$ to $2.3\times$ over autoregressive decoding across various LLMs and datasets.
DyTC improves the average speedup by $47$\% and $48$\% over cascade-based baseline and tree-based baseline algorithms, respectively.
CAS-Spec can be easily integrated into most existing LLMs and holds promising potential for further acceleration as self-speculative decoding techniques continue to evolve.
abstract_zh: 推测解码是大语言模型部署过程中广泛采用的一种有效无损推理加速技术。即时自推测方法虽具备无缝集成、适用范围广的优势，但其提速效果往往不及依赖专项训练的方法。级联分层草稿模型有望进一步提升加速效果与灵活性，但训练多个模型的高昂成本限制了其实际应用。本文提出一种新型级联自适应自推测解码方法，该方法利用包含层稀疏化、激活量化在内的动态可切换推理加速策略构建推测草稿模型。此外，传统的垂直与水平级联算法应用于自推测解码方法时效率较低。我们提出动态树级联算法，该算法基于接受率启发式规则与延迟预测，自适应路由多级草稿模型并分配草稿长度。与现有即时推测解码方法相比，本文提出的级联自适应自推测解码方法实现了当前最优的加速效果，在各类大语言模型与数据集上，其推理速度较自回归解码平均提升1.1倍至2.3倍。与基于级联的基准算法、基于树的基准算法相比，动态树级联算法的平均提速幅度分别高出47%与48%。级联自适应自推测解码方法可轻松集成至绝大多数现有大语言模型中，且随着自推测解码技术不断发展，其有望实现进一步的提速。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=25；命中=speculative decoding, self-speculative, draft model, latency, quantization, sparsity, inference, decoding

title: Preble: Efficient Distributed Prompt Scheduling for LLM Serving
abstract:
Prompts to large language models (LLMs) have evolved beyond simple user questions.
For LLMs to solve complex problems, today’s practices are to include domain-specific
instructions, illustration of tool usages, and/or long context such as textbook chapters in
prompts. As such, many parts of prompts are repetitive across requests. Recent works
propose to cache and reuse KV state of prompts. However, they are all confined to a single-
GPU optimization, while production LLM serving systems are distributed by nature.

This paper proposes Preble, the first distributed LLM serving platform that targets and op-
timizes for prompt sharing. We designed a distributed scheduling system that co-optimizes
KV state reuse and computation load-balancing with a new scheduling algorithm and a
hierarchical scheduling mechanism. Our evaluation of Preble with real workloads and re-
quest arrival patterns on two open-source LLMs shows that Preble outperforms the SOTA
serving systems by 1.5× to 14.5× on average latency and 2× to 10× on p99 latency.
abstract_zh:
大语言模型（LLM）的提示已不再局限于简单的用户问题。为使大语言模型能够解决复杂问题，当前的通行做法是在提示中加入领域专属指令、工具使用说明，以及/或教科书章节这类长上下文内容。因此，不同请求的提示存在大量重复部分。近期研究提出缓存并复用提示的键值（KV）状态，但这类研究均仅限于单GPU优化，而生产环境的大语言模型服务系统本质上是分布式的。
本文提出Preble，这是首个针对提示共享进行优化的分布式大语言模型服务平台。我们设计了一套分布式调度系统，通过全新的调度算法和分层调度机制，对KV状态复用与计算负载均衡进行协同优化。我们在两个开源大语言模型上采用真实工作负载和请求到达模式对Preble开展评估，结果表明，Preble的性能优于现有最先进的服务系统，平均延迟的优化幅度达1.5倍至14.5倍，p99延迟的优化幅度达2倍至10倍。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=17；命中=long context, latency, serving, language model, large language model, llm
final_primary_research_object: AI 系统 / 基础设施

title: Quamba: A Post-Training Quantization Recipe for Selective State Space Models
abstract: State Space Models (SSMs) have emerged as an appealing alternative to Transformers for large language models, achieving state-of-the-art accuracy with constant memory complexity which allows for holding longer context lengths than attention-based networks. The superior computational efficiency of SSMs in long sequence modeling positions them favorably over Transformers in many scenarios. However, improving the efficiency of SSMs on request-intensive cloud-serving and resource-limited edge applications is still a formidable task. SSM quantization is a possible solution to this problem, making SSMs more suitable for wide deployment, while still maintaining their accuracy. Quantization is a common technique to reduce the model size and to utilize the low bit-width acceleration features on modern computing units, yet existing quantization techniques are poorly suited for SSMs. Most notably, SSMs have highly sensitive feature maps within the selective scan mechanism (i.e., linear recurrence) and massive outliers in the output activations which are not present in the output of token-mixing in the self-attention modules. To address this issue, we propose a static 8-bit per-tensor SSM quantization method which suppresses the maximum values of the input activations to the selective SSM for finer quantization precision and quantizes the output activations in an outlier-free space with Hadamard transform. Our 8-bit weight-activation quantized Mamba 2.8B SSM benefits from hardware acceleration and achieves a 1.72 $\times$ lower generation latency on an Nvidia Orin Nano 8G, with only a 0.9\% drop in average accuracy on zero-shot tasks. When quantizing Jamba, a 52B parameter SSM-style language model, we observe only a $1\%$  drop in accuracy, demonstrating that our SSM quantization method is both effective and scalable for large language models, which require appropriate compression techniques for deployment. The experiments demonstrate the effectiveness and practical applicability of our approach for deploying SSM-based models of all sizes on both cloud and edge platforms.
abstract_zh: 状态空间模型（SSM）已成为大语言模型领域中Transformer的极具吸引力的替代方案，它以恒定内存复杂度实现最先进的精度，相较于基于注意力的网络可支持更长的上下文长度。SSM在长序列建模中的优异计算效率使其在众多场景中比Transformer更具优势。然而，在请求密集的云服务场景和资源受限的边缘应用中提升SSM的效率仍是一项艰巨的任务。SSM量化是解决这一问题的可行方案，可让SSM在保持自身精度的同时更适配广泛部署的需求。量化是缩减模型体积、利用现代计算单元低比特加速特性的常用技术，但现有量化技术很难适配SSM。最突出的问题是，SSM的选择性扫描机制（即线性循环）内部存在高度敏感的特征图，且输出激活中存在大量异常值，这一问题在自注意力模块的令牌混合输出中并不存在。为解决该问题，我们提出一种静态8比特逐张量SSM量化方法，该方法抑制输入至选择性SSM的输入激活的最大值，以获得更精细的量化精度，同时通过哈达玛变换在无异常值空间中对输出激活进行量化。我们实现的8比特权重-激活量化Mamba 2.8B SSM可获得硬件加速收益，在英伟达Orin Nano 8G上的生成延迟降低1.72倍，而零样本任务的平均精度仅下降0.9%。对拥有520亿参数的SSM类语言模型Jamba进行量化时，我们观察到精度仅下降1%，这表明我们的SSM量化方法对于需要适配压缩技术以完成部署的大语言模型而言兼具有效性和可扩展性。实验证明，我们的方法支持将各类规模的基于SSM的模型部署到云平台和边缘平台上，具备有效性和实际应用价值。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=21；命中=latency, serving, quantization, quantized, compression, generation, language model, large language model

title: ThinK: Thinner Key Cache by Query-Driven Pruning
abstract:
Large Language Models (LLMs) have revolutionized the field of natural language processing, achieving unprecedented performance across a variety of applications. 
However, their increased computational and memory demands present significant challenges, especially when handling long sequences.
This paper focuses on the long-context scenario, addressing the inefficiencies in KV cache memory consumption during inference. 
Unlike existing approaches that optimize the memory based on the sequence length, we identify substantial redundancy in the channel dimension of the KV cache, as indicated by an uneven magnitude distribution and a low-rank structure in the attention weights.
In response, we propose ThinK, a novel query-dependent KV cache pruning method designed to minimize attention weight loss while selectively pruning the least significant channels. Our approach not only maintains or enhances model accuracy but also achieves a reduction in KV cache memory costs by over 20\% compared with vanilla KV cache eviction and quantization methods. For instance, ThinK integrated with KIVI can achieve $2.8\times$ peak memory reduction while maintaining nearly the same quality, enabling a batch size increase from 4$\times$ (with KIVI alone) to 5$\times$ when using a single GPU. Extensive evaluations on the LLaMA and Mistral models across various long-sequence datasets verified the efficiency of ThinK.  Our code has been made available at https://github.com/SalesforceAIResearch/ThinK.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）彻底变革了自然语言处理领域，在各类应用中取得了前所未有的性能表现。然而，其不断增长的计算与内存需求带来了显著挑战，处理长序列时尤为突出。本文聚焦长上下文场景，解决推理过程中键值（KV）缓存内存消耗效率低下的问题。现有方法多基于序列长度优化内存，与这些方法不同，我们发现KV缓存的通道维度存在大量冗余，注意力权重的幅值分布不均及低秩结构均可证明这一点。对此，我们提出ThinK，这是一种全新的依赖查询的KV缓存剪枝方法，旨在选择性剪枝最不重要的通道的同时，尽可能减小注意力权重损失。我们的方法不仅能够保持甚至提升模型精度，与原生KV缓存淘汰和量化方法相比，还可将KV缓存的内存开销降低20%以上。例如，与KIVI集成的ThinK可实现2.8倍的峰值内存缩减，同时几乎保持原有效果，在使用单GPU时，可将批量大小从仅使用KIVI的4倍提升至5倍。我们在多个长序列数据集上针对LLaMA和Mistral模型开展了广泛评估，验证了ThinK的效率。我们的代码已公开于https://github.com/SalesforceAIResearch/ThinK。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=26；命中=kv cache, cache compression, cache eviction, quantization, pruning, compression, inference, language model

title: Efficiently Serving Large Multimodal Models Using EPD Disaggregation
abstract: Large Multimodal Models (LMMs) extend Large Language Models (LLMs) by handling diverse inputs such as images, audio, and video, but at the cost of adding a multimodal encoding stage that increases both computational and memory overhead. This step negatively affects key Service Level Objectives (SLOs), such as time to first token (TTFT) and time per output token (TPOT). We introduce Encode-Prefill-Decode (EPD) Disaggregation, a novel framework that separates the encoding, prefill, and decode stages onto dedicated resources. Unlike current systems, which bundle encoding and prefill together, our approach decouples these steps, unlocking new opportunities and optimizations. These include a mechanism to cache multimedia tokens for efficient transfer, a novel way to parallelize the encoding load within a request, a module for optimal resource allocation for disaggregated serving, and a novel role-switching method to handle changing workload characteristics. Experimental evaluations with popular LMMs show substantial gains in memory efficiency (up to 15× lower peak memory utilization), batch sizes (up to 22× larger), 10× more images per request, and 2.2× larger KV caches. Furthermore, it leads to significant improvements in SLO attainment (up to 90–100% improvement) and TTFT (up to 71% reduction), compared to systems that do not disaggregate. The code is available at [https://github.com/vbdi/epdserve](https://github.com/vbdi/epdserve).
abstract_zh: 大型多模态模型（LMM）对大语言模型（LLM）进行了扩展，可处理图像、音频、视频等多样化输入，但代价是新增了多模态编码阶段，会同时增加计算与内存开销。该步骤会对首令牌生成时间（TTFT）、单输出令牌耗时（TPOT）等关键服务水平目标（SLO）造成负面影响。我们提出编码-预填充-解码（EPD）拆分框架，这是一种将编码、预填充、解码阶段拆分至专用资源运行的新型框架。当前系统通常将编码与预填充绑定在一起，与这类系统不同，我们的方法将这些步骤解耦，解锁了新的优化空间。这些优化包括可实现高效传输的多媒体令牌缓存机制、对单请求内的编码负载进行并行处理的新方法、为拆分式服务实现最优资源分配的模块，以及可应对工作负载特征变化的新型角色切换方法。针对主流LMM的实验评估显示，该框架在内存效率（峰值内存利用率最高降低15倍）、批量大小（最高提升22倍）、单请求支持图像数量（提升10倍）、KV缓存大小（提升2.2倍）方面均实现了大幅提升。此外，与未采用拆分的系统相比，该框架还能显著改善SLO达标情况（提升幅度最高达90%–100%），且TTFT最高降低71%。代码可从https://github.com/vbdi/epdserve获取。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=20；命中=kv cache, prefill, serving, inference, decode, language model, large language model, llm

title: ShadowKV: KV Cache in Shadows for High-Throughput Long-Context LLM Inference
abstract: With the widespread deployment of long-context large language models (LLMs), there has been a growing demand for efficient support of high-throughput inference. However, as the key-value (KV) cache expands with the sequence length, the increasing memory footprint and the need to access it for decoding both result in low throughput when serving long-context LLMs. While various dynamic sparse attention methods have been proposed to accelerate inference while maintaining generation quality, they either fail to sufficiently reduce GPU memory usage or introduce significant decoding latency by offloading the KV cache to the CPU. We present ShadowKV, a high-throughput long-context LLM inference system that stores the low-rank key cache and offloads the value cache to reduce the memory footprint for larger batch sizes and longer sequences. To minimize decoding latency, ShadowKV employs an accurate KV selection strategy that reconstructs minimal sparse KV pairs on-the-fly. By evaluating ShadowKV on benchmarks like RULER, LongBench, and models such as Llama-3.1-8B and GLM-4-9B-1M, we demonstrate that it achieves up to 6$\times$ larger batch sizes and 3.04$\times$ higher throughput on an A100 GPU without sacrificing accuracy, even surpassing the performance achievable with infinite batch size under the assumption of infinite GPU memory.
abstract_zh: 随着长上下文大语言模型（LLM）的广泛部署，对高吞吐推理高效支持的需求不断增长。然而，由于键值（KV）缓存会随序列长度扩展，不断增加的内存占用以及解码时访问KV缓存的需求共同导致长上下文LLM服务时吞吐较低。尽管已提出多种动态稀疏注意力方法，用于在保持生成质量的同时加快推理速度，但这类方法要么无法充分降低GPU内存占用，要么会因将KV缓存卸载到CPU而引入显著的解码延迟。我们提出ShadowKV，一种高吞吐长上下文LLM推理系统，该系统存储低秩键缓存并卸载值缓存，以降低内存占用，从而支持更大的批大小与更长的序列。为了最小化解码延迟，ShadowKV采用精准的KV选择策略，可即时重构最少的稀疏KV对。我们在RULER、LongBench等基准测试以及Llama-3.1-8B、GLM-4-9B-1M等模型上对ShadowKV进行评估，结果表明，该系统在A100 GPU上无需牺牲精度即可实现最高6倍的批大小与3.04倍的吞吐量，其性能甚至超过了GPU内存无限假设下无限批大小可达到的性能。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=26；命中=kv cache, throughput, latency, serving, offloading, inference, decoding, generation

title: SpinQuant: LLM Quantization with Learned Rotations
abstract: Post-training quantization (PTQ) techniques applied to weights, activations, and the KV cache greatly reduce memory usage, latency, and power consumption of Large Language Models (LLMs), but may lead to large quantization errors when outliers are present. Rotating activation or weight matrices helps remove outliers and benefits quantization. In this work, we identify a collection of applicable rotation parameterizations that lead to identical outputs in full-precision Transformer architectures while enhancing quantization accuracy. In addition, we find that some random rotations lead to much better quantization than others, with an up to 13 points difference in downstream zero-shot reasoning performance. As a result, we propose SpinQuant, a novel approach that incorporates learned rotation matrices for optimal quantized network accuracy. With 4-bit quantization of weight, activation, and KV-cache, SpinQuant narrows the accuracy gap on zero-shot reasoning tasks with full precision to merely 2.9 points on the LLaMA-2 7B model, surpassing LLM-QAT by 19.1 points and SmoothQuant by 25.0 points. Furthermore, SpinQuant also outperforms concurrent work QuaRot, which applies random rotations to remove outliers. In particular, for LLaMA-3 8B models that are hard to quantize, SpinQuant reduces the gap to full precision by up to 45.1% relative to QuaRot. Code is available at https://github.com/facebookresearch/SpinQuant.
abstract_zh: 针对权重、激活值和KV缓存的训练后量化（PTQ）技术可大幅降低大语言模型（LLM）的内存占用、推理延迟与功耗，但存在异常值时可能导致较大的量化误差。旋转激活矩阵或权重矩阵有助于消除异常值，对量化性能有益。本研究中，我们识别出一系列适用的旋转参数化方案，这些方案在全精度Transformer架构中可得到完全一致的输出，同时能提升量化精度。此外，我们发现部分随机旋转的量化效果远优于其他方案，二者在下游零样本推理性能上的差距最高可达13个百分点。据此我们提出SpinQuant，这是一种引入可学习旋转矩阵以实现量化网络最优精度的新方法。在对权重、激活值和KV缓存均采用4位量化的设置下，SpinQuant将LLaMA-2 7B模型在零样本推理任务上与全精度模型的精度差距缩小至仅2.9个百分点，表现较LLM-QAT高出19.1个百分点，较SmoothQuant高出25.0个百分点。此外，SpinQuant的性能也优于采用随机旋转消除异常值的同期研究QuaRot。特别是对于难以量化的LLaMA-3 8B模型，与QuaRot相比，SpinQuant将其与全精度模型的精度差距相对缩小了最高45.1%。代码可从https://github.com/facebookresearch/SpinQuant获取。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=18；命中=kv cache, latency, quantization, quantized, 4-bit, language model, large language model, llm

title: HiFC: High-efficiency Flash-based KV Cache Swapping for Scaling LLM Inference
abstract: Large‑language‑model inference with long contexts often produces key–value (KV) caches whose footprint exceeds the capacity of high‑bandwidth memory on a GPU. Prior LLM inference frameworks such as vLLM mitigate this pressure by swapping KV cache pages to host DRAM. However, the high cost of large DRAM pools makes this solution economically unattractive. Although offloading to SSDs can be a cost-effective way to expand memory capacity relative to DRAM, conventional frameworks such as FlexGen experience a substantial throughput drop since the data path that routes SSD traffic through CPU to GPU is severely bandwidth-constrained. To overcome these limitations, we introduce HiFC, a novel DRAM‑free swapping scheme that enables direct access to SSD-resident memory with low latency and high effective bandwidth. HiFC stores KV pages in pseudo-SLC (pSLC) regions of commodity NVMe SSDs, sustaining high throughput under sequential I/O and improving write endurance by up to 8$\times$. Leveraging GPU Direct Storage, HiFC enables direct transfers between SSD and GPU, bypassing host DRAM and alleviating PCIe bottlenecks. HiFC employs fine-grained block mapping to confine writes to high-performance pSLC zones, stabilizing latency and throughput under load. HiFC achieves inference throughput comparable to DRAM-based swapping under diverse long-context workloads, such as NarrativeQA, while significantly lowering the memory expansion cost of a GPU server system by 4.5$\times$ over three years.
abstract_zh: 长上下文大语言模型推理生成的键值（KV）缓存占用空间往往超出GPU高带宽内存的容量。vLLM等现有大语言模型推理框架通过将KV缓存页交换到主机动态随机存取存储器（DRAM）来缓解这一压力。然而，大容量DRAM池的高成本使得该方案在经济上缺乏吸引力。虽然相较于DRAM，卸载到固态硬盘（SSD）是一种高性价比的内存容量扩展方式，但FlexGen等传统框架会出现吞吐量大幅下降的问题，这是因为SSD流量经CPU路由到GPU的数据路径存在严重的带宽限制。为克服这些局限，我们提出HiFC，这是一种全新的无DRAM交换方案，可实现低延迟、高有效带宽的SSD驻留内存直接访问。HiFC将KV页存储在商用NVMe SSD的伪单级单元（pSLC）区域，可在顺序I/O下维持高吞吐量，同时将写入寿命最高提升8倍。借助GPU直连存储技术，HiFC可实现SSD与GPU之间的直接数据传输，绕过主机DRAM，缓解PCIe瓶颈。HiFC采用细粒度块映射将写入操作限制在高性能pSLC区域，从而在负载下稳定延迟和吞吐量。在NarrativeQA等各类长上下文工作负载下，HiFC的推理吞吐量与基于DRAM的交换方案相当，同时可将GPU服务器系统三年周期的内存扩展成本大幅降低4.5倍。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=21；命中=kv cache, long context, throughput, latency, offloading, inference, llm

title: GANQ: GPU-Adaptive Non-Uniform Quantization for Large Language Models
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) face significant deployment challenges due to their substantial resource requirements. While low-bit quantized weights can reduce memory usage and improve inference efficiency, current hardware lacks native support for mixed-precision General Matrix Multiplication (mpGEMM), resulting in inefficient dequantization-based implementations. Moreover, uniform quantization methods often fail to capture weight distributions adequately, leading to performance degradation. We propose GANQ (GPU-Adaptive Non-Uniform Quantization), a layer-wise post-training non-uniform quantization framework optimized for hardware-efficient lookup table-based mpGEMM. GANQ achieves superior quantization performance by utilizing a training-free, GPU-adaptive optimization algorithm to  efficiently reduce layer-wise quantization errors. Extensive experiments demonstrate GANQ's ability to reduce the perplexity gap from the FP16 baseline compared to state-of-the-art methods for both 3-bit and 4-bit quantization. Furthermore, when deployed on a single NVIDIA RTX 4090 GPU, GANQ's quantized models achieve up to 2.57$\times$ speedup over the baseline, advancing memory and inference efficiency in LLM deployment.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）因其极高的资源需求面临严峻的部署挑战。低比特权重量化虽可降低内存占用、提升推理效率，但现有硬件缺乏对混合精度通用矩阵乘法（mpGEMM）的原生支持，导致基于反量化的实现方案效率低下。此外，均匀量化方法通常无法充分拟合权重分布，会造成性能下降。我们提出GANQ（GPU自适应非均匀量化），这是一种分层训练后非均匀量化框架，针对基于查找表的硬件高效mpGEMM进行了优化。GANQ采用免训练的GPU自适应优化算法，可有效降低分层量化误差，从而实现更优的量化性能。大量实验表明，在3比特和4比特量化场景下，与当前最优方法相比，GANQ可缩小量化模型与FP16基线之间的困惑度差距。此外，在单张NVIDIA RTX 4090 GPU上部署时，经GANQ量化的模型较基线实现了最高2.57倍的加速，提升了大语言模型部署的内存效率与推理效率。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=26；命中=gemm, quantization, quantized, 4-bit, 3-bit, low-bit, mixed-precision, inference

title: DP-LLM: Runtime Model Adaptation with Dynamic Layer-wise Precision Assignment
abstract: How can we effectively handle queries for on-device large language models (LLMs) with varying runtime constraints, such as latency and accuracy? Multi-scale quantization addresses this challenge by enabling memory-efficient runtime model adaptation of LLMs through the overlaying of multiple model variants quantized to different bitwidths. Meanwhile, an important question still remains open-ended: how can models be properly configured to match a target precision or latency? While mixed-precision offers a promising solution, we take this further by leveraging the key observation that the sensitivity of each layer dynamically changes across decoding steps. Building on this insight, we introduce DP-LLM, a novel mechanism that dynamically assigns precision to each layer based on input values. Experimental results across multiple models and benchmarks demonstrate that DP-LLM achieves a superior performance-latency trade-off, outperforming prior approaches.
abstract_zh: 我们如何有效应对设备端大语言模型（LLM）在延迟、准确率等不同运行时约束下的查询需求？多尺度量化通过叠加采用不同比特宽度量化的多个模型变体，实现大语言模型的内存高效运行时模型自适应，从而解决这一挑战。与此同时，一个重要问题仍未得到解决：如何合理配置模型以匹配目标精度或延迟？虽然混合精度是一种极具前景的解决方案，但我们利用各层的敏感性会随解码步骤动态变化这一关键观察结果，对相关研究做了进一步推进。基于这一洞察，我们提出DP-LLM，这是一种可根据输入值为每一层动态分配精度的新型机制。在多个模型和基准测试上的实验结果表明，DP-LLM实现了更优异的性能-延迟权衡，表现优于已有方法。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=24；命中=latency, runtime, quantization, quantized, mixed-precision, inference, decoding, language model

title: Cache What Lasts: Token Retention for Memory-Bounded KV Cache in LLMs
abstract: Memory and computation remain core bottlenecks in long-horizon LLM inference due to the quadratic cost of self-attention and the ever-growing key-value (KV) cache. Existing strategies for memory-bounded inference, such as quantization, offloading, or heuristic KV eviction, either incur high orchestration costs or rely on unreliable attention-based proxies of importance. We propose TRIM-KV, a novel approach that learns each token’s intrinsic importance at creation time via a lightweight retention gate. Each gate predicts a scalar retention score that decays over time, reflecting the long-term utility of the token for a specific layer and head. Tokens with low scores are evicted when the memory budget is exceeded, ensuring that the cache always contains the most critical tokens. TRIM-KV is trained efficiently through distillation from a frozen LLM combined with a capacity loss, requiring only gate fine-tuning and adding negligible inference overhead. Across mathematical reasoning (GSM8K, MATH-500, AIME24), procedural generation (LongProc), and conversational long-memory benchmarks (LongMemEval), TRIM-KV consistently outperforms strong eviction and learnable retrieval baselines, especially in low-memory regimes. Remarkably, it even surpasses full-cache models in some settings, showing that selective retention can serve as a form of regularization, suppressing noise from uninformative tokens. Qualitative analyses further reveal that learned retention scores align with human intuition, naturally recovering heuristics such as sink tokens, sliding windows, and gist compression without explicit design. Beyond efficiency, retention scores provide insights into layer- and head-specific roles, suggesting a new path toward LLM interpretability.
abstract_zh: 由于自注意力的二次计算开销以及持续增长的键值（KV）缓存，内存与计算仍是长序列大语言模型推理的核心瓶颈。现有的内存受限推理策略，例如量化、卸载或者启发式KV驱逐方法，要么会产生高额调度成本，要么依赖基于注意力的不可靠重要性代理指标。我们提出TRIM-KV，这是一种全新的方法，通过轻量级保留门在每个词元生成时就学习其固有重要性。每个保留门会预测一个随时间衰减的标量保留分数，该分数能够反映特定层与注意力头下对应词元的长期效用。当超出内存预算时，分数较低的词元会被驱逐，从而确保缓存始终存储最为关键的词元。TRIM-KV利用冻结大语言模型的蒸馏结果结合容量损失实现高效训练，仅需对保留门进行微调，新增的推理开销可忽略不计。在数学推理（GSM8K、MATH-500、AIME24）、程序生成（LongProc）以及对话式长记忆基准（LongMemEval）测试中，TRIM-KV的表现始终优于性能强劲的驱逐基线与可学习检索基线，在低内存配置下优势尤为显著。值得注意的是，它在部分场景下的性能甚至超过全缓存模型，这表明选择性保留可作为一种正则化手段，抑制无信息词元带来的噪声。定性分析进一步显示，学习得到的保留分数符合人类直觉，无需显式设计即可自然实现下沉词元、滑动窗口、主旨压缩等启发式策略。除提升效率外，保留分数还能揭示不同层与注意力头的特定作用，为大语言模型可解释性研究开辟了新的路径。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=24；命中=kv cache, cache eviction, quantization, distillation, compression, offloading, inference, generation

title: EPIC: Efficient Position-Independent Caching for Serving Large Language Models
abstract:
Large Language Models (LLMs) show great capabilities in a wide range of applications, but serving them efficiently becomes increasingly challenging as requests (prompts) become more complex. Context caching improves serving performance by reusing Key-Value (KV) vectors, the intermediate representations of tokens that are repeated across requests. However, existing context caching requires exact prefix matches across requests, limiting reuse cases in settings such as few-shot learning and retrieval-augmented generation, where immutable content (e.g., documents) remains unchanged across requests but is preceded by varying prefixes. Position-Independent
Caching (PIC) addresses this issue by enabling modular reuse of the KV vectors regardless of prefixes. We formalize PIC and advance prior work by introducing EPIC, a serving system incorporating our new LegoLink algorithm, which mitigates the inappropriate “attention sink” effect at every document beginning, to maintain accuracy with minimal computation. Experiments show that EPIC achieves up to 8× improvements in Time-To-First-Token (TTFT) and 7× throughput gains over existing systems, with negligible or no accuracy loss.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）在广泛的应用场景中展现出了极强的能力，但随着请求（提示词）日趋复杂，高效提供大语言模型服务的挑战也不断加剧。上下文缓存通过复用键值（KV）向量提升服务性能，这类向量是在不同请求间重复出现的词元的中间表征。然而现有上下文缓存要求不同请求间存在完全匹配的前缀，这限制了其在少样本学习、检索增强生成等场景下的复用空间，这类场景中不可变内容（例如文档）在不同请求间保持不变，但前面会带有各不相同的前缀。位置无关缓存（PIC）通过支持KV向量的模块化复用、无需考虑前缀差异，从而解决了这一问题。我们对位置无关缓存进行了形式化定义，并在现有研究基础上提出了EPIC服务系统，该系统集成了我们新提出的LegoLink算法，可缓解每个文档开头处存在的不合理“注意力沉降”效应，从而以极低的计算开销维持模型精度。实验表明，与现有系统相比，EPIC的首次令牌生成时间（TTFT）最多可提升8倍，吞吐量最多提升7倍，且精度损失可忽略甚至不存在。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=21；命中=kv cache, throughput, serving, sparsity, generation, language model, large language model, llm

title: TINY BUT MIGHTY: A SOFTWARE-HARDWARE CO- DESIGN APPROACH FOR EFFICIENT MULTIMODAL IN- FERENCE ON BATTERY-POWERED SMALL DEVICES
abstract:
Large Multimodal Models (LMMs) are inherently modular, consisting of vision
and audio encoders, projectors, and large language models. Yet, they are almost
always executed monolithically, which underutilizes the heterogeneous accelera-
tors (NPUs, GPUs, DSPs) in modern SoCs and leads to high end-to-end latency.
In this paper, we present NANOMIND, a hardware–software co-design inference
framework for Large Multimodal Models (LMMs) that breaks large models into
modular “bricks” (vision, language, audio, etc.) and maps each to its ideal accelera-
tor. The key insight is that large models can be broken into modular components and
scheduled to run on the most appropriate compute units. It performs module-level
dynamic offloading across accelerators on unified-memory SoCs. By combining
customized hardware design, system-level scheduling, and optimized low-bit com-
putation kernels, we demonstrate our framework with a compact, battery-powered
device capable of running LMMs entirely on-device. This prototype functions as
a self-contained intelligent assistant that requires no network connectivity, while
achieving higher throughput and superior power efficiency under strict resource
constraints. The design further bypasses CPU bottlenecks and reduces redundant
memory usage through token-aware buffer management and module-level coordi-
nation. Our system outperforms existing implementations in resource efficiency,
cutting energy consumption by 42.3% and GPU memory usage by 11.2%. This
enables a battery-powered device to run LlaVA-OneVision-qwen2-05B with a
camera for nearly 20.8 hours.
abstract_zh: 大型多模态模型（LMM）本质上具备模块化属性，由视觉编码器、音频编码器、投影器及大语言模型组成。但这类模型几乎始终以整体形式执行，使得现代片上系统（SoC）内置的异构加速器（神经网络处理器、图形处理器、数字信号处理器）未得到充分利用，进而导致端到端延迟偏高。本文提出NANOMIND，一款面向大型多模态模型的软硬件协同设计推理框架，该框架将大模型拆分为模块化“构件”（视觉、语言、音频等），并将每个构件映射至最适配的加速器上。该框架的核心思路是，大模型可被拆解为多个模块化组件，可调度这些组件在最合适的计算单元上运行。它在搭载统一内存的片上系统的各加速器之间执行模块级动态卸载。通过结合定制化硬件设计、系统级调度以及优化后的低比特计算内核，我们在一款紧凑型电池供电设备上验证了该框架的效果，该设备可完全在端侧运行大型多模态模型。该原型设备可作为无需网络连接的独立智能助手，在严格的资源约束下实现了更高的吞吐量和更优异的能效。该设计还通过令牌感知缓冲区管理和模块级协调规避了CPU瓶颈，减少了冗余内存占用。我们的系统在资源效率上优于现有实现方案，能耗降低42.3%，GPU内存占用降低11.2%。这使得搭载摄像头的电池供电设备运行LlaVA-OneVision-qwen2-05B模型的时长可达近20.8小时。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=27；命中=throughput, latency, kernel, quantization, low-bit, offloading, inference, language model

title: ICaRus: Identical Cache Reuse for Efficient Multi-Model Inference
abstract:
Multi model inference, where multiple task-specialized models collaborate to solve complex real-world problems, has recently emerged as a prominent paradigm, particularly in the development of agentic AI systems. However, in such scenarios, each model must maintain its own Key-Value (KV) cache for the identical prompt, leading to explosive memory consumption.
This explosive growth of KV caches forces LLM serving systems to evict previously stored caches, which in turn introduces significant recomputation overhead whenever the evicted caches are required again. 
Moreover, prefix caching is inherently infeasible across different models, forcing each model to recompute KV cache for the identical prompt, which leads to signficant overhead. To alleviate these issues, we propose Identical Cache Reuse (ICaRus), a novel architecture that allows multiple models to share identical KV caches across all layers. ICaRus is based on the key observation that a decoder-only Transformer can be conceptually decomposed into a logical encoder, which generates KV caches, and a logical decoder, which predicts output tokens from the KV caches. ICaRus fine-tunes only the logical decoder while freezing the logical encoder, enabling multiple models to share an identical KV cache. This eliminates cache memory explosion and unexpected evictions while also allowing cross-model reuse of KV caches for new input tokens, thereby removing redundant recomputation in multi model inference achieving both efficiency and scalability. Moreover, by incorporating lightweight adapters such as LoRA, ICaRus parallelizes KV cache generation and next-token prediction during decoding. ICaRus achieves comparable accuracy to task-specific fine-tuned model across a diverse set of tasks, while allowing multiple specialized models to fully share KV caches. ICaRus achieves up to $11.1\times$ lower P95 latency and $3.8\times$ higher throughput in multi agent scenarios with 8 different models, compared to prior multi model system.
abstract_zh: 多模型推理是指多个任务专用模型协作解决复杂现实问题的推理模式，近年来已成为主流范式，在智能体AI系统开发中尤为突出。但在这类场景中，每个模型都需要为相同的提示词维护各自的键值（KV）缓存，导致内存消耗激增。KV缓存的爆炸式增长会迫使大语言模型服务系统驱逐此前存储的缓存，后续如果再次需要被驱逐的缓存，又会产生极高的重计算开销。此外，前缀缓存本质上无法跨不同模型使用，导致每个模型都需要为相同的提示词重新计算KV缓存，带来显著开销。为缓解这些问题，我们提出相同缓存复用机制ICaRus，这是一种支持多个模型跨所有层共享相同KV缓存的新型架构。ICaRus的设计基于一项关键观察：仅解码器架构的Transformer在概念上可被拆解为两个部分：生成KV缓存的逻辑编码器，以及根据KV缓存预测输出令牌的逻辑解码器。ICaRus仅微调逻辑解码器，同时冻结逻辑编码器，从而让多个模型可以共享相同的KV缓存。这种设计消除了缓存内存爆炸和意外缓存驱逐问题，还支持新输入令牌的KV缓存跨模型复用，从而消除多模型推理中的冗余重计算，同时实现高效性与可扩展性。此外，通过整合LoRA等轻量适配器，ICaRus在解码阶段实现了KV缓存生成与下一令牌预测的并行处理。在各类不同任务中，ICaRus的准确度与任务专用微调模型相当，同时支持多个专用模型完全共享KV缓存。与此前的多模型系统相比，在包含8个不同模型的多智能体场景中，ICaRus的P95延迟最高降低11.1倍，吞吐量最高提升3.8倍。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=21；命中=kv cache, throughput, latency, serving, inference, decode, decoding, generation
final_primary_research_object: AI 系统 / 基础设施

title: Can Compressed LLMs Truly Act? An Empirical Evaluation of Agentic Capabilities in LLM Compression
abstract: Post-training compression reduces the computational and memory costs of large language models (LLMs), enabling resource-efficient deployment. However, existing compression benchmarks focus narrowly on language modeling (e.g., perplexity) and natural language understanding tasks (e.g., GLUE accuracy), ignoring the agentic capabilities—workflow, tool use/function call, long-context understanding and real-world application. We introduce the Agent Compression Benchmark (ACBench), the first comprehensive benchmark for evaluating how compression impacts LLMs' agentic abilities. ACBench spans (1) 12 tasks across 4 capabilities (e.g., WorfBench for workflow generation, Needle-in-Haystack for long-context retrieval), (2) 4-bit quantization (GPTQ, AWQ) and 50% pruning (Wanda, SparseGPT), and (3) 15 models, including small (Gemma-2B), standard (Qwen2.5-7B), and distilled reasoning LLMs (DeepSeek-R1-Distill). Our experiments reveal compression tradeoffs: 4-bit quantization preserves workflow generation and tool use (1%--3% drop) but degrades real-world application accuracy by 10%--15%. We introduce ERank, Top-k Ranking Correlation and Energy to systematize analysis. ACBench provides actionable insights for optimizing LLM compression in agentic scenarios, bridging the gap between algorithmic efficiency and real-world applicability.
abstract_zh: 训练后压缩可降低大语言模型的计算与内存成本，实现资源高效部署。但现有压缩基准仅片面关注语言建模（如困惑度）与自然语言理解任务（如GLUE准确率），忽略了智能体能力，包括工作流、工具使用/函数调用、长上下文理解与现实场景应用。我们提出了智能体压缩基准（ACBench），这是首个用于评估压缩对大语言模型智能体能力影响的综合基准。ACBench涵盖三类内容：一是跨4类能力的12项任务，如用于工作流生成的WorfBench、用于长上下文检索的“大海捞针”测试；二是4位量化（GPTQ、AWQ）与50%剪枝（Wanda、SparseGPT）两类压缩技术；三是15款模型，包括小尺寸模型（Gemma-2B）、标准尺寸模型（Qwen2.5-7B）以及蒸馏推理大语言模型（DeepSeek-R1-Distill）。我们的实验揭示了压缩的权衡效应：4位量化可保留工作流生成与工具使用能力，性能仅下降1%至3%，但会使现实场景应用准确率下降10%至15%。我们引入了ERank、Top-k排序相关性与能量指标来实现分析的系统化。ACBench为优化智能体场景下的大语言模型压缩技术提供了可操作的见解，填补了算法效率与现实适用性之间的空白。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=22；命中=gemm, quantization, 4-bit, pruning, compression, compressed, generation, language model

title: Spotlight Attention: Towards Efficient LLM Generation via Non-linear Hashing-based KV Cache Retrieval
abstract: Reducing the key-value (KV) cache burden in Large Language Models (LLMs) significantly accelerates inference. Dynamically selecting critical KV caches during decoding helps maintain performance. Existing methods use random linear hashing to identify important tokens, but this approach is inefficient due to the orthogonal distribution of queries and keys within two narrow cones in LLMs. We introduce Spotlight Attention, a novel method that employs non-linear hashing functions to optimize the embedding distribution of queries and keys, enhancing coding efficiency and robustness. We also developed a lightweight, stable training framework using a Bradley-Terry ranking-based loss, enabling optimization of the non-linear hashing module on GPUs with 16GB memory in 8 hours. Experimental results show that Spotlight Attention drastically improves retrieval precision while shortening the length of the hash code at least 5$\times$ compared to traditional linear hashing. Finally, we exploit the computational advantages of bitwise operations by implementing specialized CUDA kernels, achieving hashing retrieval for 512K tokens in under 100$\mu$s on a single A100 GPU, with end-to-end throughput up to 3$\times$ higher than vanilla decoding.
abstract_zh: 降低大语言模型的键值缓存负担可显著加快推理速度。解码过程中动态选择关键键值缓存有助于维持模型性能。现有方法采用随机线性哈希识别重要词元，但由于大语言模型中的查询和键正交分布在两个窄锥体内，该方法效率较低。本文提出聚光注意力这一全新方法，该方法采用非线性哈希函数优化查询与键的嵌入分布，提升编码效率与鲁棒性。我们还基于布拉德利-特里排序损失开发了一套轻量、稳定的训练框架，仅需8小时即可在显存为16GB的GPU上完成非线性哈希模块的优化。实验结果表明，与传统线性哈希相比，聚光注意力大幅提升了检索精度，同时哈希码长度至少压缩5倍。最后，我们通过实现专用CUDA内核发挥按位运算的计算优势，在单张A100 GPU上完成512K词元的哈希检索耗时不足100微秒，端到端吞吐量最高可达原始解码方法的3倍。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=25；命中=kv cache, cache compression, throughput, kernel, cuda, compression, inference, decoding

title: CommVQ: Commutative Vector Quantization for KV Cache Compression
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly used in applications requiring long context lengths, but the key-value (KV) cache often becomes a memory bottleneck on GPUs as context grows. To address this, we propose Commutative Vector Quantization (CommVQ) to significantly reduce memory usage for long-context LLM inference. We first introduce additive quantization with a lightweight encoder and codebook to compress the KV cache, which can be decoded via simple matrix multiplication. To further reduce computational costs during decoding, we design the codebook to be commutative with Rotary Position Embedding (RoPE) and train it using an Expectation-Maximization (EM) algorithm. This enables efficient integration of decoding into the self-attention mechanism. Our approach achieves high accuracy with additive quantization and low overhead via the RoPE-commutative codebook. Experiments on long-context benchmarks and GSM8K show that our method reduces FP16 KV cache size by 87.5% with 2-bit quantization, while outperforming state-of-the-art KV cache quantization methods. Notably, it enables 1-bit KV cache quantization with minimal accuracy loss, allowing a LLaMA-3.1 8B model to run with a 128K context length on a single RTX 4090 GPU. The source code is available at: https://github.com/UMass-Embodied-AGI/CommVQ.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）正越来越多地被用于要求长上下文长度的应用中，但随着上下文长度增长，键值（KV）缓存往往会成为GPU上的内存瓶颈。为解决该问题，我们提出可交换向量量化（CommVQ），以大幅降低长上下文大语言模型推理的内存占用。我们首先引入搭配轻量编码器与码本的加性量化来压缩KV缓存，该量化方式可通过简单的矩阵乘法完成解码。为进一步降低解码过程中的计算成本，我们将码本设计为与旋转位置嵌入（RoPE）可交换，并使用期望最大化（EM）算法对其进行训练。这使得解码过程能够高效集成到自注意力机制中。我们的方法通过加性量化实现了高准确率，同时借助RoPE可交换码本实现了低开销。在长上下文基准测试和GSM8K上的实验表明，我们的方法通过2比特量化将FP16精度的KV缓存体积减小了87.5%，同时性能优于当前最优的KV缓存量化方法。值得注意的是，该方法可实现准确率损失极小的1比特KV缓存量化，能够让LLaMA-3.1 8B模型在单张RTX 4090 GPU上以128K的上下文长度运行。源代码可从以下地址获取：https://github.com/UMass-Embodied-AGI/CommVQ。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=28；命中=kv cache, cache compression, long context, quantization, 2-bit, compression, inference, decode

title: iLLaVA: An Image is Worth Fewer Than 1/3 Input Tokens in Large Multimodal Models
abstract: Recent methods have made notable progress in accelerating Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) by exploiting the inherent redundancy in visual inputs. Most existing approaches, however, focus narrowly on reducing image tokens before or within the Large Language Model (LLM) stage to lower computational cost. This overlooks other major bottlenecks, particularly the image encoder, which itself requires substantial computation. As a result, these methods fall short of achieving true end-to-end acceleration. Importantly, the image encoder is the primary contributor of input tokens to the LLM. Thus, reducing visual redundancy at the encoder stage not only speeds up the encoder itself but also significantly lightens the workload for the subsequent LLM. Motivated by this, we investigate how to jointly optimize the image encoder and the LLM along with other LVLM components for comprehensive acceleration. To mitigate the risk of performance degradation from token reduction, we propose a novel token merging strategy that recycles useful information from otherwise discarded tokens. Our approach, iLLaVA, delivers consistent improvements across both image and video understanding tasks, achieving up to a 2× throughput boost and a 4× reduction in prefilling time. Notably, iLLaVA enables a larger model (e.g., InternVL-2.5 26B) to surpass a smaller counterpart (e.g., InternVL-2.5 8B) in both accuracy and efficiency. Extensive comparisons with state-of-the-art token pruning and merging techniques demonstrate the clear superiority of our method. Finally, we provide detailed visualizations for the merging steps of iLLaVA , offering deeper insights into how different LVLM components contribute to efficient computation.
abstract_zh: 近年的方法通过挖掘视觉输入的固有冗余性，在加速大视觉语言模型（LVLM）方面取得了显著进展。然而现有大多数方法仅聚焦于在大语言模型（LLM）阶段之前或阶段内部减少图像令牌以降低计算成本，这忽略了其他主要瓶颈，尤其是本身需要大量计算的图像编码器，因此这类方法无法实现真正的端到端加速。重要的是，图像编码器是大语言模型输入令牌的主要来源，因此在编码器阶段减少视觉冗余不仅能提升编码器本身的运行速度，还能大幅减轻后续大语言模型的工作负载。受此启发，我们研究了如何联合优化图像编码器、大语言模型及其他大视觉语言模型组件以实现全面加速。为了降低令牌减少导致的性能下降风险，我们提出了一种全新的令牌合并策略，该策略可回收原本会被丢弃的令牌中的有用信息。我们提出的iLLaVA方法在图像和视频理解任务上均实现了稳定的性能提升，吞吐量最高可提升2倍，预填充时间最多可缩短至原来的1/4。值得注意的是，iLLaVA可让更大的模型（例如InternVL-2.5 26B）在准确率和效率两方面均超过更小的同类模型（例如InternVL-2.5 8B）。我们与当前最先进的令牌剪枝、合并技术开展的大量对比实验表明，我们的方法具备明显优势。最后，我们对iLLaVA的合并步骤进行了详细可视化，为深入理解不同大视觉语言模型组件对高效计算的贡献机制提供了支撑。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=20；命中=token pruning, prefill, throughput, pruning, language model, large language model, llm, vision-language

title: any4: Learned 4-bit Numeric Representation for LLMs
abstract: We present any4, a learned 4-bit weight quantization solution for large language models (LLMs) providing arbitrary numeric representations without requiring pre-processing of weights or activations. any4 yields higher accuracy compared to other related 4-bit numeric representation types: int4, fp4 and nf4, as evaluated on a range of model sizes, generations and families (Llama 2, Llama 3, Mistral and Mixtral). While any4 does not require preprocessing of weights or activations, it is also competitive with orthogonal techniques that require such preprocessing (e.g., AWQ and GPTQ). We also experiment with any3 and any2 and show competitiveness at lower bits. Additionally, we show that we can calibrate using a single curated diverse sample rather than hundreds of samples from a dataset as done in most quantization approaches. We also open source tinygemm, a latency optimized GPU matrix multiplication library for LLMs, that implements any4 using a GPU-efficient lookup table strategy along with other common quantization methods. We open source our code at https://github.com/facebookresearch/any4.
abstract_zh: 我们提出any4，这是一种面向大语言模型的可学习4位权重量化方案，无需对权重或激活值进行预处理即可提供任意数值表示。在一系列不同模型尺寸、代际和模型家族（Llama 2、Llama 3、Mistral、Mixtral）上的评估结果显示，与int4、fp4、nf4等其他相关4位数值表示类型相比，any4可实现更高的准确率。尽管any4无需对权重或激活值进行预处理，其性能也可与需要此类预处理的正交技术（例如AWQ和GPTQ）相媲美。我们还对any3和any2开展了实验，证明其在更低比特场景下同样具备竞争力。此外，我们证明仅使用单个经筛选的多样化样本即可完成校准，无需如大多数量化方法那样使用数据集中的数百个样本。我们还开源了tinygemm，这是一款面向大语言模型的经延迟优化的GPU矩阵乘法库，通过GPU高效查找表策略实现了any4，同时也支持其他常用量化方法。我们的代码已在https://github.com/facebookresearch/any4开源。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=21；命中=latency, gemm, quantization, 4-bit, compression, generation, language model, large language model

title: Ada-KV: Optimizing KV Cache Eviction by Adaptive Budget Allocation for Efficient LLM Inference
abstract: Large Language Models have excelled in various domains but face efficiency challenges due to the growing Key-Value (KV) cache required for long-sequence inference. Recent efforts aim to reduce KV cache size by evicting vast non-critical cache elements during runtime while preserving generation quality. However, these methods typically allocate compression budgets uniformly across all attention heads, ignoring the unique attention patterns of each head. In this paper, we establish a theoretical loss upper bound between pre- and post-eviction attention output, explaining the optimization target of prior cache eviction methods, while guiding the optimization of adaptive budget allocation. Base on this, we propose {\it Ada-KV}, the first head-wise adaptive budget allocation strategy. It offers plug-and-play benefits, enabling seamless integration with prior cache eviction methods. Extensive evaluations on 13 datasets from Ruler and 16 datasets from LongBench, all conducted under both question-aware and question-agnostic scenarios, demonstrate substantial quality improvements over existing methods. Our code is available at https://github.com/FFY0/AdaKV.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型在各类领域中表现优异，但长序列推理所需的键值（KV）缓存持续增长，导致其面临效率挑战。现有研究尝试在运行时剔除大量非关键缓存元素以缩减KV缓存规模，同时保障生成质量，但这类方法通常为所有注意力头统一分配压缩预算，忽略了每个注意力头的独特注意力模式。本文构建了缓存剔除前后注意力输出之间的理论损失上界，阐明了现有缓存剔除方法的优化目标，同时为自适应预算分配的优化提供指导。在此基础上，我们提出Ada-KV，这是首个逐头自适应预算分配策略。该策略具备即插即用特性，可与现有缓存剔除方法无缝集成。我们在Ruler的13个数据集和LongBench的16个数据集上开展了大量评估，所有评估均在问题感知和问题无关两种场景下进行，结果表明本方法相比现有方法实现了显著的质量提升。我们的代码可从https://github.com/FFY0/AdaKV获取。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=25；命中=kv cache, cache eviction, serving, runtime, compression, inference, generation, language model

title: Balanced Token Pruning: Accelerating Vision Language Models Beyond Local Optimization
abstract: Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) have shown impressive performance across multi-modal tasks by encoding images into thousands of tokens. However, the large number of image tokens results in significant computational overhead, and the use of dynamic high-resolution inputs further increases this burden. Previous approaches have attempted to reduce the number of image tokens through token pruning, typically by selecting tokens based on attention scores or image token diversity. Through empirical studies, we observe that existing methods often overlook the joint impact of pruning on both the current layer's output (local) and the outputs of subsequent layers (global), leading to suboptimal pruning decisions. To address this challenge, we propose Balanced Token Pruning (BTP), a plug-and-play method for pruning vision tokens. Specifically, our method utilizes a small calibration set to divide the pruning process into multiple stages. In the early stages, our method emphasizes the impact of pruning on subsequent layers, whereas in the deeper stages, the focus shifts toward preserving the consistency of local outputs. Extensive experiments across various LVLMs demonstrate the broad effectiveness of our approach on multiple benchmarks. Our method achieves a 78\% compression rate while preserving 96.7\% of the original models' performance on average. Our code is available at https://github.com/EmbodiedCity/NeurIPS2025-Balanced-Token-Pruning.
abstract_zh: 大型视觉语言模型（LVLM）通过将图像编码为数千个令牌，在各类多模态任务中展现出了出色的性能。然而，数量庞大的图像令牌会产生显著的计算开销，而动态高分辨率输入的使用会进一步加重这一负担。现有方法尝试通过令牌剪枝减少图像令牌的数量，这类方法通常基于注意力得分或图像令牌的多样性来选择要保留的令牌。我们通过实证研究发现，现有方法往往忽略了剪枝对当前层输出（局部）与后续层输出（全局）的共同影响，会产生次优的剪枝决策。为应对这一挑战，我们提出平衡令牌剪枝（BTP），这是一种即插即用的视觉令牌剪枝方法。具体而言，我们的方法利用小型校准集将剪枝过程划分为多个阶段。在早期阶段，我们的方法侧重考量剪枝对后续层的影响，而在更深的阶段，方法的重心则转向维持局部输出的一致性。我们在多种大型视觉语言模型上开展了大量实验，结果表明我们的方法在多个基准测试集上具备广泛的有效性。我们的方法实现了78%的压缩率，同时平均保留了原始模型96.7%的性能。我们的代码可从https://github.com/EmbodiedCity/NeurIPS2025-Balanced-Token-Pruning获取。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=20；命中=token pruning, serving, pruning, compression, language model, vision-language, vlm

title: SeedLM: Compressing LLM Weights into Seeds of Pseudo-Random Generators
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have transformed natural language processing, but face significant challenges in widespread deployment due to their high runtime cost. In this paper, we introduce SeedLM, a novel post-training compression method that uses seeds of a pseudo-random generator to encode and compress model weights. Specifically, for each block of weights, we find a seed that is fed into a Linear Feedback Shift Register (LFSR) during inference to efficiently generate a random matrix. This matrix is then linearly combined with compressed coefficients to reconstruct the weight block. SeedLM reduces memory access and leverages idle compute cycles during inference, effectively speeding up memory-bound tasks by trading compute for fewer memory accesses. Unlike state-of-the-art methods that rely on calibration data, our approach is data-free and generalizes well across diverse tasks. Our experiments with Llama3 70B, which is particularly challenging, show zero-shot accuracy retention at 4- and 3-bit compression to be on par with or better than state-of-the-art methods, while maintaining performance comparable to FP16 baselines. Additionally, FPGA-based tests demonstrate that 4-bit SeedLM, as model size increases, approaches a 4x speed-up over an FP16 Llama 2/3 baseline.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）已为自然语言处理领域带来变革，但极高的运行时成本使其大规模部署面临显著挑战。本文提出SeedLM，这是一种全新的训练后压缩方法，利用伪随机生成器的种子对模型权重进行编码和压缩。具体而言，针对每个权重块，我们会找到对应的种子，推理阶段将该种子输入线性反馈移位寄存器（LFSR）即可高效生成随机矩阵，随后将该矩阵与压缩后的系数线性组合，以重构权重块。SeedLM减少了内存访问量，还可利用推理过程中的闲置计算周期，通过以计算换取更少内存访问的方式，有效提升内存受限任务的运行速度。与依赖校准数据的当前最优方法不同，我们的方法无需数据，且在各类不同任务中均具备良好的泛化性。我们针对难度极高的Llama3 70B模型开展的实验显示，在4位和3位压缩场景下，SeedLM的零样本准确率保留率与当前最优方法相当甚至更优，同时性能可与FP16基线水平相媲美。此外，基于现场可编程门阵列（FPGA）的测试表明，随着模型规模增大，4位精度的SeedLM相对FP16精度的Llama 2/3基线的提速倍数接近4倍。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=23；命中=runtime, quantization, 4-bit, 3-bit, compression, compressed, inference, language model

title: CateKV: On Sequential Consistency for Long-Context LLM Inference Acceleration
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated strong capabilities in handling long-context tasks, but processing such long contexts remains challenging due to the substantial memory requirements and inference latency. In this work, we discover that certain attention heads exhibit sequential consistency in their attention patterns, which can be persistently identified using a coefficient-of-variation-based algorithm. Inspired by this observation, we propose CateKV, a hybrid KV cache method that retains only critical token information for consistent heads, thereby reducing KV cache size and computational overhead, while preserving the majority of KV pairs in adaptive heads to ensure high accuracy. We show the unique characteristics of our algorithm and its extension with existing acceleration methods. Comprehensive evaluations on long-context benchmarks show that, while maintaining accuracy comparable to full attention, CateKV reduces memory usage by up to $2.72\times$ and accelerates decoding by $2.18\times$ in single-sample inputs, and boosts throughput by $3.96\times$ in batch scenarios.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）在处理长上下文任务方面已展现出强大能力，但极高的内存需求与推理延迟使得处理此类长上下文仍面临挑战。本研究发现，部分注意力头的注意力模式存在序列一致性，可通过基于变异系数的算法持续识别这类注意力头。受这一观测结果启发，我们提出混合KV缓存方法CateKV，该方法仅为一致性头保留关键令牌信息，从而缩减KV缓存规模、降低计算开销，同时为自适应头保留绝大多数KV对以确保高精度。我们阐释了本算法的独有特性，以及其与现有加速方法结合的扩展方式。针对长上下文基准的综合评估显示，CateKV在保持与全注意力相当的精度的同时，针对单样本输入可将内存占用最高降低2.72倍、解码速度提升2.18倍，在批量场景下可将吞吐量提升3.96倍。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=25；命中=kv cache, long context, throughput, latency, serving, inference, decoding, language model

title: FlatQuant: Flatness Matters for LLM Quantization
abstract: Recently, quantization has been widely used for the compression and acceleration of large language models (LLMs). Due to the outliers in LLMs, it is crucial to flatten weights and activations to minimize quantization error with equally spaced quantization points. Prior research explores various pre-quantization transformations to suppress outliers, such as per-channel scaling and Hadamard transformation. However, we observe that these transformed weights and activations can still exhibit steep and dispersed distributions. In this paper, we propose FlatQuant (Fast and Learnable Affine Transformation), a new post-training quantization approach that enhances the flatness of weights and activations. Our approach identifies optimal affine transformations for each linear layer, calibrated in hours via a lightweight objective. To reduce runtime overhead of affine transformation, we apply Kronecker product with two lightweight matrices, and fuse all operations in FlatQuant into a single kernel. Extensive experiments demonstrate that FlatQuant establishes a new state-of-the-art benchmark for quantization. For example, it achieves less than 1\% accuracy drop for W4A4 quantization on the LLaMA-3-70B model, surpassing SpinQuant by 7.5\%. Additionally, it provides up to 2.3x prefill speedup and 1.7x decoding speedup compared to the FP16 model. Code is available at: https://github.com/ruikangliu/FlatQuant.
abstract_zh: 近年来，量化技术已被广泛应用于大语言模型的压缩与加速。由于大语言模型中存在离群值，为使用等距量化点最小化量化误差，对权重和激活值进行平坦化处理至关重要。现有研究探索了逐通道缩放、哈达玛变换等多种量化前变换来抑制离群值，但我们观察到，经这些变换处理后的权重与激活值仍可能呈现陡峭且分散的分布。本文提出FlatQuant（快速可学习仿射变换），这是一种新型训练后量化方法，可提升权重与激活值的平坦度。该方法为每个线性层确定最优仿射变换，通过轻量级目标函数仅需数小时即可完成校准。为降低仿射变换的运行时开销，我们将其与两个轻量级矩阵做克罗内克积运算，且将FlatQuant中的所有运算融合到单个内核中。大量实验表明，FlatQuant创下了量化领域新的最优基准。例如，在LLaMA-3-70B模型上开展W4A4量化时，其精度下降不足1%，性能较SpinQuant高出7.5%。此外，与FP16模型相比，它可实现最高2.3倍的预填充速度提升与1.7倍的解码速度提升。代码可在https://github.com/ruikangliu/FlatQuant获取。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=20；命中=prefill, runtime, kernel, quantization, compression, decoding, language model, large language model

title: OmniKV: Dynamic Context Selection for Efficient Long-Context LLMs
abstract: During the inference phase of Large Language Models (LLMs) with long context, a substantial portion of GPU memory is allocated to the KV cache, with memory usage increasing as the sequence length grows. To mitigate the GPU memory footprint associate with KV cache, some previous studies have discarded less important tokens based on the sparsity identified in attention scores in long context scenarios. However, we argue that attention scores cannot indicate the future importance of tokens in subsequent generation iterations, because attention scores are calculated based on current hidden states. Therefore, we propose OmniKV, a token-dropping-free and training-free inference method, which achieves a 1.68x speedup without any loss in performance. It is well-suited for offloading, significantly reducing KV cache memory usage by up to 75% with it. The core innovative insight of OmniKV is: Within a single generation iteration, there is a high degree of similarity in the important tokens identified across consecutive layers. Extensive experiments demonstrate that OmniKV achieves state-of-the-art performance across multiple benchmarks, with particularly advantages in chain-of-thoughts scenarios. OmniKV extends the maximum context length supported by a single A100 for Llama-3-8B from 128K to 450K. Our code is available at https://github.com/antgroup/OmniKV.git.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）在长上下文场景下的推理阶段中，GPU内存的很大一部分会被分配给KV缓存，且内存占用会随序列长度的增长而上升。为了降低KV缓存带来的GPU内存开销，现有部分研究基于长上下文场景下注意力得分中呈现的稀疏性，丢弃重要性较低的令牌。但我们认为，注意力得分无法反映令牌在后续生成迭代中的未来重要性，因为注意力得分是基于当前隐状态计算得到的。为此我们提出OmniKV，这是一种无需丢弃令牌、无需训练的推理方法，可在无任何性能损失的前提下实现1.68倍加速。该方法非常适配卸载机制，与之结合可将KV缓存的内存占用最高降低75%。OmniKV的核心创新洞见为：在单次生成迭代内，连续层识别出的重要令牌存在高度相似性。大量实验证明，OmniKV在多个基准测试上取得了当前最优性能，在思维链场景下优势尤为突出。OmniKV将单张A100显卡支持的Llama-3-8B模型最大上下文长度从128K提升至450K。我们的代码可从https://github.com/antgroup/OmniKV.git获取。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=18；命中=kv cache, long context, sparsity, offloading, inference, generation, language model, large language model

title: DTP: Delta-Guided Two Stage Pruning for Mamba-based Multimodal Large Language Models
abstract: Multimodal large language models built on the Mamba architecture offer efficiency advantages, yet remain hampered by redundant visual tokens that inflate inference cost, with the prefill stage accounting for the majority of total inference time. We introduce Delta-guided Two stage Pruning (DTP), a method that progressively reduces token redundancy through selective pruning at early layer and complete pruning at late layer. Unlike Transformer-oriented pruning methods, our approach derives token importance directly from Mamba’s internal parameters. The statistical distribution of these importance scores, combined with implicit attention patterns, then provides the basis for determining both the pruning layers and the tokens to be removed. Extensive evaluation across diverse benchmarks shows that DTP cuts computation by nearly 50\%, maintains higher task performance than existing pruning methods, and further achieves over a 35\% reduction in prefill latency. Beyond efficiency, our analysis reveals previously underexplored behaviors of visual tokens within Mamba layers, suggesting a principled perspective for designing future pruning techniques in Mamba-based Multimodal Large Language Models.
abstract_zh: 基于Mamba架构构建的多模态大语言模型具备效率优势，但仍受冗余视觉令牌的困扰，这类冗余令牌会推高推理成本，其中预填充阶段占据总推理时间的绝大部分。我们提出德尔塔引导的两阶段剪枝（DTP）方法，该方法通过在早期层进行选择性剪枝、在后期层进行完全剪枝，逐步降低令牌冗余度。与面向Transformer的剪枝方法不同，我们的方法直接从Mamba的内部参数推导令牌重要性。这些重要性得分的统计分布结合隐式注意力模式，为确定剪枝层及待移除令牌提供了依据。在各类基准上开展的广泛评估表明，DTP将计算量削减近50%，相比现有剪枝方法可维持更高的任务性能，还能实现预填充延迟降低35%以上。除效率提升外，我们的分析还揭示了Mamba层内视觉令牌此前未被充分研究的行为，为未来设计基于Mamba的多模态大语言模型剪枝技术提供了原则性视角。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=22；命中=token pruning, prefill, latency, pruning, inference, language model, large language model, llm

title: SuffixDecoding: Extreme Speculative Decoding for Emerging AI Applications
abstract: Speculative decoding is widely adopted to reduce latency in large language model (LLM) inference by leveraging smaller draft models capable of handling diverse user tasks. However, emerging AI applications, such as LLM-based agents, present unique workload characteristics: instead of diverse independent requests, agentic frameworks typically submit repetitive inference requests, such as multi-agent pipelines performing similar subtasks or self-refinement loops iteratively enhancing outputs. These workloads result in long and highly predictable sequences, which current speculative decoding methods do not effectively exploit. To address this gap, we introduce \emph{SuffixDecoding}, a novel method that utilizes efficient suffix trees to cache long token sequences from prompts and previous outputs. By adaptively speculating more tokens when acceptance likelihood is high and fewer when it is low, SuffixDecoding effectively exploits opportunities for longer speculations while conserving computation when those opportunities are limited. Evaluations on agentic benchmarks, including SWE-Bench and Text-to-SQL, demonstrate that SuffixDecoding achieves speedups of up to 3.9$\times$, outperforming state-of-the-art methods -- 2.2$\times$ faster than model-based approaches like EAGLE-2/3 and 1.6$\times$ faster than model-free approaches such as Token Recycling. SuffixDecoding is open-sourced.
abstract_zh: 推测解码通过利用可处理各类用户任务的小型草稿模型，被广泛应用于降低大语言模型（LLM）推理时延。然而，基于大语言模型的智能体等新兴人工智能应用呈现出独特的工作负载特征：智能体框架通常提交重复的推理请求，而非多样的独立请求，例如执行相似子任务的多智能体流水线，或是迭代优化输出的自精炼循环。这类工作负载会生成长度较长、可预测性极高的序列，现有推测解码方法无法有效利用这一特性。为填补这一空白，我们提出后缀解码（SuffixDecoding）这一新型方法，它利用高效后缀树缓存来自提示词和过往输出的长令牌序列。通过在接受概率较高时自适应推测更多令牌，在接受概率较低时减少推测令牌数量，后缀解码可有效利用长推测的可行机会，同时在这类机会有限时节省计算资源。在SWE-Bench、文本转SQL等智能体基准测试上的评估结果表明，后缀解码实现了最高3.9倍的加速，性能优于现有最优方法：其速度比EAGLE-2/3等基于模型的方法快2.2倍，比令牌回收等无模型方法快1.6倍。后缀解码已开源。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=23；命中=speculative decoding, draft model, latency, serving, inference, decoding, language model, large language model

title: SVDQuant: Absorbing Outliers by Low-Rank Component for 4-Bit Diffusion Models
abstract: Diffusion models can effectively generate high-quality images. However, as they scale,  rising memory demands and higher latency pose substantial deployment challenges. In this work, we aim to accelerate diffusion models by quantizing their weights and activations to 4 bits. At such an aggressive level, both weights and activations are highly sensitive, where existing post-training quantization methods like smoothing become insufficient. To overcome this limitation, we propose *SVDQuant*, a new 4-bit quantization paradigm. Different from smoothing, which redistributes outliers between weights and activations, our approach *absorbs* these outliers using a low-rank branch. We first consolidate the outliers by shifting them from activations to weights. Then, we use a high-precision, low-rank branch to take in the weight outliers with Singular Value Decomposition (SVD), while a low-bit quantized branch handles the residuals. This process eases the quantization on both sides. However, naively running the low-rank branch independently incurs significant overhead due to extra data movement of activations, negating the quantization speedup. To address this, we co-design an inference engine *Nunchaku* that fuses the kernels of the low-rank branch into those of the low-bit branch to cut off redundant memory access. It can also seamlessly support off-the-shelf low-rank adapters (LoRAs) without re-quantization. Extensive experiments on SDXL, PixArt-$\Sigma$, and FLUX.1 validate the effectiveness of SVDQuant in preserving image quality. We reduce the memory usage for the 12B FLUX.1 models by 3.5×, achieving 3.0× speedup over the 4-bit weight-only quantization (W4A16) baseline on the 16GB laptop 4090 GPU with INT4 precision. On the latest RTX 5090 desktop with Blackwell architecture, we achieve a 3.1× speedup compared to the W4A16 model using NVFP4 precision. Our quantization library and inference engine are available at https://github.com/mit-han-lab/deepcompressor/ and https://github.com/mit-han-lab/nunchaku/, correspondingly.
abstract_zh: 扩散模型可有效生成高质量图像，但随着规模扩张，内存需求不断上升、延迟持续升高，给实际部署带来了严峻挑战。本研究旨在通过将扩散模型的权重与激活值量化为4比特，实现模型加速。在如此激进的量化精度下，权重与激活值对量化误差都高度敏感，现有训练后量化方法（如平滑量化）已不足以实现理想效果。为克服这一局限，我们提出SVDQuant这一全新的4比特量化范式。平滑量化的思路是在权重与激活值之间重新分配离群值，而我们的方法与之不同，通过一个低秩分支吸收这些离群值。我们首先将离群值从激活值转移到权重中完成整合，随后借助奇异值分解（SVD），用一个高精度低秩分支承接权重中的离群值，同时用一个低比特量化分支处理残差，这一过程可降低两端的量化难度。但直接独立运行低秩分支会因激活值的额外数据移动产生显著开销，抵消量化带来的速度提升。为解决这一问题，我们联合设计了名为Nunchaku的推理引擎，可将低秩分支的内核融合到低比特分支的内核中，消除冗余内存访问。该引擎还可无缝支持现成的低秩适配器（LoRA），无需重新量化。我们在SDXL、PixArt-Σ与FLUX.1上开展了大量实验，验证了SVDQuant在保留图像质量方面的有效性。针对12B参数的FLUX.1模型，我们将内存占用降低了3.5倍，在搭载16GB显存的笔记本端4090 GPU上，以INT4精度运行时，速度相比4比特仅权重量化（W4A16）基线提升3.0倍。在采用Blackwell架构的最新RTX 5090桌面端GPU上，以NVFP4精度运行时，速度相比W4A16模型提升3.1倍。我们的量化库与推理引擎已分别开源至https://github.com/mit-han-lab/deepcompressor/与https://github.com/mit-han-lab/nunchaku/。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=22；命中=latency, serving, kernel, quantization, quantized, 4-bit, low-bit, inference

title: KBVQ-MoE: KLT-guided SVD with Bias-Corrected Vector Quantization for MoE Large Language Models
abstract:
Mixture of Experts (MoE) models have achieved great success by significantly improving performance while maintaining computational efficiency through sparse expert activation. However, their enormous parameter sizes and memory demands pose significant challenges for deployment in resource-constrained environments.
Vector Quantization (VQ) offers a promising approach for ultra-low-bit compression in Large Language Models (LLMs) by constructing and leveraging a codebook—where weight vectors are mapped to the most similar discrete codewords within the codebook. 
However, its direct application to MoEs suffers from significant performance degradation caused by two critical obstacles:  (1) redundant representation among experts leads to VQ repeatedly quantizing similar representations for each expert, resulting in inefficient utilization of the limited codebook capacity; and (2) cumulative outputs bias, amplified by expert aggregation, leads to distributional shifts in the quantized outputs, resulting in degraded model accuracy.
To this end, we propose KBVQ-MoE, a novel VQ framework to enhance extremely low-bit quantization for MoE-based LLMs. 
KBVQ-MoE introduces two lightweight and offline techniques that introduce negligible runtime computational and memory overhead:
(1) Input-driven redundancy elimination, where a Karhunen–Loève Transform (KLT) guided singular value decomposition (SVD) extracts and shares dominant weight components across experts. 
(2) Bias-corrected output stabilization, where vector quantization is applied to expert-specific (i.e., non-redundant) representations and the quantized outputs are corrected with channel-wise affine compensation.
Experiments on various MoE LLMs demonstrate that KBVQ-MoE preserves accuracy substantially better than existing quantization methods.
