For instance, 3-bit quantization of Qwen1.5-MoE-A2.7B achieves an average accuracy of 67.99, nearly identical to the FP16 baseline of 68.07, underscoring the potential of KBVQ-MoE for efficient deployment on edge devices and other resource-constrained platforms.
abstract_zh:
混合专家（MoE）模型通过稀疏专家激活机制在大幅提升性能的同时保持了计算效率，目前已取得巨大成功。但这类模型的参数量与内存需求极高，给资源受限环境下的部署带来了严峻挑战。
向量量化（VQ）通过构建并利用码本——权重向量会被映射到码本中最相似的离散码字——为大语言模型（LLM）的超低比特压缩提供了极具前景的实现路径。
但将向量量化直接应用于混合专家模型时会出现性能大幅下降的问题，这一问题由两个核心障碍导致：（1）专家之间存在冗余表示，使得向量量化对每个专家的相似表示重复量化，造成有限码本容量的利用效率低下；（2）累积输出偏差会被专家聚合过程放大，导致量化后输出出现分布偏移，最终降低模型准确率。
为此，我们提出KBVQ-MoE，这是一种新型向量量化框架，用于优化基于混合专家的大语言模型的极低比特量化效果。
KBVQ-MoE引入了两种轻量离线技术，仅会产生可忽略的运行时计算与内存开销：
（1）输入驱动的冗余消除：采用卡洛南-洛伊变换（KLT）引导的奇异值分解（SVD），提取各专家的主导权重分量并实现跨专家共享。
（2）带偏差校正的输出稳定：对专家专属（即非冗余）表示执行向量量化，再通过逐通道仿射补偿校正量化后的输出。
在多款混合专家大语言模型上开展的实验表明，KBVQ-MoE的精度保留效果远优于现有量化方法。
例如，对Qwen1.5-MoE-A2.7B进行3比特量化后，平均准确率可达67.99，与FP16基线的68.07几乎一致，凸显了KBVQ-MoE在边缘设备及其他资源受限平台上实现高效部署的潜力。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=23；命中=runtime, quantization, quantized, 3-bit, low-bit, compression, language model, large language model

title: Flow Caching for Autoregressive Video Generation
abstract:
Autoregressive models, often built on Transformer architectures, represent a powerful paradigm for generating ultra-long videos by synthesizing content in sequential chunks. However, this sequential generation process is notoriously slow. While caching strategies have proven effective for accelerating traditional video diffusion models, existing methods assume uniform denoising across all frames—an assumption that breaks down in autoregressive models where different video chunks exhibit varying similarity patterns at identical timesteps.
In this paper, we present FlowCache, the first caching framework specifically designed for autoregressive video generation. Our key insight is that each video chunk should maintain independent caching policies, allowing fine-grained control over which chunks require recomputation at each timestep. We introduce a chunkwise caching strategy that dynamically adapts to the unique denoising characteristics of each chunk, complemented by an importance-based KV cache compression mechanism that maintains fixed memory bounds while preserving generation quality.
Our method achieves remarkable speedups of $\textbf{2.38}\times$ on MAGI-1 and $\textbf{6.7}\times$ on SkyReels-V2, with negligible quality degradation (VBench: $0.87\uparrow$ and $0.79\downarrow$ respectively). These results demonstrate that FlowCache, successfully unlocks the potential of autoregressive models for real-time, ultra-long video generation—establishing a new benchmark for efficient video synthesis at scale. The code is available at  https://anonymous.4open.science/r/FlowCache-23495iclrAnonymous
abstract_zh:
通常基于Transformer架构构建的自回归模型是通过按序列分块合成内容生成超长视频的强大范式，但这类序列生成过程的速度极慢是众所周知的问题。尽管缓存策略已被证明可有效加速传统视频扩散模型，但现有方法默认所有帧的去噪过程是统一的，这一假设并不适用于自回归模型，因为在自回归模型中，不同视频块在相同时间步会呈现出不同的相似性模式。
本文提出了首个专门为自回归视频生成设计的缓存框架FlowCache。我们的核心思路是每个视频块都应当维护独立的缓存策略，从而可以细粒度控制每个时间步需要重新计算的块。我们提出了可动态适配每个块独特去噪特性的分块缓存策略，同时搭配基于重要性的KV缓存压缩机制，可在维持固定内存上限的同时保留生成质量。
我们的方法在MAGI-1上实现了2.38倍的显著提速，在SkyReels-V2上实现了6.7倍的显著提速，同时质量下降可忽略不计（VBench指标分别为上升0.87和下降0.79）。这些结果表明FlowCache成功释放了自回归模型用于实时超长视频生成的潜力，为大规模高效视频合成建立了新的基准。代码可访问https://anonymous.4open.science/r/FlowCache-23495iclrAnonymous获取。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=20；命中=kv cache, cache compression, serving, compression, generation, autoregressive, transformer

title: SpaceServe: Spatial Multiplexing of Complementary Encoders and Decoders for Multimodal LLMs
abstract:
Recent multimodal large language models (MLLMs) marry modality-specific
vision or audio encoders with a shared text decoder. While the encoder is compute-
intensive but memory-light, the decoder is the opposite, yet state-of-the-art serving
stacks still time-multiplex these complementary kernels, idling SMs or HBM in
turn. We introduce SpaceServe, a serving system that space-multiplexes MLLMs:
it decouples all modality encoders from the decoder, and co-locates them on the
same GPU using fine-grained SM partitioning available in modern runtimes. A
cost-model-guided Space-Inference Scheduler (SIS) dynamically assigns SM slices,
while a Time-Windowed Shortest-Remaining-First (TWSRFT) policy batches en-
coder requests to minimise completion latency and smooth decoder arrivals. 
Evaluation shows that SpaceServe reduces time-per-output-token by 4.81×
on average and up to 28.9× on Nvidia A100 GPUs. SpaceServe is available at
https://github.com/gofreelee/SpaceServe
abstract_zh: 近年来的多模态大语言模型（MLLM）将模态专用的视觉或音频编码器与共享文本解码器相结合。编码器属于计算密集型但内存占用低，解码器则恰好相反，但当前最先进的服务栈仍对这些互补内核采用时分复用方式，导致流多处理器（SM）或高带宽内存（HBM）交替空闲。我们提出SpaceServe，这是一种采用空分复用方式调度多模态大语言模型的服务系统：它将所有模态编码器与解码器解耦，借助现代运行时支持的细粒度SM分区能力，将二者部署在同一GPU上运行。由成本模型引导的空间推理调度器（SIS）会动态分配SM分片，而时间窗口最短剩余优先（TWSRFT）策略会对编码器请求进行批处理，以最小化完成延迟并平滑解码器的请求到达。评估结果表明，在英伟达A100 GPU上，SpaceServe可将每个输出令牌的生成时间平均降至原来的1/4.81，最高降至原来的1/28.9。SpaceServe可在https://github.com/gofreelee/SpaceServe获取。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=23；命中=latency, serving, runtime, kernel, inference, decode, language model, large language model

title: Outlier-Aware Post-Training Quantization for Discrete Graph Diffusion Models
abstract: Discrete Graph Diffusion Models (DGDMs) mark a pivotal advancement in graph generation, effectively preserving sparsity and structural integrity, thereby enhancing the learning of graph data distributions for diverse generative applications. Despite their potential, DGDMs are computationally intensive due to the numerous low-parameter yet high-computation operations, thereby increasing the need of inference acceleration. A promising solution to mitigate this issue is model quantization. However, existing quantization techniques for Image Diffusion Models (IDMs) face limitations in DGDMs due to differing diffusion processes, while Large Language Model (LLM) quantization focuses on reducing memory access latency of loading large parameters, unlike DGDMs, where inference bottlenecks are computations due to smaller model sizes. To fill this gap, we introduce Bit-DGDM, a post-training quantization framework for DGDMs which incorporates two novel ideas: (i) sparse-dense activation quantization sparsely modeling the activation outliers through adaptively selected, data-free thresholds in full-precision and quantizing the remaining to low-bit, and (ii) ill-conditioned low-rank decomposition decomposing the weights into low-rank component enable faster inference and an $\alpha$-sparsity matrix that models outliers. Extensive experiments demonstrate that Bit-DGDM not only reducing the memory usage from the FP32 baseline by up to $2.8\times$ and achieve up to $2.5\times$ speedup, but also achieve comparable performance to ultra-low precision of up to 4-bit.
abstract_zh: 离散图扩散模型（DGDM）是图生成领域的关键进展，可有效保留稀疏性与结构完整性，从而提升图数据分布的学习效果，适用于各类生成应用。尽管DGDM具备良好发展潜力，但由于包含大量参数少、计算量高的运算，其计算密集度很高，因此推理加速的需求十分迫切。模型量化是缓解这一问题的可行方案。然而，现有面向图像扩散模型（IDM）的量化技术因扩散过程存在差异，应用于DGDM时存在局限；大语言模型（LLM）量化的核心目标是降低加载大参数时的内存访问延迟，这与DGDM的场景不同——DGDM模型规模更小，其推理瓶颈在于计算环节。为填补这一研究空白，我们提出Bit-DGDM，这是一种面向DGDM的训练后量化框架，包含两项创新思路：其一为稀疏-稠密激活量化，通过自适应选取的无数据阈值以全精度稀疏建模激活离群值，再将剩余值量化为低位宽；其二为病态低秩分解，将权重分解为可实现更快推理的低秩分量，以及用于建模离群值的α稀疏矩阵。大量实验证明，Bit-DGDM不仅可将FP32基线模型的内存占用最高降低2.8倍，实现最高2.5倍的推理加速，还能在最高4位的超低精度下取得与基线相当的性能。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=25；命中=latency, serving, quantization, 4-bit, low-bit, sparsity, inference, generation

title: FreqKV: Key-Value Compression in Frequency Domain for Context Window Extension
abstract: Existing key-value (KV) cache compression methods for large language models (LLMs) often rely on token eviction, which risks losing critical local information in both long prefilling and decoding scenarios. When extrapolating beyond the pretrained context length, their performance degrades sharply on long-context benchmarks. Motivated by the observation in the frequency domain that the context information is concentrated in the low-frequency components, we propose FreqKV, a parameter-free and architecture-agnostic approach. It iteratively compresses the increasing KV cache in the frequency domain, allowing models to process lengthy contexts efficiently. With minimal training at 8K length, FreqKV extends the context window of LLaMA-2-7B up to 256K tokens while maintaining stable perplexity. Extensive experiments on both prefilling and decoding stages demonstrate that FreqKV enables robust context window extension and consistently outperforms existing KV cache compression methods, highlighting its effectiveness for both understanding and generation in long contexts.
abstract_zh: 现有面向大语言模型（LLM）的键值（KV）缓存压缩方法通常依赖令牌逐出机制，这在长预填充和长解码场景下均存在丢失关键局部信息的风险。当外推超出预训练上下文长度时，这类方法在长上下文基准测试上的性能会急剧下降。受上下文信息集中于低频分量这一频域观测结果的启发，我们提出了FreqKV，一种无参数、与架构无关的方法。该方法在频域内对不断增长的KV缓存进行迭代压缩，使模型能够高效处理超长上下文。仅需在8K长度上进行极少量训练，FreqKV即可将LLaMA-2-7B的上下文窗口扩展至256K令牌，同时保持困惑度稳定。在预填充和解码两个阶段开展的大量实验表明，FreqKV能够实现稳健的上下文窗口扩展，性能始终优于现有KV缓存压缩方法，凸显了其在长上下文理解和生成两方面的有效性。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=21；命中=kv cache, cache compression, long context, prefill, compression, decoding, generation, language model

title: Effective Interplay between Sparsity and Quantization: From Theory to Practice
abstract: The increasing size of deep neural networks (DNNs) necessitates effective model compression to reduce their computational and memory footprints. Sparsity and quantization are two prominent compression methods that have been shown to reduce DNNs' computational and memory footprints significantly while preserving model accuracy. However, how these two methods interact when combined together remains a key question for developers, as many tacitly assume that they are orthogonal, meaning that their combined use does not introduce additional errors beyond those introduced by each method independently. In this paper, we provide the first mathematical proof that sparsity and quantization are non-orthogonal. We corroborate these results with experiments spanning a range of large language models, including the OPT and LLaMA model families (with 125M to 8B parameters), and vision models like ViT and ResNet. We show that the order in which we apply these methods matters because applying quantization before sparsity may disrupt the relative importance of tensor elements, which may inadvertently remove significant elements from a tensor. More importantly, we show that even if applied in the correct order, the compounded errors from sparsity and quantization can significantly harm accuracy. Our findings extend to the efficient deployment of large models in resource-constrained compute platforms to reduce serving cost, offering insights into best practices for applying these compression methods to maximize hardware resource efficiency without compromising accuracy.
abstract_zh: 深度神经网络（DNN）规模的持续增长使得有效的模型压缩成为必需，以降低其计算和内存开销。稀疏化和量化是两种主流的压缩方法，已被证实能够在保留模型精度的同时显著减少深度神经网络的计算和内存占用。然而，二者结合使用时的相互作用机制仍然是开发者面临的核心问题，很多研究默认二者是正交的，即二者联合使用时不会引入超出每种方法单独应用所产生误差之外的额外误差。本文首次通过数学证明证实稀疏化与量化并非正交。我们在一系列大语言模型（包括参数规模从1.25亿到80亿的OPT和LLaMA模型族）以及ViT、ResNet等视觉模型上开展了实验，验证了上述结论。我们发现，两种方法的应用顺序会对压缩效果产生影响，原因在于先执行量化再执行稀疏化可能破坏张量元素的相对重要性，进而可能意外移除张量中的重要元素。更重要的是，我们证实即使采用正确的应用顺序，稀疏化和量化带来的复合误差也会显著损害模型精度。我们的研究结果可应用于资源受限计算平台上的大模型高效部署以降低服务成本，同时为如何应用这些压缩方法在不损失精度的前提下最大化硬件资源效率提供了最佳实践参考。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=20；命中=serving, quantization, pruning, sparsity, compression, inference, language model, large language model

title: FreeKV: Boosting KV Cache Retrieval for Efficient LLM Inference
abstract:
Large language models (LLMs) have been widely deployed with rapidly expanding context windows to support increasingly demanding applications.
However, long contexts pose significant deployment challenges, primarily due to the KV cache whose size grows proportionally with context length.
While KV cache compression methods are proposed to address this issue, KV dropping methods incur considerable accuracy loss, and KV retrieval methods suffer from significant efficiency bottlenecks.
We propose FreeKV, an algorithm-system co-optimization framework to enhance KV retrieval efficiency while preserving accuracy.
On the algorithm side, FreeKV introduces speculative retrieval to shift the KV selection and recall processes out of the critical path, combined with fine-grained correction to ensure accuracy.
On the system side, FreeKV employs hybrid KV layouts across CPU and GPU memory to eliminate fragmented data transfers, and leverages double-buffered streamed recall to further improve efficiency, enabling effective overlap with computation, full latency hiding, and practical speedups from speculative recall.
Experiments demonstrate that FreeKV achieves near-lossless accuracy across various scenarios and models, delivering up to 13$\times$ speedup compared to SOTA KV retrieval methods.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）已得到广泛部署，其上下文窗口快速扩大，以支持要求不断提高的各类应用。然而，长上下文带来了突出的部署挑战，主要原因是KV缓存的规模会随上下文长度成比例增长。尽管学界已提出KV缓存压缩方法来解决这一问题，但KV丢弃方法会造成相当大的精度损失，KV检索方法则存在严重的效率瓶颈。我们提出FreeKV这一算法-系统协同优化框架，可在保持精度的同时提升KV检索效率。在算法层面，FreeKV引入推测式检索，将KV选择与召回过程移出关键路径，同时结合细粒度校正机制保障精度。在系统层面，FreeKV跨CPU和GPU内存采用混合KV布局以消除碎片化数据传输，同时利用双缓冲流式召回进一步提升效率，实现与计算的有效重叠、完全延迟隐藏，以及推测召回带来的实际加速收益。实验表明，FreeKV在各类场景和模型上均实现了近乎无损的精度，与现有最优KV检索方法相比，加速比最高可达13倍。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=27；命中=kv cache, cache compression, long context, latency, serving, compression, inference, language model

title: 70% Size, 100% Accuracy: Lossless LLM Compression for Efficient GPU Inference via Dynamic-Length Float (DFloat11)
abstract: Large-scale AI models, such as Large Language Models (LLMs) and Diffusion Models (DMs), have grown rapidly in size, creating significant challenges for efficient deployment on resource-constrained hardware. In this paper, we introduce Dynamic-Length Float (DFloat11), a lossless compression framework that reduces LLM and DM size by 30\% while preserving outputs that are bit-for-bit identical to the original model. DFloat11 is motivated by the low entropy in the BFloat16 weight representation of LLMs, which reveals significant inefficiency in the existing storage format. By applying entropy coding, DFloat11 assigns dynamic-length encodings to weights based on frequency, achieving near information-optimal compression without any loss of precision. To facilitate efficient inference with dynamic-length encodings, we develop a custom GPU kernel for fast online decompression. Our design incorporates the following: (i) compact, hierarchical lookup tables (LUTs) that fit within GPU SRAM for efficient decoding, (ii) a two-phase GPU kernel for coordinating thread read/write positions using lightweight auxiliary variables, and (iii) transformer-block-level decompression to minimize latency. Experiments on Llama 3.3, Qwen 3, Mistral 3, FLUX.1, and others validate our hypothesis that DFloat11 achieves around 30\% model size reduction while preserving bit-for-bit identical outputs. Compared to a potential alternative of offloading parts of an uncompressed model to the CPU to meet memory constraints, DFloat11 achieves 2.3--46.2$\times$ higher throughput in token generation. With a fixed GPU memory budget, DFloat11 enables 5.7--14.9$\times$ longer generation lengths than uncompressed models. Notably, our method enables lossless inference of Llama 3.1 405B, an 810GB model, on a single node equipped with 8$\times$80GB GPUs.
abstract_zh: 大规模AI模型（例如大语言模型（LLM）、扩散模型（DM））的规模近年来快速增长，给资源受限硬件上的高效部署造成了巨大挑战。本文提出动态长度浮点（DFloat11）这一无损压缩框架，可将大语言模型与扩散模型的体积缩减30%，同时保证输出与原始模型逐比特完全一致。DFloat11的设计动机源于大语言模型的BFloat16权重表示熵值较低，说明现有存储格式存在显著的效率浪费。通过应用熵编码，DFloat11根据权重出现的频率为其分配动态长度编码，在无任何精度损失的前提下实现接近信息论最优的压缩效果。为了实现基于动态长度编码的高效推理，我们开发了定制GPU内核以实现快速在线解压缩。我们的设计包含以下特性：（1）可适配GPU静态随机存取存储器（SRAM）的紧凑分层查找表（LUT），可实现高效解码；（2）两阶段GPU内核，通过轻量级辅助变量协调线程的读写位置；（3）Transformer块级解压缩机制，以最小化延迟。我们在Llama 3.3、Qwen 3、Mistral 3、FLUX.1等模型上开展的实验验证了我们的假设：DFloat11可实现约30%的模型体积缩减，同时保持逐比特完全一致的输出。与为满足内存约束将未压缩模型的部分负载卸载至CPU的可行替代方案相比，DFloat11的词元生成吞吐量提升了2.3~46.2倍。在GPU内存预算固定的情况下，DFloat11支持的生成长度是未压缩模型的5.7~14.9倍。值得注意的是，我们的方法可在搭载8块80GB GPU的单节点上实现体积达810GB的Llama 3.1 405B模型的无损推理。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=33；命中=throughput, latency, serving, kernel, compression, compressed, offloading, inference

title: PALMBENCH: A COMPREHENSIVE BENCHMARK OF COMPRESSED LARGE LANGUAGE MODELS ON MOBILE PLATFORMS
abstract:
Deploying large language models (LLMs) locally on mobile devices is advantageous in scenarios where transmitting data to remote cloud servers is either undesirable due to privacy concerns or impractical due to network connection. Recent advancements have facilitated the local deployment of LLMs. However, local deployment also presents challenges, particularly in balancing quality (generative performance), latency, and throughput within the hardware constraints of mobile devices. In this paper, we introduce our lightweight, all-in-one automated benchmarking framework that allows users to evaluate LLMs on mobile devices. We provide a comprehensive benchmark of various popular LLMs with different quantization configurations (both weights and activations) across multiple mobile platforms with varying hardware capabilities. Unlike traditional benchmarks that assess full-scale models on high-end GPU clusters, we focus on evaluating resource efficiency (memory and power consumption) and harmful output for compressed models on mobile devices. Our key observations include: i) differences in energy efficiency and throughput across mobile platforms; ii) the impact of quantization on memory usage, GPU execution time, and power consumption; and iii) accuracy and performance degradation of quantized models compared to their non-quantized counterparts; and iv) the frequency of hallucinations and toxic content generated by compressed LLMs on
mobile devices.
abstract_zh: 在因隐私顾虑不宜向远程云服务器传输数据，或因网络连接问题导致该操作不切实际的场景下，在移动设备上本地部署大语言模型（LLM）具备显著优势。近期的技术进展为大语言模型的本地部署提供了支撑，但本地部署也带来了诸多挑战，尤其是如何在移动设备的硬件约束下平衡质量（生成性能）、延迟与吞吐量。本文提出了一款轻量级、一体化的自动化基准测试框架，用户可借助该框架在移动设备上评估大语言模型的性能。我们针对多款主流大语言模型，在硬件性能各不相同的多个移动平台上，基于不同的量化配置（涵盖权重与激活量化）开展了全面的基准测试。传统基准测试针对高端GPU集群上的全尺寸模型开展评估，与这类测试不同，我们聚焦于评估移动设备上压缩模型的资源效率（内存与功耗）以及有害输出情况。我们的核心观测结论包括：一、不同移动平台的能效与吞吐量存在差异；二、量化对内存占用、GPU执行时长以及功耗存在影响；三、与非量化版本相比，量化模型的准确率与性能有所下降；四、移动设备上的压缩大语言模型生成幻觉内容与有毒内容的出现频率。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=20；命中=throughput, latency, quantization, quantized, compressed, language model, large language model, llm

title: Channel-Aware Mixed-Precision Quantization for Efficient Long-Context Inference
abstract:
The key-value (KV) cache plays a vital role in accelerating autoregressive inference for large language models (LLMs). However, its linear memory growth with sequence length poses significant memory bottlenecks, especially in long-context scenarios.
Quantization offers a promising solution for memory efficiency. While existing methods typically apply channel-wise quantization to the key cache and token-wise quantization to the value cache, they suffer from severe performance degradation under low-bit configurations. Our analysis reveals that quantization sensitivity varies across individual KV channels, presenting an opportunity for non-uniform bit allocation. Following this finding, we propose ChanMix, a mixed-precision quantization framework that supports channel-wise quantization on 2-bit setting with FP8 precision with a custom Triton kernel implementation. To improve low-bit quantization performance, we introduce a channel-aware bit reallocation strategy, which allocates bits across channel sensitivity.
Through extensive evaluation, ChanMix demonstrates superior performance across the NIAH, RULER, and InfiniteBench benchmarks for the Llama, Mistral, and Qwen model families, achieving improvements of at least 5 absolute percentage points on RULER compared to all baseline methods. Additionally, ChanMix enables a 2.3× increase in batch size and supports a 1.5× longer context length during inference.
abstract_zh:
键值（KV）缓存在加速大语言模型（LLM）的自回归推理过程中发挥着关键作用。然而，其内存占用随序列长度线性增长，会带来严重的内存瓶颈，在长上下文场景中这一问题尤为突出。
量化是提升内存效率的极具潜力的解决方案。现有方法通常对键缓存应用通道级量化，对值缓存应用令牌级量化，但这类方法在低位宽配置下会出现严重的性能下降。我们的分析表明，不同KV通道的量化敏感度存在差异，这为非均匀位宽分配提供了契机。基于这一发现，我们提出ChanMix，这一混合精度量化框架通过自定义Triton内核实现，支持2位设置下的通道级量化与FP8精度。为提升低位量化性能，我们引入了通道感知位宽重分配策略，根据各通道的敏感度分配位宽。
经广泛评估验证，ChanMix在Llama、Mistral和Qwen模型族的NIAH、RULER与InfiniteBench基准测试中均表现出更优性能，与所有基线方法相比，其在RULER基准上的性能提升至少达到5个绝对百分点。此外，ChanMix可将推理阶段的批大小提升2.3倍，同时支持的上下文长度延长1.5倍。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=27；命中=long context, kernel, quantization, 2-bit, low-bit, mixed-precision, inference, autoregressive

title: KVTuner: Sensitivity-Aware Layer-Wise Mixed-Precision KV Cache Quantization for Efficient and Nearly Lossless LLM Inference
abstract:
KV cache quantization can improve Large Language Models (LLMs) inference throughput and latency in long contexts and large batch-size scenarios while preserving LLMs effectiveness.
However, current methods have three unsolved issues: overlooking layer-wise sensitivity to KV cache quantization, high overhead of online fine-grained decision-making, and low flexibility to different LLMs and constraints. Therefore, we theoretically analyze the inherent correlation of layer-wise transformer attention patterns to KV cache quantization errors and study why key cache is generally more important than value cache for quantization error reduction. We further propose a simple yet effective framework KVTuner to adaptively search for the optimal hardware-friendly layer-wise KV quantization precision pairs for coarse-grained KV cache with multi-objective optimization and directly utilize the offline searched configurations during online inference.
To reduce the computational cost of offline calibration, we utilize the intra-layer KV precision pair pruning and inter-layer clustering to reduce the search space. Experimental results show that we can achieve nearly lossless 3.25-bit mixed precision KV cache quantization for LLMs like Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct and 4.0-bit for sensitive models like Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct on mathematical reasoning tasks. The maximum inference throughput can be improved by 21.25\% compared with KIVI-KV8 quantization over various context lengths. Our code and searched configurations are available at https://github.com/cmd2001/KVTuner.
abstract_zh: KV缓存量化可在长上下文和大批次场景下提升大语言模型（LLM）的推理吞吐量、降低推理延迟，同时保留大语言模型的性能。然而现有方法存在三个尚未解决的问题：忽视了各层对KV缓存量化的敏感度、在线细粒度决策的开销高、对不同大语言模型和约束条件的适配灵活性低。因此，我们从理论层面分析了Transformer各层注意力模式与KV缓存量化误差的内在关联，探究了在降低量化误差方面键缓存通常比值缓存更重要的原因。我们进一步提出了一个简单且有效的框架KVTuner，该框架通过多目标优化为粗粒度KV缓存自适应搜索对硬件友好的最优分层KV量化精度对，在线推理阶段可直接使用离线搜索得到的配置。为降低离线校准的计算成本，我们利用层内KV精度对剪枝和层间聚类来缩小搜索空间。实验结果表明，针对Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct这类大语言模型，我们可实现接近无损的3.25比特混合精度KV缓存量化；针对Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct这类对量化敏感的模型，我们在数学推理任务上可实现4.0比特的接近无损量化。在多种上下文长度下，与KIVI-KV8量化方法相比，我们的方法最高可将推理吞吐量提升21.25%。我们的代码和搜索得到的配置可在https://github.com/cmd2001/KVTuner获取。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=34；命中=kv cache, long context, throughput, latency, serving, quantization, pruning, mixed-precision

title: SALS: Sparse Attention in Latent Space for KV Cache Compression
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) capable of handling extended contexts are in high demand, yet their inference remains challenging due to substantial Key-Value (KV) cache size and high memory bandwidth requirements. Previous research has demonstrated that KV cache exhibits low-rank characteristics within the hidden dimension, suggesting the potential for effective compression. However, due to the widely adopted Rotary Position Embedding (RoPE) mechanism in modern LLMs, naive low‑-rank compression suffers severe accuracy degradation or creates a new speed bottleneck, as the low-rank cache must first be reconstructed in order to apply RoPE. In this paper, we introduce two key insights: first, the application of RoPE to the key vectors increases their variance, which in turn results in a higher rank; second, after the key vectors are transformed into the latent space, they largely maintain their representation across most layers. Based on these insights, we propose the Sparse Attention in Latent Space (SALS) framework. SALS projects the KV cache into a compact latent space via low-rank projection, and performs sparse token selection using RoPE-free query--key interactions in this space. By reconstructing only a small subset of important tokens, it avoids the overhead of full KV cache reconstruction. We comprehensively evaluate SALS on various tasks using two large-scale models: LLaMA2-7b-chat and Mistral-7b, and additionally verify its scalability on the RULER-128k benchmark with LLaMA3.1-8B-Instruct. Experimental results demonstrate that SALS achieves SOTA performance by maintaining competitive accuracy. Under different settings, SALS achieves 6.4-fold KV cache compression and 5.7-fold speed-up in the attention operator compared to FlashAttention2 on the 4K sequence. For the end-to-end throughput performance, we achieves 1.4-fold and 4.5-fold improvement compared to GPT-fast on 4k and 32K sequences, respectively. The source code will be publicly available in the future.
abstract_zh: 能够处理长上下文的大语言模型（LLM）需求旺盛，但由于键值（KV）缓存规模庞大、内存带宽要求高，其推理过程仍存在挑战。已有研究证明，KV缓存在隐层维度上具备低秩特性，说明其存在高效压缩的潜力。然而，由于现代大语言模型广泛采用旋转位置嵌入（RoPE）机制，朴素低秩压缩会出现严重的精度下降，或是产生新的速度瓶颈，因为要应用RoPE必须先重建低秩缓存。本文提出两项核心洞见：第一，对键向量应用RoPE会增大其方差，进而导致秩升高；第二，键向量被转换到隐空间后，其表示在绝大多数层中基本保持稳定。基于上述洞见，我们提出隐空间稀疏注意力（SALS）框架。SALS通过低秩投影将KV缓存映射到紧凑的隐空间中，并在该空间内使用无需RoPE的查询-键交互完成稀疏令牌选择。通过仅重建一小部分重要令牌，SALS避免了完整KV缓存重建带来的开销。我们使用LLaMA2-7b-chat和Mistral-7b两个大规模模型在多种任务上对SALS开展了全面评估，另外还通过LLaMA3.1-8B-Instruct在RULER-128k基准测试上验证了其可扩展性。实验结果表明，SALS在保持具备竞争力的精度的同时实现了当前最优性能。在不同设置下，针对4K长度序列，SALS相比FlashAttention2实现了6.4倍的KV缓存压缩率，注意力算子速度提升5.7倍。端到端吞吐量性能方面，针对4K和32K长度序列，我们的方法相比GPT-fast分别实现了1.4倍和4.5倍的提升。源代码未来将公开发布。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=20；命中=kv cache, cache compression, throughput, compression, inference, language model, large language model, llm

title: Attention Is All You Need for KV Cache in Diffusion LLMs
abstract: This work studies how to adaptively recompute key–value (KV) caches for diffusion large language models (DLMs) to maximize prediction accuracy while minimizing decoding latency. Prior methods' decoders recompute QKV for all tokens at every denoising step and layer, despite KV states changing little across most steps, especially in shallow layers, leading to substantial redundancy. We make three observations: (1) distant MASK tokens primarily act as a length-bias and can be cached block-wise beyond the active prediction window; (2) KV dynamics increase with depth, suggesting that selective refresh starting from deeper layers is sufficient; and (3) the most-attended token exhibits the smallest KV drift, providing a conservative lower bound on cache change for other tokens. Building on these, we propose Elastic-Cache, a training-free, architecture-agnostic strategy that jointly decides ${when}$ to refresh (via an attention-aware drift test on the most-attended token) and ${where}$ to refresh (via a depth-aware schedule that recomputes from a chosen layer onward while reusing shallow-layer caches and off-window MASK caches). Unlike fixed-period schemes, Elastic-Cache performs adaptive, layer-aware cache updates for diffusion LLMs, reducing redundant computation and accelerating decoding with negligible loss in generation quality. Experiments on LLaDA-Instruct, LLaDA-1.5, and LLaDA-V across mathematical reasoning and code generation tasks demonstrate consistent speedups: $8.7\times$ on GSM8K (256 tokens), $45.1\times$ on longer sequences, and $4.8\times$ on HumanEval, while consistently maintaining higher accuracy than the baseline. Our method achieves significantly higher throughput ($6.8\times$ on GSM8K) than existing confidence-based approaches while preserving generation quality, enabling practical deployment of diffusion LLMs.
abstract_zh: 本研究探讨如何为扩散大语言模型（DLM）自适应重计算键值（KV）缓存，以在最小化解码延迟的同时最大化预测准确率。现有方法的解码器会在每个去噪步、每一层为所有令牌重计算QKV，然而KV状态在绝大多数步骤中变化极小，在浅层中尤为明显，因此会产生大量冗余。我们得出三项观察结论：第一，远距离MASK令牌主要起到长度偏置作用，可在活跃预测窗口之外按块缓存；第二，KV的动态性随网络深度增加而提升，说明从深层开始选择性刷新即可满足需求；第三，受关注度最高的令牌的KV漂移最小，可为其他令牌的缓存变化量提供保守下界。基于上述结论，我们提出Elastic-Cache，这是一种无需训练、与架构无关的策略，可同时决定何时刷新（通过对受关注度最高的令牌执行注意力感知漂移测试）以及何处刷新（通过深度感知调度，从选定层开始重计算，同时复用浅层缓存和窗口外的MASK缓存）。与固定周期方案不同，Elastic-Cache为扩散大语言模型执行自适应的层感知缓存更新，能够减少冗余计算、加速解码，且生成质量损失可忽略不计。我们在数学推理、代码生成任务上对LLaDA-Instruct、LLaDA-1.5和LLaDA-V开展实验，结果显示该方法实现了稳定的加速效果：在GSM8K（256令牌）上达到8.7倍加速，在更长序列上达到45.1倍加速，在HumanEval上达到4.8倍加速，同时准确率始终高于基线方法。我们的方法在保留生成质量的同时，吞吐量显著高于现有基于置信度的方法（在GSM8K上为其6.8倍），可支撑扩散大语言模型的实际部署。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=20；命中=kv cache, throughput, latency, serving, decode, decoding, generation, language model

title: PROXSPARSE: REGULARIZED LEARNING OF SEMI-STRUCTURED SPARSITY MASKS FOR PRETRAINED LLMS
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated exceptional performance in natural language processing tasks, yet their massive size makes serving them inefficient and costly. Semi-structured pruning has emerged as an effective method for model acceleration, but existing approaches are suboptimal because they focus on local, layer-wise optimizations using heuristic rules, failing to leverage global feedback. We present ProxSparse, a learning-based framework for mask selection enabled by regularized optimization. ProxSparse transforms the rigid, non-differentiable mask selection process into a smoother optimization procedure, allowing gradual mask exploration with flexibility. ProxSparse does not involve additional weight updates once the mask is determined. Our extensive evaluations on 7 widely used models show that ProxSparse consistently outperforms previously proposed semi-structured mask selection methods with significant improvement, demonstrating the effectiveness of our learned approach towards semi-structured pruning.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型在自然语言处理任务中展现出了卓越性能，但其庞大的规模导致部署效率低下、成本高昂。半结构化剪枝已成为一种有效的模型加速方法，但现有方法效果欠佳，因为这类方法采用启发式规则聚焦于局部逐层优化，未能利用全局反馈。我们提出ProxSparse，这是一种依托正则化优化实现掩码选择的基于学习的框架。ProxSparse将刚性、不可微的掩码选择过程转化为更平滑的优化流程，可灵活地逐步探索掩码。掩码确定后，ProxSparse无需进行额外的权重更新。我们针对7种常用模型开展的大量评估表明，ProxSparse始终显著优于此前提出的半结构化掩码选择方法，证明了我们提出的半结构化剪枝学习方法的有效性。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=17；命中=serving, pruning, sparsity, language model, large language model, llm
